boardgame: Der Kleine Prinz: Planet der Astronomie
description:
A "The Little Prince"-themed children's game about matching and remembering shapes and colours.

Background story: Antoine, the astronomer, has been talked into stealing the stars of the universe for his planetarium by the evil snake. But the stars are essential for all the inhabitants of the planets. Without their light, plants, animals and people cannot live. Help the Little Prince convince Antoine to set the stars free. Accompany the Prince and his friend, the fox, when they return the stars to their place in the universe.

Setup: The board is placed at the middle of the table. Airplane and die are set aside. The 6 snake tiles are placed on the planet at the center of the board. The outline star tiles are shuffled and placed face up all over the room. The star tiles with the filled-in stars are shuffled and distributed evenly among the players, face down. Each player selects a player character on the board and places the stack of face-down star tiles in front of themselves.

Gameplay: The start player takes the airplane. He/she then looks at the top tile of their star tiles stack. They try to remember its colour and shape. The active player then says "start" and moves around the room, looking for the matching outline star tile. When they find it, they put it into a slit on the plane and fly back to the Planet of Astronomy. They then put the matching pair of tiles in front of them, turn over the second tile from their stack and try again to find the matching tile.
As soon as the active player has embarked on his/her flight, the player to his/her left rolls the die until the snake symbol appears. He/she then takes a snake tile from the board and passes on the die to the next player. The other players continue rolling the die until the last snake tile has been taken from the board. This means that the active player's flight phase is over and it's the next player's turn.

The game ends when a player has collected his/her last pair of tiles. The current round is continued until each player has had an equal number of turns. All players that only have complete pairs of tiles after this round win the game.

"Enforce the Law and Race to become Chief Inspector".

"Highway Patrol" is a Children's Board Game in which each player, being a Patrol Officer, caries out specific Police Duties corresponding to which space they land on.

There are five moveable markers, being police cars and players progress along spaces by throwing the two dice and following the instructions on each space.

There is a set of Random cards, a set of Action cards and a Police Fund collection box.  There are "Reward Point" token to be collected.

The winner of the game is the first Patrol Officer to accumulate 2000 Reward Points, which enables them to be promoted to Detective  Sergeant.

(from the instructions)

Game is by Tod Games.

boardgame: Mia and me: Rettet die Einhörner!
description:
A children's card game set in the universe of the 2011 live action/CGI animated television series "Mia and me".

Background story: Centopia's unicorns are in danger! Collect as many unicorn tears as possible, find all parts of the golden Trumptus and skillfully use the elves' weapons to fend off the evil Panthea.

Aim of the game: Collect the most valuable cards to reach the highest number of points and win the game. The game ends when all pieces of the Trumptus have been found. The unicorns will be safe from Panthea!

Gameplay: The cards are shuffled and placed in a face-down stack. The youngest player turns over the top card. He/she may also turn over several cards. But it is possible that he/she doesn't get to keep any of them so players should consider carefully how big a risk they are willing to take.

The cards and their significance:
The ring: With this ring, the elves are calling each other for help. The player puts this card in front of him/herself and his/her turn is over.
The Trumptus: When all the pieces of the Trumptus have been found, the unicorns are safe. The card is placed at the center of the table, where the individual pieces form a picture of the Trumptus. The player's turn is over. If there are elven or unicorn cards in the middle of the table, the player gets to take them and put them in front of him/herself.
A crying unicorn: This card is particularly valuable. It stays in the middle of the table until the active player's turn is over. The player may turn over another card if he/she wants.
Elf with water bracelet: This card helps the player in the battle against Panthea. The card stays in the middle of the table until the active player's turn is over. The player may turn over another card if he/she wants.

A player who doesn't want to turn over another card may end his/her turn and take all the open elven and unicorn cards. But first he/she has to fight a duel with Panthea. With luck, he/she will be able to fight off Panthea's snake lassos. For the duel, the 6 chips are thrown into the air. Then players count how many green and blue symbols can be seen. More blue than green symbols: The player wins the duel. Equal number: If there is an elven card in the middle of the table or in front of the player, he/she can still fend off Panthea. The snake lasso and an elven card are set aside and the player wins the duel and gets to take the cards. More green than blue symbols: The player loses the duel. The open elven, unicorn and snake lasso cards are returned to the card stack, which is then shuffled again and it's the next player's turn.

End of game: The game ends when all six Trumptus cards have been turned over. Players count the points on the cards they have collected. The player with the highest number of points wins the game.

boardgame: Mia and me: Mia und ihre Freunde
description:
A children's game set in the universe of the 2011 live action/CGI animated television series "Mia and me".

The game contains 36 cards with 4 symbols from the world of Centopia on each card. The cards are placed face up on the table. The same symbols are also printed on three dice. For each possible die roll combination there are exactly three correct cards. As soon as the active player has rolled the dice, all players simultaneously search for the corresponding cards. When a player has found such a card, he/she marks it with their player chit. When all three cards have been marked, players who have marked a correct card get to keep that card. Player chits on incorrect cards get removed from the game for the next round. The first player to collect 8 or more cards wins the game.

The Tiki are back in the latest installment of the Tiki Games Universe.

Tiki Poker is an easy-to-learn card game for four people.  It is a poker game with some fun twists.

In this game you have been chosen to represent your Polynesian tribe at the annual Poker Showdown. You've been given the awesome responsibility to play a high stakes poker game to collect valuable sand dollars for your tribe. If you win, you will live in luxury for many years... but if you loose you'll be looking for a new home.  The stakes could not be higher!

This is not your normal poker game!

In Tiki Poker you'll make decisions that affect future hands, play multiple hands from the same deck, and decide if you want to bet that you have the best or worst hand... or both at the same time!  You'll need all your wits when you play because while the Tiki can bring you some luck, you also will need to plan ahead.  Because the deck is used for more than one hand the cards that are played early are not available later.

Of course, all the fun of poker is here, the betting, the bluffing, and the joy of winning all those sand dollars!

So put on your best lei, don your Tiki Poker face and dive into a fun, fast paced card game that is both familiar and fresh at the same time!

Why First? is a racing game in which players vie to be second in order to win.

The game lasts five races, with each race lasting five turns. At the start of each race, players receive a hand of five cards, with each card bearing a value from -4 to +5. The players' pawns start each race on 0 of the racing track. Each turn, players choose a card from their hands, reveal them simultaneously, determine player order (with a higher absolute value going earlier than a lower one), then move any pawn forward or backward based on the card they played.

After five turns, the race ends and the player in second place on the racing track wins, scoring a number of points equal to his position on the race track. All other players score nothing. (If the winner is in the negative portion of the racing track, he loses points instead.) After five races, sum all the points scored; the player with the second highest total who has won at least one race wins.

Saving the Earth with Gullia
The little girl Gullia go around the planet Earth and try to remove all the 12 pollution that covers it.
Players are team to answer correctly questions about nature and environment or making mimes.
If the Earth is released from the pollution if the whole round is over, all players have won!
If there is at least one polluted place everyone has lost the game.

Баттелі, or Illomi Chess, is a two-player strategy game played on a 20 square isometric board.

During the game each player's swordmen battles it out with each other in an abstract chain reaction battle, until there is a victor. Each of these battles bring the player's closer to the goal of capturing the king.

Rules are available in the conlang Delang (both Delang script and Latin script), English and Norwegian. All game components are language neutral. No game components are supplied from publisher.

Rules:

     Each pawn can move to an adjacent square, but only one pawn may move backwards. Use a way to mark such a pawn.
     If a pawn has moved backward, no pawn can move backwards until a pawn has moved forward.
     The pawn that has moved backwards cannot move until a pawn has moved forward.
     If you do not have two swordmen more than your opponent, you may use your move to breed a new swordman.
     Only the king can breed new swordmen.
     The new swordman must be placed in an adjacing square to the king, and there has to be a swordman on the square already.
     You have to move a newly breed swordman.
     You have to kill an opponent's swordman that's in front of your's.
     If an opponent's swordman is in front of a newly breed swordman, you have to kill that swordman with your newly breed swordman.

A turn based, card driven, strategic game system, epic in scope, which focuses on the period between 1660 and 1789 (and will, at one point, will include an expansion for the age of Napoleon). Games will include (with emphasis on possible order of release): 1) The Great Northern War, 2) The Great Turkish War of 1683-1789, 3) The Seven Years War, 4) The War of Austrian Succession, 5) The War of Spanish Succession, 6) The War of Polish Succession. The Williamite War in Ireland is also planned for release as a teaching game due to its small and contained scope.

When combined, the maps for these separate, self contained modules will sprawl from Portugal to Britain to the plains and forests of Peter the Great's snow blasted Russia and the Barbary Coast and slave markets of Northern Africa. As well as colonies around the world.

A future module will tie these together with international events, diplomacy, technologies and more. Players will be challenged to build a powerful nation-state, levy taxes, develop their military advancements, build and dismantle fortresses, stay ahead of their enemies, and manage over 300 cards and events to survive the era. As the monarchs of Europe develop their nations armies and military, the rules used for campaigning will actually change to simulate the stunning advancements in theory, logistics, and capabilities of the era.

CURRENTLY IN DEVELOPMENT. MAY BE A FIVE YEAR PROJECT. It has taken several years to collect the 5000 records for fortifications of Europe in this time frame. A massively time consuming project in and of itself as multiple sources (known and unknown) had to be combined as well as endless combing through the internet to locate fortresses seemingly unknown to all of the key sources. We feel, however, it has been worth it. Eastern Europe was particularly daunting.

boardgame: Power Grid: Northern Europe/United Kingdom & Ireland
description:
Power Grid: Northern Europe/United Kingdom & Ireland is a pair of expansions for Power Grid that includes twelve new power plant cards exclusive for Northern Europe!

• Northern Europe: The seven countries in Northern Europe use very different energy sources for their electricity production. Depending on which regions are chosen the players will be confronted with a changed set of power plants.

• United Kingdom & Ireland: The players can operate two different networks on these two isles. With no direct connection between Ireland and Great Britain, starting the second network costs the player dearly. Additionally, this region changed from a resource exporter to an importer in a very short time, so »Step 3« starts earlier when playing on this map.

Chess taken to the 4th dimension. Played on a 4x4x4x4 board, it includes all the traditional chess pieces, with a slightly different number. Each player has 1 king, 1 queen, 2 rooks, 4 bishops, 4 knights and 21 pawns. Players also have 3 types of fairy pieces: 2 unicorns (moving like a knight with an additional single cell right angle move), 2 wizards (moving like a bishop, but only to other levels in the 3rd & 4th dimensions) and 1 minstrel (a non-capturing piece, that moves like a rook and prevents orthogonally adjacent pieces from making a capturing move). Players take turns moving pieces one at a time, but a variant allows players 2 moves a turn to speed up the game. The goal is to checkmate the king.

Some of the traditional pieces have altered moves, beyond the 4 dimensional aspect. The king only moves and captures orthogonally one space. Pawns move orthogonally one space and captures diagonally one space. Rooks move and capture orthogonally, but also changes direction at a right angle once it meets a wall. Bishops move on 2D diagonals and on 3D or 4D diagonals (but not on the 2D diagonals of other levels). Knights move as chess knights. The Queen moves as a Chesseract rook or bishop.

One huge challenge in Chesseract is to try and envision moves in 4 dimensions. While each 2 dimensional level is only 4x4, learning to extend the moves beyond is a little brain-burning.

Chesseract was created by Jim Aikin

boardgame: Ticket to Ride Map Collection 3: The Heart of Africa
description:
Game description from the publisher:

Set in the vast wilderness of Africa at the height of its exploration by intrepid explorers, missionaries and adventurers, Ticket to Ride Map Collection 3: The Heart of Africa, a single-sided expansion map for Ticket to Ride or Ticket to Ride: Europe, focuses on the central and southern "heart" of the continent displayed in a vertical format.

This expansion introduces 45 new terrain cards, divided into three different terrain types. Each type is associated with different route colors: Desert/Savanna cards for yellow, orange and red routes; Jungle/Forest cards for green, blue and purple routes; and Mountain/Cliff cards for black, white and grey routes. Players can draw terrain cards just like train cards and they may use these to double the value of the routes they claim, under certain conditions.

Part of Ticket to Ride series.

This is a standalone, travel-sized German Trivial Pursuit, based on the theme of 'Lifestyle'.

This is a two-player (or two teams) game, with simplified Trivial Pursuit rules.
By rolling the dice and picking up a colour, each person can answer the relevant question and win (or not) the coloured section to be inserted in a slot.

The winner is the person (or team) who completes all colours in the slots.

From back cover:

"Volume 5 presents eleven historical scenarios that follow Wellington's famous Peninsular victories from Vimiero in 1808 until the final victory at Toulouse in 1814.

All are designed for use with General de Brigade Napoleonic wargames rules, (but may be converted to any similar system). Each scenario provides palayers with terrain maps, troop deployments, objectives and orders of battle. Several of the scenarios are linked and cam be played as part of a mini-campaign or, as in the case of the Vimiero or Busaco scenarios, combined to fight one large action.

Wellington's Victories

     Vimiero 1808 - Vimiero Hill
     Vimiero 1808 - Action at Ventosa
     Talavera 1809 - Battle for the Medellin
     Talavera 1809 - Battle for the Redoubt
     Busaco 1810 - Reynier's Assault
     Busaco 1810 - Ney's Assault
     Salamanca 1812 - Clausel's Counter-Attack
     Salamanca 1812 - The French Rearguard
     Vittoria 1813 - The Hill of Arinex
     Sorauren 1813 - Cole's Ridge
     Toulouse 1814 - Final Victory

boardgame: Judge Dredd Miniatures Game: Block War
description:
From the Publisher's website***
        
    


Block War is the first supplement for the Judge Dredd miniatures game, adding new forces and new rules for the latest wave of Mega-City One models.
 Inside you will find rules for bringing vehicles to the tabletop, for waging huge riots, invasions, and block wars, as well as new forces such as the Brit-Cit Justice Department and Citi-Def squads - all guaranteed to bring mayhem to your games!
 We are constantly updating Block War, and it will always be available as a free download, allowing you to dive straight in and start playing. All forces and new rules build upon the campaign system presented in the core rulebook, allowing you to expand your games to the furthest reaches of the comic strip, past the walls of Mega-City One and into the world beyond.
 May your Lawgiver always fire true!

Perilous Pyramids is a competitive multiplayer card game in which players assume the roles of archeologists each racing to the center chamber of a pyramid.

Each player builds their own side of the pyramid using ten face-down chamber cards. Players clear chambers by playing resource cards from their hand that meet or exceed the difficulty of the chamber.

These same resource cards can be used to make the opponents' chambers even harder to overcome, swap revealed chamber cards, cancel a played card and more.

The first player to clear their pyramid completely and clear the final central chamber wins the game.

Game-play is fast and aggressive, as the game encourages players to sabotage the efforts of their opponents.

User Summary

Tumble down is a game of patience and careful balance, in the mode of Jenga.  Players take turn removing blocks from the tower and relocating them onto the top to create a more treacherous play area with each turn.

Party game. 14 attractive cards are placed around the room and up to 14 guests are invited to guess the answers to the 14 clue cards. All answers must include the word or phrase that include the word Fire or Fires in it. The clues range from easy to downright impossible (Example: Clue - Unsportsmanlike Incendiarism? Answer - Backfire). See what I mean?

At the host's call, the competition stops and the guest with the most correct answers, wins.

Abracadabra (Who are you? A creative liar or a walking encyclopedia?) is a party game based on the game Dictionary. Here’s how it works: pick a card from the deck, see the word on it and read it out loud. This word is most likely UNKNOWN to you but painfully SIMILAR to a word that you most likely do know.  
Your task is to summon your deepest knowledge or, even better, your acting skills, and convince the other players that you know exactly what the word means.

What is an Enchiridion? A collapsible folder? A Mexican food? A rare-earth metal? Do you have your own version?

There is only one word, but everyone will have their own interpretation of what it means. You have to convince everyone that YOUR definition of the word is correct. The key to Abracadabra lies in a player’s ability to improvise the definitions and persuade all the other players that you really do know what you're talking about. Players earn points by convincing other players that their definition is correct, or at least close to correct, by betting on the most accurate definitions and by actually getting the definition right.

Millennium Gravitas is a tactical science-fiction cardgame of eXploration, eXpansion, eXploitation, and eXtermination (4X). The game features Invasion and Colonization of Planets, Technology Research, Tactical Space-combat, Ship Construction and Customization, Anomalies and Resource Management.

Gameplay consists of building structures (ex. energy collectors) to gather resources, colonize planets to man your spaceships and research technology to equip your fleet, planets and shipyard.

This is accomplished by drawing cards from your deck and playing them according to various rules and time-based mechanics (building ships takes time for example).

The goal of the game is to defeat the opposing player according to three different victory-conditions:

Extinction - The opposing player loses all Population (by invasion or destruction of planets or structures)

Starvation - The opposing player loses all Energy (by stealing energy or destroying collectors)

Burnout - The opposing player loses the ability to play Units (by losing all cards in his main deck and hand)

Millennium Gravitas relies less on luck-of-the-draw than other comparable games and gives the player more control over their strategy. The core mechanics are focused on flexibility and tactics rather than deck-building or owning the most powerful cards to overpower your opponent.

The game supports different game-modes, like 1vs1, 2vs2 or +4vs (the mechanics theoretically do not limit the amount of players that are able to play together or against each other). All complex play styles are supported, from team-matches to last-man-standing. This creates many interesting games with temporary alliances and even politics as emergent game-play. Additionally Millennium does not require a game-board or sophisticated game-pieces and miniatures, all you need to play is knowledge of the rules, your own deck, and something to be used as tokens.

At the 2012 Essen Spiel, the following 5 promo cards will be given to customers that pre-order Uchronia:


     Nursery
     Ranch
     Canal
     Pterodrome
     Embassy


The Uchronia Promo Cards are a set of 5 cards to be added to those in the Uchronia base game. They are simply shuffled in with the other building cards, adding more variety from game to game.

Tactical Conflict is an tactical wargame. It's played on a hex boardgame with different futuristic units and cards. With its simple rules, the game is easy to learn and fast to play.

Two players face each other on a 8x18 hexes battlefield. Before the game begins each player chooses cards and buys an army of different units up to a fixed amount of money. The players start on the opposite side of the battlefield. Each player got three actions per turn, where he can command his units or play a card, which affects units or the battlefield itself. There are different hex types like swamp, street, bridge or forest. Some objects like buildings, cliffs and rocks may block the line of sight or can't be entered. Every unit has its special ability, like shooting through buildings for example, or climbing up rocks. Some other objects may give you protection and a hideout. The player who defeats all opposing units wins the game.

Object of the Game is to build tower with 6 floors.

In his turn player removes a column from the tower and places it a higher floor. As soon as 10 columns are placed new floor begins. The color of the column player must to remove and place is indicated by the dice.

If a player is clumsy and the tower collapses player is removed from the game. last player in game is the winner.

User summary

A stand-alone expansion to Cryptids TV. Contains more (biligual Japanese/English) monster description cards and more headlines, notepads and cards necessary to play the game. Timer and pen is not included.

Cryptids TV is a party game where players draw imaginary creatures
based on a short description and a newspaper headline, and then, on a timer, present each other's creations as if they were show hosts on a TV show.

Can be combined with the material in CryptidsTV.

User summary
An action selection game with a witch theme and the quirky mechanism that the total number of resources in every player's hands must be enough for you to perform your selected action. The more advanced actions are performed first, so players must try make educated guesses on what resources the other players have.

Actions include to collect mana, summon monsters (which will give you special powers), attack other players (to get points) or simply collect points, among others. What monsters are available for summon varies for each game.

Game ends as soon as any one player has reached 20 points, at which point the player with the most points wins.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description: The object of this game is to move around the playing board according to the numbers scored on the spinner and to accumulate as many game cards as possible. The first player to accumulate 30 game cards is the winner.

Fanticide is a whimsically grim skirmish game for battles between opposing homicidal warbands.  Open Architecture and clear guidelines for building any kind of war band you want with figures you want to use. Ruleset is friendly to any miniatures, including historicals. Starting bands include centaurs, fae, creeps, and the flying monkeys from the Land of Od.  Does not use an “I go, you go” turn sequence. Instead, it uses a card driven sequence, full of events and interesting command & control choices for determining what units can move and act.  These cards are assigned to each unit, character and monster in the game.  These activation cards are drawn one at a time and the units are activated in turn. There are also Event cards that are shuffled in with the activation cards which produce random outcomes like killer unicorns enraged by the ‘Rainbow’ Event.   Easy to learn but challenges and rewards intelligent game play.  30 to 40 figures per side is typical. D10 Combat and magic is simple but gives an interactive and satisfying result.  
 Sculpting by Mike Broadbent, Alan Marsh, Paul Clarke, and Ernest Baker

This is a standalone variant for Civilization.  Its rules are based on Advanced Civilization more than the original Civilization. Included are more civilization advancements (both quantity and type), which give the players new options for advancement, as well as a slightly restructured commodity trading round, which has new resources and disasters to be traded. 
Included in the complete package is a new map, different cultures, new trade cards and calamities, a new AST board for the cultures, and a few new advances.
This does not require the original game to play as everything for it comes in a new package for you to Print and Play. 
Variant Designed by Martin DeOlden, User Tawnos76

2-4 player game, 15-20 minute game play. Players are the bosses of their families in the 1930's gangster era. Players hire thugs, bookies and torpedos to help them gain control of the various rackets in the city. Card play weakens/enhances family strengths duing the game. The major twist is the HIT die, played during a racket round, where every character is a target!

Mature themes (nothing you couldn't read about in any Time-Life book from the library), recommended for 12+ years.

From the designer of Battle of the bands, Backstage Pass and Portable Adventures card games.

Players (families) battle each other to control a set number of rackets, 2 of one kind of racket or 3 of any kind racket.  You build your family using thug, bookie and torpedo cards, collectively known as mug cards. They're point value is your power during rackets. When a racket card is being fought over, the players all roll a HIT die (1d6),any opponent mugs in play that match your die roll (a die is printed on the mug cards), those cards are eithered flipped-unusable during this round, or discarded. The die roll is then added to the remaining value of your mugs in play, highest score wins the contract. But, you must have a mug who can control that racket, otherwise it is discarded. Family cards are played during non racket rounds to help your family or hinder the opposition.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
The objective of the game is: all the tokens of one player have to reach the opposite side of the board, the first player to do this, wins. 
Each player takes 6 tokens of the same color and places them face down on one of the four sides of the board.
In each turn, the player moves a token to any of the neighboring cells that are not occupied; you can also jump a token on any other, only if the following cell is empty.
As all tokens back are the same, the player will have to remember which ones are his tokens and which those of their adversaries.
When a token reaches the opposite side of the board, the player has to check if that token is of his color. 
If is of his color, the token is put it off the board waiting for the other 5 tokens until the 6 ones reach their destination.
If is not of his color, the token has to return to the cell where it started the move.
When the 6 tokens of one of the players reaches the opposite side of the board the game ends.

Operational level wargame for the battle of the Atlantic between 1939 to 1942. Standard game is covering commerce raiding by the capital ships and Armed Merchant Cruisers. U-boat and aircraft can add by using optional rules.

Players control either Kriegsmarine(German Navy) or Allied Navy. 
The main part of the game is search and pursuit of opponent ships with dual maps and secret units deployment.

The purpose of German player is sinking of Allied convoy. 
The purpose of Allied player is escort of convoy, and also sinking of the German warships.

Put your overalls on and prepare for some family fun of the swine kind.

Pig-Pen is a quick fire family strategy game where each player does their best to capture pigs in pens.  Grab your hand and play cards building your pen to capture the pigs.  However, be careful, as your opponent will be looking to ruin your hard work and steal your pigs!

In a game of Pig-Pen, players are dealt a hand of five cards,  with these cards they are going to build pens to capture pigs.  A stack of Pigs, depending on the number of players, is the timer for the game.  Once these pigs are gone the game is over.  On their turn players replenish their hands back to five cards, then they are allowed to play two cards and finally they may discard any number of cards they choose.  The choices they have are to either build their pen(s) or attack their opponent(s.)  Pens are made six cards: Four Pen cards, a Feed card to entice the pig and finally a Gate card.  Players can attack their opponents with attack cards that destroy Pen cards or Unlock Gates.  Even when a Pig is captured, it still isn't safe as players can attempt to steal it with Attack cards.  Players ultimate goal is to have the best point Pigs in their Pens at games end.

Pig-Pen also features three levels of play.  A simple children's level, an intermediate level with some number comparisons.  And the always fun Secret Bacon Pack, where no Pig is safe from destruction of the bacon kind.

The die is rolled and each player must "catch" the small fish with the color designated by the dice. But beware, if the fish bite black fish by mistake, the player must put all catched fish back into play. When there are no more fish on the table, except the black one the game ends and the player with the most fish in his pile is the winner.

boardgame: Ni Oui Ni Non: edition Premium
description:
An expansion of the classic Nicht Ja, Nicht Nein with a case of 55 additional cards included in the box.

Object of the game is during the questions try other players
to answer forbidden words YES or NO. If somebody answer so
others must then quickly ring the bell to move their pawn on the gameboard if they are the quickest.

The winner is who gets there first with his character in the middle of the board.

This is a financial game about saving money and becoming a millionaire. The object of the game is to own a $200K home, a $50K auto, a $300 business, 100K in stocks, $200K in bonds, 100K in gold or collectibles, and $100K in savings.

Players move along a pathway after twirling a special spinner that results in percentage increases in a designated form of investment or requires the player to draw an event card. There is score card that spells R-E-T-I-R-E-D as the player reaches each financial goal. Event cards reward a player who follows up on a hot "tip" and puts his or her cash into that form of investment on the following turn.

The first player to spell RETIRE on the score card is declared the winner.

Includes the colorful board with a built-in spinner, tokens, play money, cards, score sheets, rules, storage insert, booklet about banking, and a cassette tape with a seminar about the banking industry.

Pyramids race to the other side, trying not to get frozen along the way.

Freeze Tag is a Looney Pyramids game that uses the pyramids, a Pyramid Die, and a 5x5 board.  Each turn, a player rolls the Pyramid Die and moves the piece(s) shown on the die.  They can land on larger pieces to freeze them or smaller pieces to teleport them.  The first person to cross the board with three pieces wins.

This game requires:
• 2 Looney Pyramid trios of the same color for each player
• Volcano Board
• The Pyramid Die (available in either Ice Dice or Pyramid Arcade)

Each player starts with 16 numbered tiles (two each of numbers 1 through 8) and take turns playing one of them.  The number on the tile dictates how many spaces the player moves around the board, and an arrow on the space where the player ends his turn indicates the row or column where that tile must be placed.  This row or column is then scored based on the tiles present in it.  Red and blue tiles belong to each player; yellow tiles placed in the center can be scored by either player provided the row or column including them contains more points in his color than his opponent.  Once a row or column is scored, it cannot be scored again for the rest of the game.  Highest score after all possible tiles have been played wins.

Pickle is an elimination style card game for 2-18 players. A game consists of repeating "hands".  A hand consists of "tricks" which may range from a single card up to four card sets from each player.  The object is not to end with the highest card.  Players are eliminated when they acquire 36 points or if they run out of chips.

The custom The Pickle deck features 16 characters that help players learn the game by hinting at the strength of the card as well as unique play options.

Military strategy game was published in 1942 during the middle of WWII.

The red army and the blue army face each other on a terrain grid which features rivers, mountains, forests, and cities. Each army includes 1 headquarters unit, 8 infantry units, and 6 tank units. The illustrated playing pieces are stand-up tokens similar to the kind used in Stratego.

Includes a colorful board, tokens, rules, and a storage insert.

Naval combat dexterity game was published in 1940.

Two plastic and metal ships are involved with the battle. A submarine fires an aluminum torpedo at a battleship. If the torpedo strikes the proper target zone on the battleship the vessel "explodes" and falls apart... the game mechanic is similar to the 1980s Milton Bradley classic Torpedo Run.

Includes the ships, the torpedo, and the rules. More experienced captains can take longer shots or otherwise embellish the game, but the concept was quite simple.

(From the back of the box)
This game is a celebration of FallCon.

In this light filler game, you get to collect Personality Cards worth varying victory points.

Roll the dice and match up the numbers.

If you get lucky and roll the FallCon logo, your task just got easier as you can cover any number with that logo.

Keep rolling the dice if you feel lucky.

But if you can't match up a number with the dice rolled then you lose everything you collected during your turn.

Of course, like any good game, there is an opportunity to steal Personality Cards from other players.

You attended the Convention, now play the game and see all your favourite FallCon personalities come to life in your home.

In Yamunda, players are expedition members who are searching for unusual creatures – and now they've discovered an incredibly bewildering variety of life around the Yamunda, a small river region in the Amazon. Whoever collects the most objects by the end of this journey will be the most successful expedition member and the winner of this game.

You hold in your hands a magic mirror which reflects the girl will turn into a beautiful mermaid!
Just turn the arrow on the wheel to win one at a piece of the puzzle that make up the body of your siren (hair,
face, chest, tail ...) and put them on the mirror in the correct order.
But beware of the evil sea witch: she may resume at any time parts of the mirror!
The first player who has transformed the girl to mermaid in her mirror win the game!

Aristoocrazy is a game about the Middle Ages which deals not with the clichés of that period – such as religious fanaticism or cruelty – but rather with slightly exaggerated relationships within the noble families. It offers deep strategic choices, but spices them with fun, humor, and player communication. Relive this comedy about the Middle Ages and have fun with Aristoocrazy!

Each player has ten unique characters from his family at his disposal – five noblemen and five noblewomen – but he starts with only one pair of aristrocrats. Throughout the game, characters will struggle to conquer neutral regions or those controlled by an opponent, in addition to using the benefits of their abilities. Each character has three abilities, which are helpful for gaining victory points (VPs) or attacking other characters. The game has nine different abilities, which are represented by cardboard tokens. When a male and female character marry, new characters are born into play, with the newborn children inheriting its parents' abilities.

If two characters with a close family relationship meet, however, there is a risk that their child will be "different". When such a child grows up, naturally you'll need him to take care of your domain or a military campaign that you're running – but if his wits aren't in the right place, he won't be of much help. (And who would want to marry such a dud in the first place?)

Aristoocrazy lasts twelve rounds, and at the end of each round the victory conditions for the current round are evaluated. The players try to optimize their actions – moves on the board, attacks, the use of abilities – in order to fulfill the victory conditions defined on the event card in place for that round. The players who best fulfill the victory conditions are rewarded with VPs or special action cards, which can help them in certain situations in future rounds. The player with the most VPs after the final round wins!

boardgame: Le Cochon Qui Rit Encore Plus
description:
Modernized version of the classic game of The Laughing Pig

The pigs are dressed in funny outfits and accessories, becoming a bimbo, a football fan, a player lasso or a rocker.

The die has been replaced by a electric mechanism with the pig character sounds and voices.

The object is get his/her pig ready first spinning the wheel and fitting the pig together. But players must be careful and avoid the butcher!

My First The Laughing Pig for the littles ones. Recognize shapes. 2 levels of play for two years and olders.

Roll the dice and construct the pig. First player who gets his pig ready is the winner.

Are you too scared to go to the TV show quiz game? Than try it at home first! Quiz type of party game for up to four players. The game comes with buzzer which indicates who is on turn, game board, four players pieces, 56 cards and four pencils and notebooks. Superkvíz is also four variants in one package.

Travel implemetation of beloved czech TV quiz show. Two competitors have to connect all three sides of the hexagonal board by correctly answering questions.  Game comes with 55 cards, on each is 10 questions. It could be also use as expansion to the board game version.

Game from the inside covers of the 1964 Boy's Own Paper Annual.

The rules:

FOLLOW THE TRAIL AND FIND THE PIRATE'S TREASURE!
Here's an exciting game that calls for some skill and judgement. Up to 13 players can take part. All you need is a small, coloured counter for each player and one die. Players throw the die in turn and move their counter according to the table below.
First throw a 6 and then start from any square at the west end of the map. STOP if you come up against a natural obstruction (such as a volcano or swamp), or the edge of the map, or a square occupied by an opponent’s counter, and lose the remainder of that move.
If you throw:
1 - Move one square in any direction and have another throw.
2 - Rest on your present square until the next throw.
3 - Bonus. Move three squares in any direction — including a combination of forward and sideways.
4 - Move four squares South.
5 - Move five squares North.
6 - Move six squares East.

The winner will be the first player to reach the treasure island on the right hand side of the board.

Game in the inside covers to the Boy's Own Paper Annual, 1964.

The rules:
Here is a brand new idea! A thrilling cycle race game to liven up the long winter evenings. Any number of players can take part, and all you need is one die (two dice will speed up the game) and a few small coloured counters. The rules common to all dice games are too familiar to repeat - just remember that each space is one mile. Jump on your cycles. Right then, all set? You’re off to a flying start!

It is a game in the grand tradition of 'Game of Goose' race games.The winner is the first player to reach Big Ben in London.

New take on classic game "Člověče, nezlob se!" also known as "Pachisi". The game is almost the same, but with one new game element. When your piece should go out from the game, here you put it under the piece who "eats" your piece. The whole bunch is in posetion of player, who have the top most piece. Game is family friendly and playes in 30 minutes.

Umakartové is experimental take of Aleš Pilgr on mixing indie music CD and family co-op board game for 2 to 6 players. The game plays exactly 41 minutes; so as long as music CD which comes with game (or it´s other way around?). The players are Umakartové (something like home elf) and after the human family goes on holiday, they take over the house and tries solving problems in 12 rooms; like hot iron left on table, milk on stove etc. If they save the house, all players win.

What you need to do to save the house? Prepare twelve playing fields that correspond to individual rooms. Then shuffle the word cards and give them out among the players. Everyone holds their cards hidden. When you insert a CD into the player and turn on the shuffle mode. The game begins. Youngest player chooses a card and pantomime the word shown. Other players have one attempt to guess words, anyone can guess. Correct guess is the first successful step to avert danger in the room (you put the card in the appropriate room). If guessing is not successful, then take a card in your hand and performs a new word. Words are selected from the songs, so playing song can serve as a hint. You have to guess 5 words for each room. Compositions have a length of about three minutes, that means to guess each word have approximately 20 to 50 seconds. Guessing must be quick, otherwise fail. The entire game fits into a box with music CD.
---
Po zběsilém balení odjíždí rodina na dovolenou a nevědomky za sebou zanechává v domě celou řadu malérů, v každém z dvanácti pokojů jeden: tu zapnutou žehličku, tu mléko pálící se na plotně… Je na vás – tajemných UMAKARTECH, skrytých obyvatelích lidského stavení – abyste zažehnali všechny problémy a zabránili nejhoršímu. Váš čas je ale omezen – než dohraje CD! Originální hra spojená s poslechem CD skupiny Biorchestr. Zcela nové prožití poslechu hraní hry a poslechu CD.

Game description from the publisher:

Following on the heels of Thunderstone Advance: Caverns of Bane, Thunderstone emerges into the deepest trouble yet. The tunnels under the fortress at Cheah Dell are clear. The lost dwarven kingdom of Caligin is reclaimed. And the djinni lich, Mowtil, has been defeated, sealing the portal of black flame into Tala. The adventurers have collected three more Thunderstones, and Doom's full plans are revealed. He has corrupted the heart of Tala, Dun Ordha. A monstrous army marches on the capital city, even as the final Thunderstone is revealed to be held by King Caelan himself. Can the adventurers race ahead of an army to save the city from a siege outside the walls and sickly corruption within?

Thunderstone Advance: Root of Corruption, the second expansion for Thunderstone Advance, introduces new challenges and an all-new way to play: co-op! It also features ruinous sin incarnates, vicious scavenger gnolls, thrall summoners to bring back memorable foes, seven new heroes, six new monster groups, fifteen new village cards, and a new poster map board that creates options for co-op play using existing cards and rules! What's more, all Ambusher cards from Towers of Ruin and Caverns of Bane have been reprinted with updated graphics to increase visibility.

description:
A dexterity game in which players take turns placing wooden branches, leaves and cherries on a trunk that wobbles around. If you knock any pieces off they go into your pile of tree parts. First one to get rid of all their tree parts wins the game.

In addition to the basic game described above, a deck of action cards can be used. Starting with the second round, each player draws a card per turn and follows the instructions (e.g. "place three leaves on your turn", "assign a player of your choice to move a tree part of your choice", "swap your leaves for those of the player to your right).

Arbos XXL is a re-implementation of Arbos. It differs from the regular game in three main aspects: size, a fixed (rather than adjustable) base, and wooden cherries as an additional element. The XXL version was originally intended for nursery schools.

Board game.  Roll dice and move one of six pieces around the board to collect rings which are randomly placed on the board.  Object is to be the first to collect 6 rings (one of each color or all one color) with no extras.

Roll of die give 1-6 moves per turn.  Use colored dots (or "bubbles") on each space to jump to other spaces that have same combinations of bubbles.  Board spaces go from 1 to 199.

Can attack and slow down other players by beating them to rings, leaving rings in "bad" places for others to get, "swapping" rings at end of turn, or "bumping" (landing on another player's space) and knocking their rings off.

Whenever a ring is picked up, another of the same color is immediately placed using dice which generate 0-199 (0 = "whammy" = lose a ring), so there are always six rings somewhere on the board, one of each color.

Two to four players campaign against each other to capture the presidency.  As the players move their tokens (elephant, donkey, bull, bear) around the board through the states they use dirty tricks and smear tactics to gain the 270 electoral votes needed to be elected.

The game is somewhat similar to Monopoly with a square board made up of state squares.  In the middle of the board are stacks of Media cards and Power cards as well as a stack of Bribery Checks.  Players are given money to "buy" the votes of states as they land on them.

Each turn the candidate rolls the dice and whatever state square the player lands on he can win that state by paying the required fee or draw a Power card to give the player a further advantage.  If the player lands on a state owned by another candidate, that player draws a Media card and suffering the consequences.  Various cards drawn require the candidate to either gain or lose influence and votes.

Play continues until one candidate accumulates the required 270 electoral votes or all other players run out of money.

Logico is a memory-style game for young children, which helps them learn about the logical sequence of events.

The game consists of sets of 3 illustrated tiles depicting sequences of 3 events.  Example:  Event 1 is a car starting a race, Event 2 is a car winning a race, and Event 3 is a car getting a trophy for winning the race.

All the Event 1 tiles are distributed among the players.  All Event 2 tiles are put face down in one pile, and all Event 3 tiles are put face down in another pile.  Players take turns picking tiles and trying to match them to one of the Event tiles they already have.  The first player to complete all their sequences is the winner.

boardgame: Mighty Morphin Power Rangers Battling Wager Game
description:
Power Rangers, the evil Rita Repulsa and Lord Zedd have sent out the Evil Space Aliens and Putty Patrol Warriors to destroy Angel Grove. It’s up to Power Rangers to put a stop to Rita and Zedd’s diabolical plan and save Angel Grove before it’s too late.

Players choose their favorite Power Ranger and use their battle die to knock down Evil Villain character pieces by tossing their Battle Die down a Battle Ramp. Players battle each other by placing wagers on how many Evil Villains their Power Ranger will knock down on their turn.

Lighting Bolt Tokens are used by players to place wagers on their Power Ranger. The Tokens are each worth different value amounts to estimate the number of knocked down Evil Villains the player's Power Ranger Battle Die will knock down on their turn.

Battles consist of two players battling on a player's turn. Each will wager using their Lighting Bolt Tokens. The winner of the battle will note how many Evil Villains were knocked down. As an added bonus for when players are going, each round they may place their Power Ranger character piece on the Battle Tray in one of the marked spots. If they are successful in knocking it down on their turn, they'll receive an added bonus if they win the wager.

Game play continues until each of the players have gone, then a round has been done. Players play three rounds. After three rounds players tally up their scores. The player with the highest score is the winner!

The railways of today are amazing things and bullet trains, freight trains and more keep entire countries running. From transporting the populace to carrying essential materials, trains play an integral part in a nation's power and economic development.

Trains: Map Pack 1 – Germany/Northeastern USA consists of a double-sided game board featuring the map of Germany on one side and Northeastern USA on the other. Now players can expand their railways into new countries. Each map features extensive replay value that you won't want to leave the station without!

Wild Oltrenatura leads players into the most uncontaminated habitats on Earth: Kenya's savannah, Namibia's desert, Colombia's jungle, and Alaska's glaciers and forests. Explorers will parachute into regions waiting to be explored. Here, they will have to walk or swim, seeking out the best routes, in order to meet the wildest animals and survive. They will have to face – and collect – scary sharks, huge cats, aggressive pachyderms, the most poisonous snakes, and many more animals they've probably never even heard of, all while carefully monitoring their energy, water and health supplies!

The object of Wild Oltrenatura is to meet as many animals as possible and, of course, to survive in the different extreme environments. During your turn, you have to decide whether to move 1-3 spaces (consuming water and energy) to receive an animal card or special natural resource benefits, feed (discard an animal card to get energy back), or rest (to recover one energy, water or health). Players can challenge opponents to take over their animal cards, and the game ends as soon as one player has explored all four environments.

A card game similar to "Go Fish" or "Authors", it is designed to help learn fifteen Bible stories from the Old Testament. Aimed at all age levels from Preschool to Seniors.

Each Requester begins play by asking another player if he/she has a certain story and play continues around the table until all 4 cards in a particular story has been collected by a Requestor for 10 points. The person on the left of the Requestor must then try to tell the story or pass to the person on the left. If the story is told correctly, that person also collects 10 points. If not, the Requestor reads it aloud and play continues until until someone is out of cards. That person collects 10 points plus 1 point for each card remaining in opponent hands.

First person to reach 100 points after a few rounds wins.
 Comes in a deck of 62 cards including "Directions for Play" and a "Publisher" fact card. Illustrated by Daniela Ianculescu.

description:
A fun game for fans of Czech music.

A fun game for fans of Czech music and not only for them. In this game you will find 1800 questions, which are divided into 6 groups; such as popular music, film music, traditional music, folk etc.

Double-sided game board allows you to play family or party version of this game. You will perform tasks as humming songs, mime or drawing. The most successful of the end of the game you get "Český slavík" award.

Zábavná hra pro fanoušky české hudby

Zábavná hra pro fanoušky české hudby a nejen pro ně. V této vědomostní hře najdete 1 800 otázek, které jsou rozděleny do 6 okruhů: populární hudba, filmová hudba, tradiční hudba, folk a country, různé a dozpívej.

Oboustranný herní plán umožňuje hrát i párty verzi této hry, ve které budete plnit i zábavné úkoly formou broukání písniček, pantomimy nebo kreslení. Ten nejúspěšnější z Vás na konci hry získá Českého slavíka.

boardgame: Mansions of Madness: The Yellow Sign
description:
Julian Glen, a talented up-and-coming playwright, has penned a work that he claims will be his masterpiece. There's only one problem...it's turning its audience into mindless fanatics! As some of the few audience members to retain your sanity, you and the other investigators must now work together to find Glen and uncover his true plot.

Mansions of Madness: The Yellow Sign is a print-on-demand story expansion for Mansions of Madness. Based on a design by a collaboration of fans at FFG's Arkham Nights 2011, The Yellow Sign thrusts players into a house filled with deranged actors. Can you survive until your next curtain call?
family: Series: Arkham Horror Files (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
Magistrat is an expansion to SPEICHERSTADT HAMBURG and consists of 35 action cards, which give the players additional options to take control over their success.
The cards are shuffled with 15 Storehouse cards and from that pile you take 10 cards in each harbour phase, one to each package of 2 ware cards to create a three card set. Likewise the basic game each player buys two of these sets in the harbour phase.
New options of the Magistrat cards are for example:
- exchange cards from the sets in the harbour phase
- take your own cards into account only when evaluating the income
- increase the value of your gift to the senate
- get more victory points for your gift
- count MAGISTRAT-cards to get victory points at the end (the more cards the more efficient are they)

The expansion was distributed at Essen 2012 and is now available everywhere you get the base game.
The expansion comes along with short rules. Complete rules are on the website for download.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Endless: Fantasy Tactics is a tactical miniatures game designed by On The Lamb Games. With a focus on boutique style miniatures and an art aesthetic harkening back to console RPGs of the 90s, you can build your own custom team of knights, wizards, and monsters. Equip them with items and pick their abilities. Fight it out in story scenarios, or recreations of your favorite games. A standard game takes about 20 to 30 minutes to play with 5-10 miniatures and uses a D6 pool-based system of gameplay.

Endless uses 40mm miniatures on a 25mm/1” square grid. Unlike most other miniatures games, Endless does not feature faction specific models. All models are available for use by any player, and can easily be mixed and matched to suit any personal taste or play-style.

Print and Play dungeon crawl board game in a 17-page PDF.  Complete with paper miniatures and terrain.  You'll need to supply your own dice.  In two-player games, one person controls the monsters, and the other person controls one or two heroes.  Treasure can be spent on better equipment to fight bigger and badder monsters.  can you take out the Boss monster?

From the creator of Flipit Paper Combat.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
1660 is a strategic game in which the players take the roles of European powers (Spain, France, the United Provinces, and the English Commonwealth) and try to extract wealth from their colonies and conquer those of other players.

The game is played over three years, at the beginning of each of which the players collect taxes and can build ships and armies. There are then fourteen movement and combat turns each year. Ships are moved via dice roll (reflecting the unpredictability of the winds), but players can choose how to allocate each die. There are also prevailing winds and currents which can affect movement.

Players can attack other players' colonies, but can also send merchant ships to obtain trade goods and carry them back to home ports (where they add to the treasury) or send warships to attack other players' treasure fleets. There are occasional random events.

At the end of three years victory points are awarded for gold in the treasury, ships, and captured colonies. Each nation subtracts a different amount from their final score to reflect imbalance in the positions; each nation starts with different amounts of money and units, and also collects a different amount of taxes each year.

description:
The Endangered Species Game was specifically developed to encourage an awareness of just how vulnerable the various species of life can be. This game has been produced with the endorsement of the World Wide Fund for Nature and provides an insight into the lifestyles and dangers to mammals, insects, fish, plants, birds, reptiles and amphibians.

Be the first player to complete a jigsaw puzzle of an endangered species before the species is extinct. Players earn puzzle pieces by answering questions about the endangered species but beware - your loss through the roll of the die could mean your opponents' gain. The sixty cards are superb photographs of Australia's endangered species.

boardgame: Professor Pugnacious: Treachery on the Trains
description:
Treachery! is the first expansion to Professor Pugnacious' Portfolio of Perils, Pugilism, and Perfidy-the Card Game of Victorian Combat.

It focuses on Failure-both making opponents lose, and avoiding being forced to fail. It introduces a new class, the Traitor, who benefits by making the other players fail in their endeavors, a new mechanic, Stealth, which lets you play cards facedown to surprise your opponents, the game's first Mastermind-a new kind of Finale-and a host of new purchasable cards for the Main Deck, and new enemies for the Adversary Deck.

Using the enchanting May Gibbs characters from the classic story of Snugglepot & Cuddlepie that has delighted generations: be the first to grow the grandest gumtree in the land. Protect your tree and survive the fires and dangers of the forest.

The Gumnut Game pits up to 8 gumnuts babies, as they attempt to survive the harsh conditions of Australia. This is determined by spinning both a Weather Forecast and Fire Danger Indicator as determined by the spaces that players land on as they progress through the game. Along the way players have the opportunity to pick up Reward, Danger, Surprise and Safety cards which either provide advantages or disadvantages to the players.

The winner of The Gumnut Game is the first player whose gumnut baby is able to traverse all 99 spaces and survives the dangers allowing it to grow into a River Red Gum.

A German card-based game intended for teaching teenagers about society/ethics/sociopolitical issues. The cards can also be used as a starting point for discussion.

The game centers around questions like "What would you do if you wanted to get home from a party at night and the driver is drunk?" or "What would you do if a friend tells you he is homosexual?"

The game confronts you with 60 tricky situations from the area of politics, ethics and society. There is one card for each situation. The front side introduces the situation and the back describes three possible reactions to the situation. Players decide how they would react and try to guess what others would do. If you know the other players well, you earn points.

There are three variants for playing the game:

1) In the most basic variant, one player reads one of the reactions from the back of a card. The other players try to guess what the situation is by asking yes/no questions. The player who first guesses the situation wins the card. The game ends when each player has been the "reader" once or twice. The player with the most cards wins the game.
Suggested minimum number of players: 3

2) One player reads a situation and the three reactions from the back of the card. All players (including the reader, R) guess how R would most likely react. They write down the letter assigned to this reaction on a slip of paper. Before the players hand in their guesses, R chooses three players of which he/she thinks that they've guessed correctly. The chosen players show their guesses and R shows his/her answer. For each matching answer R gets one point, the chosen players get 2 points if they've guessed correctly. Now all the other guesses are collected and counted. All other players (i.e. everyone except R and the 3 chosen players) get one point if the majority has guessed correctly. Then the next player becomes R. The game ends when each player has been R once. The player with the most points wins the game.
Suggested minimum number of players: 8

3) A situation and the three reactions are read. Each player decides how he/she would react and writes down the corresponding letter on a slip of paper. The answers are handed in. Now each player guesses how many people have chosen answers A, B and C. They right the numbers down in a table. Then the answers are counted. Players get 3 points for a correct guess, one points if their guess is off by one.
The game ends after 14 situations. The player with the most points wins the game.
Suggested minimum number of players: 4

Example situation: 
Situation: "The party is over. We've all been drinking. This is particularly obvious in the person who was intended to be the driver. He gets behind the wheel. It's a 20-minutes' drive. There is no bus that late at night and we want to get home fast.
Possible reactions: "A: I ask the driver if he's confident he can do it. If he says yes, I'll go with him this one time. After all, it isn't far and we can take back roads." 
"B: We judge who's most likely to be capable of driving and decide on a driver that way." 
"C: Under no circumstances am I going to drive or take a ride with a drunken driver. We'll have to call a taxi and collect the car later."

This map represents one of the Balearic Islands, Mallorca  (or Majorca), which is located in the Mediterranean Sea  and is one of the most visited vacation spots in the Spain. 
Also printed on the map are various details about many towns on the island, and some history.
Rules apply like normal Catan, and like Catan Geographies: Österreich the map can be played with up to 6 People
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
The year 2020. It's been 5 years since the zombie uprising that we now refer to as "Zombie Summer". You could say that we've since adapted to living with the undead… All you need to do is stay indoors, keep your weapons close, and don't trust anyone or anything. But as humans, we are a greedy species, and we found a way to make money off of the zombies. And that is where the ZombieZone comes in.

ZombieZone is a battle arena where 10 humans and 10 zombies will fight to the death… on live television. From the safety of our homes, we watch the destruction unfold, placing bets on or against mankind. And some of us will even risk our own lives for a chance at fame and fortune. Will the money hungry-humans, armed with a shotgun and a brain make it out alive? Or will the flesh-hungry Zombies multiply and take them over?

So many questions, so little time. The battle is about to begin. Enter the ZombieZone!


ZombieZone is a head to head strategic battle for survival of humans vs. zombies. 2 players each use double-sided disc tokens and dice to maneuver around a gridded parking lot game board. Humans are armed with a shotgun for ultimate zombie destruction, and zombies spread quickly by taking over the humans (flipping the token over to reveal a zombie on the other side). The last one standing wins.

This edition has a larger board, more units, bombs, and a second win condition.

boardgame: Volu Puzzle: Drôles D'Animaux – Funny Animals
description:
6 Funny animal Stories to put together: puzzles for young children.
This puzzle contains 6 small puzzles whose pieces fit together to create animals in three dimensions.
After building these six funny animals, children can have fun with and tell fantastic stories!
Tha small pieces can be put in to a bag, each body handled to the players. Then the pieces get out of the bag, and the first to finish the animal or animals wins.

There are 13 dices to be thrown, letters are drawn on their faces instead of numbers, then the hourglass is put up. When it is exhausted, the players must read the longest (and obvioulsy meaningful) word they could had found with the letters shown in the upper dices' faces. Each letter has a point value, so they will sum up and score the points the word found is totally worth and go on with the roll launchs.
Players choose at the beginning how many throws the game is going to be long, after the last one the higher scorer is the winner.
There is a jolly side that can be used for any word, proper nouns and abbreviatons are not scored. Also, each unused letter will have the points worth subtracted from the score.

Railways of North America expands the basic game of Railways of the World (ROTW) to Canada and requires a copy of the base game to play it. Fans of the series will be happy to see new features and an advanced map designed specifically for this expansion.

Designed for 2-5 players (but best with 3 or 4) this map has been in development for over a year! This new Canadian map introduces new features – Mines and Ferry Lines – using cards that start out on the table from the onset and can be claimed anytime (just like Railroad Operations cards) for the right price!

Essen Eagles is an expansion for the American football game 1st & Goal that, unlike the half-dozen expansions released with the base game, consists of only a single team. 
This limited edition team contains 7 specialty dice for the Essen Eagles. Requires 1st & Goal® game to play.

Game to teach negotiation skills and also The Business Model Generation. This is a methodology to help entrepreneurs to establish a successful business model based on a graphic approach called Canvas. The Canvas has 9 areas that any strategic plan and/or business plan should focus: Key Resources, Key Partners, Value Proposition, Customer Segments, Customer Relationship, Key Activities, Cost Structure, Revenue streams, Channels.

Each player or group of players will represent a character. The character is one of the managers a typical company will find. Sales Manager, Production Manager and HR Manager are some examples. Each character has some expertise and this expertise allows him to have some skill cards. The game has 5 boards, each board is a company of the same corporation. Each company has a sales goal and the corporation also has a sales goal.

Each round a new crisis/opportunity must be faced by the players, that must co-operate to get the business results desired. They must discuss the crisis/opportunity, suggest a solution to the problem and add skill cards with enough points to solve the problem. The players roleplay their character, move the character thru the board representing the Canvas areas, get new skills, negotiate with the other companies for resources and pick actions to help the company goals. The game ends after 3 hours of play and the results are analized to check if the goals were achieved.

boardgame: Sporting Challenge: The Game of Games
description:
For up to 8 players to complete a circuit of the board, by rolling the dice to move, progressing through four performance levels: Club, County, National, International, then to complete a lap of the stadium and to finish on the podium first, having obtained sufficient: Skill, Publicity, Funding, Success, on the way.

Route: The first player throws the die to start and moves forward the number of spaces indicated. When a player lands on a square that is marked "?", a question card is taken. The question that corresponds with the number rolled on the die to get there should be answered. If it is answered correctly, the player is to move on one space. If incorrectly answered, the player is to move back one space. When a player lands on a square that is marked “♯”, a stumble card is taken from the top of the pile and the instructions followed.

Players complete 2 laps. The first lap - completing the four performance levels: each to be attained during the first circuit of the stadium, which are in order as each player progresses around the track: First quarter is Club, Second quarter is County, Third quarter is National, Fourth quarter is International
Before reaching the podium, the player needs to achieve an Element of: Success, Publicity, Funding, Skill. One should be obtained during each performance level as follows: Club - skill, County - success, National - funding, International - publicity

The second lap - around the stadium
By the end of the international performance level, the player should have obtained skill, success, funding and publicity. Players should then complete a second circuit of the board, a lap around the stadium, in a race to the podium. The game continues as before with the number of squares moved on each go determined by rolling the die. As before, question cards should be answered if landed on, moving on a square if correctly answered and back a square if the answer is incorrect. Stumble cards are also taken if appropriate. If an Element of skill, success, funding or publicity is lost during the final lap, it should be regained as before. The winner is the first player to arrive at the podium with all four Elements.

Electronic children's race game where 2-4 kids try to "feed" (flip) "flies" (plastic bits) into a roving Chameleon (moving electronic piece with wheels and a mouth that opens and closes) and be the one with the most bugs in the Chameleon's mouth.  But watch out, the Chameleon may spit out the bugs (when its tongue flips them out).

Agricola: Belgium Deck brings Uwe Rosenberg's farming game to a new thematic setting! Discover Belgium's two largest regions – the Dutch-speaking region of Flanders in the north and the French-speaking region of Wallonia in the south – in this 120-card set that consists of sixty new Minor Improvements and sixty new Occupations.

A card game for 4 Players, Tontine is a game of Victorian murder and backstabbing.  Years after a group of long time friends had pooled their money into a tontine, it is down to the last four surviving members. The former friends have become poor and desperate. Who be the last alive to claim the cash?

Players take turns trying to stabilize their position and forcing opponents to discard cards. Players may play "accidents", which if a player has no cards in hand, will be their undoing.

Scheme - remains in play, grants various effects
Action - gives card advantage or information

Hry pro předškoláky a malé školáky, při kterých zábavnou cestou získají základní zeměpisné znalosti. Při jejich hraní vzniká prostor pro vytváření vlastních příběhů a vzájemnému špičkování. Základní princip her je založen na náhodném hodu kostkou, ale mincové žetony přinášejí do hry možnost taktizovat a rozhodovat se.

Při Objevitelské hře budete objevovat známá místa všech kontinentů, získávat tolary, bojovat s piráty a obírat je o jejich poklady.

Ve hře Evropou křížem krážem musíte navštívit daný počet hlavních měst. Zábava graduje v momentě, kdy je většina měst obsazena a hráč se musí rozhodnout, do které části Evropy se vydá pro své poslední město.

Hedvábná stezka nás provede starobylou obchodní cestou přes celou Asii až do Číny a Indie, kde si za získané peníze hráči koupí loď, se kterou plují do cíle. Cesta je plná bonusů a nástrah, kde mnohé těžkosti můžou být i výhodou.

Horolezecká čtyřka je jednoduchá logická hra pro dva hráče, vycházející z principu piškvorek.

The Pulsar is a rotating neutron star, born from a collapsing Supernova. Its regular pulse can help with your tactical timing.

The expansion contains two Pulsar sector hexes and one Nova Burst Discovery Tile.

The two Pulsar hexes are shuffled with the other Outer Sector hexes before building the Outer Sector stack. The Discovery Tile is Shuffled with the other Discovery Tiles.

When you place your Influence Disc on the hex (when Exploring or later on with the Influence action), place the disc on the Move action space on the hex. Once per round, you may activate the Pulsar instead of taking a normal Action. When activating the Pulsar, move the Influence Disc to either of the two free action spaces (for example, from the Move action to the Build or Upgrade action) and take that action. The actions are weaker versions of the regular actions (one Move, one Build, one Upgrade), same as the normal Reactions. The Pulsar may not be used after you have passed. You may not place more than one Influence Disc on the Pulsar.

When you collect the Nova Burst tile, place it next to your Player Board. The Nova Burst may be activated by removing the tile from the game before the battle begins or at any time during a battle when it is your turn in Initiative order. This causes 1 damage to every opposing Ship in the battle. The tile is worth 1VP if still held at the end of the game. The Magellan do not receive their species bonus for the Nova Burst if it is removed from the game.
