Game description from the publisher:

Every autumn, hungry trolls roam the area between the Wild Lands and the countries of the Halflings, looking for supplies for the winter. "Young halfling on a spit is their favorite dish," halfling mothers warn their children. During this time, most halfling scout wagons stay safe along the border, but now a daredevil halfling is trekking into the Wild Lands for he has bet the house and yard. In Quest: Zeit der Helden - Trollfutter, it's time for courageous men and women to keep their little friend from heading into a big disaster...

Storm of Shadows is the third Malifaux expansion, and contains new characters, background information and faction development, all in fanastic full colour. Storm of Shadows features the introduction of the Ten Thunders faction...

A simple card game that uses the rock-paper-scissors mechanisms.

The game consists of 55 cards. Each card shows one of x symbols. In addition to rock, paper and scissor symbols, there are fire (burns paper), well (extinguishes fire etc.), and joker pictures. Each player receives one joker card and puts it face-up in front of him/herself. The other cards are shuffled and each player gets six cards. Players take turns choosing one other player for a duel. The duel partners each play one card of their choice simultaneously. The winner of the duel gets the card that the loser played. Then the next player chooses his/her partner for this turn's duel.

If both players play the same symbol, both cards stay in the pool and the next symbols decide who wins. If a player has lost all his/her cards, he/she uses the joker card. This means that the joker player uses a hand gesture while his/her duel partner plays a card as usual. If the joker player wins, he/she gets to draw two new cards. If the joker player loses, he/she is eliminated from the game. The first player to double their hand cards to 12 wins the game.

Greed is a concise and fascinating eurogame from substantial developer of board games - Reiner Knizia. In addition to a good mechanic this game can show off a peculiar look styled as picture post cards of the Russian 19th century lifestyle designed by Oleg Ershov.
The game is of the sort when rules understanding takes 15 minutes but mastering of playing skills requires indefinite time.

You will play a role of a pre-revolutionary businessman beating up among the winds of city life and trying to make money in these fickle conditions – you are not surprised, are you? Bargaining with proper people, using contacts and getting biggest profit by turning the circumstances to your own advantage.
The most skilled merchant wins the game. And what is the better evidence of business success then undertaking profit? That’s why the winning point counters look like cash notes and for the mostly successful ones – like gold bar cards.

Gameplay

Each player acquires two “Фарт” cards, one “Big Фарт” card and 10 more cards with random characters (8 cards if there are five players). Characters have positive and negative values. The first ones add the winning points, the others withdraw. “Student” is a special character which has a neutral, zero value. These two card types differ at the back so as to know how many “Фарт” cards each player has at the hand. Players shouldn’t hide this information.

Define by any suitable way who will start the game. Further the turn will dispose clockwise. In your game-business action several card piles will be formed – one for each player and one in the center. Only upper cards make sense during the game.

Player should put a card from his hand during his turn. Cards with positive or negative values can be placed to the own pile or at the center. “Student” card can be placed to any pile except the one with upper “Фарт” or “Big фарт” card on it. These cards can be played only to the own card piles.

When you’ve got all needed contacts and character positions at the table brings you delight and rejoicing, it’s time to catch up a moment of gain and hit the jackpot playing “Фарт” or “Big фарт” card. It’s placed near player’s pile and incites the instant count of all cards values at the table. Sum up the positive values, subtract negative and acquire or lose the sum of cash notes equal to the outcome. It’s simple! Though not as simple as it seems because all other “Фарт” cards at the table also work at this moment. In this case their owners are swept over the commercial tide and they get or lose the congruous sum of banknotes. If “Big фарт” has been played or provoked the sum of points, positive and negative as well, doubles.

When everyone plays all the cards the round ends. Then there can be more rounds at will. The amount of rounds can be equal to the number of players to let everyone to make the first turn for the most unbiased result.

Firstly, the game is logical so the word «фарт» obtains a bit different meaning then luck or risk. It can be associated with business acumen, enterprise and skill to use the chance. Remember: maybe business path loves the lucky ones but respects only gentlemen who are sober-minded and prudent.

A trivia game produced by "Deutsche Wanderjugend", a German hiking organisation for young people.

The game consists of 360 cards, a frog clicker and 50 chits. The cards are used to build a one-meter long path. The card backs have path segments, bridges and turns printed on them. In order to be allowed to add a card to the path, one has to complete a task or answer a question from the card. The cards come in 4 different categories, but they are all related to hiking, conservation, youth politics and youth culture, and knowledge about the "Deutsche Wanderjugend".
Questions come in different levels of difficulty and players earn different numbers of chits ("Wandertaler") accordingly.
The game ends when the path has been completed. The team with the most chits wins the game.

Evil Spares None is a 2-4 player Cooperative Survival Horror game. Players are teens in a slasher movie escaping a masked psychopath.

The goal of the game is for players to survive until dawn while the Psychopath hunts down you and your friends.  While on the run, your characters can come across crime scenes and gather evidence to hand over to the authorities and add to their investigation.  Meanwhile, the Psychopath, independently controlled by a deck of cards, is busy adding to his score by killing and stealing evidence.  If the player’s investigation reaches 16 points first, they have a chance to defeat and destroy the Psychopath permanently.  If they fail, well then it’s a mad dash race for the exit,  go ahead and throw your friends in the path of the enraged unstoppable madman.

As happens every year, the coach paves the way in the dark of night through the mountains to the Devil's Castle – but this time the signs are more ominous than ever before! Which of the two secret societies will prevail on the next trip to the Devil's Castle – or will the traitor triumph in the end?

Power Grid: Oracle & Industrial Espionage (Funkenschlag: Orakel & Industriespionage) are two promotional cards for Power Grid and Power Grid: The First Sparks that were first available in October 2012 for a donation at the 2F-Spiele booth during Spiel 2012. The donation is for the self-organized youth center and youth cultural center "Die Friese" located at Friesenstraße in Bremen, Germany.

An abstract game played on a 9x9 grid, in which players have to find their pieces and every player can move every piece.

The game consists of a game board, 16 ear-shaped player's pieces, 12 joker cards and a standard six-sided die.
On each side of the board there are four coloured spaces to which the ears have to be moved.

Each player is assigned a colour and receives three joker cards. Players try to locate the ears in their colour (the colour markings are at the bottom, face-down side) and to move them onto the target spaces of the same colour. At the start of the game, the ears are randomly (and without knowing the colour) distributed on the starting spaces.
Players take turns rolling the die. A die roll can be used to move one ear the corresponding number of spaces vertically or horizontally, or the roll can be split among several ears. If a player rolls a six, he/she has several options:
- He/she can move ears as in a normal roll.
- He/she can roll again and look at the colour markings of a number of ears corresponding to the die roll.
- He/she can place an ear of which he/she knows it is his/her colour directly onto a target space.
A player can decide not to move on his/her turn and use a joker card instead. The active player then rolls the die to determine how many ears he/she may inspect.
When players have found out which ears are theirs, they try to move them to their target spaces as secretly as possible. When an ear has reached a target space, it is turned over and the colour is shown to all players. If it is the wrong colour, it goes to one of the target spaces of the player it belongs to.

The game ends when all of one player's ears have reached their target spaces. Each ear that has reached its target space is worth 5 points, each joker card that hasn't been used up is worth two points. The player with the most points wins the game.

Game description from the publisher:

Six brave travelers have set off to find the philosophers' stone. To do so they first have to traverse the mysterious magic wood, where a rather crazy magician with strange magic powers is hiding. He sneakily bewitches the travelers transforming them into a camel, egg or even a roast chicken.

Help the travelers transform themselves back and continue on their way! The magic dice disclose what you need to free them from the spell. You have to react in a matter of seconds and snatch the right things. Whoever is the best at snatching will also be the first to transform the six travelers back to their true selves and find the philosophers' stone.

Farmer Bert looks puzzled: Where have all the cheeky animals hidden today? But luckily he hears the animals shouting as loud as they can from their hiding places. What a noise! In What's That Racket?, Farmer Bert has to strain his ears to find out where each animal is hiding. Who will be the first to find the five animals in their hiding places?

Game description from the publisher:

From one end to the other, the journey goes up and down through the 51 countries of Europe. No wonder the tourists no longer know where they are. Who can, guided by some hints, guess where they are at a given moment? The quicker you guess correctly, the more points you score.

In The European Countries, the player who knows Europe best and is the first to travel around the continent with his tourist wins the game. The game includes a variant for European experts.

A card game inspired by the imagery of traditional Portuguese tales and studied in order to stimulate creativity and love for reading.  Aimed at parents, educators, psychologists and therapists of children between 8 and 12 years, according to a concept experienced students in the preparatory cycle.

The cards are divided into seven groups, eleven cards each: human characters, animal characters, spaces, magical objects, actions, key words and characterizations.

The players make groups of 1 to 4 persons maximum.
Each group pick one card of the seven types.
They should invent a story with the cards they have in hand and present it to the other groups.

Whjen all the stories are finnaly presented, each person or group must choose their favourite story. They can not vote for their own story. Each card left out is deducted from the story poins and the winner is the group/person with the most points.

A second way to play the game is sorting out only the word cards. They are shuffled and the top card revealed. Each person writes down the most synonyms and antonyms for that word card. The winner is the person who writes the most synonyms or anonyms on a selected number of cards.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
The first 4x strategy game fully integrated into an immersive storytelling/choose-your-own-adventure system.

60 years after the most powerful nations on Earth obliterated the planet, small tribes emerge to transition from mere survivors into a functioning society. You play as the leader of one of these tribes, each with a unique method of governing their people, a specific moral alignment, and explicit spiritual beliefs. You are leading a small group of lieutenants and followers. Your objective, specific to your unique character and motivations, is to mold this emerging world in the way you best see fit.

Explore the wasteland to find essential materials, find and recruit other small pockets of survivors, and discover the competing tribes. You must conquer hearts, minds and bodies if you want to emerge as your region's guiding force in forging the world to come The Day After Tomorrow.

In the early game, players explore and expand by revealing hidden tiles, hoping to find modest food sources that will allow them to add tribe members, find groups of other survivors who may be interested in joining forces, fighting for their autonomy, or simply keeping an arms-length relationship. Most importantly players are hoping to find precious rare materials such as ammunition, horses and power sources to assist their growth and expansion.

Through exploration players discover one another on our unique modular hex map system. Then the main phase of the game begins, where culture and religion pass between connected tribes, where the leaders fight for the best people, and each tribe's rare and valuable resources are being plundered during daring raids and defended by courageous sacrifices. Tribes will need to work together in order to achieve shared ends, but ultimately the most important thing is: will your particular vision for the world to come end up proving dominant?

The characters in The Day After Tomorrow are brought to life with voluminous text in a choose-your-own-adventure style. You will need to carefully roleplay your leader as adhering to his or her particular moral, spiritual and governmental predilections is required to achieve success. There will be numerous character choices for players to make and, for those who want The Day After Tomorrow to do so, the design is intended to support deep RPG-style character play.

2260

It is a time of war.

Over one hundred and fifty years ago, mankind began its relentless conquest of the stars. With the realization of infinite resources came infinite greed. What is the point of difficult compromise and drawn out negotiations when strength could gain anything a nation, corporation, or unaligned military force could desire? What need for a minority population to bend to the will of the majority when they could just as easily forge a nation of their own on a virgin world?

Human space is dominated by intense nationalism, rampant balkanization, and brutal competition. Only the Watch keep man from tipping into a new dark age and utter self destruction.

And man is not the only predator in space...


Alien War is a game of ground combat for 28mm (1/56 scale) models set in the year 2260. The rules are designed to give you an authentic feel for future warfare including unpredictable events – sudden rushes, explosions, ambushes, and malfunctioning weapons – that challenge your strategy and tactics as a tabletop general. The terrain can be as elaborate or as simple as you like; but the game is best played with lots of cover (buildings, trees, rocks, downed landing ships, etc). The playing area can represent anything from a futuristic cityscape to an alien forest to a Bug-infested hive!

Each player controls a Force that can be as small as a squad (6-15 models) up to a platoon or even a company in size (40-100 models or more). Some Forces may involve more models than others since they consist either of less skilled human troops (like colonial militia or poorly armed and trained conscripts) or aliens whose strength lies in their overwhelming numbers. As players become more experienced, they can control larger numbers of troops as well as vehicles and incorporate more advanced rules for future warfare. At its most basic size (squad level), a game of Alien War should take an hour or less to play.

Game description from the publisher:

Master builder Matt is the head of the large construction site and there are lots of things to do! He has many plans for the colorful houses that have to be built today. The children help Tim the laborer transport new building blocks from the quarry to the construction sites.

In Little Builders, however, time passes quicker than you might think. Will you finish your houses in time before Matt and Tim's working day is over?

boardgame: I.Q. Games: Wonders of the World
description:
From the back of the box:

Learn about fascinating places in the world as you play this fast-paced quiz game!  Each card contains five clues.  Answer on the first clue, and you earn five points.  If you answer after all five clues (including a picture clue), you still earn a point!  The player with the most points at the end of the game wins.

A "NOW YOU KNOW" section on each card contains fascinating facts to learn and tell.

boardgame: Mines of the Sacred Dragon: Tile Exp Set #2
description: MOSD Tile Expansion Set #2 comes with 10 unique hexagon tiles. Each tile has two connected gray (wild) sides, one color on opposing side plus two unique colors on other two sides.

boardgame: Mines of the Sacred Dragon: Tile Exp Set #3
description: MOSD Tile Expansion Set #3 comes with 10 unique hexagon tiles. Each tile has two colors and each color is on three connected sides.

Game description from the publisher:

Welcome to the number zoo! There's always a lot going on because a wild diversity of animals can be found in these enclosures: rhinos, parrots, wildebeest, cockatoos, and many more.

Keeper Fred naturally knows all the animals personally and taking care of the rascals requires a lot of work as he needs to count them, send them to the proper enclosures, and maintain order in the zoo. But who can help Fred count carefully so that no animal is lost?

With the three games in 3 Gnus und 7 Kakadus, children learn to deal with the numbers to 20, count, sort, organize and develop an understanding for varying amounts.

Game description from the publisher:

Yikes, what's going on here? Once again a small chaos monster has been at work in the house and messed things up. Why else would the refrigerator be in the bathroom? And should the bed really be in the kitchen?

In the five games in Monsterquatsch, you're going to try to catch the chaos monster in the act and clean up the house once again, making sure you know which items can normally be found in which rooms. To maintain order in Lucas' house will require good eyes, quick response, and communication skills.

Game description from the publisher:

Did something just crack under your feet? In Ab durch den Dschungel, brave treasure hunters must find their way through the jungle, following tortuous paths, balancing on a log over a raging river, protecting themselves from pesky mosquitoes, and keeping an eye out for poisonous snakes.

In the end, though, these adventurers might find themselves a big sparkling treasure if they dive into the world of treasure hunters and guide themselves through the many different tasks that await them in the jungle...

Game description from the publisher:

The tomcat Fridolin is very curious, so he looks at all the people on the playground and in the market very carefully. Filled with fascination, Fridlin observes the faces of both children and adults, and he sees that they show very different feelings.

In Was ist da los?, children travel with the tomcat Fridolin into the world of feelings. During the game you'll look at the depicted children and explain the many different behaviors, emotions, and situations they're experiencing.

Game description from the publisher:

In Entdeckerquiz Deutschland, Robby the Robot and his brilliant team of kids are going to quiz players over ten rounds on all they know about Germany.

A different question is asked each round, then all players place one of their chips into one of the four response areas on the "Quizzomat". Players then turn the wheel in the window of the Quizzomat to reveal the solution code and see who guessed correctly. Whether the subject is history, culture, or unusual features, the Quizzomat always knows the right answer. Whoever scores the most points after ten rounds wins.

Feudality: Dark Clouds, Pretty Flowers is a 70-tile expansion for Feudality that includes Bridge Trolls, Rainbows, the Pink Dragon, Lucky Shrooms, Bards, Hermits, and many more new tiles to add to the mix.

The Dark Clouds, Pretty Flowers is a 70-tile expansion.

It contains tiles of:
Unicorn – The Fox – Bearbarian
These three powerful beings will fight for your fiefdom.

Black Cloud – Volcano – Bridge Troll – Franz
These four tiles all have in common that you do not place them in a space on your FMC, but on that of an opponent.

Pink Dragon - Bard - Lookout - Gazebo
The tiles all modify the effects of event cards when you have them in your FMC.

Hill Goats – River Goats – Agtfos
Hill Goats must be placed on a hill, River Goats on the river.

Rogue’s Guild – Rose Garden - Rosenberg
These tiles modify baronial actions.

Caravan – The Prince
The Caravan must be placed adjacent to your keep.

Gravel-Pit Lady – Ferryman
These tiles modify your already placed tiles.

Surveyor
The Surveyor allows you to shift one of your tiles so that it comes to rest on the dividing lines between two spaces.

Apprentice Mage (white/blue)
The Wizard is a very clever guy.

Wizard’s Tower – Hermit
These two particularly powerful tiles must be “hit” directly with the die roll to access their abilities.

Dwarven Mine – Orchard Grove – Magic Forest –
Ruins – Rainbow – Lucky Shroom
These tiles do not introduce any new rules as such.

Dominion of the Gods is a tactical miniatures game played on a board created by the players. The players buy, assemble and paint miniatures for use with the game. The miniatures come with stat cards, these stats will also be available in a rulebook. The stat card tells the player how far the model can move, how many dice it uses to attack, how many dice it gets while defending, how far its combat reach is how many hits the model can sustain before being removed from gameplay as dead and the level of faith it has in its God.

Monsters, heroes, and gods of different pantheons battle in a miniatures skirmish game. Intuitive dice-based combat.  Cards list what models can accomplish, and dice rolls determine hits and blocks. Rolling the power die in special situations can prevent un-blockable hits.

Players start off by choosing a God from a pantheon to play as their main character. Players then decide on a point total for the game they want to play. Once the point total is decided, each player puts together an army with models (using the models point cost) that equal up to the point total decided by the players. Players can only use models from their chosen pantheon at this time. There is the possibility that some  pantheons may be able to benefit from rogue legends in the future. Players are allowed to select one coin that they possess to use during the game. Different coins will be manufactured with different abilities. Some coins will be standard while others will be collectible items sold only during certain times of the year or at specific locations.

If a God is killed during the game, the faith scores of all the models on that team are reduced making them more likely to fail psychology checks. If a God is the only model left on the board, the God loses the ability to use any influence. Influence is a score that tells a God how much magic he can use every round. When a God is left without followers, he loses his magic ability.

Mathable Quattro is a special counting card game that combines classical games Mathable and counting points cards.
Quattro hiding in a small box for card games, two stacks of 53 cards. So if you count well, it means total supply 106 cards in the deck. All cards have number from 0-50.
At the start of every game mix whole package properly, so you don´t have too much of the same numbers together. Lower numbers are usual in the package than the high ones. Each player gets seven cards in hand, which is required to discard as soon as possible. Who do it first, is the winner of the lot.
Players must combine the numbers in hands with the results in any row or column of the table. For example, if you are over twelve and four, you play sixteen (12 4 = 16), eight (12-4 = 8) threesome (12:4 = 3) or osmačtyřicítku (12x4 = 48).
---
Mathable Quattro je speciální počítací hra, která kombinuje herní systémy her Mathable a klasického přebíjení karet.
Quattro je nejmenším bratříčkem ze čtveřice a ukrývá se v malé krabičce pro karetní hry, ve dvou sloupečcích po 53 kartách. Pokud tedy dobře počítáte, znamená to celkovou nabídku 106 karet v balíčku. 
Na začátku každé partie pořádně zamíchejte balíček, ať nemáte příliš stejných čísel pohromadě. Nižších čísel je celkem v balíčku víc, než těch vysokých. Každý z hráčů dostane do ruky sedm karet, kterých se musí zbavit. Komu se to podaří jako prvnímu, se stává vítězem partie. 
Hráči nyní musí zkombinovat čísla ve své ruce s výsledky v některé řadě nebo sloupci na stole. Například pokud nad sebou leží dvanáctka a čtyřka, můžete zahrát šestnáctku (12+4=16), osmičku (12-4=8), trojku (12:4=3) nebo osmačtyřicítku (12x4=48).

From the publisher's website:

Ready, Set, Go! Nimble hands and sharp eyes will lead to victory! An action packed and fun-filled game. Great for the entire family.

The Object:

The moment a card is drawn, quickly imitate the card hands pattern. The last one is the loser! Be the first, and be aware of any illogical hand combinations.

The ballista was an ancient Greek and Roman missile weapon, which launched a large projectile at a distant target.

Introduction

Ballista is an area enclosure, area control abstract game for 2 players: Black and White. It is played on the spaces of a 10x10 board. The board is divided by two red intersecting lines creating four 5 x 5 quadrants. You will also need a set of reversi pieces. The game starts out with 2 black and 2 white reversi pieces placed on the four marked squares on the board.

Play

Players decide who goes first then play alternates.

On each turn players must take two actions:

1)Move one of your color reversi pieces “ballista” exactly like a queen in Chess, orthogonally or diagonally onto any empty space as long as the piece stays within the quadrant where the move initiated from.

2)Fire a “projectile” reversi piece in your color away in a straight line orthogonally or diagonally from the piece just moved, then placing the new reversi piece in your color onto any empty space within one of the other three remaining quadrants.

Players also have the option on their turn to capture an enemy stone. Captures in Ballista are custodian, i.e., an enemy stone is captured when sandwiched between two friendly stones (orthogonal or diagonal). Captured enemy pieces are not removed from the board but flipped over to your color.

Note: A ballista or fired projectile reversi piece may not jump over a friend or enemy piece, also any friendly pieces that were projectiles may now become a ballista.

The game ends when a player has no valid move.

Rockit Science is a game with a steam punk meets the energy industry flavor that was commissioned by XTO Energy to hand out as a holiday gift for its employes.

The concept is simple, just draw a card and move accordingly and try to be the first player to reach the end of the path. The game is functionally equivalent to Candy Land.

Les Fugitifs is a strategic game for 2 or 4 people. 
The theme of the game is the escape of four men from a city. The men need to find their way to the center of the city and then out of the city as quickly as possible. Each of the players tries to find their way and also obstructing the other players in their progress.

It is a tile placing and moving a character game.

Each turn a player can move his character on the board, place tiles or turn a tile on the board.

Level XI uses the components and basic rules of Level X and can be played only in conjunction with the base game. Now your playing pieces can be placed on this supplemental board in addition to the tracks on the main game board. The pieces are not moved here, but they stay where you once placed them - however, you may remove them for other use at any time giving up your position.
The playing pieces on the highest spaces of the Level XI board earn you special points at the end of the game – and you can even score for more than one marker. Beware: you may miss a playing piece you placed on the supplemental board for the regular tracks - but if you are too late for it, the best spaces will be occupied there.

_______________________
This AddOn is specially for Level X (Schmidt Spiele) and is not a 5th-Player-Upgrade,other than the AddOns for "Mountain Goats".

Traverse the greatest landmass on Earth and harvest the riches of the East. Trade between cities in and around Siberia, investing the profits in railroads to facilitate your transports. Work together and against each other in conquering the frozen taiga and the endless steppes. Pay to use your competitors' connections, and watch your pile of currency grow as they use yours. Make the smartest trades and claim the most strategic routes in Trans-Siberian, and you might sit with the most railroads, resources, and money when the game ends and the winner is crowned.

INTRODUCTION

Mapello is a Reversi derivative that includes some special pieces that add new features to the game. These pieces are placed on the board to create a particular board configuration before the main game starts: this is called the setup phase.
A given board configuration may be played repeatedly, with players taking turns to play as black and as white. Since each board configuration will have its own nuances, repeated play enables players to experiment with strategies best suited to each setup.
Alternatively, Mapello can be played in free-play mode. In free-play mode, players take it in turns to setup the board. In order to encourage balance in the setup, the player not setting up the board has the option to play as black or as white (hence to move first or second).

The extra pieces are as follows:
-    Walls: They act like the walls of the board border, but placed inside the board. They are immovable obstacles that cannot be played on.
-    Bonuses: They reward the player with extra points when captured.
-    Jokers (addition by Néstor Romeral Andrés): They are placed on the border and act as pieces of the colour of the player in turn.

By combining these pieces, players can create millions of different boards.

COMPONENTS

-    Game board of 10x10 squares. The inner 8x8 board is used for playing, while the border is only used to place the jokers during the setup phase. 
-    64 Reversi discs (black/white).
-    8 walls (gray squares).
-    8 bonuses (blue discs).
-    8 jokers (red squares).

SETUP PHASE

Optionally, the players may agree some constraints on the number of each type of piece to be placed during the setup phase. Then, subject to these constraints the setup player places their chosen number of pieces. Walls and power-ups can be placed anywhere on the inner 8x8 area (except on the four centre squares), and jokers can be placed anywhere on the outer 10x10 border. The game commences with an initial 2x2 configuration of black and white pieces in the following diagonal pattern:
 PLAYING PHASE

Two players (black and white) alternate turns placing a piece with their colour facing up onto an empty cell of the inner 8x8 board. Black always plays first. Each piece must be placed adjacent to an opponent's piece (either horizontally, vertically or diagonally). Then, all the opponent's pieces that are trapped in a contiguous straight line (either diagonal, vertical, or horizontal) between the newly placed piece and an existing piece of that colour, or a joker, are flipped over to the player's colour. A legal move must case at least one opponent piece to be flipped. If a player cannot make a legal move then they pass.
      
If a player places a piece on top of a blue bonus disc, they take the disc (this will score 3 points at the end of the game).

The game ends when neither player can make a legal move. Players count the pieces of their colour facing up and add the points of the blue discs. Highest score wins. Over a series of games the first aim is to win the most games. In the event of a tie in the number of games won, the point difference over the set of games decides the winner.

Re-implements:
    Othello

Concurrent is a tabletop adaptation of the PC game, Risk 2, which is a computer adaptation of the classic board game, Risk.

Instead of the standard turn-taking that occurs in Risk, Concurrent utilizes a system where the players determine their troop deployment and attacks simultaneously.

The game consists of a game board, representing various countries and territories across the Earth, with connections between the countries by borders and some lines across continents and to island countries/territories.  In addition, there are cards which have the various countries/territories listed on the card, and plastic figures which represent the player's army.

At the beginning of the game, all cards are passed out to the players, which represents the starting countries for each player.  Afterward, the players are allowed a certain number of army units, relative to the total number of players, which they are allow to place on their starting countries.

At the beginning of each round, players are given a piece of paper to write all their actions on.  Included is new unit deployment (based on total countries controlled, largest connected empire, continents controlled, and any matching card set the player can turn in), attacks, and the player's surge attack.  Players may attack any country using army units they control on adjacent countries, but must leave at least one unit in their country as a defender.  In addition, players may elect to make one surge attack, where if one of their attacks is successful, after all other attacks are resolved, they may make one more attack to attempt to conquer another country.

Once all players have completed their decisions for the round, they reveal said decisions, deploy new units, and announce all attacks.  If there are any border clashes (two countries trying to attack each other), they are resolved first.  Battles are resolved using custom 12-sided dice.  There are multiple dice that are rolled relative to the army size.  Each die is numbered 1-6, but the distribution of numbers changes relative to the army size (eg. the larger the army, the more likely the die is to roll higher numbers).  Each player rolls the die, with the player rolling the lowest number losing a number of units relative to the smallest army (the larger the army, the more units lost per roll).  In the event of a tie on the die roll, both players reroll.  The battle is over once one attacking army in the border clash is eliminated.

After border clashes are resolved, mass invasions are resolved.  Mass invasions occur when multiple countries attack the same country.  This can be multiple armies from one player, or more than one.  Mass invasions are resolved the same as border clashes, the only difference being that, unlike border clashes where there is not a specific attacker, the attacking player wins ties.  Just like border clashes, the player who loses the roll also loses units relative to the smallest army size in the battle.  The mass invasion is completed when one of the armies is eliminated.

Invasions are then resolved, which are the same as mass invasions, but are only from one country to another.  After invasions are resolved, spoils of war are resolved.  Spoils of war occur when two or more players attack the same country in a mass invasion and multiple attacking armies remain.  Spoils of war are resolved like border clashes, where ties are resolved with a reroll.

After all attacks are resolved, players will resolve the surge attack they noted on their paper.  Surge attacks are resolved like invasions, or mass invasions if multiple players surge into country.  In the event of a successful mass invasion surge attack, the players resolve the conquest with a spoils of war.

Each round occurs the same as listed above, until one player finishes the round with a previously agreed upon control limit (60% control of countries, 80%, etc).  That player is the winner of Concurrent!

A casual, resource-management game about nature, beauty, and connections.

This is an unusual game because it is dual-purpose. Firstly, and most importantly, it is a good game: fun and interesting, and especially so when played with friends. However, at the same time, it is also designed to share and promote understanding and appreciation of nature.

The game is centered around plant cards. Every plant card has a point value, and the winner of the game is the first player to collect 10 points. Plant Cards grow and change in response to randomly drawn Event Cards, and player-used Resource Cards. There are thirteen different species of plant in this game, and each has its own personality, which means that each responds to Events and Resources in different ways.

To be successful, you must collect, trade, and play Resources in order to improve the plants on your own landscape, foil your opponents' plans, protect yourself from dangerous Events, and defend yourself from the snarkiness of your opponents.

When brushing isn’t enough the Plaque Attackers come to the rescue to save your teeth from decay. But this time they really have their work cut out for them as the four plaque leaders are rushing forward for a tasty meal of tooth!

Out last the Plaque Lords attack until they give up and leave for an easier meal.

Plaque Attackers is a dice rolling game where you and your allies work together to stay alive until the survival meter runs out.

Go on a real party with unrestrained entertainment and a number of pitfalls. The game is great, especially when meeting the company of close friends. Joint moments full of fun!

Each player has two same color pieces with which progresses to a party and then back home to bed after party. First player marching with one, and only after a successful trip with the other one.

Players roll a dice, make move and complete tasks; such as beg coin from other players, make them give their hair, tell some story etc.

---
Vydej se na opravdový mejdan s nevázanou zábavou a množstvím nástrah. Hra je skvělá, zejména sejde-li se společnost blízkých přátel. Společné chvíle plné zábavy!

The purpose of this game is to travel around the Czech countryside and visit 12 different castles, learn about their history and learn to manage given resources.
 The goal is to get all cards and castles (12 in total, from each of the three colors for 4 pieces) - each card with a picture of another castle.
 A player can end the game, even if the card gets at least 10 castles, while he may miss a maximum of two colors of 1.
 Thus, the game ends but only in the case where this is for itself to be favorable. In the event that the game fails to get at least 10 different cards, the game ends when all the pictures of castles have been played.
 The player, who has the most money at end wins, even if he does not have all the cards and castles.

---
Cílem hry je získání kartiček všech hradů a zámků (celkem 12, od každé ze tří barev po 4 kusech)  — každá  kartička s obrázkem jiného hradu.
 Hráč může hru ukončit i tehdy, když získá kartičky alespoň 10 hradů a zámků, přičemž mu smí chybět od dvou barev nejvýše po 1 kuse.
 Takto však ukončuje hru pouze v tom případě, pokládá-li to pro sebe za výhodné. V případě, že se během hry nepodaří získat alespoň 10 různých kartiček, končí hra tehdy, když jsou všechny obrázky hradů a zámků vydány.
 Vyhrává ten hráč, kterému zůstane nejvíc peněz, i když nemá všechny kartičky hradů a zámků.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Ever wondered how life is at the police station? Definitely do not expect a large bureaucracy, you'd better get ready diaphragm! As a target for criminal bullet as a torture device laughter ...
 To play, you will need:
Two six-sided dice
  Pencil and paper for counting points
  Matches or coins to progress cards and inserted them into action
 Try out what it's like to be a true guardian of the law. Learn to use service weapons to fight criminals, starting with hooligans and thieves, through repulsive deviants, to the rough and dangerous mafia assassins. You collect royalty, grant and fulfill orders. And the best is awarded the title police president!

Each player starts with four random cards. Then you prepare pencil and paper which is for tracking current status of policemen level; very level means more cards during turn. At each turn, players have a total of three events. Usually, they will all revolve around playing cards. The most important thing in the entire game cards are interventions and arrest that brings gamers the most points to policemen. To do this you need set of card and support of a partner or have necessary equipment (body armor, torch etc.). 
 Includes: 108 playing cards, rules, ranks, police summary


Zajímá vás, jak vypadá život na policejní stanici? Rozhodně nečekejte velkou úředničinu, raději si připravte bránice! Jako terč pro zločineckou kulku, i jako mučící nástroj smíchu...
 Ke hře budete potřebovat:
Dvě šestistěnné kostky
 Tužku a papír na počítání bodů
 Zápalky nebo mince na rozpracované karty a do nich vložené akce


 Vyzkoušíte si, jaké to je, být opravdovým strážcem zákona. Naučíte se používat služební zbraně, bojovat s kriminálními živly, počínaje chuligány a zlodějíčky, přes odpudivé devianty, až po drsné mafiány a nebezpečné vrahy. Budete sbírat hodnosti, udílet i plnit rozkazy. A ten nejlepší získá titul polecejní prezident!
 Obsahuje: 108 herních karet, pravidla, hodnosti, policejní přehled

A party-style card game where players use their creativity to finish prompts by building off of starter words. These are judged by another player who selects his/her favorite. The player who submitted this is then awarded a point.

The game begins with one player being chosen as the judge. A blue prompt card and an orange starter card are pulled from the deck and read out loud to everyone.

The remaining players finish the prompt by writing a phrase that incorporates at least one of the starter words. These phrases are known as wordners, and may make starter words plural, change their tense, and add other words to them.

The players submit their wordners on a piece of paper to the player on the judge's left, who reads them out loud. The judge selects his/her favorite, and the creator of this is awarded a point represented by the round's blue card.

Now, the player to the judge's left becomes the judge and a new round begins. The first player to collect 5 blue cards wins!

"Reservoir Dogs" The board game

Six professional criminals are hired by Joe Cabot and his son Nice Guy Eddie for a job. Not know each other and remain anonymous, hidden under color names: Mr. Orange, Mr. White, Mr. Pink, Mr. Blonde, Mr. Brown and Mr. Blue. Prepare thoroughly robbery diamond store, but police shows up unexpectedly at the time of making a slaughter robbery resulting in the deaths of several police officers, and employees. All suspect that there is a traitor infiltrated in the group.

Thieves who manage to survive and get to the warehouse with the diamonds will win the game, but there is an undercover agent who will try to sabotage them. Police will also further complicated the getaway. Besides the briefcase with diamonds can change hands and perhaps one of the robbers take not to share with others

" The memory game that gets you moving"

Tune up both your reflexes and memory by waving your hand in loops that can sense your motions. Seven game modes available for 1-4 players, including a mode that allows you to create your own music.

- Repeat the Beat
- Rhythm and Flow
- Versus Mode
- Reflex master
- Musicology
- Freestyle DJ
- Music studio

(Battery included!)

Players compete against each other or the game by repeating sounds or combinations of sounds played by the game. Moving your hand over  a sensor in the loops triggers a correct or incorrect response from the game. Sometimes you must use both hands to trigger a correct response. The game demands both memory ability to replay the right sequence of sounds as well as dexterity in moving your hands to the correct location(s). The non-game modes teach music scales and you can create songs with the correct hand placement in the open loops or "LOOPZ".
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
In Tricky Wildlife, each of the players is a wildlife manager who's trying to keep many different animal species in his wildlife park, while also preserving the food chain of predators and prey and helping endangered species.

The food chain of animals has top predators who eat the lower predators, who in turn eat the next species down the "animal pyramid", who in turn eats the lowest prey – for example: Sharks eat Penguins, Penguins eat Fish, Fish eat Shrimp. Missing a prey animal means that the higher animal type starves, which gives you penalty points as a wildlife manager. Thus, you want to maintain all species in your wildlife park, especially the rarest species as they give you prestige points.

In each of the eight game years (i.e., rounds), you can add 1 or 2 animal groups to your park; each group first consists of 1-4 animals of one species. When you attract them to your park, these are young animals. They can grow old and die naturally after five years, but to do this they need enough prey to feed on during their lifetime. Otherwise they starve. At the same time, higher predators in the food chain can eat these animals.

An animal's age is shown by its location on your lifetime track, which uses four sticks to divide the track into five sections (i.e., years). After each year, you shift your rightmost stick to the leftmost side, which ages all of your animals by one year.

When you don't have all four different species, you gain one penalty point per missing species (and higher level animals may starve). If you have the most (or second-most) of an animal which was rare before, you score 1-2 prestige points. After year eight, you add the number of your animal groups to your prestige point total to calculate your final score.

The challenge in Tricky Wildlife is to balance your desire for prestige points with the need to preserve your complete food chain.

In Mad Barrington's Gyre players compete to accumulate the shares worth the most bottle caps by the end of the game. "Why bottle caps?" Because in the post-apocalyptic future of flying castles, giant robber baron cockroaches and mutated broccoli.... bottle caps are the coin of the realm.

Each player starts with a identical set of cards and can play, buy or sell cards each turn that improve their deck's purchasing power or allow them to impact the value of shares (Guns, Ammo, Food, Water, Meds & Pennies) directly or indirectly through rumour cards or affecting the number of stock value cards in play.

Shares are bought and banked separately from each player's main deck but the stock value cards that determine the value of the shares are bought and sold from a common pool that is manipulated by the players throughout the game.

As well, each player has a character card that gives them a distinct bonus in the game ranging from Humbles Wuggles-Roach's ability to buy Ammo shares at a reduced rate to Lord Fat Pockets ' improved starting bottle caps and Mad Barrington's ability to buy rumour cards on the cheap.

From the game box:

"The Game of Rhyme and Reason.
In this hilarious cross between Chinese whispers and drawing charades you start by inventing a rhyme... Anything from "A Rat on a Mat"... to "A Fish on a Dish"... or even "Plaice in Space" The next player then has to draw your rhyme! Moving clockwise, the initial rhyme is hidden and the drawing interpreted as a new rhyme... ... and that is where the fun begins! With everyone drawing and guessing at the same time it's a crazy, frantic, befuddled muddle of a game!"

At the end of a round points are scored for drawing a phrase correctly and for recognising the original rhyme. Several rounds are played and the winner is the first person to score 20 points.
Contents: game pad, pencils, score pad, rules

A simple trivia game played on a map of the German State of Schleswig-Holstein.

A long track winds around the State, starting in Hamburg and ending on the Isle of Sylt. Movement is decided by a die. There are four categories of questions:
A = General knowledge
K = Culture
W = Economic
P = Politics and History
Obviously, all the 500 questions in the game have some connection to Schleswig Holstein.

There is an interesting shortcut that may be taken where no questions are asked. Instead you roll the die and if you get a number from 1 to 5, you move two spaces. But if you roll a 6, you must go back 15 spaces.

First to reach the end (Ziel) wins.

On a little bit of extra trivia, the board shows clearly that the Eastern neighbour to Schleswig-Holstein is, in fact, the DDR (East Germany). As the game was published in 1989 and the wall came down that year, then this might be the last non-historical game to feature the DDR as a neighbouring country.

Crazy Cakes is a race game where players try to be the first to stack their 12 triangular wooden pieces on top of each other to represent 12 layers of a cake. Be the first to match the recipe card and win that card.

The challenge is that each piece is 3 sided, and each player has two of each flavor but the sides are different. EX: Each player has two strawberry pieces - 1 piece will have two vanilla sides while the other has two chocolate sides, use the wrong side of that piece and the puzzle (cake) can't be made. This is when the frantic fun begins!

It's the "Game of High-Stacking fun."

Stavropol Bashni is a stacking game from the Checkers family. It features shared pieces. As in all Checkers games, the aim of the game is to leave the opponent without a legal move. It is played on a traditional 8x8 Checkers board with stones that are suitable for Bashni, i.e. stackable and with kings being turned men or pieces of a different color, as most traditional Russian sets are.

The rules follow Bashni. In short, when a man or king is captured, it is not taken from the board, but put under the piece which captures the man. This way, stacks are formed. The topmost of piece of a stack decides which player the stack belongs to, how the stack moves, which piece is promoted on reaching a back rank, and, importantly, which piece is captured, when you capture a stack: the topmost piece only. We will repeat that. If the topmost piece of a stack is captured and the next piece at the top belongs to the opponent, the stack switches allegiance and belongs to the opponent then.

The difference to Bashni is that pieces are shared - as in Stavropol Checkers. A player may move stacks owned by the opponent, too, according to the rules for the topmost piece. The exact winning condition is therefor: a player loses when it is his turn and the stacks belonging to him give no legal move.

Online play:

     igGameCenter

Stage Chaser is a bicycle race themed board game.  It allows players to compete against each other in any of the world’s greatest bike races; including ones they’ve just made up.

The board is made up of interchangeable terrain mats which, when fitted together, form the profile of a stage.  The stage can be flat, hilly or mountainous – whatever the players choose.

Each player is in a charge of star rider.  They move their rider along the board by playing action cards, whose values vary according to the kind of terrain they are played on.  The majority of the action cards show other riders in the race (team mates of the star riders) who can be slipstreamed, but there are also ‘attack’ and ‘block!’ cards.   Players need energy tokens to move forward when they play attack cards and these are fought over during the race.  Block! cards allow players to sabotage the moves of their competitors.

The aim of the game is to cross the finish line first.  If just one stage is played, the winner of the stage is the winner of the game.  If more than one stage is played, the winner of the game is the player with the best overall score when all the stage results have been added together.

This Promo Research introduces the Stock Market (Borsa).
If a player chose to use this research, he will gain extra money.

A car race game that includes elements of Memory.

Each player is given a set of 20 cards. Your set is shuffled and layed face down in two rows of 10 cards. This will be your 'track'.

Every turn, a player turns over two cards. If they match, his car can move on to one of them if one of the two cards is 'next in line' on the track. Thus, the first thing to do is to get a matched card on one of the two start cards. Having acheived that, you want to get a card face up in row 2, and so on. If you do not find a match,your turn is over. If you do find one, you can turn over two more cards and keep going until you fail to expose a matching pair.

The first player to reach row 10 on his track, wins.

Welcome True Believers! Ka-pow is an action-packed card game of super-heroic mayhem. Create a heroic identity, form a superhero team and then battle the worlds super-villains and save the earth.

Each player takes on the role of a superhero, fighting for truth and justice as part of a superhero team made up of the other player’s superheroes. Your team must foil the diabolical plots of a series of nefarious super-villains and in doing so the superheroes will receive victory points based on their contribution towards defeating each super-villain. At the end of the game the superhero with the most victory points is the winner.

As you will see, the superheroes must work together to defeat each super-villain, otherwise the villain will win and all of the players will lose! However, once all of the super-villains are defeated, only one of the superheroes can win the game.  Collecting victory points calls for playing the right card at the right time, so you will need to think very carefully about which cards to use each round and how to use your superpowers in order to score as many points as possible. On the other hand, being too selfish in the use of your cards and superpowers may result in a super-villain being able to carry out their diabolical plot, and if that happens everyone will lose the game. It’s a dreadful dilemma make no mistake – but then nobody said being a superhero was going to be easy!

Compete over 3-5 story arcs to be the most heroic Superhero and earn the adulation of the public.

The land of Nippon is riven by civil war and the leaders of the different factions have a number of secret missions they need carried out. As master of one of the four great Ninja clans you must train your warriors so they are ready to carry out the missions offered. But beware, if you take too long one of your rival clans may undertake a mission before you and steal the rich reward offered for carrying it out.

In Shinobi: Way of the Ninja, you control a ninja clan and must perform different missions in order to obtain wealth and win the game. The missions you are called on to carry out are represented by three Mission cards that will tell you what you need to do (the task), where the mission is to be carried out (the location), and what problems may arise that will hinder you in the missions execution (complications). Each of these things requires one or more Skill cards from your hand to overcome. For example, if the mission required the use of unarmed combat you would need to play that Skill card in order to carry it out.

Once you have collected all of the Skill cards you need you may carry out a mission and reap the rewards for your clan. Unfortunately you will rarely have the right cards in your hand to perform a mission straight away. Instead you will need to store cards from your hand for future use by placing cards face-down from your hand into one of two dojos you control. Storing cards in this way represents you training the ninjas in your clan to perform a mission.

Keeping track of the cards placed in each dojo is challenging as you are not normally allowed to examine the cards once they are placed. You must use your powers of concentration and memory to remember which cards you have placed so that you can use them when you are ready to carry out a mission. Although this is difficult at first, you will find that your mental abilities quickly sharpen and that you will become increasingly skilled at the game. This is as it should be: being the master of a ninja clan requires great mental fortitude, and Shinobi: Way of the Ninja will test it to the utmost. However, if you get completely stuck, you can always visit the sensei who will (at a cost) allow you to examine the cards you have placed.

You receive a reward for completing each mission in the form of gold. The harder a mission the more gold you will receive, but the more cards you will need to play to carry the mission out. At the end of the game the player who has amassed the largest fortune wins.

Arche Extra Mix 2 includes even more animals for you to board in the card game Ark before the floods come and cleanse the Earth of everything that isn't bolted down. How could you have missed all these animals the first two times you looked? And what have you overlooked this time?!

A dexterity game in which two players are each equipped with a small plastic frying pan and a squishy plastic "egg". The opponents simultaneously toss their egg to the other person, who attempts to catch it in their frying pan. This repeats until someone drops their egg and loses.

There is also a solo version in which one person sees how many times they can toss the egg up in the air and catch it.

boardgame: 1836: Short Lines in the West Riding
description:
1836 is a railway operations and share trading board game in the 18xx series. Set in a 10 x 10 mile area of the West Riding area of England that had a staggering number of very small railway companies called "short lines", each of which competed very aggressively across duplicative routes.

The core features of the game:

- Highly dynamic track – There are a relatively high number of named locations that can be laid / upgraded in a variety of competitive ways to ensure that the map develops differently each game.

- Evolving trains – Each train type evolves as it is bought; e.g. there are 2* 2+1, 2* 2+2 then 1* 2X (think of it as a mini diesel that can count two cities and infinite towns). The 2+1 is rusted by the first type of four (4+2), 2+2 by the second (4+3) and so on.

- Staged Minor Companies – Minors are available throughout the game, 18 are shuffled and 4 are available in each of phases 2, 3 and 4 (all 12 available privates are visible from the start). Minors convey a benefit (control over a hex, cheaper track etc) and about half are railway companies that can be absorbed along with a "free" train that can be run immediately.

- Loans with Variable Interest Rate – When a Minor is absorbed by a Major Company it inherits the Minor's liabilities. Loans suppress stock price making stock jumps easier but the interest rate increases as loans are taken, so a balance must be found.

boardgame: Time: A Game of Chance for Many Players
description:
The time theme is very loosely pasted onto this game. The clocks used on the board and the cards could have just as easily been card denominations. This is a simple game of match.

A dealer/banker is assigned. Each player is dealt five cards face up. The dealer then deals one card face down on each of the 12 clocks on the board. In the first round of play the dealer turns up one of the twelve cards at a time. If a player has a matching card in one of his/her upturned five, then the player has to pay in the amount of chips/points indicated at that point on the board in large red letters. If the player has two matching cards, the player must pay double and so on. If the overturned card matches the time on the board at that spot, TIME is called and all payments are doubled. In this round all payments are made to the pot. After all twelve cards are overturned they are returned to the deck, reshuffled, and a fresh twelve are distributed to the twelve clock positions face down. The players keep their original five cards.

In the second round, as each of the twelve cards are overturned, payments are made to the players following the same rules as in round one. The rules do not explain the end of the game scenario. Nor do they explain if the rounds continue to alternate put/take. It is also unclear how many chips the players start with. It is suggested that the white chips equal 1, the yellow chips 2, the red chips 5 and the blue chips 10.

Theme:
You and your friends are heroes standing before the vanguard of an invading army.  Use your array of very large, sometimes ridiculous, weapons to try to defeat the vanguard.

Goal of the game:
Players try to keep a grid of enemies from growing too large, all while trying to outscore the other players in the end.  If the enemy army gets too large, everyone loses!

Gameplay:
Players draw a weapon from the Weapon deck.  Weapons have two stats: Damage done and how many times the weapon can be swung.  They use this weapon to deal damage to the enemy formation, starting with the front line.  Players can keep hitting the same enemy, or proceed to an adjacent enemy. Players keep any enemies they kill.  The player can cash in the enemies they've killed to draw from the Epic deck.  Epic attacks function the same as a weapon, but are far more powerful.

A racing game with a theme of environmental protection. The first player to collect an eco medal on all six continents and to reach his/her starting location with 1.000 eco dollars wins the game.

Players travel around the world to take part in environmental protection activities and by doing so, win eco medals. Open cards present the actions that have to be taken. There is one card fewer than there are players, so there is competition for actions.
Movement on the board is determined by dice. One can choose to use only one dice and earn 200 dollars, or use two dice and earn no money, or one can pay 500 dollars to use the plane. When a player reaches the target destination, they earn an eco medal of this continent and the card is removed from the game and replaced with a new one. If a player lands on the same space as another, he/she can take away money or a medal from that player.

The game ends when one player has earned a medal of each continent and reaches his/her home town with at least 1.000 eco dollars in his/her pocket.

Space Battle is a power struggle between you and your opponents for control of the galaxy. This is a "take that" card game with silly cards.

There are two similar versions of the game in the box. 
Everyone starts with some ship cards randomly drawn from the ship deck.  Each ship gives you access to use particular kinds of weapon cards that you draw from a shared deck.  These cards like "red laser" and "drones" are used to wear down other ships.  Some cards destroy ships in ways outside of weaponry.

When you destroy another ship you get the printed Victory Points for it. If all your ships are destroyed you go to the deck to see if you are lucky enough to get a card that gives you another ship to stay in the game, otherwise you are eliminated from play, but victory (in one version of the game is based on points).

boardgame: Die Lieben Sieben: Heute steigt die Party!
description:
Childrens dexterity game.

From the box:
The Friendly Seven want to celebrate a big party in the mansion Siebenklein. They cleaned up the house already, only the balloon decoration is missing. Four of the friendly Seven blow up the ballons and the other three hang them up.
Would you like to help the Friendly Seven to decorate the house?
A game ofskills to develop hand-eye coordination.

The game is a dexterity game for younger children.
The board is in 3 segments, with coloured spaces, at the far end   a goal is constructed looking like the front of a house, with an open doorway. Players take it in turns to flick or shove a "puck", down the long gameboard attempting to stop on spaces of a particular colour, landing on a colour gets the  player a token to add to their playerboard.
When all 6 different colours are collected, then the player may attempt to shoot the puck through the doorway of the house at the far end of the board to win.

A simple racing game for two players. Movement is determined by a six-sided die.

The game consists of a board (showing a 37-space racing track), 4 player tokens and a die. Both players try to get their soapbox derby racer (i.e. one of their two player tokens) to the finish line first. Players take turns rolling the die. The player's first token (the racer) is moved according to the result of the die roll. If the racer lands on a stop space, the player has to send his/her mechanic (= the second token) the following turns. Only when the mechanic has reached the racer, the racer can move on. The mechanic stays on the stop space until the racer lands on another stop space.

The player whose racer reaches the finish line first wins the game.

A children's game in which judging length and shape by touch is the central element.

Game idea: A long time ago Schnuffel, the dog, and his friends hid many bones in the garden. Now they want to dig them up again but not any old bones, it has to be specific ones. The bone has to be the right shape and size. And the dogs are chasing away each other too!

Aim of the game: To find the correct wooden bone by touch as quickly as possible in order to collect the most bone cards.

How it is played: The board shows a circular path consisting of single-colour spaces and multi-coloured dog-house spaces. Arrows next to the spaces show the direction in which the dogs move. Each player has a dog-shaped player piece. A colour die determines movement. The active player puts his/her dog on the next empty space of that colour. If he/she lands on a dog-house space, he/she is allowed to search for bones. This means that he/she puts his/her hand into the bag with the wooden bones and tries to find the bone that is shown on the top bone card. While he/she is trying to identify the correct bone by touch, the other players continue taking turns rolling the die and moving their dogs. As soon as the active player thinks he/she has found the correct bone, he/she shouts "Stop!" and the other players stop rolling. The chosen bone is compared to the one on the card. If they match, the active player gets to keep the card. The wooden bone is returned to the bag. Then the active player starts searching for the next bone. His/her turn ends as soon as either one of the other players lands on a dog-house space, or he/she chooses the wrong bone.

End of game: The game ends when all the bone cards have been used up. The player with the most cards wins the game.

Game components:
- game board
- 4 dog-shaped wooden player pieces
- 9 wooden bones (different lengths, round piece on one end, or on both, or none)
- 18 bone cards (showing the different lengths and shapes)
- color die
- cloth bag

Yellow Jersey is a cycling race for 2-4 players. Starting in Paris, you move your team of three cyclists around France through a series of race stages by choosing (a variable number of) movement tokens from your stack, with the token being numbered 1-6. Each round the player holding the Yellow Jersey moves first. You will need to plan how to move your bikes so that you retain maximum movement and take advantage of opportunities to slipstream. Crashes, uphill slopes, and the outside lane will slow you down, whereas downhill slopes and the inside lane may work in your favor.

The race can be conducted either way around the circuit and the first player to get one cyclist back to Paris wins. (The game includes a team winning variant.)

An educational game aimed at teaching the rules of conduct on ski pistes ("Pistenregeln").

The game combines roll-and-move elements with trivia game elements. The board shows four different skiing areas. Players move their player tokens up the ski lift according to their die roll results. Once they've reached the top, they can ski downhill (again using die roll results), but first they have to answer a question connected with skiing, such as "What is forbidden on a chair lift?", "What is important when overtaking other skiers?" etc. Apart from questions on the rules of conduct, there are general skiing trivia questions like "Where was skiing invented?" or "When did the first skiing race in Central Europe take place?"
The first player to complete four downhill races wins the game.

Die Windrosen is a six-tile mini-expansion for Carcassonne. Each tile depicts all or part of a wind rose (AKA compass rose). The red, full wind rose is a new starting tile, and divides the playing area into four fields.

This expansion was released at Modell-Hobby-Spiel 2012 in Leipzig. Attendees had to go on a scavenger hunt to collect clues which would permit them to obtain the expansion from the Carcassonne-on-Tour booth.

Short rules:

    Orange full rose is new start tile and defines the four quarters: SW, SE, NW, NE. 
    Blue full rose  (reg. backside) redefines the four quarters.
    Orange quarter rose tiles  (reg. backside) are worth 3 bonus points if put down in the corresponding quarter of the playing field, e.g. the South-West rose tile put down in the SW quarter defined by the current valid full wind rose.
    Appart from that the roses have no influence on the play and the features on the tiles and other points aquired through the tile.
    The blue full and the 4 orange quarter rose tiles are shuffled in the stack at the beginning.


Part of the Carcassonne series.

In Život v Česku ("Life in the Czech Republic"), players start with a bit of free time, some volunteer work, and experience in primary school. During the course of the game, they can study at different schools, try a range of jobs, buy a house, start a family, and (alas) suffer from negative phenomena, such as an arrest.

Život v Česku, which is tailored to the Bohemian Czech mentality and environment, features a new unique gaming system that gives players a lot of latitude in what's possible within the game.

Bonus station card. Base game has three 'placeholder' station cards that can be cashed in for a promo of choice 'from the box'.

It was given in Essen 2011 for the buyers of the basegame

From the card:
"When Built: Agree a number with at least one other player, roughly equal to the number of games being played in the current room (or hall). Gain 1 point for every two games."

boardgame: Paperclip Railways: Top left-hand corner of Wales
description:
Bonus station card. Base game has three 'placeholder' station cards that can be cashed in for a promo of choice 'from the box'.

From the card:
"Whenever you make the sound of a steam train while building a link, gain 1 point. Maximum of 1 point per link."

A simple, luck-based racing game for children.

The game consists of one island tile, 4 wooden turtles, 43 cards and one six-sided number die.

The island tile is the target space that all the turtles (= wooden player pieces) want to reach as fast as possible. The cards, which show either one of several sea creatures or a smaller island, are used to create a path towards the target island tile. They are placed face down.
Players take turns rolling the die. The die roll determines how far the player may move his/her turtle. The card it lands on is turned over and depending on the type of card, the turtle is moved several spaces forwards or backwards.

The player whose turtle is first to reach the main island wins the game.

boardgame: Armored Knights North Africa: Operation Venezia
description:
The German offensive to take Tobruk / May 27 - June 28, 1942

Armored Knights North Afrika - Operation Venezia allows you to plan and fight the battle that won Rommel Tobruk.  It was a close and at the same time complete victory for Rommel.  His forces made their way around the flank of the British Gazala Line and met with initial success.  But after a few days the Germans found themsleves encircled by a strongly entrenched British Brigade, the 150th, which sat astride the German supply route.
Rommel came close the throwing in the towel, within about a day, before clinching success by finally forcing the 150th Brigade to surrender first and open the supply route.  Immediately after beating the 150th Brigade the Brtish armour decided to make a final effort to beat Rommel in the Cauldron.  They failed and Rommel turned the tables on them.  So much so that almost 90% of the remaining British armour was destroyed and gave Rommel his opportunity to take Tobruk and move past the Egyptian frontier wire and towards Alexandria.

All units modeled as battalions, companies/batteries.
Hexes represent approximately 2km of terrain.
Each turn represents 2 hours of ff daylight or 2-4 hours of night.

This game comes with: 
•Armored Knights North Afrika Rules 
•(9) 11x17 Scenario Cards 
•Numerous Player Aides and Examples 
•Game Turn Record Track for Venezia 
•Game Turn Record Track for Battleaxe 
•(4) Full Color Order of Battle Charts 
•Full Color Battleaxe Order of Battle Chart 
•Very Sturdy box 
•Approximately 1000 double sided unit counters and markers

Incredible Courage at Elchingen  is the battle where Marshall Ney defeated the Austrian General Reisch just north of the Danube River, just before the surrender at Ulm.  Ney must move 2 of his divisions over the river first and then fight a larger enemy force.  The Austrians helped the French immensely by keeping themselves in their defensive positions.

The game system has a variable strength system that provides for an endless amount of replayability.  This game provides an excellent introduction to the Incredible Courage game system as the forces involved are fairly small, one French Corps vs One Austrian Corps.  This game has some improvements over the Incredible Courage at Austerlitz series with a better organized rulebook and Level of Order markers that better portray what level the unit is currently at.

Play proceeds based on a set of initiative die rolls and Leadership values for Division and Corps Commanders.  Players then choose to either fire or move.  Play alternates between the players until all divisional and corps forces have had the opportunity to move or fire.  Firing is accomplished unit by unit.  Units may perform assault before moving on to other Divisions.  Units are modeled as Infantry Companies (150 men), with Battaion substitute counters, Cavalry Squadrons (100 mounted soldiers), and Artillery Sections (2-3 guns).  Units take losses in Levels of Order, starting at 5 for regular troops and 6 for Elite troops, and when they reach 0 they are eliminated.

Players must use the tactics of the day to put the right forces in the right place at the right time to ensure the success of the mission.  Putting infantry, cavalry, and artillery together will enhance your chances of success.

CCULELE Champion is an action and memory game, with everyone participating at the same time to follow the poses presented to them. One by one, more poses will be presented and the challenge for you is to remember them all so that you can take actions of your own. Are you ready to challenge others to become the champion?

Players have special marbles and the slide on the 3' table. The try to have their pieces stop in the 3-2-1 zone for scoring. The directions state that players may play going the same direction or in opposite directions (unlike similar shuffle board type games).

boardgame: Die Fürsten von Catan: Sonderkarte Frühjahr 2012 – Catan Mobil
description:
Catan Mobil is the 2012 special card for "The Rivals for Catan".

Ability of the card:
"Wird die Zahl einer angrenzenden Landschaft gewürfelt, erhalten Sie dort einen weiteren Rohstoff. Legen Sie das Catan Mobil danach auf einen anderen Bauplatz, ggf. auch auf einen des Mitspielers. Gibt es keinen, legen Sie es auf die (eigene) Ablage."

In a free translation:
"In case a number of a neighbouring territory came in the dice roll, you receive one extra material from this territory. Place the Catan-mobil in another construction field after that, even in a rival construction field. If there are no free construction fields, place it in your discard pile."

"Lingon, lådvin & långkalsonger" is an entertaining game where you win or loose points based on how typically swedish you behave.

The game contains 300 questions about everything from Eurovision song contest and croqs shoes to the swedish sausage "falukorv".

The rules state some examples on how to play the game but leaves a lot to the players to decide. But the main course of the game is that a question is given about how you would act/think in a certain situation (there are also some traditional quiz questions). The players then say or write down their answers. After this points are added/subtracted according to the backside of the card. The winner is the player with the most points at the end. This player is a so called "Svensson", a typical swede.

In Copyright, players need to reproduce a pattern as quickly as possible using the transparent image sheets that they have in hand. Each sheet is a 3x3 grid that has a circle, a square, and a plus in three of its nine squares.

At the start of a round, one player takes four image sheets at random, then stacks them together to form a model for that round. Players receive ten sheets at random, then try to reproduce the model by flipping the sheets and stacking them any way they like; the sheets do not have to overlap perfectly, but a player will lose points for any non-perfect overlaps. How does a player indicate which squares in his stack are to be considered the solution? Through the use of a 3x3 grid that has a thick black outline; each player has one of these in addition to the ten sheets.

As soon as a player recreates the model, the round ends. Each player scores one point for each square in the model that she reproduced in her image; whoever ended the round receives one bonus point, scoring 10 points (instead of nine). All players then lose one point for each row and column that "sticks out" from her 3x3 black-bordered grid. Players then shuffle the sheets and start a new round.

When a player's score totals 20 or more points, the game ends and this player wins.

boardgame: Pz8 Two Pages Wargaming Rules Collection
description:
A collection of miniature wargaming rules that are only 2 pages (A4) long: the Pz8 rules are intended for a small table, ideally with "micro" models, play fast (from 30' to 1 hour) and are solo friendly.
