Classic miniature wargaming mechanics are used, in the simplest possible way, but trying to keep a minimum degree of realism and allowing historical tactics. All rules use D6 for resolving combat. Some of the rules, like the WW2 and Modern land, use rulers for movement and weapons ranges, while others use a grid system, like the WW2 and Modern aerial (hexes) the WW1(squares) and the WW2 naval (large areas). Representational scales vary from 1 base of miniatures = 1 Battalion or 1 Platoon, to 1 model = 1 aircraft or ship, again depending on the rules set.

Available as a free .pdf the Pz8 Wargaming Rules Collection includes:

- Pz8 WW2 rules 
- Pz8 1950-75 rules 
- Pz8 1975-2010 rules 
- Pz8 Sci-Fi rules 
- Pz8 WW2 Divisional rules 
- Pz8 1939-41 Naval rules
- Pz8 WW2 Coastal rules
- Pz8 Space rules
- Pz8 WW1 Divisional rules
- Pz8 1935-65 & WW1 Aerial rules
- Pz8 1965-75 Aerial rules
- Pz8 Colonial Wargame rules
- Pz8 Fantasy Wargame rules
- Legio VIII Ancient / Medieval Wargame rules

Aim of the game: Each of the players tries to create the L-shaped sign of the Flying Crane with his/her pieces. Their opponent tries to block him/her.

Each player has 9 pieces, 7 normal pieces and two blocking pieces in a different colour. Each player tries to get 4 of his/her pieces into an L-shaped position. The active player can either move one of his/her normal pieces (one space horizontally or vertically, and only onto empty spaces) or move one of their blocking pieces (up to two spaces horizontally or vertically, or one space diagonally).

The player who manages to create the sign of the Flying Crane wins the game.

A roll-and-move game that aims at teaching children how to safely get to school (traffic rules).

The game consists of a game board, 4 player pieces, 16 cards, 60 plastic chips, a number die and a colour die. The board shows a path to school.  The aim of the game is decided by the players - either being first to reach school or winning the most chips.
Players take turns rolling the number die and move their piece according to the die roll. If you land on a space with a circle next to it, you earn a chip. Reaching a stop sign means you have to stop. On your next turn, you roll the colour die and can move on if you roll green. Landing on a space with an exclamation mark means you've put yourself in danger (board shows a dangerous situation) and you lose a chip.
There is also a variant that uses cards: Before crossing a road, you have to answer a road safety question printed on one of the cards.

The Box provides seventy-eight quizzes. Each card contains a fact and number of questions on a common theme.
Answer correctly and score a point. The player with the most points wins.

Several other rules are proposed including soloplay rules.

Similar to La Boîte à Quiz.

Questions about WWE and wrestling.

The Box provides seventy-eight quizzes. Each card contains a fact and number of questions on a common theme.
Answer correctly and score a point. The player with the most points wins.

Similar to La Boîte à Quiz.

Questions about Asterix.

The Box provides seventy-eight quizzes. Each card contains a fact and number of questions on a common theme.
Answer correctly and score a point. The player with the most points wins.

Similar to La Boîte à Quiz.

Topics are nations and customs, words and phrases, inventions and discoveries, history and geography etc. A thick booklet contains extensive responses.

The Box provides seventy-eight quizzes. Each card contains a fact and number of questions on a common theme.
Answer correctly and score a point. The player with the most points wins.

boardgame: La Boîte à énigmes  spéciale police scientifique
description:
Another version of the La Boîte à énigmes. 
Twenty-five murder mysteries in three levels of difficulty. The game master becomes aware of the full puzzle with its solution, and then reads the beginning of the investigation to the players. These can then ask questions that will be answered by yes or no. The gamemaster can help players by giving up three indices.

boardgame: La Boîte à énigmes pas si scientifiques que ça
description:
Another version of the La Boîte à énigmes. This time the riddles refer to areas very familiar, and we wonder why we never asked these questions before. The answers, in turn, are often a good sense appalling.

Players or teams compete in puzzle-solving (sometimes timed, sometimes not) in order to score points in different categories. The first team (or player) to score a point in 4 of the 6 categories wins the game.

Every turn, the current player/team draws a card and hands it to the Dealer, a non-competitive figure that reads it aloud and then manages the competition between the people trying to solve the riddle.

Based on the La Boîte à Quiz the game is more suitable for young audiences. The box contains everything needed to play except an hourglass, paper and pencil.

Topics are animals, plants, the earth, the human body, space, history and science. Each card contains a fact and number of questions on a common theme. Answer correctly and score a point. The player with the most points wins.

Junior version with fantasy theme

Players or teams compete in puzzle-solving (sometimes timed, sometimes not) in order to score points in different categories. The first team (or player) to score a point in 4 of the 6 categories wins the game.

Every turn, the current player/team draws a card and hands it to the Dealer, a non-competitive figure that reads it aloud and then manages the competition between the people trying to solve the riddle.

Similar to La Boîte à Quiz with 150 general knowledge questions.

The Box provides seventy-eight quizzes. Each card contains a fact and number of questions on a common theme.
Answer correctly and score a point. The player with the most points wins.

160 cards featuring 300 questions with six categories ("Letters", "Science", "History", "Geography", "Arts "and" Sports "). A book with a general introduction, the rules and the detailed responses of the tests.

Each card contains a fact and number of questions on a common theme. Answer correctly and score a point. The player with the most points wins.

Several other rules are proposed including soloplay rules.

THON is a fast-paced tactical miniatures wargame for two or more players pitting the Starlight defending Thonians against the ruthless Sons of Ord. Will you defend the Warfront or raze it to ash?

Players play the role of the LAR, their faction's supreme leader, and control an elite force led by a Commander or Overlord.

THON uses a D6 system called SCOMA - Shooting, Combat, Movement, Armor.

boardgame: Mansions of Madness: Call of the Wild
description:
Game description from the publisher:

"Briefly summarized, it hinted at a hidden race of monstrous beings which lurked somewhere among the remoter hills – in the deep woods of the highest peaks, and the dark valleys where streams trickle from unknown sources. These beings were seldom glimpsed, but evidences of their presence were reported by those who had ventured farther than usual up the slopes of certain mountains or into certain deep, steep-sided gorges that even the wolves shunned." –H.P. Lovecraft, "The Whisperer in Darkness"

Venture into the unknown with Mansions of Madness: Call of the Wild! Five new scenarios and eleven double-sided board tiles plunge investigators into the mysterious backwoods, shaking the foundations of what players have come to expect. The keeper has even more surprises at his disposal, and at times must solve ritual puzzles and seek out clues. A focus on outdoor maps encourages non-linear exploration, and new Allies assist in combat while driving the macabre narrative. Survive the wilderness in Call of the Wild!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Series: Arkham Horror Files (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
This is an abstract game with two boards. The action board is a 2X2 square grid with four actions (Place, Place, Move, Remove) in the spaces.  The network board is also a 2x2 square grid but the pieces are placed on the 9 vertices of the grid.

Each player has five workers that use to take actions and/or place on the network board. Each turn a player has two actions per turn (except for the first player on the first turn who only has one action). To take an action a player takes one of their own workers from their supply and places it in an empty space on the action board and resolve the action accordingly.  Instead of taking an action they may they may pass on one of their available actions - if they do so they must remove one of their workers from the action board (if they have any).

The three types of action on the action boards are:
Place: Place one of the activating workers in the Network board.  Take the worker from either the General Supply or the "Place" Space just activated.
Move: Move either player's worker on the Network Board to an adjacent vertice or move either player's worker on the Action board to an adjacent space (do not activate the action under the moved worker).
Remove: Remove either player's worker from either the Network Board or the Action Board.  The worker is returned to the player's general supply.

The game ends immediately when a player has three workers in a row on the Network Board.

Hungarian trivia game. Random draw a letter to different categories (country, city, famous man) each round. The players try to write things with beginning the suitable letters. What somebody else did not write is worth one point. The player collect the most points is the winner.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
A roll-and-move game based on the cartoon series "The Country Mouse and the City Mouse Adventures".
The aim of this cooperative children's game is to lead all 4 pawns through the story of the stolen diamond and to solve the case together with Emily, Alexander and Sherlock Mouse before Notail Nogoodnik, the thief, is seen five times.

The board shows a track consisting of lots of coloured spaces and pictures from the cartoon series. There are four pawns. Players take turns rolling the colour die. The active player moves a pawn of his/her choice to the next space of the colour on the die roll (all players can move all pawns). If a pawn lands on a yellow space, one of the twenty, randomly distributed face-down tiles has to be turned over. If it shows a picture from the board, the pawn is moved to the corresponding space. If the tile shows Notail Nogoodnik, it stays face-up and the player's turn is over.
To get a pawn to the finish line, the spaces has to be reached exactly.

If all four player pawns reach the finish line, all players win together. If five tiles showing Notail Nogoodnik are turned over, they all lose together.

cHTeMeLe the first HTML5 certified boardgame, the nearly collaborative and nearly educational game about HTML language

The aim of this new board game is fairly easy : create THE HTML page with the cards you have to hand... the game is played from 2 to 5 players, each player endorses a web browser (Firefox, Safari, Chrome, Opera, Explorer). Lay out your tag cards, and respect the web standards and rules and accumulate points. Play the "events" cards to step in each others browser's way (crash, bug, etc.) or help you in the race for building the web page.

Can you become the biggest hipster? Collect cappuccinos, canvas bags, and LPs while avoiding mainstream items to prove your Cred.

Each player is uniquely assigned two traits (from the pool of Academic, Activist, Elitist, or Grungeling) and is dealt a hand of eight items. An item has a trait - one of the above four or the detestable Mainstream. In turn order, each player selects a card from that hand to play. A player can play the card on himself, which will give him positive score if it matches one of his traits and negative score otherwise. If the player already has a negative scoring card, he can play a negative scoring card on another player. After each player has chosen played one card, everyone rotates hands to the next player.

Each trait has one card of each of ten point values. If a card is played on a player who already has a card of that value, the new one covers and replaces the old card. At the end of the game, each player totals their score and adds or subtracts points for a run of at least two cards of the same color - scoring or losing a point for every card in the run, depending on whether that color is positive or negative for the player. Highest total score wins!

Expansion for Kérdezz ! Felelek.

Several new cards with new well-known men/women of literature and arts, geographical locations, animals and abstractions can be used with those from the base game.

The game does not contain scoring board and tokens and not change the base game rules.

A post-apocalyptic vehicular combat game for 2 to 8 players.  It uses the quick setup and rapid play of a card game and combines it with the tactile and visual strategy and excitement of a miniatures game.  Nothing needs to be written down during play, it's a great way to introduce younger card game players to the world of miniature gaming.  Gameplay is normally 15-30 minutes and most players will understand the rules after their first game.  How many wargames can fit all their rules on a single sheet of paper?

ScrapIron is 100% Zombie Free!

Players each use a miniature vehicle as their playing piece.  The game was designed around the popular Hot Wheels and Matchbox lines but can be easily adapted to any size vehicle.

Players choose equipment from an Equipment Deck prior to the start of the game.

Action cards are chosen during the planning phase and placed face down.  Order if play is determined randomly and actions are then carried out in that order.

When damage is dealt, cards of the Damage Deck are given to the target player.  Damage cards will either assign Structural Damage to the vehicle, 10 points and the car is out of the game, or cause special effects.  These effects could cause the car to spin out and face a random direction, cause the player to lose Action cards or otherwise harm the target.

When the last car remains that player is the winner!  That did not take long, why not play again?

Kavelspel is a four player game. Each player has 9 tiles of the same colour. At the beginning of the game the tiles are placed on squares of corresponding colour: f.i. blue tiles on blue squares. Each group of 9 squares (in the corners) is rimmed by a line in one of the four colours, blue, green, red or yellow.
The object of the game is to move the tiles to the corner rimmed by the colour corresponding with the colour of the players tiles.
In each move the players may move one of their tiles a number of squares (1 or more) in a horizontal or vertical direction.
From the moment a player has reached the target square with one of his tiles, he is allowed to remove one of the tiles of the opponents from his target area, thus making room for his own tiles.
When a target area is filled with eight of the required tiles of the same colour, there is one square unoccupied. The only one that may fill that square at this stage of the game is the player that needs to complete the number of nine tiles in the target area. That would be his final move. However he is only allowed to move that final piece one square per turn.

Game description from the publisher:

The City provides those who live within it a chance to pursue their lives with a modicum of safety and comfort. However, though the outside world is kept outside, any alert adventurer strolling through the City Gate will feel the tension humming in the air.

Talisman (fourth edition): The City Expansion provides players with six new characters, three alternative endings, its own deck, and a host of shops for all your questing needs. Collect bounties listed on the Wanted Posters at the City Gate and fill your pockets with gold before doing some serious spending. Secure a steed from the Stables, pick up Pets or Potions, or head to the Armoury and the Magic Emporium. Visit The City in Talisman as you compete for the Crown of Command!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
"Sip and Strip is an exciting game designed for swinging adults of all ages" -- so says the instruction booklet that comes from the game.

This is a Frank Bresee game, similar to nearly everything else he made, it includes a Monopoly-like board, drinks, and this time stripping.  The game includes a routine board, "sip" cards and "strip" cards (standing in for Community Chest and Chance).  The game can be played by two to six players or even more if you want to play with couples as teams.

The roll of the dice moves your pieces around the board and you are required to do what the squares tell you to do.  Mostly this involves taking sips of a beverage, sometimes taking a Sip or Strip card.  Some of the Sip cards direct personal action like "take a sip and kiss any player" and other cards require all participants to take an action, usually involving another sip of a beverage.

Each complete round of the board includes more Sip cards and more Strip cards requiring you to remove a piece of clothing.  The game proceeds until the sips catch up with you (which is the point of Bresee's more notorious game "Pass Out") or until everyone has removed all articles of clothing.  The game suggests the last player to remove the last article of clothing is the winner, but local rules may suggest other winners may be declared.

The length of the game depends greatly on potency of beverages, how many articles of clothes players are wearing, and natural distractions that arise from game situations.  Unless all participants are really committed, the games can easily break up from prudishness or embarrassment.

Cibola is a head-to-head race to find the Seven Cities of Gold. In Cibola, players seek to collect and place the five required keys to unlock each of the seven gold statues. Players can use various tools and cunning to hinder their opponent.

There are also Silver (wild) statues for players to unlock. However, these will cause players to decrease their hand limit by one card, making their journey all that much harder.

The game ends once one of the two players has unlocked all seven statues.

This game consists of decks of cards each with five words and their definitions on them.  The Blarney Master - chosen by the roll of the die - selects a card, then chooses one of the words by speaking it.  The 3-minute timer is begun and the players have that long to write down their definition of the word.

Scoring is determined the players voting for the "best definition" and points are awarded to the player with the most votes and the player with the best definition and the Blarney Master if no one votes for the correct definition.  The one with the most points gets a green Blarney stone to indicate such.  That ends a round; then the Blarney Master position passes to the next player.

On certain cards, one of the words has no meaning so is simply "Blarney" and the Master must make up a plausible definition.

The game ends after 10 rounds; the winner being the player with the most Blarney stones.

boardgame: DAM IT the River Conquest Game
description:
DAM IT the River Conquest Game

Great strategy board game DAM IT takes place on a grand river system. With your crew of skilled beavers you will be charged with the task of damming rivers that legends say can’t be dammed. To make matters worse your enemies on the other team will be fighting for the same goal. A good balance of military strategy and well placed dams will be the key to your success. To master this game you will have to use strategy, team work, and just a little bit of luck. During game play you and your team will be faced with tough choices, but don’t be afraid to get wet just make sure you can swim. DAM IT Two Sides… As the game begins, six beaver tribes march onto the battlefield. The Red, Green, and Yellow tribes form the Honored Beaver Alliance, fighting for peace, justice, and wood. The tribes of Blue, Black, and Purple unite to form the Midnight Warriors, fighting to protect the homeland of their ancestors. One Battle… There is an uneasy calm as both teams face each other from across the river. Each tribe raises its flag as the architects discuss their building strategy. The kings stand nobly to ensure victory, and out of the silence comes the command of a warrior: “Charge!” One River. There can be only one victor, the team with the will to Dam It.

The game Dam It is a team game that can be played by 2 players, 4 players or 6 players. Games involving 4 or 6 players are always played in teams. There are 4 ways to win. 
(1)_eliminate the kings of opposing players 
(2)_capture the dens of opposing players 
(3)_dam the end of the main channel river and control it for 6 consecutive allied turns; and 
(4)_control 50 or more worker beavers.

Game time: approximately 1 hour

Note: It’s important to spend 10-15 minutes, or so reading the rules carefully. The more time you spend learning the rules, the more fluid and enjoyable the game will be.

The five types of beavers 
1. Worker beavers 
2. Flag beaver 
3. Warrior beaver 
4. Architect beaver 
5. King beaver

In Dam It, each beaver serves a different purpose and has unique skills. All +1s and +2s are numbers added to die rolls during the combat phase. The combat abilities of these beavers are summarized on the reference sheet under “Combat Statistics”.

1. Worker beavers comprise the bulk of your team. These beavers are easily persuaded. These beavers can collect wood and may be led into battle. The red and blue chips are used to represent the worker beavers of each team. 
2. A Flag beaver is an elite beaver that has been trained to repel attacks. To represent this, he is given a +2 while defending. This is the beaver holding a flag. 
3. A Warrior beaver is an elite beaver that has been trained in the art of war. To represent this he is given a +2 while attacking. This is the beaver with a Mohawk or with a Viking helmet (for early editions the warrior is also represented by: muscles, snorkel, eye patch, and hair). 
4. An Architect beaver is an elite beaver that has studied his/her whole life to become the leader in dam construction. This beaver must be present to construct dams. If the Architect beaver is eliminated, that player can not build dams. This is the beaver holding a book. 
5. A King beaver is an elite beaver that is cautious and effective in his war tactics. To represent this he is given a +1 while attacking and a +1 while defending. If this beaver is eliminated from the game, then all other elite beavers from that player are also eliminated, and that player’s turn is skipped for the rest of the game. This beaver is the one with a crown on his head.

An solitaire game placing the player in the role of a downed aircrew member behind enemy lines. Utilizes the paragraph-driven RANGER game system. A team of active duty military pilots helped during the development of this game. It can also be linked with RANGER with one player leading a patrol to locate and recover the other player.

This expansion contains 6 new cards for use in Autokrator.

The 6 battle cards of the Neutral Generals.
These cards are used for the attacks (raids) of the Neutral Generals.

The 6 battle cards of the Neutral Areas. (already in the base game)
These cards are used for the defense of the Neutral Areas.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
From the publisher's website:
YOLO is the addicting card game where the lowest score wins! Players are dealt four cards face down. Throughout the round you'll be swapping cards in and out trying to memorize which card is which number. The YOLO cards are the highest and lowest cards in the deck and can push your total into the stratosphere or save you! If you think your set of four cards is lower than everyone else's, then it is time to GO FOR IT! Why not? YOU ONLY LIVE ONCE!

The Congregrations is a small expansion for Karnag (Sit Down!) by Pascal Cadot.

Every player is member of a druidic congregation. Each congregation awards Victory Points to its members when they fulfill certain conditions. Before the game begins, each player draws a Congregation card. The remaining Congregation cards are put back in the box.

New York, millions of zombies, and you. Fight alone or with friends, and smash a way through
hordes of zombies. Throw your dice as if your life depends on iit, and find support and supplies in the city that now never sleeps. Clear zombies out of critical locations. Use your best tactics, throw the dice and pray for a little bit of luck. Find weapons, tools and fellow survivors. Help each other out of perilous situations. Be ready to free New York from the virus.

Either win the game or be overrun and turned into a rotting zombie.
Three games in one package!
Play alone, with a partner or against each other; all the time fighting an endless stream of the living dead.

1 or 2 player, solitaire, co-operative or against eacht other.
A new kind of dice game with a strong theme.
Luck and tactic is needed here!

A Components: Looney Pyramid Games game in the spirit of Ricochet Robots: an 8x8 board has 9 stationary towers and 3 mobile robots. Each turn, a randomly chosen robot must move onto a randomly chosen tower. Towers and the map edge and other robots block movement (but the goal tower does not block movement).

Player simultaneously try to find the smallest sequence of moves to achieve the goal, and the lowest bidder who successfully moves the specified robot to the specified goal earns a point.

3 stashes of pyramids (in 3 different colors), an 8x8 board, and an optional 30-second timer are the only equipment needed.

A game for 3 to 6 year olds, helping them with their counting skills.

There are 24 monkey cards with a maths sum on them and 24 cards with different numbers of bananas on them. Three games are described but all really relate to recognising the correct banana tile to go with the correct sum (E.g. Monkey card says 3 + 2 so the tile with 5 bananas is the correct match.

The games include a Memory game (instead of matching like tiles you match the sum to the correct banana tile, played just like Memory.

This is fantasy board game for 2 – 4 players who wander through underground corridors and rooms, fight with monsters and collect some objects. 
Mechanic is standard -I go you go 
The aim of this game is to find three magic objects: golden crown, diamond dagger and magic ring. 
The winner is the one who leaves the underground with at least two of three magic objects.
The game probably  inspired by Talisman

A domino variant that uses dice as well as the domino cards. Of course, the cards can be used for a standard game of dominoes except that they feature an image on half the card and letters on the other half. You match a correct initial letter to an image (M next to Moon for example or vice versa). The game is housed in an attractive tin box.

Other games are included that bring in to play the special dice. One dice is what wargamers would call a Fire Die. It has 1 x two on it, 2 x two on it and 3 times two.The other die has letters on each face, four letters or five letters. And sometimes they use the wooden Wizard figure that is included with the game.

In game 1,2,3 - Magic! each player gets a hand of five cards and 12 cards are laid out in a circle, like a clock. However, a clock with 24 divisions because the cards are laid end-on thus forming a chequered track, picture, letter, picture, and so on. The Wizard is placed on one of them. The number die is used in this game. In your turn, you roll the die and advance the Wizard that number of spaces. When he has moved and lands on a picture space, the player looks at the picture and sees if he can think of a word that describes the picture. If he can then he must check his hand and see if the word he wants to use begins with one of the letters in his hand. If he does have such a card he can return that card to the box. If the Wizard lands on a letter space the reverse applies. The player looks to see if he has a picture card that begins with the letter. The first to use up all their cards, wins.

In Enchanting Fairy, the letter die is used. Each gets a hand of cards. The Wizard is placed in the centre of the table comfortably in reach of all the players. The die is roilled and all the players look to see if they have a picture beginning with one of the letters face-up on the die. If they do think they have the right answer, they can grab for the Wizard. Whoever gets it first (several players might grab for it at the same time), announces his answer and, if correct, returns his card to the box. If he is wrong he must draw another card from the shuffled deck.The first to use all their cards, wins.

Interesting additional fact. HABA add the usual disclaimer that the game is not suitable for under threes as some components could be swallowed. Nothing unusual in that except they write it in 40 languages!!

The ant queen has decided to move! So now the two top ant engineers have come to the new land, and while the nest engineering commenced smoothly, the two engineers are secretly competing to prove themselves more competent than the other ant country's first engineer!

In Ant Nest, you'll play one of the two engineers  charged with designing a new nest to house the country.

Publisher Blurb:

The young merchant Anna Never is a complete mystery. As she travels around Gaia she seems to unconsciously position herself to be involved with major issues and problems facing the world. Additionally she has exhibited martial abilities unheard of for a girl of her age an apparent lack of training to possess. With no known connection to any established power block, one can't help but wonder that maybe there is more to her than she is letting on ...

Light Wanderer Warrior — Lv. 45
 Innate Abilities: Critical Mastery.
 Destiny's Chosen: After seeing the roll for an attack that targets Anna, you may spend one Gnosis Point to have your opponent to reroll the attack.
 The One: You cannot spend Gnosis to improve any roll made by Anna. However, Anna rolls an additional dice an keeps the highest when making any roll.
 Stubborn: Anna cannot be included in a party that has more Dark units than Light units in it.
 Void God (Ki, Attack) +3 Attack.
 Libera Me (Ki, Dodge) +3 Defense.
 Infinity Requiem (Ki, Attack) +1 Attack / +2 Damage. You may spend any amount of Gnosis that you wish when making the attack. Anna makes an additional Infinity Requiem attack with the same target as the first one for each Gnosis point spent when making this attack (up to 2 Gnosis points).

Aeroplanes: PWS-24 is a plane card expansion for Aeroplanes: Aviation Ascendant boardgame.

The PWS-24 was a Polish single-engine passenger aircraft for 4 passengers, built in PWS (Podlaska Wytwórnia Samolotów) factory, used from 1933 to 1936 by LOT Polish Airlines. It was the only series-built airliner of domestic design ever used by LOT.

Aeroplanes: PWS-24 is a free expansion, created by Phalanx Games Polska to celebrate the release of the Polish edition of the Aeroplanes: Aviation Ascendant boardgame.

Who's the hungriest Zombie among your friends? Find out with this outrageous, fast-paced party game. This game is based on the popular "I Have" card game. The winner is the first player to run out of cards. Players race each other to drop Zombie, Werewolf, Ghost, Spider, and other horror creature cards into the Darkness Pit.

Players are dealt cards with "I Ate" side up and these cards will be laid out in front of themselves, same side up. The first player reads his/her "I Ate" card. Example: "I Ate a Werewolf". After reading the "I Ate" statement, he/she flips that card over and reads the "Who Ate" side of the card. Example: "I ate a Werewolf. Who ate a Zombie?" All players then race to respond to the "Who Ate" question if they have that specific card. To respond to a "Who Ate" guestion, a player says, "I Ate a...Who Ate a...?" and places his/her card "Who Ate" side up in the darkness pit (card pile). Play continues as stated above until a player gets rid of all of his/her cards to the darkness pit.

Based on the world famous novel Don Quixote de la Mancha written by Miguel de Cervantes, in this game  players take the roles of Don Quixote and his fellow knights Amadís de Gaula and Tirant lo Blanc or a group of monsters opposed to them. Don Quixote will travel around the board, which represents the spanish central region of La Mancha, trying to reach victory by following and completing a route through a series of tiles that represent the different adventures that the real character has to face in the book. In the meanwhile, the other knights should protect Don  Quixote from the attacks of the monsters, who will try to hurt, imprison and finally kill him to win the game.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Viaje al Centro de la Tierra
description: The game is based on the novel "Journey to the Center of the Earth" written by Joules Verne. Players take the roles of up to four adventurers who will race through the 3D board trying to be the first ones to achieve the center of the Earth and therefore win the game. To do this, they will face a hard ascension of the ancient Sneffels volcano, a descent into its dark and misterious insides and finally a nightmarish counter-clock run against the lava flowing in the center of the Earth. During the journey they might face dangerous secondary adventures but also find precious treasures. But beware! Only those adventurers to equip themselves with the right object for each stage of the journey will be able to successfully race against the others.

From the Introduction:

Players can link all four of the Peiper Pocket Battle Games to form The Peiper Dream and play a "Grand" (using the term very loosely here!) Campaign that simulates the entire series of battles with KG Peiper during the Ardennes Offensive.  The Campaign Game will also require the use of one six-sided die (not included) to adjudicate certain game outcomes. There can be up to 20 turns (run through the card deck up to 20 times) in the Campaign Game.

Contents:


    Campaign Game and Optional Rules
    Game Turn Record Track
    Sheet of 40 die-cut counters
    Paying the Peiper
    A Dash of Peiper
    The Bagged Peiper
    Just a Peiper Dream?


See the Web Links below for a download of the rules and Turn Record Track.

In the game Demon Busters, the players take the role of one of five soulkeepers. Through their magical powers they mend the holes in the fabric that separate the world of men from the realms of evil beings.

The five soulkeepers agree, over the time of one year, who ever got the most accomplishments in battle, will become the head of the five soulkeepers. Each month, a demon of a thousand years, equipped with supernatural powers, leads the armies of darkness through the holes between the worlds. Now, the soulkeepers have to compete with each other. Through the use of superior magical powers they entrap and capture the demons. Once a demon is captured, the soulkeeper who entrapped him can make use of his special powers to capture even more demons.

At the end of the game, who fixed the most holes in the fabrics of the world had the highest contribution to saving our world and is the winner of the contest!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
As lobby and campaign spending soars to record highs, Congressional approval ratings plummet to all-time lows.  Explore the influence of money on Congress while playing this educational card game.

There are two kinds of cards in the Politics and Corruption deck: bill cards representing legislation of the 112th Congress, and capital cards that are used to solicit votes and champion bills.  Players take turns playing capital cards on bills until the draw pile is exhausted.  Bills pass with a simple majority, meaning more than half of the votes on a bill are in support.  Bills may either occupy the center of the table or be placed in front of a player who has chosen to champion them.  Championed bills earns their champion more points when passed, but costs that player a point when blocked.  Players also earn points by passing bills that are favored by their party, and by ending the game with lots of cards left in their hand.  The player with the most points at the end of a game is the winner.

The Wharfside Casino License Cards are a fun and strategic promo/expansion for Fleet!

This set of three (3) Wharfside Casino Premium License Cards can be used with Fleet as a substitute for the Fishermen's Pub.

Once the Casino has been purchased, players may take one Crate from any Boat and place it on the Wharfside Casino. They then roll a die (the Fleet die if you have it!) and take the appropriate action:

1-3) Bust! Discard the gambled Crate of Fish from the game.

4) Win! +1 Crate of Fish. Keep the gambled Crate of Fish, and place one additional Crate of Fish from the Fish Crate supply onto the Casino license card.

5) Win! +2 Crate of Fish. Keep the gambled Crate of Fish, and place two additional Crates of Fish from the Fish Crate supply onto the Casino license card.

6/Fleet) Winner Winner Seafood Dinner! +3 Crate of Fish. Keep the gambled Crate of Fish, and place three additional Crates of Fish from the Fish Crate supply onto the Casino license card.

The Wharfside Casino is fun way to add a new twist to Fleet!

The players have all arrived at the company as new employees, full of ambition and eager to work hard in order to dominate the company and rise up from a nobody to a somebody thanks to the admiration of others.

But to rise like a phoenix and transform into a dragon at the company, as everyone wants to do in Workaholic Liver War, you'll need to gain the cooperation of your colleagues while taking good advantage of your personal skills and random events inside the company walls. Experience in-fighting, develop a fantastic career, suffer from a burst liver due to all the stress and pressure, and ideally in the end get everyone else to bow to you respectfully...

Whoever in Darkminded Merchants is most conscienceless, most unscrupulous will make the most money and win the game? Using "fresh" tofu as substitutes in your dishes? Putting unsavory sauces in the shrimp? Adding plasticizers to the drinks? If you can be this darkminded, you might just win the game.

In this war between businessmen, you must see through the black-hearted tactics of others while also being able to reap the maximum benefits possible from a time of crisis to dominate the industry and prove that your heart is the blackest of all.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: Nothing more for now that the Godzilla Boom name and the detail that the design is a party/card game.

Animals contains 70 cards for three different level games.

• In Pets of King, each of Kings in the forest has his/her beloved pets. When these animals appear in the forest, take the chance and stay them. But, other Kings may also have same interest or unfavorable animals may also be captured. This game tests response and actions to catch different Animals cards.

• In Forest Olympic, animals in the forest join the Olympic game. Help them to win and the most effective team will seek love from animals. Players have to form Animals team to compete each round and be the first one to play all cards from his/her hand.

• In Rescue the Animals, there is a big fire in the forest! Help rescue animals to life boats. The more the Animals a player save, the more is the point the chance to win he/she gets. However, players have to be think carefully how to arrange Animals in their only life boat.

Desire is a quick-playing card game that features women in outfits that are two sizes too small. I'm not sure how that relates to the game play, but that's the only detail I can speak of for now other than the title, so there it is.

Woof and Me – Learn Pet Care While You Play!

Woof and Me is a fun, educational board game that teaches children ages 5+ about responsible pet care while keeping gameplay exciting and interactive. Players move their doggy game pieces along the board, collecting bones, Doggy Dollars, and cards showing different dog breeds receiving essentials like food, water, and healthcare. The player with the most bones at the end wins—and gets the special reward of choosing a dog from the Animal Shelter!

Why Woof and Me is Special:

Educational & Fun: Kids practice counting, basic number recognition, and simple money exchange while enjoying the game.

Pet Care Responsibility: Players learn about the real-life needs of pets, shelter adoption, and the costs of caring for a dog.

Problem-Solving & Social Skills: The game introduces everyday pet problems and encourages players to find solutions, fostering communication and interaction with other players.

Complete Game Set: Includes game board, picture-book directions, 6 dog game pieces, Doggy Dollars, 130 bone cards, 8 spay/neuter cards, 6 license cards, 14 problem/solution cards, and 2 dice.

Woof and Me is a unique, colorful game praised by after-school programs for engaging children while teaching them to be responsible pet owners. It’s a fun way to prepare kids for adopting a new pet, helping them understand the joys and responsibilities of caring for a dog.

Players: 2–5 | Ages: 5+

Sands 500 is a motor racing game for 2-6 players. The object of the game is to be the first player to have their racing car cross the finish line after an agreed upon number of laps of the circuit.

To begin the game the first player to spin a four on the Spinner goes first and has their choice of which coloured lane to place their racing car. Then the player to the left has their choice of lane and so on until all players have had a lane allocated.

The player who goes first spins the Spinner and whoever has a racing car on the same lane as the colour indicated by the dial moves the number of spaces shown in that segment. The leader can then change lanes to whatever colour they think will come up next. After the leader has chosen a lane the next player in position order can do likewise (it is permissible to have two or more racing cars in the same lane).

The game progresses with each player following the direction of course until the leader crosses the finish line either by a 'flying finish' (crossing the line on any number) or finishes on exactly the right number as decided before the start of the game.

Contents:

1x Board
1x Spinner with rules
4x Racing cars (one each of red, blue, green and yellow)
Winter: Reaction Cards is a promotional pack of six cards for Winter that gives you advantages based on your opponents' actions. These cards can also be used with 51st State or The New Era.

Tabletop miniatures naval wargame, designed for small scale (1:3000 or smaller) miniatures. 1 mini=1 ship, rules are designed for large actions, and are relatively simple, allowing for fairly simple bookkeeping (6+ ships per A4/letter sheet easily). Considerable detail is still preserved, such as ships carrying multiple size classes of guns per arc (ahead, broadside & aft), differing effects of boiler hits due to fuel type, etc. Submarine & aircraft actions are included, as are formations and campaign tracking.

Naval lists are included for Royal Navy & Kriegsmarine. Layout & typeface are rather old-fashioned in appearance.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
TradeOff is the game of stock market supremacy. It pits two to six players against each other in one simple challenge - to make the most money on the stock market so as to out-bid each other for luxury items. However the stock market is volatile and unpredictable. Take-over bids, profit warnings, insider trading and much more, all contribute to make TradeOff an exciting and strategic roller-coaster ride from beginning to end.

HOW IT WORKS: The mat acts as the stock market and consists of four stocks. During the course of the game each stock's value independently rises and falls as dictated by the cards that players collect. Players can predict these rises and falls by studying their own cards and watching how their opponents are investing.

Making an investment is easy. Players select a stock they think will rise in value and simply place a number of their chips on the mat next to the stock's share price marker. Profit and losses are easily calculated by either using the numbers printed on the mat or by using the free app available on our website. But it's not only the cards that determine how the stock's values will change. When players invest in, or sell an investment, the stock's value rises or falls accordingly. This gives players the chance to be nasty to each other by reducing the value of their opponents investments!

Throughout the course of the game auctions are held for luxury items. During these auctions players must out-bid each other to secure the purchase of the item. The first player to obtain the requisite number of luxury items wins the game.

boardgame: Jabberwocky: a diceless battle game for fantasy armies
description:
Tactical scale tabletop miniatures game. A diceless mechanic means A always beats B always beats C... but B can get advantages to beat A, etc.

For instance, 
Double ranked pikes can not be defeated in frontal attacks
but
Blades defeat Pikes in frontal attacks

Therefore, you CAN defeat double ranked pikes in a frontal attack if the attacking unit is Blades. Similarly, you read down a list of conditions until you get to the first applicable one & use the result.

Figure scale & numbers, & base sizes, are left up to the player. The intended standard game size is 10 stands. Rules allow for play of historical battles, but also include rules on monster types & abilities, magic effects, etc. A number of army lists and a point-build system are included.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Before Framer Bert can take his tractor out in the field, he must quickly feed the animals on his farm. But where are they anyway? They've been hiding in their stables!

In Wo sind Schaf & Co.?, you'll find out who can help Bert find the animals so that he can get on with his work. Whoever has some luck with the dice and good eyes will finish his puzzling animal farm first.

boardgame: Elder Sign: The Log of the Persephone – Promotional Adventure Card
description:
The Log of the Persephone is a mail order card from Fantasy Flights Games. The card can only be acquired by using the order form in the back of the novel 'Bones of the Yopasi' also by FFG.

The card provides a new location for Elder Sign based on the Novel.

Update March 30 2013: The card was (sparsely) given away on TableTopDay by retailers who received the goodie-box.

Update February 5th 2014: The card was found in the gift bags sent out to backers of Season 1 of Shut Up & Sit Down
family: Series: Arkham Horror Files (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
The cheeky Indian boy Puzzlenase would like to be a brave Indian chief one day, so each day he runs around the village and practices searching for trails that barely exist. Through his curiosity he discovers new things.

In Häuptling Puzzlenase only sharp eagle eyes and a good memory can help you remember and find all that the Indian boy is looking for.

boardgame: Die Wilden Fussballkerle: Das Wilde Fußball-Quiz
description: Another game in this popular TV franchise. Players take turns to ask the other players a question about the TV show, football rules or football tips. The most right answers, wins.

boardgame: Shingle: The Anzio Beachhead 22 Jan. - 3 Mar. 1944
description:
From the editor's website:

To reinvigorate their stalled Italian campaign, the Allies landed a reinforced corps at the small coastal town of Anzio, just 30 miles from Rome and the German supply arteries. A hesitant advance and a rapid German reaction set the stage for a nip-and-tuck battle for the beachhead.
 This classic campaign is covered using the Fire & Movement system at one kilometer per hex and one day per turn. The standard unit is the infantry battalion, which can be strengthened by a multitude of tanks and other heavy weapons, plus fire support markers rated for different weapon types up to and including a massive heavy bomber strike.
 Each player has several strategic options, kept secret from his opponent, to use the fight for the beachhead to further the larger campaign. All strategies and operations are constrained by limited logistics; players must balance carefully his expenditures for reinforcements, replacements, and fire support. The player who strikes the best balance, and who best utilizes the various weapons at his disposal, has the chance to achieve a war-changing victory.

Components: One 22 x 34" map & 280 counters
family: Series: Fire & Movement combat system (Decision Games)

description:
The second expansion of Pyramideto - this time with twelve Jewish months and holidays theme.  With Hanukka Pyramideto you get 12 new goal cards to the game.  As in the Original Pyramideto you move the magical pyramids to form your secret goal.

Twelve cards of Hanukka Pyramideto corresponds to twelve months of the Jewish years and their holidays.  All cards have a typical six-color frame, Hebrew name (both in Hebrew and Latin letters), pyramid form and the sign - little Menorah.

Hanukkah Pyramideto is playable also separately.

Integrates with:

    Pyramideto

Seeking your fortune, you have bought a mining spaceship, but you have a limited time to pay off the loan. You must fly from planet to planet, obtaining minerals on one before selling for a profit on the next. The blue planet is richer in ore, but you will receive a lower price for the blue than for the relatively rare red minerals. You may purchase upgrades to your spaceship but it will cost you time and money. Can you pay off your loan before the bailiffs turn up?

A solo card game. There is a deck for each of four planets. The player must choose one of four actions - mine, fly, sell, upgrade - then draw a card from the relevant planet deck. Mining and selling will vary in their level of success or failure, depending on the card drawn. Upgrade cards are available which give special abilities. The aim is to earn 160 Credits before running out of cards.

Designed for the 2012 Solitaire Print and Play Contest.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: In whole card game of Sansi, everybody plays at the same time and everything is spinning around the quick reflexes and decision making. Players try to pick up and gather in their hand at least three cards of one number/color. This is done by changing one card from hand for one that lies on the display. When player have full a set of numbers ready, he play them straight on to the order cards. Players must decide, if you they keep three cards in hand with chance to have four in time or play it to block order card for others.

Publisher Blurb:

As one of the dark spirits of death, Ayl combines strong magic abilities with an incredible combat skill. With her powers, she is able cast even the souls of living creatures into oblivion.

Dark Samael Warrior/Mystic — Lv. 50
 Unholy Beam (Magic, Ranged Attack) Distance (16 inch).
 Mimic Damage (Magic, Effect, Reactive) Cast when an enemy declares an attack on a friendly unit within your Control Zone. If the attack successfully causes damage to the friendly unit, apply the same damage to any enemy unit within your Control Zone. Effect 15.
 Death Scythe (Magic, Attack) +2 Attack. If Death Scythe causes any damage, target unit suffer Lv1 Doom. Effect 15.

More Wonders... is a fan expansion for 7 Wonders made by Shima Tetsuo. It contains 7 new Wonders: Atlantis, Sparta, Helvetia, Angkor Wat, Venezia, Persepolis, and Caylus.

Re-implemented by:

     Lost Wonders (fan expansion for 7 Wonders)

Vote for me! is the game on the presidential election where all cards are inspired by real events and characters.

Each player is a candidate here ą presidential election and must earn popularity points to eliminate his political opponents.

To do this, there are action cards to act on the curve polls.
As soon as all candidates have reached the maximum number of points, they will enter the televised debate in which they will answer the questions, a quiz that will determine their skills and knowledge.
Finally, they will compete in a final duel, the second round of elections, after which elected the new President of the Republic.

Game description from the publisher:

Hark, friend! Let us... help... each other. We live in a time of altruism on Catan. We give away resources to help lagging players. We move the robber to isolated locations to ensure the safety of our fellows. We even go out of our way to connect our roads with our neighbors' networks. Has everyone gone nuts?

Ah! Thanks to their beneficence, Catan's guilds reward such noble-minded behavior by handing out "Favor Tokens" (FTs). Each guild helps us in different ways: granting us roads, development cards, random resources, preferential trades, or even Victory Points.

In Catan Scenarios: Frenemies, everyone is still just in it for themselves as there is an ulterior motive for everything. In fact, sometimes you can "help a friend" while making things far worse for them.

Sigh. If goodwill really exists, it's not part of this scenario...

The 2023 German re-release of this expansion includes enough tokens for a 5-6 player game.

from Amazon
Now is your chance to build the custom Hot Wheels car you've dreamed of! Packed in a clever and portable lunch box, Body WorX has you put together parts of Hot Wheels cars printed on cards. The player who puts together the most cars wins the game. Box measures about 5-inches tall x 5 1/2-inches wide x 2 1/4-inches deep. Ages 4 and up, 2-4 players.

Players have a hand of cards. They are attempting to complete a Hot Wheels set: one "tires" card, one "chasis", one interior, one exterior. The cards have to be collected and played in order.

There is a drafting mechanic not unlike Uno. Players may draw from the top of the deck blindly or select the top discard.

There is some "take-that" where players can slow down their opponent's progress by giving them a "flat tire" card or some other setback card.

This is a free do-it-yourself print-and-play game for parents and kids to use to divide up the variety of Halloween treats they acquire, although any good variety of treats can be used, and not just on Halloween!  Playing the game without a decent variety of treats (using generic tokens for treats for example) eliminates much of the fun in choosing which delicious morsels your monsters get to grab from the pot, and steal from other players.

The game is played by rolling 3 custom dice (which have to be assembled from the files included here) in order to roll a MONSTER(s) which then can use the other 1 or 2 dice to perform an action.  One-headed and two-headed MONSTERS can GRAB a treat, STEAL a treat, or EAT a treat, for each head they have.  A player who GRABS takes a treat from the pot and puts it on their STASH plate, a player who STEALS can take a treat from the pot OR take a treat from another player's STASH.  A player who EATS a treat puts it in their STOMACH CONTENTS bag and that treat can not be stolen by any other players.  Rolling triples has special effects, like being caught stealing.  After all the treats are gone from the pot, players score 1 point for each treat in their STASH and 2 points for each treat in their STOMACH CONTENTS bag.  I suggest having at least 10 treats per player to play with.  For fairness reasons, sometimes the players agree that if they don't roll at least one monster they can physically eat one treat from the pot.

The dice are made by cutting out and gluing the sides on 3/4 inch wood blocks, or printing them out with adhesive backed paper and pasting them on cubes.  The file might have to be re-sized to fit smaller dice; it contains enough icons to fill 6 dice even though you only use 3 to play.  If anyone would like to have me make a custom set for them for a little $ I will make them for you.  Geekmail me here (NoodleArtist).  within a year have a few laser-engraved dice made, and re-release the game with some added rules as a more professional, packaged game.

This game was invented by Henry Bird in 1874.

The game is played on a 10 by 8 board. Players have, in addition to the usual pieces, two additional pawns and an guard and equerry

The guard has the combined moves of rook and knight. The equerry has the combined moves of bishop and knight. Pawns can promote to queen, guard, equerry, bishop, rook, or knight
Grotesque Chess uses all the pieces of Chess plus two more, known as the Equerry and Guard. Tthe Guard is a compound of Rook and Knight, and the Equerry is a compound of Bishop and Knight.

from the designer

Magnapoco is a game for 2 players, played with black and white stones on a grid board.  You can play on larger grids (they must have an odd number of spaces), but I recommend starting with a 7x7. Good players will swiftly graduate to 9x9.

You want to create, ideally, two large groups without being forced to connect them together, while trying to force your opponent to make small groups in small territories.

from the designer

Breach is for 2 players and is played with Go stones on an initially-empty square grid.

When you take the lead (i.e. when you build a row longer than any built up to that point in the game), your opponent can (optionally) replace one of your stones with her own, in lieu of placing one of her stones on her next turn.

Rules

1. White begins by placing 1 stone on any empty space.
 2. Then, starting with Black, the players take turns. On your turn you must place 2 stones on any 2 empty spaces. 
 3. If you complete a row with a score of at least 2, and that score is higher than any previous score by either player, you must move your scoring marker to that score on the scoring track. 
 4. After you move your scoring marker, your opponent gets an additional option on her next turn (and only on that turn): instead of placing 2 stones, she may choose to place 1 stone and then replace any one of your stones on the board with one of her own. 
 5. If the longest row is broken up due to a stone replacement, the scoring marker is not moved back - scoring markers never move backwards.
 6. The game ends when the board is full and the player with the highest score on the scoring track at that time wins (in practice the winner will be obvious well before the board is full, and the trailing player should resign at that point).

from the designer

It's a way to solve a problem for a well-known win condition and an excuse to create another game on a colorful board I enjoy (the first was Swaparound). The game is called Spry, which is a contraction of the phrase "Spread Y". The problem is that if you play the game Y on a hexhex board, the center of the board is much more important than the periphery, especially on a small board. Spry is a way to fix that.

It's for two players, played with Black and White stones, on a colorful board.

boardgame: Party Joy Guidemaster Series #6: The Legend of Zelda – A Link to the Past
description:
Back from 1992 in Japan is an era where board games aren't popular much. A board game based on the acclaimed Super Famicom title , The Legend of Zelda: Triforce of the Gods,(or A link to the Past in english), plunges you into the world of obscure board gaming. An ancient relic unknown to many non-japanese speaking Zelda fans everywhere.

Not much is known about this little piece of history with no rule book opened for public eye. According to a fellow bgg translator, the back of the box vaguely claims to "teach you the secrets of the Super Famicom game". If you play this game, you can learn how to play A Link To the Past. In Japaneses! The player(s) start at town and thru playing the game, one can find out about various items and how things work. And best of all, up to three players can play! Wow!

Components used are a big mat to place cards. Cards with various enemies, tokens and tiles, a stickered die, and three poker chips as player pawns!What's different of this Zelda board game from the other two known Zelda board games so far is that it features authentic sprite art from the super famicom/Super Nes title!

"… very strange."
~Z****n

boardgame: When the Navy Walked Airships and Skypirates
description: Airships and Skypirates! introduces a new threat to the world powers, from the darkness below. Rules featuring all airship or landship combat in the fleet rules, three dimensional combat using advanced rules to add more depth to your game and the Great Powers mighty Airship fleets including the massive Tesla Broadcast Carrier! Contains new rules for airship combat as well as fleet scale using 1/1200 scale miniatures and new edges and flaws!

boardgame: When the Navy Walked: Earthin – The Hollow Earth
description: Earthin: A Hollow World features long lost civilizations like the Holy Egyptian Empire, Roma Secundus, Fallen Aztecs, and the origins of the Skypirates as well as the Protean Races that have been here longer than recorded time – the Atlantians, the Devlyn and the Lumerians! Featuring new rules for tunneling and underwater combat as well as new army lists including the use of dinosaurs and prehistoric beasts! With all new dino inspired edges and flaws!

From the publisher, Smart Kids:
"Memorise, Match and Spot the Difference with this revolutionary set of 6 x A3 Memory Skills Board Games! Practise linking techniques for memorising lists; spot picture differences and visually match the same cavemen and dinosaurs to move around exciting game boards. If that’s not enough for one set, the other 3 games contain opportunities to memorise patterns, position and pictures. Don’t forget to tell everyone you know about these brilliant new games, with everything you need to play included in the box. Fun for all ages! Minimal Reading Required - once the instructions have been read, all you need to do is play and have fun!"

The Game "Het Groot Pretparkenspel" is about amusement parks in The Netherlands. The aim of the game is that each player buys, builds and manages one or more parks. Everyone gets a working capital to do this. One player controls the Central Bank. Each player buys a site belonging to an amusement park and tries to fill it up with attractions. As in reality this will not all go smoothly. Financial difficulties will arise because attraction prices will fluctuate and because players are forced to conclude transactions with the Central Bank and the other players. The visitors (players which end up at attractions during play) will pay entrance fees to the owner of the park, which will have to manage on it. Moreover, the "Plus-Pech"-cards (Luck-Bad Luck) will provide ups and downs. The game ends when all players except one are bankrupt. Alternative game ends are at a pre-determined time or property limit.

The Death Ride Kursk Air Enhancement provides players a significanlty more detailed window into the air operations and their effect on combat operations in the XLVIII Panzer Korps area.  Units are modeled as flights and significant parts of many squadrons are reflected in supporting both sides.  At this time the Germans had local area air superiority for a time, but this would finally end after the battle and the Red Air Force became a force to be reconed with on its own.

The original system made Close Air Support (CAS) a fairly easy mechanic to understand and implement.  This enhancement provides just the right amount of sophistication, while still remaining easy to learn, and forces both sides to do more planning for the air part of the campaign.  In the end that part of why we play, to see how the planning takes place to support your ground combat.

You will now be able to have air to air combat, interception of enemy CAS, and the possibility of hitting friendly troops with air strikes.

This has to be the most fun of all the enhancement packages so far.

This enhancement package was recently updated with an expanded set of rules.

This enhancement comes in a photo envelope.  Components include:
(1) counter sheets
(16) pages of rules
German Airfield Chart
Russian Airfield Chart
Airfield definition card
Close Air Support results and targeting determination chart

The Distant Reaches of space hold a planet whispered in tales to frighten children, to threaten criminals and sway tyrants. The horrible truth is that the planet is the last hope before all out war for entire species that struggle to coexist within the Hedonix empire: the planet wide arena of Hollow Cell.

Hollow Cell is an easy to play, fast paced, arena game designed by Garret Herdter.  Take up the challenge as one of 18 unique warrior races. Draw hands of hollow cell cards to either equip on your character or play to damage your opponent. Roll dice both in defense and attack while you dodge deadly planet wide events sent by the Hedonix watchers to speed up the match.

Take cover in builds and raid forgotten caches of weapons and armor to best your opponents in an all out free-for-all battle where only the luckiest and the most skilled will survive to claim victory!

Game description from the publisher:

The Allies possess the largest air fleet on the planet. In the skies, their power is currently unmatched, and they know they have to maintain this supremacy in order to achieve global victory. However, in Zverograd, SSU attack helicopters harass ground troops, and new Axis jets cruise the skies. Each bloc realizes the importance of seizing the skies, so as the Allies prepare to launch a vital series of raids against Zverograd’s airfield, they’re bringing their best soldiers, toughest walkers, and deadliest new aircraft.

As it follows the Allies' efforts to gain a firmer foothold in the battle for Zverograd, Dust Tactics: Operation "Icarus" introduces new rules for minefields, the limited cover provided by the trees that line the airfield's landing strips, and the quonset huts in which the Axis forces await the Allied assault. Both sides benefit from the leadership of new heroes. Panzerprinz is an ace Axis pilot, and Sgt. Victory stands alone among the Allied troops, accomplishing amazing feats with his superhuman speed, strength, and resilience. All these elements come together to create the dangerous new battleground that comes sharply into focus with the seven tense and ferocious scenarios of the Operation "Icarus" campaign.

Quonset huts are a new type of structure introduced in Operation “Icarus.” Extremely popular in the world of Dust Tactics and Dust Warfare, quonset huts can be erected quickly and provide soldiers with a sturdy shelter against the elements. They can also be joined together to create longer structures with a variety of strategic arrangements.

Operation “Icarus” comes with four quonset hut sections, and the Airfield Accessory Pack allows you to further enhance your battlefields with another six sections. Add more cover. Develop new arrangements for your structures. Take your battles in all new directions.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: USMC Heavy Weapons Teams – "The Choppers / Leathernecks"
description:
Game description from the publisher:

In the alternate-reality 1940s of Dust Tactics and Dust Warfare, the United States Marine Corps have come to value the ability to move quickly over terrain, and a small team carrying heavy weaponry is sometimes the best option for getting fire support into position. The two squads of the Dust Tactics: USMC Heavy Weapons Teams are veterans of many campaigns, men who remain fearless even when heavily outnumbered.

With their .50 cal machine gun, The Choppers serve primarily as defensive support for allied units. When placed in cover, or inside a building, The Choppers can work wonders!

The Leathernecks weapon team has traded its .50 cal. for a 120mm heavy mortar, one of the most reliable weapons you can find for harsh environments. The mortar never jams, is easy to clean, and can be quickly moved from place to place. It also delivers quite a punch!

boardgame: Dust Tactics: KV47 Recovery & Demolition Walker – "Maksim / Marlen / Melor / Mikhail"
description:
For years the Soviet army had little concern for its vehicular losses. The Axis enemy battered at the gate and had to be repelled at all costs. SSU forces considered it fortunate if their heavy equipment could be salvaged or repaired, but many damaged machines were simply left to rust on the battlefield. Now, the SSU’s development of VK-powered walkers has led to a change in philosophy. Even destroyed, these units are still  valuable for their rare minerals and parts. Accordingly, SSU forces operate under standing orders to salvage their wreckage or, at the least, destroy fallen vehicles properly, in order to prevent their enemies from capturing their VK technology.

The KV47 Recovery & Demolition Walker introduces one medium combat walker that can be configured as one of four different models: Maksim, Marlen, Melor, or Mikhail. Each of these walkers affords the SSU strategic advantages from its salvage operations and can pack a punch in close combat.

boardgame: Dust Tactics: Horten HO-347 – "Fledermaus III / Fledermaus IV"
description:
Game description from the publisher:

Dust Tactics: Horten HO-347 comes with one miniature that can be configured in either of two jet variations that have placed the Axis on the brink of a long-awaited resurgence of air superiority in the fierce battles of Dust Tactics and Dust Warfare. The introduction of VK engines has permitted the Axis' newest aircraft to fly faster and climb higher than anyone believed to be possible just a few years ago. The latest generation engines even allow for near-stationary flight and vertical take-off and landing (VTOL).

The HO-347 Fledermaus III is both a fighter and a very effective ground-attack plane. Its light cannons can destroy other flying units in mere seconds. Its larger 75mm gun is perfect for attacking tanks or walkers at their weakest point – their thinner top armor.

The fighter/bomber variant of the new Horten, the HO-347 Fledermaus IV carries numerous bombs under its fuselage. Despite their relatively small size, these bombs can be devastating when dropped directly on a target during a screaming low altitude run.

In Frogs! 2-4 players vie for control of the pond by surrounding their opponents' frogs to eliminate them. When only five frogs are left in the pond, the player with the most frogs left is declared the winner. (You can play until only one player is left, but that could make for a long game.)

The pond is composed of hexagonal cards with a lily pad on one side and water on the other. Frogs can move in any direction, but only on the lily pads and only one pad at a time. Every time a frog moves, the next card over gets flipped, providing a constantly-changing playing field. Frogs are eliminated when they are surrounded by a combination of water and/or frogs of a different color.

Game description from the publisher:

The cards in Android: Netrunner - Trace Amount – the second Data Pack in the "Genesis Cycle", with sixty cards (three copies each of twenty different cards) – enable players to dabble with bold new deck configurations, even as they reinforce the core mechanisms and personalities of the game's seven factions. Runners gain new events while corporate executives look to fund new operations. Players will also find new hardware, resources, icebreakers, ice, agendas, assets, and upgrades. Trace Amount is a perfect Data Pack for fans of bluffing as its many events and operations open new surprises and synergies, and prove in more than one way that a card in the hand is worth two or more actions on the table. And as one might expect, in a Data Pack all about bluffing and surprises, Jinteki features prominently, gaining a new identity.
