family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
My Barter Game is a fun interactive game for ages 7-700.
 How to play
Players roll the dice and land on a square, say the accountant which allows you to buy a trade card. Trade card have values on them of cash or trade and are able to be redeemed at the location listed. You only start this game with $100 cash and $100 trade so you need to use your trade to get you cash. When you go around bottom line (our start) you have to pay $200 in cash (one of the rare things in this game that specifies cash as opposed to being able to use either for anything).

There are twists like shark attack or gold mine where you either earn a fortune or loose one.

Everything in My Barter Game is trade-able so if you want to continue play and someone needs help you can bail them out, or if they are on a square you hold the card to you can trade them something.

Scared to Death is a horror monster battle card game for 1 to 4 players. The goal of the game is to battle your way through an opponents monsters and steal all of their precious victims.  When a player has no victims left in play, they lose the game and their opponent is the winner!

Scared to Death uses the Ignats Card System; a battle card system designed by October Games that enables players to play with an infinite amount of cards in one match.  Players may choose to play a quick game by each using one, 28-card deck, or they may opt for a longer, more complex game, where each player uses five, 28 card decks!  Using multiple decks makes it possible to “level up” your monsters, making them stronger over the coarse of the game... yeah, you heard me: your cards can level up like characters in a video game.  Level 9 is currently the highest, although the system is designed in a way that makes it possible to release more expansions in the future; meaning your cards could level up infinitely!

The game was designed for 2 players, although variations for 1, 3 and 4 players have been added. Most games that claim you can play solo, end up being designed for 1 person, leaving players feeling as if they are playing a solo game alongside other players. This system uses a card driven AI, so when you are playing solo, you still feel that you are playing against another person. Of coarse the artificial intelligence is not as smart as a human brain, so the AI is given some special advantages to make it a worthy opponent. The four player variant is simply a 2 on 2 battle, that feels almost the same as a 2 player game, but you are partnered up with a teammate, with whom you can trade cards and devise strategies.

Further, Scared to Death can be played using only the cards, or with an add-on betting system that uses tokens, money, or even Halloween candy!   Overall, the Card System opens up a wide range of possibilities, including new, and entirely separate games that can be played using your existing Scared to Death cards.

boardgame: The Chronicles of Abakan: The Titan Campaigns
description:
Welcome to the dystopian future of The Chronicles of Abakan: The Titan Campaigns, a new board game based on the visually stunning and evocative world imagined by the brilliant international sculptor and painter, Luca Zampriolo. Set in a resource-starved future, The Chronicles of Abakan centers around a world controlled by dynastic corporate families who rule through an apex of technology: HDMs (Hard Doll Machines), powerful mechas that serve as both industrial laborers and military enforcers.
 The most powerful of these families is House Weingart, a tyrannical and fascist regime whose monopoly over advanced HDM technologies and, most importantly, the super-efficient refined fuel known as “Wein,” has granted them a long-standing and unchallenged authority…
 …until now.
 Deep in the solar system, where Wein is fueled on distant planets, the colony of Terra Nova has rebelled. Hailing mostly from North America, the Neo-Terrans have summoned the revolutionary spirit of their ancestors and successfully repulsed their Weingart overlords, and now an interplanetary Civil War has begun.
 The Chronicles of Abakan is a world of mercenaries and warlords, of bounty hunters and generals, and revolutionaries and military soldiers. Where HDMs drop from transport ships high in orbit around distant planets to do battle on strange and alien terrains, from the crumbling streets of New York City to the frozen tundras of Saturn’s moon Titan, and beyond.

So join in on the strategic and tactical boardgame pitting players against one another in an attempt to secure their dominion or their freedom in a merciless civil war set in humanity’s grim future. The Titan Campaigns chronicle the early years of the Neo-Terrans’ revolution, where players battle for control of resource giving holdings in the Novan Sector, near Saturn. Moons, asteroids, and space stations serve as the battlegrounds, and players maneuver against each other by moving their fleets around the Campaign Map, building up shipyards, factories, and refineries and balancing their resources with expansion and military might.
 When battle is engaged, players move to tile-based battlegrounds, pitting their HDMs (Hard Doll Machines)–giant weaponized mechs–against one another. These miniatures are based on Luca Zampriolo’s original designs, and the models themselves are sculpted by Luca Zampriolo himself, as well as Filippo Zanicotti of Dust Tactics fame. When a player conquers his opponent’s holding, he adds it to his own, thus increasing his 4 Resources: Labor, Oil, Iron, and Wein until only one player is left standing.
 The board game is set to have two factions: The oppressive Weingart Dynasty and the Neo-Terrans--choose your side today!

- English
Pro Manager Mesa (soccer) is a game based on the management, purchase and sale of players, strategy and sport.
As Manager of your club you must manage the best way possible, making the best buys for your team, select the best training and take the greatest care with the use of money from the club. All this don't takes you away from the most exciting part of the sport, the game. As positions your players play when the meetings is essential to leave the field victorious.

Buy the best dribbler to stop letting all the opposing defense, the best keeper to pick all the balls from all angles. Everything depends on you.

- Español
Pro Manager Mensa es un juego de fútbol basado en la gestión, compra y venta de jugadores, estrategia y deporte.
Como Manager de tu club debés administrarlo de la mejor forma posible, realizar las mejores compras para tu equipo, seleccionar los mejores entrenamientos y  tener el mayor cuidado con el uso del dinero del club. 
Todo esto no te aleja de la parte mas emocionante de este deporte; el juego. Cómo posiciones a tus jugadores a la hora de disputar los encuentros es fundamental para salir victorioso de la cancha.
Partidos dinámicos con árbitros no siempre parciales, tribunas alborotadas que pueden llegar a provocarte un dolor de cabeza, una lluvia inesperada que embarra el campo de juego o un sol encandilante que enceguezca a tu arquero son factores que te van a sorprender partido a partido.
Compra al mejor gambeteador para dejar parada a toda la defensa rival, al mejor arquero para descolgar todas las pelotas de todos los ángulos o al pulpo defensor que atemorice a cualquier delantero. Todo depende de vos.

description:
Cauldron: Bubble and Boil is a game in which the players are witches and warlocks try to win the coveted Witches Eye in a potion brewing contest.  The game involves planting gardens, harvesting ingredients and hexing your opponents.  There are multiple strategies to earn victory points and win the game.

CONTENTS:
Retail Edition of Cauldron: Bubble and Boil

1 Rulebook
1 Lore book (with stories by Kickstarter backers)
4 Cauldrons
4 player mats (with diablerie score and turn order guide)
1 Victory Point scoreboard
49 Crone Tokens
32 Green cubes
32 Orange cubes
32 Red cubes
32 Yellow cubes
32 black Corruption discs
2 player markers for 4 players (8 total)
80 cards
10 Advanced Victory Cards

Each card has three types of game information: hex, ingredient and recipe.

The players have a number of choices each turn.  They may chose to cast a hex spell which has various effects (e.g. stealing resources, lowering or raising victory points, etc.).  Casting a hex raises your position on the "Diablerie Track" which yields victory points at the end of the game.

They may take cards in their hand that produce the same resource and plant them in a garden (minimum 2 cards).  A garden is a set of cards that produce ingredients for brewing recipes at the end of the game.  You may have a maximum of three gardens.  gardens cannot normally have cards added to it once it is planted.  The player takes ingredients from their garden every turn and places it in their cauldron.  These are used at the end of the game to brew recipes.  Additionally, each time a garden is successfully harvested completely (i.e. no resource remaining in a garden)the player receives a Crone Token (each worth 3 points at the end of the game).

The third option a card provides is for a player to play as a recipe card.  Each recipe lists a victory point total and the ingredients required to brew it.  If a player chooses to save a recipe, the player places it face down under the player's cauldron.  no one is allowed to look at recipes once they are placed under a cauldron (this is called "recording a recipe in a cook book").

Additional information:
Casting a hex requires paying the listed cost.  Often the cost is accepting a black corruption token.  A corruption token is placed in your cauldron and at the end of the game each corruption token destroys one resource of your choice.

The game ends when two resources are depleted or when one player has recorded seven recipes. For a quicker game or longer game, adjust the number of recipes that can be recorded.

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – The Steward's Fear
description:
Game description from the publisher:

The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game - The Steward's Fear, the first Adventure Pack in the "Against the Shadow" cycle, is a tale of corruption within the heart of Gondor that follows immediately after the events of the Heirs of Númenor expansion. Its sixty new cards include a new hero, three copies of each new player card, and a clever new scenario. When Middle-earth's heroes investigate a plot against the realm of Gondor, their search for clues leads them out of the main streets and back alleys of Minas Tirith – deep into an underworld where nefarious plots and deadly villains await them.

Requires both the Core Set and the Heirs of Númenor expansion in order to play.

description:
Carcassonne: Winter-Edition: Der Lebkuchenmann – which is playable only with the Winter-Edition of Carcassonne – consists of six new tiles, a wooden gingerbread man token, and rules. Whenever one of these new tiles is placed, the gingerbread man is moved, leaving gifts for the knights of the city from which he exits. Finally, everyone should have some treats from his visit...

Carcassonne: Winter-Edition: Der Lebkuchenmann debuted as a promotional item at Spiel 2012.

The 2012 Asmodee Special Card set is a set of 3 cards that can be added to any Dixit game simply by shuffling them into the deck before the game.  The three cards are:


     An aerial scene depicting a knight on a purple dragon, a cowboy  on a giant eagle, and a gypsy on a flying carpet.  You might recognize this illustration, as it was used as the box cover of Dixit 3!
     An image of earth with a halo of fire (or maybe smoke or something else?) surrounding it.  There is also a giant hand palm-side up beneath the earth.
     A forest scene, where a harp is built into one of the trees and plays a song.  Someone has also carved a heart into one of the trees and shot it with an arrow.


These cards are new and not available in the USA (other than being the cover image on the Dixit 3 box!)!  The set will be made available in the BoardGameGeek Geek Store beginning in October or November of 2012. These cards are regular part of a european edition of Dixit 3.

Swedish card game to teach children math.

Two decks of cards - one with questions, one with answers. Each player get five question cards. One answer card is placed face up on the board. The first player looks to see if he has any question card that matches.

For instance if in the answer pile lays "=7" and the first player has the card "7x1" then he can pick up the answer card and take the pair and put it to the side. If the first player has a pair but does not know the answer another player can take his question card and get a pair for himself.

If nobody has the question card to the answer card it is put into the center of the table. Then a new answer card can be turned up and the game continues to the next player in turn. Each player gets a chance to see if they have a matching pair.

Swedish "Go fish" type card game.

One deck of card, consisting of the letters of the alphabet in small and bid sizes. The players take turn asking the other players after a specific card, trying to get a two card set collection of each letter (one nig and one small letter). The most sets when the deck has run out is the winner.

boardgame: Conflict of Heroes: Wrecks and Destruction on the Eastern Front
description:
Wrecks and Destruction on the Eastern Front is an expansion counter set includes optional rules and counters for Wrecks, Artillery, Rubble and Fire. You can use these optional rules and counters with any published Conflict of Heroes firefight.

Wrecks: Included are Soviet and German wreck counters that modify Line of Sight through the hex and defense modifiers in the hex. See the tide of battle as wrecks begin to litter the country side.

Off-Board Artillery: Rules and targeting counters for drifting artillery strikes. Damn that friendly fire!

Rubble: Buildings can be destroyed, changing LOS and defense. So you think that building was safe to hide in? Hmmm...lets demolish it and THEN set it on fire.

Fire: Oooohh...burn them suckers out. For those that just don't want to surrender.

boardgame: Conflict of Heroes: Monster Tanks of the Eastern Front
description:
Add Monster Tanks to your gaming arsenal. Dozens of bad a*# tanks including the 200 ton Maus, the King Tiger, the Brummbär, the Joseph Stalin, and many more.  Then we threw in a few fun units like Mine Dogs (NYET, not that tank!) and the remote controlled Goliath (Kaboom!).

Academy Games has posted fire fights on their website that use these counters.

Spin and move on a train track from New York to Chicago. Produced in the 1930s. Win the greatest number of points in a previously determined number of trips or be the first to obtain a total of 1000 points.
The board is actually the inside bottom of the box.

Looking for something different with game play that seems familiar?  Like playing spades or hearts, but want to spice it up a bit?  Night and Day is an exciting new trick taking game that throws in a little suspense each round of the hand!

Cards have two values, one for the day and one for the night. Players play a card each round with the intent of being the highest value to take the trick. But no one knows if it’s night or day until everyone has made their play…

boardgame: He-Man and the Masters of the Universe Battling Warriors Game
description:
He-Man and the Masters of the Universe battle the evil Skeletor to save Castle Grayskull from it falling into the hands of evil.

Players choose to defend the Kingdoms of the evil Skeletor or mighty He-Man as they battle against each other or as a team! Each player has matching pairs of their Character Tokens and three Safety Tokens. In addition to these tokens are the Lose a Turn Tokens. Each player has two tokens that can make the other player lose a turn if knocked down.

Game play consists of players sitting 5ft apart from one another and taking turns firing their Battle Weapon. Players try to knock down matching character pairs. First player (or team) to knock down at least three matching character pairs wins!

Each player gets their evil or good side (Skeletor or He-Man) character tokens and places them on the character token strip. Each character token has an individual marked place where they’re token will be placed.

boardgame: Additional Sherlock Holmes Cases for 221B Baker Street, The Master Detective Game
description: Expansion set for the Gibsons Games edition of 221B Baker Street. Rules, Clues and Solutions for cases 41-80. Includes "three-pipe problem" cases - The Mystery of the Mummy's Curse, The Strange Affair of the Missing Major, The Case of the Campanologist's Last Clanger and Sherlock Holmes, the Diogenes Head, etc.

Sprec (Stacking + Pyramidal + Recursive) is a Spred derivative (that is a LOT derivative itself) that rescues LOT's winning condition and adds a 4th ball colour to the Shibumi set.

It features a simple ball-removal game mechanism that makes loops impossible, but at the same time, produces potentially long games in a very small playing area. The game can last from 9 turns to around 50.

It can be played in any board size, but 5x5 or 6x6 is recommended.

RULES

Player A uses white balls as the base colour and yellow balls as the upgrade colour. Player B uses the red bals as the base colour and the black balls as the upgrade colour.

The board starts empty. Players take alternate turns placing a ball of their base colour (White or Red) on an empty position.

If, after a player's move, there is at least one straight line of three connected* balls (touching) of their base colour (the lines can be orthogonal or across levels), the player does the following:

1. Chooses any of those lines of three.
2. Removes the maximum number of movable balls from that line. This means that the player must find out the correct order to remove them so he removes the most. Balls may drop as a result of these removals.
3. Places one ball of their upgrade colour on any of the empty spaces that composed the removed line.

The game ends when a player creates at least one straight line of three connected* balls (touching) of their upgrade colour (the lines can be orthogonal or across levels).

If the pyramid fills up without reaching the victory codition the player with most upgrade balls on the board wins.

(*)- Note that flat diagonal lines are not connected!

boardgame: So zählen und rechnen wir gern
description:
East German educational game to help youngsters with their numbers and counting. Based on picture lotto, each player gets a player-card with 10 spaces to fill.The spaces are numbered 1 to 10 but not in order so that the player must count to know where to place a card. Each player-card has a different illustration. The illustration is important because only cards that match that illustration are eligible to be placed on the player-card.

The first player to fill their player-card, wins.

There is game board with a spiral path what ends on the center of the board.
Each player gets hand of cards (red, blue, green, orange or white) numbered from 1 to 10. The number of the card determines the movement of the player, the color corresponds to the colors of the boxes of the route. After distribution, the upper card heel indicates the trump suit.

In each round, all players choose one of their cards and pose face down in front of them. All cards are opened and the strongest card wins the trick (the trump outweighs the other colors). Only the player who wins the trick moves his pawn as many spaces indicated by the card., it is necessary that the color of the card matches the color of the box when the pawn arrives.
The white cards are bonus cards in which the color is optional.

The winner is the player who reaches to finish first.

Similar to La Boîte à Quiz.

Topics are Literature, Science, History, Geography, Arts, and Sports. A thick booklet contains extensive responses.

The Box provides 80 cards with about 150 questions. Each card contains a fact and number of questions on a common theme.
Answer correctly and score a point. The player with the most points wins.

Another version of the La Scatola degli Enigmi. This time topic is math.

Players or teams compete in mathematical puzzle-solving (sometimes timed, sometimes not) in order to score points in different categories. The first team (or player) to score a point in 4 of the 6 categories wins the game.

Every turn, the current player/team draws a card and hands it to the Dealer, a non-competitive figure that reads it aloud and then manages the competition between the people trying to solve the riddle.

Another version of the La Boîte à énigmes. This riddles about vampires.

Players or teams compete in puzzle-solving (sometimes timed, sometimes not) in order to score points in different categories. The first team (or player) to score a point in 4 of the 6 categories wins the game.

Every turn, the current player/team draws a card and hands it to the Dealer, a non-competitive figure that reads it aloud and then manages the competition between the people trying to solve the riddle.

Another version of the La Boîte à énigmes. 
There are some crimes to be solved in this game.

Players or teams compete in crime-solving (sometimes timed, sometimes not) in order to score points in different categories. The first team (or player) to score a point in 4 of the 6 categories wins the game.

Every turn, the current player/team draws a card and hands it to the Dealer, a non-competitive figure that reads it aloud and then manages the competition between the people trying to solve the riddle.

68 additional cards with 680 questions for Laste mälumängudoomino

Laste Mälumängu Doomino (Trivia Dominoes) is a boardgame that puts a trivia element into the well known dominoes game. The tiles have two categories marked on them, out of total ten. The tile placement follows normal domino rules, but each time you are placing a tile, you have to answer a question on the topic marked on the tile in question.

boardgame: Eins, zwei, drei, fertig ist die Reih
description:
A multi-use card game. This East German game was probably intended to be educational as well as fun. There are 12 sets of three, each card in a set having the same letter in the bottom corner. The three cards are related i.e. A sheep, a ball of wool and a jumper makes up the set F. In addition there is a Clever Child Card and a Schwarzer Peter card.

Various uses of the cards are described. You could play as a quartett game (as a game of Authors in America), the one collecting the most sets of three will be the winner. Or you could play as a Schwarzer Peter (Old Maid) game, the one finishing with the Schwarzer Peter card being the loser. If the parents thought that losing was harsh, then you could play a 'White Peter', the child having the Clever Child gard at the end being the winner. And you could play it as a quiz game where the parent will reveal one card of a set and ask the children in turn to name another card they might expect to see. If they can, they get the card and so on, the one with the most cards at the end being the winner.

A cooperative die rolling game for toddlers that trains recognition and matching of basic colours as well as turn-taking.

The board shows 16 flower spaces and a track consisting of 7 butterfly spaces. Players take turns rolling the die. If it shows the flower symbol, the active player places one of the wooden flower chips on an empty flower space of the same colour on the board. If the butterfly symbol is rolled, the active player advances the butterfly token one space.

If all the flowers have been placed before the butterfly reaches the end of the track, the players win together. If the butterfly reaches the last space on its track before all the flowers have been placed, the butterfly wins instead.

User summary:

A bonus Class, Weapon, and Monster for Gauntlet of Fools that was provided as a bonus to Kickstarter backers.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
A cooperative game for young children that focuses on colour recognition and dexterity.

Background story: Max, the snail, travels the world. He meets new friends and is given lots of gifts in different sizes, weights and shapes. He wants to bring them home before caterpillar Rosi has eaten all the lettuce.

Aim of the game: The players cooperate in trying to get Max and his presents home before it is too late.

Setup: The six round cards - five showing one of Max' animal friends and one showing the garden in which Rosi lives - are placed in a circle. The six lettuce chips are placed on the garden card. The colourful gifts (mostly wooden objects but also a pillow and a blanket) are placed in the center and Max' journey starts. He is placed outside the circle of cards, next to the garden card.

Gameplay: Players take turns rolling the die. If a colour is rolled, Max (a wooden plate with stalk eyes and a semi-circle shaped house) is moved with a wooden pushing rod along the circle of cards to the animal of that color. Then a gift of the same colour is loaded onto Max. If the next player rolls the same colour, he/she must push Max one full circle. If there is no gift left in that colour, the die is re-rolled. If a star is rolled, the player may choose any colour and doesn't have to move Max.
If Max loses a gift (i.e. it falls down while Max is being pushed), one lettuce chip is turned over and the gifts are put back on Max.

As soon as gifts in all five colours have been loaded onto Max, the active player returns him to the garden. If there is at least one head of lettuce left, the players win the game.

A new, official Ancient One card, given to attendees of the Arkham Nights event on 12-14 October 2012.

Consists of a new card for Yig, with alternate abilities and art to the one in the base game.
family: Series: Arkham Horror Files (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
A party and trivia game.

Components: Board, 330 cards, 6 player tokens, card box, sand timer, 36 action chips, 34 joker chips, die, noisemaker

There are six categories of questions/activities, and several per category are printed on each card:
- Pantomime - mime a word from the card, the other players guess what it is.
- Drawing - draw a word from the card, the other players guess.
- Taboo words - explain the word without using the forbidden words.
- Proverbs - the active player's neighbour reads the first part of the phrase from the card, the active player has to complete it.
- Psycho quiz - the active player chooses a partner. The partner chooses a situation from the card and reads it. The card also lists four possible answers, of which both partners choose one. The aim is to guess what the other one will choose.
- Logic: The active player is given a logic problem to solve.

The game is to collect one action chip per colour (colours correspond to the different categories of tasks). Action chips are collected by landing on an action space on the board and completing a task from that category. Joker chips can be earned by guessing the words other players are miming/drawing or if the player's token lands on a joker space. Three joker chips can be exchanged for an action chip of the player's choice.

The first player to collect one action chip from each of the six different categories wins the game.

A press-your-luck and bluffing game with a Moorhuhn (German "Crazy Chicken" computer game franchise) theme.

Components: 58 number chips, 14 Moorhuhn chips, one sock

The number chips show values from 1 to 4 and come in 5 different colours. The Moorhuhn chips are the same size but show the chicken. All the chips are put into the sock, from which the players draw them, trying to collect as many victory points as possible.
Players take turns drawing a chip from the sock. If it is a number chip, the player chooses if he/she wants to continue or keep all the chips he/she has drawn this round. The active player continues drawing chips until he/she either decides to stop, or he/she draws a Moorhuhn chip.
When a player decides to stop, he/she gets to keep the chips drawn this round, sorts them into colours and puts them into rows so that the other players can see them. Black is a joker and the player has to decide which colour he/she wants to use the black chip for at the end of the round.
If a Moorhuhn is drawn, the player's turn ends and he/she doesn't get any chips that round - they go to the next player instead.

The game ends when the last Moorhuhn turns up. Each player counts the point in their row with the smallest number of chips. The player with the most points in that row wins the game.

In a variant players get to keep the chips if a Moorhuhn turns up, but they have to give their row of chips of the same colour as the Moorhuhn to the next player. Scoring is also different: The player with the highest number of points in any of their rows wins the game.

Players use number combinations to decipher which words these combinations would produce on the keypad of a mobile phone.

Players take turns rolling a six-sided die (numbers 1-4, x, "no reception"). The active player rolls the die. If he/she rolls "no reception", the die is passed on to the next player. If a number is rolled, the red token is advanced the corresponding number of spaces on the board and all players try to work out the word marked with that number on the top card at the same time. The first player to correctly guess the word earns the number of chips specified on the card. An x on the die roll means that the active player chooses which of the four codes on the top card has to be deciphered.

The game ends when the red token passes the finish line. The player with the most chips wins the game.

1945 is an easy to learn, fun to play strategic card game of world conquest for 2-6 people. Players collect armed forces cards and use them to conquer territories. Battles can be changed dramatically by special action cards that have a variety of effects, from helping your troops to bombing your enemies to smithereens. Ultimately, whoever gathers enough points by conquering territories and collecting victory point cards is declared the winner!

Players take turns drawing cards from the deck. When a territory is drawn, all the players join in to battle for it. The player who drew it declares a Theater of War -- Air, Land, or Sea -- and players deploy troops from their hands face down to hide their strength. (Individual troop cards range in strength from 1 to 5, so while other players can see how many cards you've laid down, they don't know how strong your forces are.)

Players can also play special cards, which increase the strength of one's forces, allow one to see other players' cards, let a player play a card from the discard pile, drop an A-Bomb on a territory to remove it from play altogether, or various other effects. When all players have committed as many forces they wish, all of the cards are revealed, special cards are resolved, and the player with the strongest remaining forces wins the territory.

The number of Victory Points required to win the game varies with the number of players participating. One gains a Victory Point for each territory held, as well as for each Victory Point card held in one's hand. The first player to accumulate the required number of Victory Points wins the game.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
This expansion was released in connection with Mayday Games' 2012 Kickstarter campaign for their Deluxe version of Get Bit!.

You will use the special Squid die for this expansion. At the start of every turn, before cards are chosen, have the swimmer in front roll the die. Leave the die up for everyone to see for the entire round.

Consult the die: 
• Calm Seas (Even number of 2,4 or 6 comes up):  Oh how nice... no change at all! You are "even" with the sea. The round is played as normal.

• Tidal Wave (Odd number 3 or 5 comes up):  What the heck happened?  You are "at Odds" with the sea! After selecting cards and running the Get Bit! phase as usual, with the shark biting the swimmer in the back, and the swimmer jumping to the front, the shark then is also moved to the front, reversing the order of the entire line, so the front becomes the back and vice versa.


• Ink Cloud (Squid image comes up):  It's chaos I tell you!  Chaos! For this round, all cards played are randomly chosen, and the Get Bit! phase is skipped.

Sharkspansion Note: The shark also plays a random card, but it has no effect. The Ink Cloud does not cause the shark to “miss.”

(Squid Die: 6 sides, Numbers 2-6 with one side Ink Cloud icon)

For Walk the Plank:

The die has 6 sides, with The Calm Sea (3 sides), Tidal Wave (2 sides), Kraken (Squid) on one side. It is rolled any time someone falls off the end. Calm Sea nothing happens, Tidal Wave pushes all pirates that are furthest off the plank back toward the ship and Squid would mean anyone on the very last plank is pulled off into the sea by the monster!

This expansion was released in connection with Mayday Games' 2012 Kickstarter campaign for their Deluxe version of Get Bit!.

You will use the Dolphin die for this expansion, as well as the special Dolphin card for each player. At the start of the game, give each swimmer the extra “D” card for their color, replacing the highest number in your hand. (So the swimmers should have the numbers 1 to however many players there are, plus the Dolphin card.)

When a “D” card is played, before swimmers are moved, that player rolls the Dolphin die. That player’s card is considered to be whatever number was rolled for all purposes, including tying with other dice/cards. Multiple “D” cards are NOT considered to tie each other unless they roll the same value.

The dolphin comes up if the player rolls a “6” but it is treated as if the player had played a "9", and the shark icon is considered a “0.” As these numbers are not present on cards, they only ever tie if multiple swimmers play their dolphin card in the same round and roll the same icon.

(Dolphin Die: 2, 3, 4, 5, Dolphin(6), Shark/(0))

For Walk the Plank:

This is a single D6 Die that will have on it the numbers 2-5 as well as a number 0 (with a shark) and a number 9 (with a dolphin). This shark/dolphin die would be fun for the first person to lose a man off the edge to roll a single time. If it is a 0 then he loses his guy for good and cannot do anything (eaten by the shark), if a number 2-5 comes in it is good luck for him, he can move that many pirates one length toward the edge of the plank, but cannot use it to push anyone overboard. If the #9 comes up with the dolphin, it is extremely good luck and his man actually gets pushed back onto the plank, but remains on the last plank spot. Additionally no one can play the "shorten the plank" on the round after the #9 comes up to protect this one man for one round.

boardgame: Die Minen von Zavandor: Die verlorenen Spruchrollen
description:
"Die verlorenen Spruchrollen" (English: "The lost scrolls") is a micro expansion for The Mines of Zavandor by Alexander Pfister.

It adds 4 "scrolls" to the game, which give the players certain benefits. The players can "spend" the benefits after an auction to increase their bid afterwards, but lose the benefit for the rest of the game.

At the moment only a german version of the expansion exists. It was released as give away at the Spiel 2011.

boardgame: Escape: The Curse of the Temple – Queenie 2: Doomed
description:
A set of three promotional tiles given to Kickstarter backers of the game.

The first two new tiles are curses: Doomed and Stand up.  If a player is cursed with Doomed, they cannot escape from the temple until lifting the curse.  If a player is cursed with Stand up, they must remain standing until lifting the curse.

The third new tile is a treasure: Counterspell.  Counterspell can be used to discard one curse card.

A game with which children can discover the world of visual arts in a playful way, by answering questions and completing art-related tasks.

Components: 72 picture cards, 91 task tiles, 1 tile bag, 1 sculpture, 24 sculpture parts, 1 learning poster

Each player gets 6 cardboard sculpture parts. The aim of the game is to be the first player to get rid of all their sculpture parts by hanging it onto the tree-shaped sculpture. Players are allowed to hang a sculpture part on the sculpture if they correctly answer a question/complete a task.

24 picture cards with paintings from different eras are put in the middle of the table. On the back of each card there are 3 questions and some general information on the painting and its creator. The task tiles are put in the tile bag. Players take turns drawing a tile from the bag. The active player chooses one of the two tasks and reads it aloud. Now all players try to be the first to complete the task. Tasks include:
- Finding an object (specified on the task tile) on one of the picture cards.
- The active player describes a picture, the other players try to guess which one it is.
- The active player tries to draw one of the pictures, the others guess.
- Players have to answer a question on one of the cards (cards are turned face down).
- Guessing which painting is the oldest or most recent.

The game ends when one of the players has managed to get rid of all his/her sculpture parts.

A set collection game where the object is obtain 3 cards of the same sound in a set.  The player with the most sets wins the game.

Players start with 5 cards.  The rest of the cards are placed face down on the table.  If a player has a set of 3, they can place it on the table right away, then draw 3 more cards.  After this is completed, the main game begins.

On each turn, the player asks the others for the sound for which they are hunting.  If they complete a set upon receiving cards, they can ask for another sound and continue until they cannot make a set.  At that point, the player is told to "go hunt" and they draw a card.  If this card completes a set, the player can draw again and continue until they cannot make a set.

If the player runs out of cards, they get 5 new cards.

The player with the most sets wins the game.

This is an expansion of Quest: Angriff der Orks and Quest: Wolfsjagd, not a basic game. To play this adventure, you will need both of these games.

The included adventure is meant as a prequel to the campaign in Quest: Wolfsjagd and needs both gaming material from Quest: Angriff der Orks and Quest: Wolfsjagd.

A somewhat streamlined version of Uno, first given away as a promotional item in Burger King Kid's Meals in 2012. The Deck is only 36 cards. Each suite (green, yellow, blue and red) is comprised of 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7 and Draw 2. Finally, there are 4 Wild Cards.

As in regular Uno, the object is to eliminate your hand before your opponents. Because of the reduced card counts, this edition seems to be optimized for 2-player play: the set lacks the traditional skips, reverses and draw 4 wilds of the normal edition.

The winds of change were in the air for German Fascism and its allies. Having reached its furthest limits of conquest in the fall of 1942, the on rush of the southern German armies had been stopped on the Volga, at Stalingrad, along the crest of the Caucasus Mountains, and on the Black Sea coast on the outskirts of Novorossiysk. Through the fall of 1942, the Germans had launched attack after attack, but had been stopped in bitter fighting among the ruins of Stalingrad, in the mountain passes and river valleys of the Caucasus, and at the Oktyabr Cement Works on the Black Sea. Then, as the Winter came on, the trapped the Sixth Army at Stalingrad, scattered Germany's Romanian, Italian, and Hungarian Armies, and threatened to cut off Army Group A in the Kuban region. By February 1943, the Sixth Army was finished, the First Panzer Army was withdrawing through the Rostov Gap, hotly pursued, and the 17th Army of General Ruoff was falling back into the Taman peninsula.

OzB 1 Vines of Red Marines: In the early morning hours, Soviet seaborne forces landed on the peninsula along the west side of the Ozerekya Bay. These forces were led by tank spearheads and quickly advanced towards Glebovka, where they came across a Romanian Mortar position set up in the vineyard of an old manor.

OzB 2 From America, Tanks: In the early morning hours, Soviet seaborne forces landed on the peninsula along the west side of the Ozerekya Bay. The Soviet forces were led by tank spearheads of the American built Stuart tanks of the 563rd Tank Brigade.

OzB 3 By Fire and Flame: By the late afternoon, this Soviet seaborne force threatened to capture the Axis held town of Glebovka. In desperation, the Romanians, in an attempt to slow the Russians’ advance, threw whatever units they had on hand. The Engineers of the Romanian 58th Battalion located in nearby Krasno Medvedovskaya and the freshly arriving panzerjägers of the German Panzerjäger Abteilung 173 were quickly rushed into battle.

OzB 4 Jackboot to the Rear: Despite having three barges, four launches, two steiners, one tug, and one patrol boat sunk, and sixteen patrol boats, two tugs and two launches damaged; the Soviets were able to get 1,427 men and 10 tanks ashore before dawn. On the other hand, the landing force had no radio communication with the fleet.

Ozerekya Breakout offers two ways of experiencing the battle. Players can sample a portion of the fighting by playing one of four standalone scenarios. Scenarios OzB1-OzB4 also serve to familiarize players with the battlefield, and the opposing forces. Players who enjoy a strategic aspect are encouraged to experience the entire battle by playing the Tactical Mission (TM). The TM is comprised of seven inter-connected scenarios that begin on the morning of 4 February and continue over the course of six additional time periods (PM) 4 Feb, (AM) 5 Feb (PM) 5 Feb, (AM) 6 Feb (PM) 6 Feb, and (AM) 7 Feb (PM) 7 Feb. The TM lends itself well to team play.

It contains:

        one 33" x 24" map with one inch hexes
        Chapter: Ozerekya Breakout Rules and tables (16 pages)
        four ASL scenarios
        one Tactical Mission (aka Campaign Game)


Ozerekya Breakout is not a complete game and ownership of a tactical wargaming system such as MMP’s Advanced Squad Leader, Critical Hit’s Advanced Tobruk System, or Firefly  WWII Battlegroup Actions is required.

(from the publisher and BGG user)

The farmer left the gate open again! Would you be so kind as to help the farmer's wife get the absentminded farmer and his frolicking animals back to the farm?

In Farmeroo!, players take turns rolling a die, then moving the appropriate number of steps on the game board. If you end your movement on a square with an animal or the farmer, you may pick the corresponding tile from the center of the game board and place it on your personal board – but only if you first lure it by making the sound of the animal or a tractor! If your turn ends on the farmer's wife, you may pick any missing tile. The first player to collect all six animals and the farmer wins!

7 Wonders: Myths is a multifaceted fan expansion for 7 Wonders.

A city sacrifices resources, or meets building prerequisites, to invoke the "divine blessing" of an ancient deity from Greek, Egyptian, and/or Babylonian mythology. Divine blessings occur in the age in which they are invoked and are gone at the end of the age.

It contains:
- 35 myth cards representing the pantheon of ancient gods
- 2 new wonder boards (Ithaca & Tártaros)
- 3 guild cards
- 6 leader cards
- 1 tuckbox
- 1 rulebook

This expansion is available as a print-and-play in the 7 Wonders files section at the bottom of this page.

boardgame: Forlorn Hopes: The Defense of the Dutch East Indies 1942
description:
(from ATO website:)

World War Two history has a pattern in both the European and Pacific Theaters. Constant Axis expansion, followed by steady Axis contraction, as the Allies recapture lost territory. In Europe, it is hard to pinpoint the transition point (a good barroom discussion any time!). In the Pacific, it is a little easier to focus precisely at the campaign where the Japanese tide of conquest began to ebb-- their unsuccessful campaign to take Port Moresby, the "step too far."

What then represents the "high tide" of the Japanese Empire? That would be their successful conquest of the Dutch East Indies, one of their most strategically vital targets for the oil and rubber it could provide. A broad look at the record suggest the Japanese mostly had things their own way against the hasty "ABDA" alliance (American, British, Dutch, Australian), with its varied mix of modern and obsolete weapons and widely differing priorities. But a closer look shows that this was a much closer campaign, stretching the Japanese to complete. With a few breaks, the Allies might have held on.

Can you do better? Find out for yourself.

Forlorn Hopes covers the entire two-month campaign for the region, using a system that fully integrates air/sea/land operations. Derived from the popular titles "Chennault's First Fight" and "Operation Cartwheel" (both published by ATO), this game give both sides difficult choices in facing their different challenges.

The Allies are spread thin and facing multiple routes by which the enemy can attack. They know the maxim that "those who defend everything defend nothing" but deciding what NOT to hold is difficult. You must make the Japanese pay for every gain...

The Japanese face an enemy already in control of much of the battlefield and fighting with their backs to the wall. They have a schedule crunch and must weigh to benefits of using high cost assets to gain objectives. The cost of success at too high a price is long term failure (and who will tell the Emperor?).

BOTH players can choose to increase their available forces, representing greater emphasis in overall planning. But the reinforcements come at a cost in victory points that could tip the scales to the enemy. Both sides are charged with using assets as effectively as possible. This is not an easy win for either side.

Forlorn Hopes, the 2012 Annual, delivers the extra size and contents you expect from ATO. Full color 22" x 34" map. 288 die-cut, full color large 5/8" counters provide players with the air, ground, and naval units which actually fought in the campaign, plus some which could have been there.

Maps - One full color 22"x34" mapsheet
Counters - 288 full color die-cut 5/8" pieces
Rules length - 16 pages
Charts and tables - 4 pages
Complexity - Medium
Solitaire suitability - Average
Playing time - Up to 2 to 3 hours for the smaller scenarios, 12 to 15 hours for the full campaign game

Design - Paul Rohrbaugh
Development - Lembit Tohver
Graphic Design - Mark Mahaffey
family: Series: Evolution of Airpower in World War 2

description:
Age of the Clankomatics is a free customizable card game, set in a cyber punk world of mindless cyborgs, giant man eating plants, biplane squadrons and criminal samurai for hire.

The game is usually played with two players, though there is a variant for more.

The goal of the game is to destroy your opponents headquarters while trying to defend their own.

The game is played in rounds, each player taking it in turns to play. During a round a player will deploy unit cards into their field of battle and attack their opponent. Once a player has worn down his opponent's forces, he can go in for the kill and attack his opponents HQ.

Another element of the game are the special units, giant monsters and constructions that can only be brought into play if you have the resources. These cards are hard to deal with and prevent games from becoming a defensive stalemate.

All of the cards played have a cost, and this cost is dealt with in a unique fashion. Each player has a number of power tokens, and when you play a card, you must give your opponent a number of tokens equal to the cost of the card. This way, if you play a very expensive card, you enable your opponent to match you on their turn. Play a very cheap card however, and you choke your opponents resources, limiting their options.

boardgame: Lancaster: Henry V – The Power of the King
description:
Lancaster: Henry V - The Power of the King, which can be played only with the Lancaster base game, consists of two expansions that can be added to the game either independently or in combination. When used together, eight new laws (which reference materials in the expansion) can also be added to the game.

With At the King's Court, new offices are available to be filled, with the Lords (i.e., the players) applying for them in search of power and privileges. Each player receives a token representing himself, the Lord of his land, and to claim these offices they'll need to bring the Lord token – along with supporting knights and a particular form of tribute, such as a voting marker or gold – to the office in question, whether Lord High Chancellor, Lord High Constable, or Great Lord Chamberlain. If you win the position, you'll collect official seals, an archer to use in battle against France, or the first Tudor knight in England. When you upgrade a knight, you may now collect a bishop – and the number of bishops you hold is multiplied by the seals you've acquired for an endgame bonus.

With The King's Resentment, the players now feel the king's wrath after each lost conflict. Lack of support results in dishonor, which can be extinguished only with the help of the tower – an additional location to place knights during the game. At game's end, each player loses one point for each dishonor token held, and the players with the most and second most tokens lose additional points based on the total amount of dishonor dished out by the king during the game.

Players take turns rolling dice and matching them to sets of up to 4 orthogonally-adjacent tiles selected from an 8x8 game board.  Matched tiles are claimed by each player in an attempt to be the first to claim seven sets of four tiles arranged in different patterns.  Players earn bonuses for being the first to claim a pattern and for collecting sets of like-numbered tiles.

In Glorieta, 2 players compete to be the first to form a loop of stones on a hexagonally tiled board. This would be an impossible task except in each match, stones on board are slowly converted to neutral stones, owned by both players. As a result, the barriers to forming a loop gradually erode, and the player who most cleverly navigates the rubble completes a loop.

from designer's site:

The game is played on a Hexhex board divided into concentric sections. I don't know the best board size, and I don't yet know the best way to section the board. The different versions of the game arise from the different ways to section the board. I'll start by describing the rules with the simplest board.

Rules

1. The board begins empty. One player owns the black stones, and the other owns the white. Starting with Black, the players take turns.
 2. On your turn, you must either place one stone on any empty space on the inner section, or two stones on any two empty spaces in the outer section.
 3. The game ends when the board is full. The player with the largest group wins.  If the players’ largest groups are the same size, compare their second-largest groups, and so on, until you come to a pair which aren't the same size.  Whoever owns the larger of the two wins.

from the designer's site:

It has a novel mechanism with some aesthetic appeal, along with a festive board.  It's a variant of Y on a hexhex board, but with a balancing twist.  It's for two players, played with white and black stones on the board [used by Spry].

The board consists of blue, red, and yellow borders, as well as concentric rings, each a different shade of green.   The central space is also considered a “ring”, even though it’s just one space.

Rules

1. The board begins empty.
 2. The players take turns.  On each turn, a player must place one stone of her color onto any empty space, but not on any red, yellow or blue spaces.
 3. If a player makes a first placement on a ring interior to all other rings with stones currently on them, then her opponent may optionally switch sides with her, rather than place a stone, on his next turn.
 4. The game ends when a chain of either white or black stones touches at least one red, one yellow, and one blue space.  The player who owns that chain at game’s end wins.

Liberators! Books and Miniatures allow you to recreate the Wars of South American Independence on your game table.  Liberators! Books give you all the information you need to learn about and game this fascinating period.  The books include the history of the conflict; organization, tactics and uniforms of the armies and scenarios to play including map and order of battle information. Liberators! Miniatures are a museum quality range of "heroic" scale 15mm figures that allow you to build the armies listed in the scenarios...or to create scenarios of your own!

The game features:


     Players take the role of a brigade or division commander in the South America Independence War in the XIX Century.



     Take your side as Liberators Armies of the North “Simon Bolivar” or South “San Martin” Against the Royalist Spanish Army Forces.



     Build your armies with information on military uniformology, the colors pattern and symbols of the XIX Century independence Era; like Gran Colombian Army, Llanero/Peasant infantry, Argentine Grenadiers, Royalist/Spanish Infantry or even Irish/British legion forces involved in the conflict. 



     The game is tactical in nature, focusing on the movement of individual battalions and regiments of cavalry and use of artillery units. 



     The game is designed to cover different periods of time, divided into several volumes, including data on the theaters of operations involved in every war and the organization of the armies involved. 



     Numerous scenarios each side and ready to be played
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: History: Latin American Wars of Independence

description:
For thousands of years, the island gods slumbered. Now, mortals have arrived, bringing with them the power of belief and the hunger for the miraculous. The gods now vie to become the chief deity of these islanders through enacting various miracles to fulfill the community’s needs, to inspire the creation of shrines, and to shape the very earth to advance their own divine message and thwart the blasphemies of the other gods.

Players take on the role of these gods. The game centers around manipulating the mortals by performing miracles such as the creation of manna, idols, and auras, transforming the landscape, and acquiring the power such miracles require. After each player has performed miracles for the turn, some of the mortals act, randomly selecting from the options of exploring, praying, or enshrining idols.

Players earn victory points through the mortals' prayer, the enshrining and worship of their idols, and through providing the manna for the island's feasts. However, players can subvert the victory point race by vying to gain enough zealous worshipers to win the game outright.

from the designer's site:

Arrello is a new game, a cousin of Othello (also known as Reversi).

The game's name is a combination of the words "Arrow" and "Othello".  I chose this name because the relationship between the stone you place and the stones you flip is similar to the relationship between an Amazon and an Arrow in the classic abstract game Amazons.

Arrello is a 2-player game played with two-sided stones (green on one side, red on the other), on a 7x7 square grid.

Players take turns, one player playing the stones red side up, the other green side up onto an empty space.  If it is placed adjacent to a same-color stone, one can choose to flip a opponent's stone(s).

The game ends when the board is full.  The player with the majority of stones in their color wins, because of the odd number of space ties are not possible.

These ten cards expand The Resistance: Avalon for backers of its original Kickstarter project.

Excalibur: An additional mechanic that gives a player selected by the leader a strong ability every Quest.  But this great strength must be used wisely, so choose carefully whom to entrust with its power.

Lancelot: 2 additional character cards, and 7 supporting cards, that can be played in three different ways.
family: Theme: King Arthur / The Knights of the Round Table / Camelot

description:
Elvish archers are advancing through the forest, but you already have a line of loyal pikemen in place to hold them off while your mages drive the rebels out of the town. Suddenly word comes that your bowmen are falling back in disarray before the shadows rising out of the graveyard. With your reserves already stretched thin, you have only one chance to turn this around -- it's time to bring in the paladin.

Card Armies: Battle For Wesnoth is a strategy game. You control an army in the fantasy world of Wesnoth, and must combine planning, tactics, and luck to emerge victorious over your rivals. The game can be played as one-on-one battles, multi-player mayhem, cooperative campaigns, and even solo.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
The object is to "collect as many kittens" to gain more victory points.

12 cards are placed in the middle of the board and players in order to launch the eight dice to get the kittens.
Kittens can be eight different colors (red, green, blue, yellow, purple, orange, pink or multicolored). To get a card from a kitten from a color one should have two equal colors (example: two reds to win a red kitten), or two different colors (example: blue and yellow to make green a kitten).
Pink kittens are obtained with three colors (red, blue and yellow), and multicolor kitten obtained with four different colors.
Each kitten has its score and those who have not been obtained by any player are discarded to play another round with 12 kittens in the table. (rotative turns).

The game ends when all cards are played.

An expansion for The Great Battles of Alexander, this module is found in RBM/GMT's C3i Magazine Nr 1. It is the first C3i magazine scenario for Great Battles of History. As with other games in the series, it uses a combat system that emphasizes weapon/armor superiority and maneuverability over quantity.

According to designer Mark Herman, "Hoplite warfare is a rather stolid affair. In most battles it's a question of whether Phalanx "a" can outpush and outsnarl Phalanx "b". This, for the most part, is why we chose not to begin our Great Battles of History series with something like HOPLITE: Classical Greek Warfare. With only a few exceptions, the scenario pickings alternate between dreary and grim. Marathon, one of the pivotal battles in classical history, is one of those exceptions." Marathon features the first bloody meeting between the great Persian Empire and the emerging power of Athens. The Persian army utilized light infantry and light cavalry from across their cosmopolitan empire. The Athenians used the famed Greek hoplites.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet
family: Series: Great Battles of History (GMT Games)

description:
(from MMP website:)

ASL Journal 10 features six brand-new articles, the latest Q&A/errata, sixteen ASL scenarios printed on cardstock, and two cardstock player aids.
  
ASL Journal 10’s new format features separate, cardstock scenarios, rather than the traditional method of binding the scenarios in with the rest of the pages, with the magazine itself now printed on the same 80-pound gloss paper used for MMP's Special Ops magazine. As such, players will no longer have to photocopy the scenarios or remove them from the magazine prior to playing, and the new glossy paper for the 48-page magazine proper will significantly improve the brightness and clarity of articles therein. Using scenario card inserts also makes it easier to include other inserts, such as the player aids also provided in ASL Journal 10.
 ASL Journal 10 contents include the following:

     Scenario Replay of the Festung Budapest scenario FB17 Stalingrad Redux, with Phil Palmer, John Slotwinski, and Sean Deller;
     Spencer Armstrong’s When In The War? is a survey of date-dependent rules in table format;
     Robert Wolkey aims to ease newbie pain in The Beginner Blues;
     Bruno Nitrosso’s Close Combat Simulator guides you to a better approach in CC;
     Bret Hildebran’s Son of Squad Bleeder is a DYO-esque trio of scenarios;
     Aaron Cleavin explores low-odds FP attacks and Sniper tactics in Bullet In The Head;
     Debriefing, this issue’s errata updates;
     The Umpires’ View editorial, with various deep thoughts from MMP HQ;
     Frequently Asked Questions for Festung Budapest;
     The Festung Budapest Player Aid Chart; and
     16 ASL scenarios (printed on 8 x 11 inch cardstock):
        J147 Into The Grinding Mill (Chinese vs Japanese, 12 September, 1937)
        J148 Last Minute War (Hungarians vs Slovakians, 24 March 1939)
        J149 Taking A Stand At Rosario (Americans vs Japanese, 22 December 1941)
        J150 The Sangshak Redemption (British vs Japanese, 26 March 1944)
        J151 Squeeze Play (Japanese vs British, 25 May 1944)
        J152 Messenger Boys (German vs British, 2 August 1944)
        J153 Dawn’s Early Light (American vs German, 7 August 1944)
        J154 Cradle To Grave (American/FFI vs German, 1 September 1944)
        J155 It’s Hardly Fair (Americans vs Germans, 20 September 1944)
        J156 Mageret Mixer (Germans vs Americans, 19 December 1944)
        J157 Rage Against The Machine (Germans vs Russians, 21 December 1944)
        J158 It Don’t Come Easy (Americans vs Germans, 15 January 1945)
        J159 Tropic Lightning (Japanese vs Americans, 24 January 1945)
        J160 Bienen Burnout (Germans vs Canadians, 25 March 1945)
        VotG25 Urban Nightmare (Germans vs Russians, 28 September 1942)
        FB18 Red Banner Days (Germans/Hungarians vs Russians, 26 January 1945)
    


ASL Journal 10 scenarios use the following boards and overlays:

    Boards: 4, 10, 11, 12, 13, 18, 24, 35, 39, 40, 41, 42, 44, 46, 48, 49, 50, 54, 56, 64, 65, 2a, 3a, b, c, d, FB(NE), FB(NW), VotG, x, y
    Overlays: O1, O4, O5, OW1, Wd1, Wd2, Wd4, X11, X14, X15, X16, X18, X22, X27, X29

boardgame: BN1: A Board Game All About Brighton
description:
"Welcome to Brighton - a place where individuality is a competition, self- consciousness is an art form and nobody can hear the real world over the hum of cafetieres.  In short, the perfect setting for a day out."

You and up to four friends must travel the length and breadth of the city looking for interesting things to do and see. And adventure can be found in the most unlikely places.

From the South Downs to Black Rock,the day is yours to spend as you please. You could explore the obvious sights or uncover hidden corners. You could stalk local celebrities or stumble upon resident legends. You could go busking or shopping, gambling or picnicking. You could be mugged in broad daylight or handed a flyer for the best party ever.

But you won’t know till you dive in, so what are you waiting for?

HOW TO PLAY

Starting at Brighton train station, each player takes a coloured pawn and their first Destination card. This is where they're supposed to visit first to score the associated points, but there's nothing to say they can't just explore off the beaten track and find adventure that way as well. Either approach will score Anecdote Points, which are the currency and aim of the game - score 50 Anecdote Points and get back to Brighton station, and you've won.

Moving around the board either on foot or using public transport such as trains, buses and taxis, players can discover local characters, eccentrics, celebrities and city events - some of these are activated immediately, others act as subquests for the players to compete for. As well as these encounters, players can also go shopping for a variety of items that will help them or protect them from the dastardly tricks that'll be played on them through the course of the game via the Sneak cards deck players can accrue.

For instance, if you meet a local celebrity there's every chance another player will play a Lookalike card on you, so that celebrity was just a close likeness and therefore worth no points. If you trawl across the city to a destination miles away, a player may decide it's been closed for repairs. You might get a taxi only to be at the mercy of someone who doesn't know north from south, and end up streets away from where you intended.

Combining the skills of maze orientation, inventory and money management, player interaction and risk assessment, BN1 also relies on a healthy dose of luck. And with over 100 different encounters, 40 odd unique destinations, a brace of shop items and a couple of dozen different Sneak cards, it's an expansive adventure with a high replay value.


The first edition of BN1, released at the start of November 2012, was a limited run of 500 hand-numbered copies. It's available from shops around Brighton, and also the Bedsit Games website). It is now in its 3rd edition.

boardgame: Warhammer: Invasion – Glory of Days Past
description:
Game description from the publisher:

The races of the Old World all have rich histories and hold ancient customs and traditions that date back many millennia. And in a world of eternal war, these races each harbor ancient enmities. As the races of the Old World continue to seek new means of routing their old foes, Warhammer: Invasion - Glory of Days Past – the fourth Battle Pack in the "Eternal War" cycle – presents them with sixty new cards (three copies each of twenty individual cards) that explore their traditional strengths while developing strategies based around dynamic battlefield phases and the Ambush and Raider keywords.

As ceaseless battles deplete each army's resources, the Dwarfs rely upon the riches of their mines and the talents of a legendary runesmith to forge new defenses. Elite Greenskin Warriors and Cavalry surge forward in an endless tide of violence. Men of the Empire ready to meet them with both Spells and steel, though the forces of Chaos seek to corrupt every army’s mightiest heroes. High Elf Mages seek to protect themselves and their armies by harnessing the Eight Winds, but their dark kin, the Dark Elves, draw strength from their hatred and malice.
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: Warhammer Invasion – Living Card Game

description: This box set expands the number of units available in Malifaux by adding the Spirits of Vengeance crew. The set contains Kirai (leader), Ikiryo, Datsue-ba, Ouryo (X2), and Seishin.
This box set expands the number of units available for Malifaux by adding the Cult of December crew. This set contains Rasputina (leader), Ice Golem, and 3 Ice Gamlins.
This box set expands the number of units available for Malifaux by adding the Mercenaries crew. The set contains Viktoria (X2), Taelor, Bishop and Johan.

from the designer's website:

Two tribes are competing for territory by breeding.  Couples make babies.  My most thematic game :)

Population Pressure is a game for 2 players, played on a hexhex board.  I recommend Hexhex5 to start.

Rules

1. The board begins empty.
 2. To begin, one player places one white and one black stone on any vacant spaces on the board, and the other player decides whether to play as White or Black.
 3. Starting with Black, the players take turns.  You may place one stone  on any vacant space on each turn, until you have a group on the board of size 2 or larger.  After that, on your turn you may place as many stones on vacant spaces as you have groups of size 2 or larger on the board. 
 4. The game ends when the board is full, and the player with the largest group wins.   If the players’ largest groups are the same size, the player with the fewest stones on the board wins.

On larger boards, you might experiment with higher minimum group sizes (polyamory?)

Rally is a fast racing game where players try to be the first to reach the end of the Race bar and win the game. Players drive from place to place all over the country to get Race and Rally points which they can use to refine their cars. Along the way players sometimes need to draw Rally cards. Some are lucky and get extra points while others lose some instead.
Speed and excitement from start to finish!

The game is for 2-5 players, age 8 years and older.

A game for the whole family from 5 years and older. There are two games in the box, one on each side, Gift Race and Sledge Race. 
In Gift Race, players assist Santa to deliver x mas gifts. you follow a path, deliver gift tokens to houses ans try to be the first to reach the finish line. But sometimes you land  on a slide and move backwards or get shot forwards with a rocket. The first player to reach the finish line wins.
In Sledge race players follow a crocked path to try to be the first to reach the finish line. But which path will you follow?
The Xmas game take 10-30 minutes to play, for 2-4 players.

From the manufacturer:

It’s a bouncin’, snackin’ barrel of fun with the Snackin’ Safari game! Grab your elephant with his attached springy truck and go to home base, then start bouncing your elephant’s trunk on the snack cards to pick up a tasty snack! Once it’s caught, run back to home base and drop it off, then get back to the snackin’ as quick as you can! Make a pile of snacks, and if your pile is the biggest at the end, you win!

Snatch up snack cards with your elephant’s springy trunk! Whoever grabs the most snacks wins! 2 elephant heads come with handles and springy trunks. Bonus game: Find the golden tutti frutti cake snack card and win! Press on the trunk to lock the spring in place for storage. Stretch out the trunk to unlock the spring.

---

Action Game for the very young. Each player holds an Elefun head in a hand. The had has a spiral with which tiles on the table can be picked up. The tiles have different images on both sides,

There are three game ideas supplied:

- Both players have a starting tile. They pick up as many tiles as they can as quickly as they can and drop them on their starting tile. The player who finds a (face-down) golden muffin wins.
- Both players try to pick up as many tiles as possible, but without looking at the underside. The player with most pairs when all tiles have been picked up is the winner.
- Both players try to pick up the tiles in their playing color. The first player to collect all of them is the winner.

A magazine dedicated to Advanced Squad Leader. This issue has an article on gun duels, tournament review and preview, Tactical Tips and perspectives on four scenarios. This issue also marks the 100th scenario. Also included are four scenarios:

DB099 Gin Drinkers Line - After the fall of the Shing Mum Redoubt, the Japanese 38th division set about the task of clearing out the last of the Commonwealth forces from the 'Gin Drinkers Line' and assault Hong Kong proper.

DB100 The Gateway - As Operation Nordwind proceeded, Panzergrenadier Division 25 had its mission to break through the American defenses on the old Maginot Line.

DB101 A Willingness to Die - On day two of Operation Barbarossa, Kampfgruppe Raus of the 6th Panzer Division set off to take Rossienie, but was held up by the Russian 48th Rifle Division at Paislinus.

DB102 Les Hommes de Neige - With Narvik still beyond Allied reach, French Alpine troops would replace the British at Ankenes and make an overland assault to take the heights there.

Each player has a wooden sheep that must jump four hurdles first to win the game.
The hurdles are dice-sticks. The first player rolls the first stick. Whichever result is on top is the result the player has to fulfill by rolling five dice, setting aside dice and re-rolling up to two times. If successful, the player places his/her sheep in front of the next hurdle and the second player rolls the first hurdle, etc. The first player to jump the fourth hurdle is the winner (the hurdles have more difficult tasks the further one gets).

The monkeys need to cross a river full of dangers. The length of the playing field is determined at the players' discretion. A starting shore is set up. The active player rolls a die, then picks up the corresponding number of tiles from a stack and places them adjacent to the starting shore. The tiles have different effects, there are crocodiles or tree trunks which slow down the monkeys, other tiles speed them up. The first player to reach the other side of the river wins.

From the publisher website:

"Marauders, pirates, raiders, corsairs, call them what you will, this full colour Firestorm Armada Campaign Guide delivers to you page-after-page of thrilling information to take your space combat games to a new level."

Six new factions are introduced: The Syndacate, OmnyDine, The Rift Traders League, Supermax-89, the Corsairs, Pathogen X9-V.
Along them statistics for numerous new space vehicles, mercenary fleets are provided, as new in-game mechanics, a full campaign background and8 new tabletop scenarios.

Paperback, full color.

If you could fight anyone, who would you fight?

Fight Club: Project Mayhem is an unofficial, non-profit card game based on Fight Club (both the book, and the movie). Two players struggle to control the outcome of Project Mayhem tasks by controlling the members of the project (also known as space monkeys).

Jack and Tyler can use their actions to play cards (such as Homework, Cigarette Burns, House on Paper Street, or Ikea Nesting Instinct), recruit space monkeys, assign them to various tasks, or bid for control over the Jack/Tyler pawn. Whoever has the most space monkeys on a task when it is triggered collects victory points and the first one to have 10 points wins the game!

It's time to learn who's in control!

You can get the files here: Fight Club: Project Mayhem available!
or print the game on Artscow: If you don't like cutting your fingers - Fight Club available on Artscow!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Gardening season has begun, and you and your image-conscious neighbors are going to have a race to see who can mow their lawn the fastest.

In Giftzwerge, each player starts with an identical layout of lawn tiles. Your movement each turn depends on a die roll, with you needing to move the lawnmower exactly the number of spaces rolled; as your lawnmower token touches each tile, you "mow" the grass there and flip it over to its "short" side. Some tiles have special action symbols on the reserve side, possibly slowing you down when you run over a garden gnome (and need to stop and mend it) and possibly slowing down your neighbors (as when you send a mole in their direction to dig holes through the turf).

The Setting: Queen's Blade Universe expands to a land of endless summer.  A foe who calls himself a "devil of winter" appears, trying to put an end to the summer.  Prophecy says that the woman who wields the Queen's Blade is the only one who can defeat this devil.

クイーンズブレイド　グリムワール - Queen's Blade Grimoire is a licensed Series: Lost Worlds (Nova Game Designs) series, so it is compatible with all other Series: Lost Worlds (Nova Game Designs) books.
family: Series: Picture Book Games (Nova Game Designs)

description:
The goal is to get "a golden axe & knife".

Each player has five cards may have a knife or an axe, besides each of these will have a number between 3 and 10.
Players throw a card knowing that must be of equal or greater number of which is in the discard pile.
If a player can not play this move, will take all the cards from the pile and the player who threw before he will take as many bronze axes and knives as cards of them have had that pile.

The bronze axes and knives can be exchanged for the same silver (8 bronze axes = 1 silver axe / 10 bronze knives = 1 silver knife), and silver is exchanged for the same golden (2 silver axes = a golden axe / 3 silver knives = a golden knife).

A Disney related card game. The game-play is the same as Pairs (turn over two cards and if they match, claim the pair). This differs in one important way. Each pair has a points value printed on the card itself. It is the points scored by your pairs at the end of the game, not the number of pairs you have obtained. The points range from 1 to 5 mostly but one pair (Donald's face in close-up) scores 10 points.

Card game using representations of four continents as the suits, Europe, Asia, Africa and Europe.

The suits have 13 cards. Special cards rank higher, in this order. Map, Man, Woman, Child. These are called the Titles. A random card is selected and that suit are trumps, here called Stars.

It is for four players divided into two teams and is essentially Whist. Players must follow suit, can trump, etc. Every trick above 6 scores a point. There are also bonus points for Titles. After the deal before play begins, should your side have all the four Titles in the Stars suit, your team scores four extra points. If three of them you score 3 points. Two each per team scores nothing extra.

The game also includes rules for playing The Game of Monkey. Any number can play. Take out one card at random. The value of that card means that only three others are in play. Deal out all the cards that are left. In turn a player may discard any pair in his hand on to the table, calling out, say, "A pair of Maps" or "A pair of sixes" and so on. Then the next player throws out a pair. If a player does not have a pair, he draws a card from the hand of the player on his right. Eventually, using this mechanic, all the cards will be matched as pairs except for the Monkey, the card drawn at random at the start.

The player holding the Monkey at the end - quote "rejoices in the pleasure of being called the 'Monkey'. Curiously, it doesn't say if the 'Monkey' wins the game or loses it. If it is a 'pleasure' I suppose he wins.

In "More Peanuts!", each player will have a herd of elephants whose objective is to collect as many peanuts.

Each player will start at a different point on the board (already marked) and throwing dice you can move up to six different elephants to collect peanuts. In addition, the board has special spaces where players can advance more places, adding trappers on the board, or pull water from the lake.

The objective of the game is to collect the peanut with six elephants and keep them coming back to your starting point.

Historical card game, featuring episodes in the life of the Pilgrim Fathers on their arrival in the 'New World'. The cards are divided into 4 suits of 13 cards and numbered A through D.

All 52 cards are dealt completely out to any amount of players.  The player to the left of the dealer goes first and must begin by placing a card numbered either 1 or 13.  If the player cannot set a card of that number down then he or she receives a penalty point places a counter in the Pool) and play moves on to the next person.  This player can lay a 1 or 13 card down of any letter or may lay a 2 on a 1 or a 12 on a 13 on a matching letter, if the first person was able to set a card down.
