description: This promo card for The Expanse allows the user to change the position of Anomalies while moving.  This card was provided to Board Game Bash attendees at the premiere event.

Pop 'n' Spawn is a 2-player abstract strategy board game where players attempt to occupy territory with mushrooms. Once placed on the board, mushrooms have the capacity to spawn endless copies of themselves in different directions. Players struggle for supremacy by growing, defending and capturing mushrooms.

Players take alternating turns to play. On each turn a player places one mushroom of his colour on an empty square on the board. Before placing the mushroom, the player chooses the direction of the mushroom to be up, down, left or right; the direction is indicated by an arrow on the mushroom. After playing the mushroom the player's turn ends and his mushroom(s) will now spawn: each of his mushrooms on the board will make a duplicate of itself in the direction of the arrow on the mushroom.

Mushrooms that spawn towards each other will combine their directions allowing them to make copies of themselves in multiple directions. If a player's mushroom spawns towards his opponent's mushroom, it will attack and capture it, changing the colour to its own. However a capture can be prevented if the defending mushroom has a direction opposed to the attacking mushroom. After spawning the turn passes to the next player and so on.

The board is made up of grass squares which are worth 1 point as well as flower squares which are worth 3 points. A player earns the points on the square in which he has a mushroom. The game ends when there are no more empty squares on the board and the winner is the player with the most points.

Play online: http://www.popnspawn.com

Black Hole was invented some years ago as a result of the game designer's fascination with strange cosmic phenomena.

Black Hole is a strategy game, not hard to learn, but extremely hard to play well. It should appeal to anyone who enjoys spatial games and puzzles like the Rubik's Cube or strategic games like Chess.

The aim of the game is simple, capture as many stars as possible by creating black holes while preventing your opponents from doing the same. The game plays much like the pen and paper game boxes. Each turn the player draws one "star" token from the supplied cloth bag (Affectionately known as the "nebula" and places it into an empty region of "space" on the the board.

Once a certain threshold number of adjacent stars is reached the whole zone collapses into a black hole and the player who played the star captures all of the stars in the collapsed zone before playing another star. With more than two players the game becomes mind-bogglingly difficult to think more than one move ahead making the tactics almost impossible to work out.

The game ships with two play modes, a basic mode in which all stars score the same and a more complicated advanced game where players compete to not only capture the most stars but the highest scoring ones as well. The advanced game also introduces gravity charges that allow players to remove a black hole from the board if stuck.

All in all a great little time waster that will continue to be played for years to come.

A traditional roll the dice and move on game that incorporates the theme of safety in the workplace.
The game allows for you to jump ahead in the game by taking 'short cuts'. You may get in the lead quickly but there is a cost involved. You may get away with gambe, you may have an accident and have to put a plaster on you. Get 3 plasters and you are killed off.
The idea is to train people that where taking a short cut in processes may pay off in the short term, in the long term you are gambling with your life.
The game is Print & Play and involves a board, sets of cards and a rule sheet. You will also need a box of plasters and a simple timer if you want to keep the game to a specified time.
If you decide to incorporate this into your workplace you could use counters that have something to do with that business: As a can manufacturer we used mini aerosol cans.
An understanding of PPE would be helpful. (Personal Protective Equipment)

Legacy: Forbidden Machines is the first full expansion for Legacy: Gears of Time.

In the world of Legacy, the course of history is maintained by the operators of the Ancient Machine, fueled by mysterious forces. As an Antiquitect, the machine binds you to the present while you travel back through time to ensure the technological advancements of the world remain intact.

The dark energy fueling the Ancient Machine, called Vesper, is growing out of control, eroding the timeline and causing it to crumble faster than ever. Restoring balance is imperative; to do so, the Antiquitects must break their oath and create the Forbidden Machines – powerful contraptions they were sworn to never bring into existence!

Legacy: Forbidden Machines uses all the familiar rules and components from Legacy: Gears of Time and includes a new set of technology cards, many of them introducing the Activation mechanism. Players with the most influence over these technologies can activate them once during their turn to perform powerful game effects such as drawing extra cards, swapping influence cubes, gaining an additional turn, and many more! Several of the fundamental technologies introduce When Established game effects, giving a little boost to hatching your time travel plans when these technologies are established. Prepare for a whole new level of depth and strategy while you travel back in time to Build Your Legacy.

Legacy is a Eurostyle tabletop card game packed with strategic depth, tactical control, and meaningful choices. Each game is played in a fixed number of turns and features an interesting blend of familiar mechanisms such as hand-management, area-control, variable player order, and scaling victory point rewards.

Legacy: Forbidden Machines is not a stand-alone game, it requires the base game (Legacy: Gears of Time) to play.

This is a children's game designed to teach younger kids about safety. Players move along a pathway and answer simple questions that offer educational information. The board is divided into zones, and each zone has a matching card deck:

Neighborhood Safety
School Safety
Travel Safety
Street Safety
Home Safety
Shopping Safety
Internet Safety

There is also an "Interactive" deck which requires the child to perform a simple safety-related charade.

Sample Question: On a class trip to the museum, is it best to stay with your group?

Answer: Yes. Never go off on your own. Stay safe and stay with your class. Enjoy your visit.

Players can advance on the next turn with a correct answer. The first player to reach the finish line is declared the winner.

Includes a colorful board, cards, six tokens, a die, and a storage insert. The rules are printed on the box.

Designed for 2 to 6 children at least six years old.

A promotional expansion for Octopus' Garden.

Beauty Score 5. Add all 4 Crown of Thorns to the tile pool.  The Crown of Thorns is Planted as a normal tile, with the side showing the sandy background facing up.  During Move Sea Stars do one of the following:

1. Sandy background facing up: Move towards the nearest Coral using normal Sea Star movement rules.  If it ends its move on a Coral tile, turn the Crown of Thorns to the side showing a Coral background.

2. Coral background facing up: Discard the Coral tile and turn the Crown of Thorns back to a sandy background.

The Crown of Thorns does not eat Oysters but does affect the Feather Worm like a Sea Star.

A promotional expansion for Octopus' Garden.

Beauty Score --.  Add 1 Shark tile to the pool for each player.  The Shark may not be purchased.  Instead, when a Shark tile is put into the Marketplace, the Shark prevents players from purchasing its row and column and remains in the Marketplace for one whole round.  Players may each add a Pearl to the Shark tile to track how long it has been in the Marketplace.  There may never be more than one Shark tile in the Marketplace at a time- if a second Shark is drawn, the first is discarded and replaced with a random tile and the new Shark remains for a whole new round.

A promotional expansion for Octopus' Garden.

Beauty Score 2.  Add all Large Harlequin Shrimp to the tile pool.  The Large Harlequin Shrimp may be placed in any empty space or on any plain Oyster.  Sea Stars will not move onto any tile containing a Large Harlequin Shrimp.

A promotional expansion for Octopus' Garden.

Beauty Score 3.  Add all Boxer Crabs to the tile pool.  You may only Place the Boxer Crab if you already have at least one Anemone in your garden.  Place the Boxer Crab on top on an Anemone- the two tiles behave as one now, and may be moved to any adjacent empty space during the Move Sea Stars phase.  This duo blocks Sea Stars, just as Coral does.  If an Anemone tile is moved this way, a placed Clownfish remains in the Garden even if their initial placement conditions no longer exist.

A promotional expansion for Octopus' Garden.

Beauty Score --.  Put 1 Research Sub in the draw bag for each player.  When the Research Sub is drawn, do not place it in the Market.  Instead, have each player immediately take 1 Pearl from the supply for every different colour Coral in their Garden.  Discard the Research Sub and finish refilling the Market.

World at War: Counterattack - A scenario expansion pack for Blood and Bridges and Into the Breach.

World at War: Counterattack recreates the American and West German counterattack into the southern flank of the Soviet thrust into West Germany. The battles occurred just after the events chronicled in Blood and Bridges, and prior to the final battles for Paris.

Nine scenarios comprise the module. Four are stand alone, another three make up one campaign, and four more build the second campaign (two of the campaign scenarios can also be played as stand-alone battles). See below for additional details on the scenarios.

The module uses a similar campaign system as the one that we employed in Battles within Battles. Winning scenarios awards the player with refit points, these points can be used to repair damaged units, buy new units, or buy unique skills for your units and leaders The system rewards the gamer who conserves his forces. Win the scenario, but decimate your forces, and you’ll lose the war…. Or at least this small piece of it.
family: Series: World at War (Lock 'n Load Publishing)

description:
At 4 hours and 45 minutes of the 1st of September 1939 began the greatest of all wars ... was the beginning of the Second World War! During the following six years, nearly 100 million people lost their lives, Churchill, Stalin, Roosevelt, Hitler, Mussolini and Hirohito changed the world ... It is time to relive history commanding your armies in the most important battles of this unprecedented conflict! Welcome to WWII!

"WWII" is a board game for 2 players created by Julio Trois, which has the theme of the Second World War. During 10 rounds each army will win the largest number of cities...

The game has several maps of battles of WWII, being able to play a single battle or play the Campaign Mode with 19 maps!

"WWII" is a tactical game, where military strategy is very important. Each player must organize his troops, composed of tokens of infantry, tanks, artillery and bombers. The clashes follow the style of the best wargames using dices and tables, for the resolution of battles.

Each player has a press-o-matic tornado​​. 
They place them to the arena and both press the start button. The tornado that spins the longest wins and score points (indicated on the tornado). Who scored the most points after 5 duels wins.

BIOS: Origins is an expansion for Bios: Megafauna and Bios: Origins (Second Edition) / Origins: How We Became Human, which combines both games to make something distinct from either.

From the publisher: "Use the map to expand your Bios Megafauna game to cover the entire world (instead of just America).  Or use it to add luxuries (tobacco, silk, tourists, tea, diamonds, and much more) to your Origins game.  Or use it for a rollicking combined game, with flying and mole-like humans, able to domesticate new "sushi" aquaculture or ride killer whales into battle."

Promotional game by a producer of egg-noodles.

The enormous board features 8 starting points (one for each player and up to 8 players can play). From your starting point there is a track up the bell-tower, to reach the bells in the centre of the board.

The game is a simple roll and move game but with the slight originality of the separate tracks. However the tracks are all identical. When you land on the special spaces, follow the instructions (that are on the inside of the lid). First to reach the centre, wins.

"Estonian Adventure Trivia game"

Players roll the dice, move through the Estonia and answer the trivia questions or do surprise actions in certain places.

Game ends if someone reaches to finish. Player with the most correct answers is the winner.

This board game bring the players to fishing trip.

Players have cards in hand with different fishes and special cards. In his turn player either move his boat to different fishing place on the game board or change the fishing tool. Then player may play cards.
Different fishes need different places and tools to catch them and they score points.

Game ends if the card deck is exhausted and first player plays his last card. Players add up points from the cards.
Player with the most points is the winner.

boardgame: Östergötland in the 1930's (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
Östergötland in the 1930's is an unofficial expansion to Alan R Moon’s and Days of Wonder's Ticket to Ride series.  The map covers the Swedish county of Östergötland and can be played by 2-3 players.

The goal for this game is to collect points by claiming routes, completing destination tickets, and completing more tickets than the opponents.


NOTE: "Ticket to Ride" and "Days of Wonder" are registered trademarks of Days of Wonder, Inc. This fan-created expansion map is not a Days of Wonder product, but has been created with permission of Days of Wonder, Inc, solely for unpaid, non-commercial use. Some of the text and illustrations used herein may be Copyright © 2004-2012, Days of Wonder, Inc. All Rights Reserved.

Politocracy The Game is a presidential election campaign board game in which Conservative, Progressive and Centrist candidates compete in a race to occupy the White House. The game is a fun and educational political game for the family, friends, adversaries and Politocrats.

Players roll a die to move along the Campaign Trail, amassing popularity at each Constituent, depending upon Ideology, and whether the Candidate has sufficient funds to acquire the Constituents. At a Political Poll, Candidates can roll the die to access the 'Money Trail' – to collect funds - Deficit Dollars - from Interest Groups needed to acquire the support of Constituents.  The cost and benefit of each Constituent and the collection of Constituents owned or controlled by a Candidate are based on their Ideology.  Decisions must be made by the Candidates as to which Ideology they should chose or be to win: Conservative or Progressive.

Hope and Change can drastically affect a Candidate’s quest for the White House, either advancing or hindering. This is where things can get “dirty”.

Towards the end, an election is held where two final Candidates compete in each of the 10 U.S. Regions to secure the majority (270) Electoral Votes needed to claim the White House.  Winning a region may seem as simple as rolling the highest die.  However, elections (die rolls) can be influenced by money, advertising and more. Investing in both positive and negative ads is essential.

Mozaa is a tile laying game, consisting of 64 square, unique colored tiles.
There are 3 different rule sets included.
The base game lets each player put down tiles and score points for completed color fields. These fields are completed when all open edges of a area are closed with an adjacent tile. To close an edge you need a tile with a single triangular area to that edge. Sand clock shapes are not considered connected.
The first rules variant lets each player choose a color and only score points for completed fields in that color.
The second variant lets you claim uncompleted areas by placing a marker (not included) before they are completed and scored.
In each variant you have a different number of tiles in your hand, enhancing the strategical aspect of the game(s).

CodeX is a card game where you try to be the first player to complete your personal code. Every players' code consist of five colorcoded numbers. You must play your hand cards in to the table in right order to win.

At the start of the game, every player gets one of the ten code cards determine players personal goal. Then turns go clockwise until someone wins. On your turn you may play from zero to three cards in to your code and then you must discard two cards. Start of the next turn you fill up your hand to five cards. The game deck consist of three type of cards. 50 number cards which range from 1 to 5 in 5 colors. 15 wild cards in 5 colors. Wild cards are used to change color of your number cards. Then there is 5 lock cards to make some special moves.

From the package:
Dental care - healthy eating - an educational game

They players roll alternately a die and move their pawns (styled like a tooth) along the track on the board. The first player moving its pawn on the last field wins.
There are some arrows on special fields of the board that move the pawns forward or backwards. That depends on the picture drawn near to it (eating sweets moves you backwards, brushing your teeth moves you forward).

Manspel is a two player game with fairly simple rules. 
Each player starts with 12 pieces (men) arranged in opposite corners of the board. The object is to occupy the corner-field of the opponent with one of your own men. Pawns of the opponent are caught by jumping, like in checkers. Certain fields in the corners in the middle of the board may be jumped, but not occupied, excepting of course the one corner-field that must be conquered.

From the package: The funny animal catchers game

The players are animal catchers trying to catch as many of the animals as possible. 
The board is rectangle of 7x10 squares. The catchers and the animal parts (all animals consist of two parts) are cardboard tiles.
Every player moves his catcher and one part of an animal. If a catcher lands on a correctly assembled animal the player can take it.

Garden Dice: The Card Expansion mixes up the Garden Dice base game with 46 Action cards (with 23 unique images) and 8 Event cards. At the start of the game, each player receives a hand of three Action cards; a player can play one card per turn, drawing a replacement at the end of the turn. Action cards, as the name suggests, allow a player to perform one special action, while Event cards (which are shuffled into the deck) affect everyone in the game until a new Event card replaces it.

boardgame: Last Night on Earth 'Darkest Night' Scenario
description:
From Flying Frog's website:

Trapped and alone in the Radio Station atop Timber Peak, Samantha the DJ has broadcast for help as the Zombies close in. The only thing keeping them at bay is a thin net of arcing electrical wires she has set up surrounding the building, powered by a pair of old generators. Sputtering as their fuel runs low and the lights flicker, it is clearly only a matter of time before the generators give out. Her only hope is that someone will hear the sound of her voice before it's too late.

boardgame: Battle of the Sexes: Chocolate After Dinner Mint Edition
description:
Objective: To test your knowledge of the opposite sex through a series of gender based questions. The first player ro team to get their token across the board via the fastest route to their opponents start position wins the "I am the superior sex" badge and wins the remaining after dinner mints.

Contents:
24 After Dinner Mints (medals)
1 gameboard, 6 counters (3 male, 3 female)
1 "I am the superior sex" badge
60 game cards: 20x his, 20x hers, 10 female wild cards, 10 male wild cards.

boardgame: Lietuva (fan expansion for Ticket to Ride)
description:
Ticket to Ride in Lithuania. The expansion contains a map of Lithuania, destination ticket cards and a player aid card, which lists the number of points obtained by building routes of different length. The game is played according to Ticket to Ride: Switzerland rules and the destination ticket cards include country to country and city to country cards. The map also contains tunnels, however the designer explains that because Lithuania doesn't have mountains and therefore doesn't need tunnels, they are actually unsurfaced roads.


Ticket to Ride Lietuvoje. Šis papildymas susideda iš Lietuvos žemėlapio, bilietų kortelių ir pagalbos žaidėjams kortelės, kuri nurodo, kiek taškų gauna žaidėjas nutiesęs vienokio ar kitokio ilgio kelią. Žaidimas žaidžiamas pagal Ticket to Ride: Switzerland (Šveicarija) taisykles ir tarp bilietų kortelių yra tokių, kurios yra iš valstybės (Lietuvos kaimynės) į valstybę ir iš miesto į valstybę. Žemėlapyje taip pat yra tuneliai, tačiau kaip pažymi autorius, todėl kad Lietuvoje nėra kalnų ir tunelių nereikia, jie tiesiog gali būti suprantami kaip sudėtingesnio pravažumo ruožai.

Adomoc is an abstract strategy board game for two players. Each player has a "band" of fifteen playing pieces of which there are three types: a foot, a moon, and a sun. The game is played on a circular board with eighty-one spaces arranged in nine concentric cycles. The goal is to move one's prime sun from the outer edge of the board to the centre.

Adomoc games tend to involve a fair amount of motion. Players may conceptualize strategies that focus on organizing movement as opposed to blocking the opponent's movement. Because both players are aiming for the same goal and can in some situations use an opposing piece to aid a win, the game incorporates a collaborative ebb-and-flow.

Players alternate turns. Each of the three types of playing piece have a particular pattern in which it can move. Feet can move around the cycles or back and forth along columns from cycle-to-cycle. Moons can move in spiral patterns between cycles, and suns can move in any of the directions. The pieces can behave in three different ways. These behaviours are: originating (sending an opponent's or one's own piece back to an origin space), consuming (removing an opponent's piece from play), and attracting (holding a foot in place).

Adomoc was originally invented in 2001 and has evolved such that there are three variants to the official rules. Adomoc rules promote thinking beyond dual polarities. Its design reflects universal symbols that are grounding and expand beyond the concrete immediacy of common human experience. The game's rules and designs are available under both Creative Commons licences and the unique Adomoc Artisan Licence (AAL), which enables other people to produce their own Adomoc sets. Adomoc has a standard notation for moves, which enables it be played by correspondence. In 2026, an online version of the game was launched, enabling people to play each other for free (either correspondence or real time).

Bar Hopping is a roll-and-move drinking game which instructs players on alternatives to drinking and driving. Players (or teams of players) move their playing pieces (cars) around the board and perform actions on cards that they draw, depending on which space they land on. The first player (or team) to complete the predetermined number of kilometers is the winner.

From the game box:

"You and your friends are out bar hopping, when you suddenly realize that nobody can legally drive. So you all decide that the smartest thing to do would be to take a cab home. Great move, but here is where the twist in the game begins. While riding home in the cab, everyone falls asleep and starts to dream. These dreams turn into the worst nightmares of your life. Believe it or not, you actually start dreaming that you are driving home from the bar. Like most dreams, this one is just as crazy, as you will soon find out. When the dreams begin, so does the game. The only way to wake up and end these horrible nightmares is to win the game by being the first person, or team, to obtain the predetermined amount of kilometers set before starting the game."

More Convoluted takes the Convoluted series to the next level; even more ways to win! With new actions like Salvage and Recycle. Don’t have  the winning card? Try taking it from the discard pile. Players race to complete words that change so be  quick, only one can claim a word!  Too late? Try for a different word.  Wild cards replace any letter! But too many will hurt  your score. In a game where scoring matters. 10 points to win! More Convoluted: social, engaging, fun and fraught.

User Summary: Stig-Helmers sällskapsspel is a game with theme from the five swedish movies about Stig-Helmer Olsson. The game is a point to point movement game, but with a twist. With money ("Peseta") you can bribe yourself forward. And there is also cards (Händelsekort/"Filmisar") that can help you or make you overturn. First to the finish with a full itinerary wins.

boardgame: Core Worlds: Galactic Orders Promo Pack
description: The Core Worlds: Galactic Orders Promo Pack consists of 7 cards, including Buono (a new Core World) and reprints of the original 6 Core Worlds with added flavor text. The Promo Pack was available at Spiel 2012 and BGG.CON 2012, as well as with the pre-order for Core Worlds: Galactic Orders.

Each player gets one dragon boat in his color.

During his move, the player can use his dragon boat in addition to placing a boat. It can only used once during the game, the player gives away his dragon boat after using it. While playing the action dragon boat, the player can move one of his own boats to another free area anywhere on the game plan which need not be connected. The player only has to consider the laying rule that in any one market area only 3 boats can be placed. The move can be made either before or after placing a new boat as usual, also in addition to a movement card.

Both warehouse tiles will be mixed up together with the investment tiles during the preparation period. A warehouse can be obtained like the other investment tiles during the Investment action.

If a player obtains one of the warehouse tiles, he places it beside his gaming board visible for the other players. After a Resource action, resources can be stored in the warehouse. They can be sold at a later time. Storing resources is voluntary, i.e. a player can choose to sell his resources immediately after the Resource action. But he can also choose to store one or two resources in his warehouse, up to a total capacity of 2 resources (same or different). Resources in the warehouse can be sold later in addition to any other action of the player, either before or after that action. If a player has stored two resources, he decides whether to sell both or only one resource. If he sells both resources, he may sell them at different stock exchanges. Each player can only own one warehouse.

Two Mafiosi cards will be placed on two of the seven Key West decks already assembled. Both decks have to be placed aside, they are used in play rounds 6 and 7. In those rounds, during the bid phase, 7 instead of 6 cards are available. In round 8 again only 6 Key West cards can be used.

The player who obtains a Mafiosi card acts immediately in the following way: He removes a wood disk which indicates the ownership of a hotel, a factory or a plantation and gives it back to the owner. Instead he places one of his own wood disks. This replacement is free of charge.

Gemblo Mini uses the same gameplay as Gemblo, but on a smaller playing surface and with pieces for only two players.

In Gemblo Mini, players take turns placing pieces onto the game board; each piece placed cannot be adjacent to any other pieces of the same color but rather must be a distance of "1" from another piece of the same color, where 1 is the length of a side of one of the small hexagons. (This distance cannot cross an opponent's piece.)

The game ends once no one can place pieces on the board. Players then count the number of hexagonal units in the pieces they couldn't place, and the player with the lowest score wins.

From the box:
Shake'n Play is an acoustic memory game and consists of 16 nearly identical shakers arranged on an eight-sided board. The 16 shakers make up eight matching pairs, each pair with a distinct sound. The object of the game is to find the matching pairs by trying the sound of one shaker and another in turns.

Respawn is a action packed board game that blends a sci-fi setting with the action of PC shooters and some tactical elements of miniature games into a sizable slug fest. 
On each turn, a player gets 3 actions in which to make their moves/attacks, then it's on to the next player. During ones turn, you collect from dozens of items and weapons, blow up your buddy and score points. Be careful though, as the more points you score, the more points you are worth to others. 
The game features 6 different characters, 10 unique weapons, and a dozen items and gadgets. Maps can be made differently each game with 4 double sided map sheets, and contains rules and components for multiple game types.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
This is a "Mensch Ärgere Dich Nicht" typ of game. It is played with trucks (as pawns) and with the same rules but has one extra rule that gives it an extra twist.
If you land on specific fields you get one extra token (regenerating brake system). These tokens can be spent in future turns to go extra steps.

This game was given out by REXROTH (a Bosch Group member)

Attach, strech and above all don't snap !

The wheels indicate which elastic band the player must use, and which pillar it has to go round. The player may only use one hand to position the elastic band, while holding onto the board with the other hand.lf he fails, he receives a penalty point. Once a player has received ten penalty points, he is eliminated. The aim is ta be the last player left in the game!
More you play more it's tight!

Attack the Tower is a card game inspired by defend the tower videogames. There are a print & play version on the publishers website. Two players duel in a fast paced turn based battle. Each participant chooses an army, and must dispose of their heavy and light infantry, shooters, cavalry, siege weapons and special abilities to defeat the opponent.

There are 2 types of cards: bases and abilities. Bases must be played on a territory. Each player has 3 territories and each territory can have up to 3 bases. Troops are recruited on a base and a base can have up to 2 troops.

On his turn a player must decide to reload or not. If he does both players recruit a troop on each of his bases and draw 2 cards. The player can also attack on his turn. Attacks are always from a territory to another. All troops must participate on an attack.

Each troop has a power that counts both for attack and defense. Bases always have a power of 4. To destroy a base one must destroy all its troops first. The goal is to destroy the opponent's Palace that sits behind his territories and has a power of 10. The Palace can only be attacked if at least one of the territories has no bases on it.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Four steering wheels anchor the Brain Shift game, with one wheel designated for each player. In the center of the game sits the stick shift, surrounded by six color (and numbered) lights: (1) red; (2) green; (3) yellow; (4) blue; (5) orange; and (6) white. During a game, players will shift to each of these lights according to the prescribed pattern.

There are six games in all:
Stick Shift: Brain Shift calls out a player, followed by a color, the player must shift to that color.  Additional colors are added and the time to complete the shift decreases until only one player is left. Later on in the game, the timer in the game becomes so fast that the player is forced to 'shift' the color whilst it's being called out. Most notability, at later shift levels such as trying to get 14 shifts, the game will cheat the player at the very last shift. The last green on level 14 becomes impossible to shift to and will cause the player to fail. 
Memory Shift: Brain Shift calls out a player, plus a sequence of colors.  Players must shift to these colors or be eliminated.  Players are called in order.
Who Shifts It: Just like Memory Shift except random players are chosen each turn.
Ear Shift: A sound is associated to each light.  Brain Shift plays the sounds and players must shift to the appropriate color that is assigned to that sound to stay in the game.
Secret Shift: Brain Shift gives you one color, then you must guess the others in 60 seconds.  One player only.
Shift Around: Brain Shift provides a starting sequence.  Thereafter, each player adds one color after completing the previous sequence.

The game also has a hidden 'test mode'. To access the test mode, you must hold down the 'POWER' and 'PAUSE' buttons, remove battery cover, take out one battery 'not the top left or bottom left batteries' and then release the two buttons. You will hear a hidden 'Happy Birthday' music. If you shift green, you will hear most of the voices in the game. If you shift yellow, you'll hear the main menu music and if you shift blue, you'll hear most of the voices used in game and the gameplay background music. If you shift red you will hear a beep and if you shift white you'll hear the Happy Birthday music again. Don't shift white too many times or the game will shut off and exit test mode.

In 2018, Samtastic Games Studios successfully created a game called Mob Shift that remakes the Brain Shift toy for iOS and Android and it also works nicely on PC and Mac. Mob Shift version 2.0 can be downloaded from the iTunes Store and itch.lo for $1.99.

Windows, Mac Version Download link: https://samtastic.itch.io/mob-shift

iTunes Store link: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/mob-shift/id1361156814

What motivates you, Hero: Fame, Fortune, Friendship, or Forbidden Knowledge?

In Questor, the Warrior wields the Fiery Phoenix Cleaver, seeking only glory for slaying foul dungeon beasts. The Rogue wears the Cloak of the River Spirit, hiding in shadows to hunt priceless treasure and a quick escape. The Mage carries the Mystic Staff of the Winds, exploring with a thirst for knowledge alone. The Healer holds aloft the Sacred Emblem of Gaia, Goddess of Earth and Life, desiring nothing more than to help and inspire the others.

Which fearless adventurer are you? With unique powers and a unique quest and victory condition, only one will triumph in this 15-minute dungeon crawl for 2 to 4 players — unless you play with special cooperative or solo rules, in which case you can win alone or work together to all be victorious!

Roll a die to discover how the dark tunnels develop, explore the labyrinth of a dungeon crawling with monsters, and work (or manipulate the other players to help you with your personal objective) so that you can be the first to complete your individual goal and win.

Simple dice word game, published by Alba, a British radio and television manufacturer. The game comprises of four dice, kept in a leather wallet,

Any number can play. The four dice are thrown by each player in turn and the first player to throw the word 'ALBA', wins and takes the prize or kitty. Those are the rules although the publisher does say that "many variations can be thought up of this popular party game". For instance, a player throwing all four of the same letter can get a second place prize, and so on.

Dice game with elements of Shut the Box.

Each player lines up a set of cards in two rows:
6 7 8 9 10 
12 13 14 15 16
First player throws the numbered blocks (i.e. dice) and turns face down the card that corresponds to the sum of the top numbers of the blocks. If he should throw 11 on the first turn he merely misses turning over a card, but at all later turns he must turn up a card he has already turned down if he has any.
Play passes to the right. On the second and all other turns, if a player throws a number that he has already turned over, the first player to the right who still has the number may turn it over and have a turn throwing the blocks. 
Play continues to the right, it does not go back to those who lost a turn. If every player has turned over the number that is thrown, it is “dead" and the one who threw it may throw again until he throws a number that he or some other player can use, or until he throws an 11.
If a player throws 11, it is good strategy to turn up a number that is "dead" or one that has three chances to one to be thrown. For example, only 3 and 3 make 6, but 3 and 7, 4 and 6, or 5 and 5 make 10.

The first player to get all the cards turned over is winner and the first player next game. Four to six players recommended.

The game consists of 4 playing boards, 1 spinner and animal dice. The object of the game ia to be the first to fill Old MacDonald’s farm with animals.

Each player is glven a playing board and presses out the animal pieces. The youngest player spins the arrow on the spinner and if it points to Old MacOonald then he/she throws the dice snd fits the appropriate animal into his/her playing board. If the arrow points to the tractor or farm house then the play moves clockwise to tha next player in turn. The first player to collect all hls/hsr animals is the winner.

Young players may play without the spinner and just use the dice, collecting animals either randomly or in the sequence of the song.
Older players may extend the playing tlme of the game by using the spinner and collecting tha animals in the sequence of the song.

This game is similar to the same publisher's game Old MacDonald's Farm Game, but this game uses special dice and a spinner, not just a spinner.

Description from the publisher:

Britain’s favourite playground game as a board game!  Can the Runners make it safely across the playground avoiding the Bulldog?  The Teachers can help, but if the Runners get caught it’s “British Bulldog”!

Further details:

One player moves the Runners by rolling a number die and a direction die, whilst the other player moves the Bulldog a number of squares restricted by how many Runners have been caught to date.  If all the Runners make it safely across the playground without being caught that player is the winner; if the Bulldog catches all the Runners then that player is the winner.  Additional Teacher Squares offer protection for the Runners as the Bulldog cannot enter or pass through these squares.

The game introduces elements of both chess and draughts, and combines luck with strategy making it an easy game to learn but with additional depth for older players.

The Familiarisation Area is a small promo for Zooloretto that allows a place for players to place an obtained animal that doesn't fit legally into their zoo.

When taking a delivery truck, if there is no legal place for an animal in a player's zoo, he may place the animal into his familiarisation area.  The area may only hold one animal.

Players may also transfer an animal in or out of the area via the move or exchange remodel actions.

At the end of the game, no points are scored or lost for an animal in the familiarisation area.

Also available as part of the Zooloretto: Goodie-Box.

From the publisher's press release:

Rapid Response is the fast-paced, question and answer game that pits your basic knowledge up against a twisted timer! Families and friends will have a blast trying to multitask as the questions fly, the balls drop and the bell dings. Don’t get distracted by the “DING!” – as time quickly runs out! Quickly give your partner clues to guess the category on the card: “Cake…Ice Cream, Candles…Streamers“ Your partner shouts “Birthday Parties!” Right! Now grab a marble from the bottom trough and put it back into the funnel at the top of the slide to buy yourself more time.
 The object of the game is to quickly (and correctly) guess the phrases being described by your partner before all the marbles run down. Open-ended categories make it easy for all ages to get into the action – hints can be as complex or simple as you need them to be.

boardgame: Expedite: Limited Edition Word Trade Card Set
description:
Set of the Limited Edition, 54 World Trade Card Set. This was only available through Kickstarter and brings the total number of World Trade Cards to 108.
Doubling the number of routes available makes it much harder for seasoned players to predict what routes you are trying to complete based on purchasing of an individual city.

From the Rules:

"Beach Party Bing is a fast to learn and fun to play, and like most Coin Creature games all you need is dice and some pocket change.


      *  *


Object of the game - the first player to cover all 12 spots on their game card is the winner, with the losers giving the winner a Monster coin from the Coin Creature based game or a card front the expansion pack!"

Basically, this a dice version of the classic bingo game incorporated into the Coin Creature world of RAK Graphics.
Roll the dice and move around the world with the modern estonian travelers. Who finishes first is the winner.

A tile-on-tile laying word game for 2 or more players. The board consists of seven tiles, each with a two-by-two grid of spaces. In the early turns, these are laid out and letter tiles placed on them. Once they are all out, turns consist of moving a board tile and replacing the letters. Score more for longer words and multiple words. The game can be played for a predetermined number of rounds or to a points target.

Space race game. Object of the game is to move 4 satellites completely around the orbit in a clockwise direction and into their correct position in "Final stage" marked by corresponding colour.

Movement of satellites is by the roll of the dice with a 1 or 6 to start a satellite from inside its rocket to the blast off position to begin the orbit . The satellite then moves around the orbit by exact number of spaces indicated by the dice. If a player lands on his opponents satellite, he knocks his opponents satellite out of orbit sending it back to the start. Satellites may land on hazards which sends them back to their original position in the rocket.

The satellites are coloured marbles

Copyright 1958 by Quality - Craft Products

Distributed by Raoul Inmports Inc. Dallas Texas

Early space exploration to the moon

A simple spin and move game based very much on the Jules Verne From the Earth to the Moon novel. The instructions describe a group of men who form the Cannon Club with the objective of firing a projectile (air coach) from a cannon to the moon. The players travel inside the air coach to the moon and return safely to earth.
Two versions of this game exist. A large version (and possibly earlier) is titled "The Journey to the Moon". This differs from the smaller version by name and having cards that determine the number of spaces a player moves during his turn. The smaller version uses a spinner to direct movement. The larger version includes metal playing pieces that are in the form of both men and women. The smaller version most likely used markers
The playing board has the moon central and imaginary graphics of what space travel might be like in early 20th century. Players advance 35 spaces to reach the moon and another 35 to return to earth. The first player to reach the 70th space by direct number is the winner. Along the way, players landing on red numbers follow the rules to incur either a penalty or benefit.

Unknown German publisher but from the graphics and costumes on the playing pieces early 20th century.

Space exploration game from earth to the moon, where the object is to navigate 2 rocket ships from earth to the moon which keeps circling the earth. Three artificial satellites also circle the earth and rocket ships land on each of the moving bases before reaching their objective. First to reach both ships on the moon is the winner.

Moon and satellites are flat coloured disks. Rocket ships and similar to wooden golf tees.

Devised by  B. and A. Reid

Published by Geographia probably late 1950s

Simple spin and move game that advances through space. For two players, th objective is to see which Colonel Jet can get his armada of 3 space ships to the top of the world.

Interestingly, the playing pieces also can be worn as lapels with pins for hats, button holes, shirts etc. The tin spinner (windsock indicator) is secured into the board.

Produced by Silver Rich Corp. 32-36 Greenpoint Av, Long Island, N.Y. Dated 1952.

Space adventure, real estate game where players travel around the solar system to control planets, moons and rocket companies. The objective is to have the most property and money at the end of the game.

Large monopoly style space game board where each player moves his rocket ship around the outside of the board buying up planets or rocket stations. He then builds rocket fields on his planets and returns to earth to collect more money from the space bank. The game has a three dimensional feel with planets and moons as solid coloured balls on the central area of the playing board. Rocket fields are smaller orange balls place atop of the planets by coloured sticks. Space-o-gram cards are similar to chance cards in monopoly.

Tactical space war game similar to the boardgame Battleship where two players each with sets of grids (strato-maps) place their space ships upon the grid. Oposing players then give corodinates on the Strato-maps to locate and destroy their enemies spaceships.

The inside of the lid is used by one player while the inside box by the other. Colorful 1950s graphics.

.

Source: spielbox magazine (2015):

PUZZLE STRIKE is a card game played with cardboard chips rather than cards. It simulates a puzzle video game called 'Puzzle Fighter' that was based on fighting games like Street Fighter.

On this promo board are 3 additional types of Puzzlechips that can be added to the base game.

.

From the Sirlin Games website:

Five copies of each of three new chips, to expand Puzzle Strike's gameplay:

    Combinatorics assists your rushdown.
    Dashing Strike helps you control your pile size while filling your opponent's gem pile.
    Custom Combo is an explosion of 15 actions, enabling insane combos!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Tell Me a Story: Animal Village
description:
Join the animals in this village as they dance, sing, and play. Recurring characters, locations and props encourage linking the cards in endless combinations for fresh, new stories every time it’s played.

Variations for gameplay exist, but the basic elements that apply to all are: players are dealt an opening hand of cards. Cards consist of characters, objects, locations. Gameplay consists of some combination of trade/discard/draw from deck in order to complete one's collection with the required elements for the story. The first player to complete his or her story hand of cards, then "tell" the story - wins!

Naval Battle Group is a modern naval wargame designed to be played with naval miniatures.  
Game scale (both time and distance) is variable, and is determined prior to play, while time scale can be changed based on circumstances.  
Its rules tell how individual ships and aircraft move, detect enemy contacts, and attack them. Weapons inflict realistic effects, and proper naval tactics produce accurate and believable results.
Game-play is with dice, and no game boards are used - a protractor and tape-measure are used to measure distances and angles. A calculator is useful for determining distances based on the scale used.
Included are statistics for real-world surface, subsurface, and air units, allowing for any number of historical or what-if scenarios.

boardgame: Tell Me a Story: Fairytale Mix-ups
description:
Lizzy Rockwell’s charming fairy tale characters, locations, and iconic objects can be arranged and rearranged to create logical and/or hilarious tales. Kids will build critical thinking skills when they devise a way to incorporate a fire-breathing dragon and a flask of magic potion into their tale of a clever boot-wearing cat.

The deck of cards consist of cards of 3 categories: characters, settings, and objects. Instructions with the game offer a variety of gameplay options. One of these is that the players start with exactly 4 cards in their hand. In turn, they choose from the face-down stack. Keep one and discard one they do not want... play continues until someone has 2 characters, a setting and an object. This player is "winner" and for reward gets to tell a story using the cards in hand.

Object: Be the first with the correct combination of cards in hand.

boardgame: Tell Me a Story: Circus Adventures
Tell Me a Story: Mystery In the Forest
description:
Melissa Sweet has painted scenes and characters full of intrigue and suspense. Storytellers will find plenty of images to develop a mystery and solve it, too. Will they connect the squirrel eavesdropping on chipmunks with the squirrel hiding golden coins under his acorns? Which happened first? Recurring characters, locations and props encourage linking the cards in endless combinations for fresh, new stories every time it’s played.

There are variations involved in this card deck game. In each variant, however, the players must use a certain number and combination of cards to create a story. Gameplay proceeds a bit differently in each case, but one must build a hand with the appropriate card types and then be the first to create a story with them. The winner is the first to reach that goal.

boardgame: Tell Me a Story: Robot's Mission
description:
Robot characters in a Robot World engineered by Saxton Freymann line up to assemble a science fiction fantasy. Pick some automated animals and a space-aged skyline to start a futuristic story.

The included deck of 36 cards is dealt to the players so that each has six cards. Keep them or trade up to 2 from the remaining deck pile. The winner is first to tell a story with the cards in his or her hand.

Spin for a Hero, or something to ride,
A Rival, a Treasure (to find or to hide)

Choose a cave, or a castle with fortified towers
A friend and an object with magical powers

The object of the game is:
Get all of the elements first and you’ll get the glory,
Of winning (the hero, a ride, a rival and a treasure)

Then the winner has the privilege of using them all in a story!

Players spin to select the elements for their fairy tale. When they have collected a scene, a hero, a magical helper, a rival, a magical object, transportation, and a treasure, they tell the tale that these elements evoke. Game extensions encourage compassion in suggesting using a misunderstood rival as a hero on occasion, and longer tales for two players.

King of 20 is a new competitive board game that is simple yet challenging. It can be played by anyone who understands basic math operations.

In King of 20 players use number and operation tiles to form expressions which are placed on the game board. The goal of the game is to receive the lowest score possible by making expressions that are as close to the number 20 as possible. A player's score is determined by comparing the solution of their expression to the number 20. For example: an expression with a solution of 18 would have a score of 2. Players must use tiles already on the board and those in their hand to form or add to expressions.

Although King of 20 is an effective educational tool, players of all ages and skill-sets can enjoy it. Young children and adults alike will love the games competitiveness, simple gameplay and sleek design.

Ishmael King created King of 20 in March of 2011 when he was a freshman in high school. He noticed that subjects such as English and History were regularly adapted into board game forms, while the subject of Math was poorly represented in the industry. The few math board games that existed were too simple or too complicated to play, or were made for an age range that was too specific. So he took the opportunity to make a game himself.

boardgame: ASL Comp: Platoon Leader 3.0 – Campaign Game Rules
description:
Platoon Leader 3.0 CG rules for ASLComp with color cover and CEMETERY HILL campaign game and overlays.

Platoon Leader 3.0 is a system for playing tactical-level battles using a series of Scenarios referred to as a "campaign game" or CG. This campaign game lets players recreate, in detail, the fighting of various great battles. Players take up the part of both the overall commanders, choosing which units will reinforce the initial troops, as well as the local commanders, fighting it out for each objective of the campaign. Players use a 'purchase' method to select troops for the first Scenario for the campaign. They set up their troops, determine who will attack and defend in that action then play that Scenario. The end of the Scenario brings the players to the Reorganization Phase, where they resolve the location of the front lines, determine the fate of disorganized and damaged units, and then purchase new units for the next Scenario.

PL 3.0 aims to provide a concise, enjoyable campaign experience, playable by the average gamer over a couple of evenings. Critical Hit regularly releases new CG's for use with Platoon Leader. The latest version of PL is available at Critical Hit as a free download. This new version supersedes versions 1.0 and 2.0 and all errata. PL 3.0 remains compatible with all previously-released campaigns.

Players have four pegs in your base and you need get them to HOME. HOME is only place (and base) where your safe from other players from landing on your peg and knocking you back to base. To move; players pop the middle dome and move the number indicated on the die.

The game supports four players. Players can choose to be (the male characters from series) Red Ranger, Blue Ranger, Green Ranger or Gold Ranger. Players need to pop a 6 in order to move one of their four pegs from base to start and they get another turn. Players get another turn whenever a 6 is popped.

Players will spend about 15mins playing and having fun getting your Power Ranger pegs into HOME (safety from other players) and knocking other player's Power Ranger pegs back to their base!

Objective of the game: Be the first player to get all four of your Power Ranger pegs into HOME.

Seems like a themed version of Trouble

You go around the city several times with your pawn to get Aliens tokens which score most points of influence, so you can become the Master of Kuriosït.

Each player will have to move cunningly his pawn around the edge of the board to get Aliens tokens scoring most points, without neglecting the others, that can be used to make some special moves (see in front page, the special actions).

On every turn, the players will necessarily have to move their pawns from at least one space to be able to get an Alien token. If you think it is not interesting to take the token, you don’t have to. Because every time a token is taken, another one is going to move on to its space and if this last one is very interesting it is the player behind who may get it.

boardgame: Power Rangers Super Samurai Make A Match Memory Game
description:
Find the most matching pairs to win! The Power Rangers are scattered around as you try to find matching pairs to collect.

On your turn pick any two cards. If they are the same, you get to keep them. Find a pair of the special Take Red Ranger cards and you get to take the Red Ranger figure. Be the one who has it at the end of the game and you'll get bonus points.

Game objective: Be the player with the most points at the end of the game wins.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
A flicking game similar to PitchCar but with plastic components.

Plastic caps are flicked along a track composed of plastic  pieces. Two flicks are allowed per turn. If your cap leaves the track, it is put back in its previous position. The first player to complete the course three times wins the game.

Several variants are described in the rules. The number of flicks allowed can be changed as well as the number of rounds, bonus points can be assigned for being the first to reach certain points on the track etc.

The pieces can be put together differently to allow for differently-shaped tracks.

From publisher blurb:

Once a year, wizards from all over the realm come together to fight for the title of Archmage. The winner of this tournament is crowned the most powerful sorcerer in the entire world and becomes the head of the Council of High Mages. Wizard’s Tournament for Kids is a fantasy deck building game of magic combat where players fight to defeat each other using spells, monsters, and relics. The game comes with thirty three different sets of cards composed of six copies of that specific card.

During play, each player starts the game with nine copies of the Spirit card, which is his starting deck. Cards are purchased from the twelve active stacks of cards and placed in a player’s discard pile. Once a player’s deck runs out of cards, the player shuffles his discard pile together to make a new deck. The changing sets of cards means that every time you play Wizard’s Tournament for Kids, it is a brand new but balanced game. Spells cards allow a player to manipulate, heal, and damage monsters, your opponent, and yourself. Monsters are brutal attackers and valiant defenders that you can summon to your side during a battle to protect you from rampaging monsters and attack your opponent. Relics are ancient artifacts that give a player a special power that can be used each turn. Each player starts with thirty Life and if you reduce your opponent’s Life to zero, you win the game.

This Deck Building Game Includes:

     12 Monster Cards
     15 Spell Cards
     6 Relic Cards
     Spirit Cards

A simple dice game for children that incorporates drawing using a drawing template, and a rock-paper-scissors element.

In the basic variant, the active player rolls the five colour dice up to three times per turn. The highest number of identical colours is the number of spaces he/she may paint or cross out on the penguin's racing track on a sheet of the notepad. To make this easier, there is a drawing template that covers all five possible cases (1-5 spaces). The first player to have covered the complete track wins the game.

In the advanced variant, an ice pyramid has to be covered instead of a straight track. In addition, all players roll at least one die, even if it is not their turn: each player gets one die, the active player the remaining dice. The active player can try to win matching dice from other players by winning a rock-paper-scissors duel (which is re-themed as ice floe, water and rock). The first player to have covered his/her pyramid wins the game.

The drawing template also includes a penguin shape that the children can draw on their sheet and add further details, like hats or clothes as a drawing activity.

Exodus: General is an expansion which requires the base game Exodus: Proxima Centauri. The main component of the Generals expansion is the pack of 14 unique Ability cards, which add a special, once-per-game, special ability to each faction, adding an unknown factor to the game.

These abilities are designed to influence the normal course of the game while still preserving the balance.

The extra Centaurian Resistance cards are also part of the Generals expansion and they should be used together with the base game resistance cards. They provide more challenging Centaurian opponents and offer more important rewards.

Supplies is an extra Bonus Action card which was designed to be used together with the base game, Exodus: Proxima Centauri.

The Supplies bonus action allows every player to receive one free Upgrade of their choice, provided they have already researched the required technology.

Note that, as is, this card is not compatible with the Revised Edition of the base game (it is larger than the other cards and the card back is also different). However, this card was reprinted in 2017 with new artwork and matching card back as part of the Exodus: Event Horizon expansion.

boardgame: Die Lieben Sieben: Auf dem Jahrmarkt
description:
The game board is a sliding lane with six fields, each showing a fun fair attraction. At the end of the field, there are three wooden "tin cans" stacked up.
The active player draws a card and tries to slide the marble slider onto the corresponding field. If successful, s/he gets the card. For two of the same cards, the player gets a free ride chip. If the player flips up the tin can card, s/he has to try to topple the three tin cans to get a free ride; if unsuccessful, the other players can try (in clockwise order).
At the end of the game, the player with most chips is the winner.

The Golden Age is an expansion which requires the Wild Fun West base game. It is made of 30 Event cards and 1 Event info card which describes the rules of the expansion.

Every event card is written in the memory of an important event of the Old American West period between 1840 and 1890 and the effects on each card reflect in some way the historical significance of that event.

Although not a game changer, the Event cards bring another layer of planning and fun to Wild Fun West.

boardgame: Wild Fun West: Rustler promo card
description: The Rustles is a promo card for Wild Fun West and requires the base game. It features a special ability, every time it is used to steal money it must be passed on to the next player, thus more people will have the chance of using its unique ability.

boardgame: Wild Fun West: Chieftain promo card
description:
The Chieftain is a promo card for Wild Fun West and it requires the base game.
The Chieftain has a special ability allowing its owner to collect extra income if no building has been built during the current turn.

Simple roll and move game to the moon and back, with the objective to be first back

Graphics similar to Dan Dare type rockets and space vehicles and probably dated around mid 1950s

Published by Holdson products, New Zealand

boardgame: Angry Birds: Star Wars – Jenga Tatooine Battle Game
description:
This game one of the many games in the Angry Birds Series.

Take turns bombarding the porky kingpin in the sandblasted terrain of Tatoonie (back of box.)  "Roll, Launch, Win"

This is a new edition to the Jenga & Angry Birds line. The game comes with a Luke Skywalker bird, a Jabba the Hutt pig, a Rancor pig, a Millennium Falcon launcher, 12 plastic Jenga blocks, 2 dice, label sheet, and game guide. The object of the game is to launch birds and knock down pigs to score the most points.  One player is the builder, and the other player is the launcher.  You score 1000 points for each pig you knock completely off the structure.  In addition, you score a bonus 1000 points if you have any birds left unlaunched after you knock all the pigs off.  Take turns until you've played three rounds.  If you scored the highest, you win!

The game is based on the Star Wars Angry Bird app.  In addition, the game comes with a App code to unlock game content.

Hex Rampart is a variant of Rampart played on a hex hex board. Play starts with black. On his turn a player may either drop a stone of his color or remove a dead enemy group. Players may only legally drop stones adjacent to at least one of their stones already on the board. A group is considered dead if any stone within it has no adjacent empty cells. Removal of a group takes a whole turn. Removing own dead groups is strictly prohibited. Passing is prohibited unless a player has no legal move on his turn. The player to remove all enemy groups from the board is the winner.

This is a roll-and-move spanish trivia game where players must answer only environmental questions at specific spaces to get progress toward victory, like in Trivial Pursuit. Same gameplay with different and cheaper components.



Un juego español al estilo Trivial Pursuit, pero sólo con preguntas relacionadas con el medio ambiente. Misma mecánica de juego con componentes diferentes, aunque más baratos.

A race game of motorbike that you have to ride your team to victory obtaining more points with your team.

Each player has a team of three motorbikes (Yellow, purple, red and green).
The aim of the game is to move all your team to gain the highest score by summing the result of the arrival of the 3 motorbikes.
The movement of each motorbike is done by a wheel with an arrow in the middle, at the turn player spins the arrow on the wheel, that will indicate how many squares move the 3 motorbikes.
The tactic is dodging oil spills, and take the wake of the bike in front of you.

boardgame: Combat Commander: Battle Pack #6 – Sea Lion
description:
Combat Commander: Battle Pack #6 – Sea Lion is the sixth themed collection of scenarios for use with the Combat Commander series of games. The theme of this Battle Pack carries forward from BP #5 and presents a what if situation of a successful invasion of Great Britain.  The early and successful conclusion to the Battle of Britain allows the Luftwaffe to dominate the skies over southern England and the Channel Ports of Southampton and Portsmouth.   The actions represented in the battle pack cover the period of late September thru December 1940 and showcase the initial invasion, the Axis advance towards London, its eventual capture, the early entry of the United States into the war, and finally the British counter offensive, Operation Tremor, to drive the hated enemy from its shores.  CC-Sea Lion features ten new scenarios printed on card stock and ten new maps.

Players will find scenario special rules to cover such topics as:

• Airdrops (both Fallschirmjäger and supplies)
• Prison Breaks
• British Fascist uprisings
• Targeted Sniper Attacks
• Partisan attacks on German Rear Areas
• Alternative night rules for Opportunity Fire


The new maps depict specific locations in southern England, several of which the designer has personally visited.  The player will get to fight over critical areas such as Portsmouth, the Medway River, Trafalgar Square and Holloway Prison in London, and the Port of Dover.  Can you command the troops at your disposal to crush the British spirit and win the war for Germany or muster the remnants of the forces rescued from Dunkirk and the Home Guard to repel the invader and send him back across the Channel?  Operation Sea Lion is active and commences now.
