description:
Promotional Game for Neste Oil Estonia.

The players travel around a beautiful country called Estonia. When the fuel tank of the car gets empty, there
will be a short stop at some of Neste’s fuelling stations to refuel the car, and then you are able to continue
Your trip.

Each player has a die as a car and if it shows "6" the tank is full, "1"-spot means the tank is empty.

In his turn player choose one of the four cards aside the game board and
1) place the card to his route and travel to this city showed on the upper part of the card. With each move the die must be turned one spot fewer.
2) discard the card and refueling his car to add as many spots to his die showed on the lower part of the card.
Chosen card is always replaced.

If player reaches a town where is opponent´s car he may exchange any card from opponent´s route for the card from his route.

Game ends if player in his turn has 8/9/10 cards (depends on  the number of players) with different cities in his route or the deck is exhausted.
Players add up their points from the fuel pumps.
Winner is the player with the most points.

"EMPRESARIOS - corruptus in extremis" is a game of strategy and political negotiation in which each player assumes the profession/role of one of the wealthiest characters of the city. Votes, fraud, alliances, revenge, and management of funds make this game one of the most hilarius games ever. Players will have Millions of Pesos (M$) to negociate between the players and buy new buildings. The Player whom ends with more money in the personal bank account will win.

GAME-PLAY
         "Empresarios - corruptus in extremis" is played in turns, which for the purposes of the game represents 1 year of government. During each turn, players follow a sequence of the game that consists of eight specific phases (see Reference Dashboard). Each phase is resolved by all the players always starting with the Mayor, followed by the player to his left (clockwise), up to complete the turn.

       1) Each player must choose one of the twelve cards of profession/role. Each player must read the information and abilities of their respective profession card. 
       2) Each player receive the initial money and the initial cards that indicates the profession/role card. This will become part of your "pocket money". 
       3) At the end of each year (game time), each player may have up to six cards in the hand. In case of having more than six must immediately play or discard to reduce his hand to a maximum of six.
       4) The game begins with the Democratic Election of the Mayor. All players votes their candidates (with Votes cards) and the candidate who receives the most votes is elected Mayor. The Mayor may decide how to use the funds received by the Taxes of other players Buildings. 
       5) The game have two different types of cards: 81 "Buildings" and 99 "Situations". The combination and the diversity of each card makes the real fun and estrategic of "Empresarios". 
       6) The only safe money is the money that has been deposited in a Personal Bank Account, and the only way to get to the bank is making a "Bank Deposit" with some special Situation cards.
       7) The key of the game is the negotiation between the players to gain votes and cards. The Players whom ends with more money in the personal bank account will win. 
       The goal is to amass the greatest wealth in your Personal Bank Account.


EMPRESARIOS - corruptus in extremis® es un divertido y emocionante juego de estrategia y negociaciones para 3 a 8 jugadores. Cada uno encarna el papel de un adinerado empresario que deberá realizar diferentes construcciones de Edificios para poder reclamar las Ganancias que les brinden dichas inversiones. Además podrán realizar diferentes negocios ilegales que también aportarán Ganancias, pero que a diferencia de los negocios legales no pagarán impuestos al Alcalde de turno. 
El cargo de Alcalde de la ciudad será elegido por votación entre todos los empresarios mediante una Elección Democrática del Alcalde. Así, utilizando las respectivas influencias políticas de cada uno, quién obtenga el cargo pasará a tener muchos beneficios políticos y monetarios. Puesto que es una democracia, cada dos años de mandato deberá volver a realizarse una Elección Democrática del Alcalde pero sin la posibilidad de reelección. 
Además de las inversiones, la diversión del juego se centra en las diferentes Situaciones y Eventos que cada empresario podrá utilizar para frenar el avance monetario de los demás jugadores. 
Así, quién finalice con la mayor cantidad de dinero en su Cuenta Bancaria Personal será el ganador.

description:
QuickDraft is a fast tactical card game about drafting the best cards for your hand, maximizing your Victory Points and disrupting your opponents plans.

To reach this goal, the players have different card types at their disposal. 
There are coloured cards with different victory point values and quantities and there are white cards offering a wide selection of effects - ranging from improving or copying another card to stealing cards from another player and bonus victory points if specific conditions are met.

The game plays in three phases:

During the Drafting Phase the players are selecting a total of nine cards for their hand using a drafting mechanic like in "7 Wonders" or in "Magic: the Gathering" sealed tournaments.

During the Playing Phase the six of the nine cards collected during the first phase are played and the different card effects are resolved.

During the Counting Phase each player determines how many victory points he has using all the cards he has in front of him (+ eventual bonuses from cards on his hand).

The player with the most victory points wins the game.


            
        
    


Though QuickDraw is a multiplayer game, there are rules for a solo game included where a single player trys to beat 10 levels of increasing difficulty.


            
        
    


One deck of cards (60 playing cards) is enough to accomodate up to 5 players. If however, two decks of cards are combined, up to 10 players can play QuickDraw.


            
        
    


In January 2013 the second edition of this game was published by the Gamecrafter. The game now features a new card type - black cards, new card effects on the white cards and offers several new strategies by strengthening the coloured cards.
This new version also includes a Hold'em variant - essentially offering two different games in one small package.

From the publishers homepage:

The next big release for the year occurs with Catalyst’s publication of Shadowrun: Sprawl Gangers, a tactical miniatures game designed by Ross Watson and Randall N. Bills.

Sprawl Gangers is a competitive, skirmish-level miniatures game for 2 players, with everything needed to game right in the box. Players will take on the task of building gangs (Ancients, Halloweeners, First Nation, and so on) following specific point values of a scenario, and modifying the various miniatures based upon what new resources (weapons/gear/magic/tech) a player gained through previous games. Gangs won’t simply fight for the sake of fighting, but will actively building their turfs and resources. Among other things, this will allow for the hiring of the exact right shadowrunner when they need that ace in the hole. All this adds up to the experience of watching your gang grow and expand through exciting play across a swath of games.

Sprawl Gangers will be sculpted and manufactured in conjunction with CoolMiniOrNot, who have run three of the most successful miniatures game Kickstarter projects to date: Zombicide, Relic Knights, and Sedition Wars.

“We could not be more excited to be a part of this new facet of Shadowrun. When we initially started talking about the possibility of doing a Shadowrun miniatures game with Catalyst, I personally jumped for joy. We have some big fans of Shadowrun in the Cool Mini family; so you know Sprawl Gangers will be receiving some major blood, sweat and tears from our staff. Old and new Shadowrun fans alike would do well to expect great things from Sprawl Gangers,” said Kevin Clark, Director of Research and Development at Cool Mini Or Not.
family: Versions & Editions: Board Game Versions of Role-Playing Games

description:
The game 'Les 24 heures du Mans' is played on a board representing the famous race circuit in France.

The object of the game is to finish in first place (winner 'performance') or by having the most points (winner 'energy').

You advance on the board by throwing one or two dice. You get points or encounter some hazards on different boxes on the track.

You have got three possibilities to throw the dice:
- one die: no risk
- two dice: normal hazards
- two dice (speed up): double points but more possible hazards

From the box: "The world has been infiltrated with hackers who are taking over computers and out to steal any of your personal data if they can get their hands on it. The cyber defense agents are well armed with forensic investigation technicians and sworn to catch these cyberpunks. You decide, will you be a hacker and get away without a trace or will you be the agent that catches the hacker with all the stolen data."

Rid yourself of all the cards in your hand before every other player and obtain as few points as possible. Points are obtained by cards left in your hand after one player has placed all of his/her cards on the PLAYCARD pile. Each player scores penalty points for cards left in their hand. As soon as one player has reached 300 points the game is over and the person with the lowest score is the winner.

boardgame: Nations at War: White Star Rising – Airborne
description:
White Star Rising: Airborne 2nd Edition

White Star Rising: Airborne is an expansion to the popular Nations At War White Star Rising series. Featuring fourteen scenarios and a set of new map overlays, the expansion details British and American paratroopers fighting in Europe during 1944-45.

Included are two persistent campaigns: The first is a five scenario campaign featuring British Paratroopers at Arnhem. The second is a three scenario campaign detailing the battles fought by the Screaming Eagles 101st Airborne troopers on June 6th, 1944 at Normandy. Also included are six stand alone scenarios detailing paratrooper actions of the Americans, British and Germans.

It's a game of risk and reward as players must learn to conserve their forces. The more lives you spend during one scenario, the fewer theyíll have to start the next. However, the more objectives players complete earn them more points which they can use to outfit and re-fit their squads. Choose wisely commander.

Requires Nations At War: White Star Rising first or second edition to play.

Other Games in Series:

    Nations at War: Desert Heat
    Nations at War: Stalin's Triumph
    Nations at War: White Star Rising – Operation Cobra
family: Series: Nations at War (Lock 'n Load Publishing)

boardgame: Mighty Morphin Power Rangers Dart Target Game
description:
Power Rangers! Rita is up to her old tricks again. You need to grab your Power Gun and stop the Evil Space Aliens attacking Angel Grove!
Objective of the game: Using your Dart Gun with only 6 darts, you need to shoot the 4 Evil Space Alien Targets.

Game includes:
4 Targets (King Sphinx, Goldar, Squat and Putty)
6 Safety Darts
1 Dart Holder
1 Dart Gun

Line up your goals on any flat surface and you're ready to play Foooz, the table football (Soccer) in your pocket. Simply grip the static arm and use the other to quickly twist and flick the player into the right position to score a goal! For extra fun, use the trick and competition cards to compete in various challenges!

Ritter Rost: Eisenhart & Voll Verbeult is based on the "musical book"-series and upcoming animated movie about Ritter Rost, a knight built of rusty pieces of scrap. In the game, each player has his own "Ritter Rost" tableau to equip with arms, horse, and so on. These are auctioned from three card piles: one face-down and two face-up. The players then decide when their "Ritter Rost" is ready for dice-rolling adventures. There are three adventures he has to go through successfully in the following order:


     Joust
     Fight a dragon
     Attack the castle


On their turn, players decide whether to start an auction of one of the topmost open cards (paid with "sheet metal cards") or to send his "Ritter Rost" out to an adventure.

Auctions are held by bidding with face-down cards with values 1-8; these values are "circular", so a "1" beats an "8". Each card can be used only once and is discarded after the auction. When all cards are played, the discarded piles of each player is handed to the next player, since there are tie-breaking symbols on them.

To adventure, players equip their "Ritter Rost" with the items they gained via the auctions. Icons on these items tell how many dice the player uses trying to fulfill the adventure. To fulfill an adventure, the player reveals a matching adventure tile that tells him a number he has to reach by rolling the dice. The dice have only "positive" and "negative" sides. The player rolls all the dice he gets. All negative ones are laid aside, all positive ones are added and rolled again. Each consecutive roll is added (positive sides) or subtracted (negative sides) from the total until the number is reached, one die roll is entirely negative, or the player gives up.

Supplemental helper cards as well as cards that make adventures even harder add variety and more tactical elements to the game.

The first player who fulfills all three adventures wins the game.

A board game with mini games.
After you roll the dice you can walk in the school. If you finish at a door of a class room, you may enter that room and challenge another player for a mini game.

Inspired on the books and movie of Mees Kees.

Greetings devoted ones! Seven Gods of Fortune is a set collection card game in which players earn favors from the Seven Gods of ancient Japan by praying at a temple built each round. A set of four cards of a single God gains one favor, while a set of one of each of the Seven Gods gains two favors. The first to six completed favors, wins.

A good memory and keen eye can help predict the return of the Seven Gods as the deck recycles, while the Gods of Justice and Winds are on hand to mix things up. Some cards have Powers of the Gods and at times it may be more important to play than pray, or even complete a favor.

Specific cards are added for three player, four player and team games; and patience-style solo-play is supported. Optional rules are suggested to speed up and simplify the game considerably for younger gamers.

May fortune see you upon the path to enlightenment but remember, you'll need more than just good luck...
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
From the bottom of the box:
[quote]
DallasOpoly: A Custom Board Game
It’s the Community Adaptation of the Traditional Parlor Game. Players travel through Dallas buying, selling, and trading such Local Landmarks as Texas Stadium and Southfork Ranch on their way to Riches.
[/quote]
The traditional parlor game they refer to is Monopoly, with a few minor differences. The rules make no mention of auctions or trading. The rules specify that money is to be redistributed through the Southfork Ranch space, which corresponds to Free Parking. Dollar values for properties are ten times as high as in standard Monopoly ($500 to $4000), and the salary collected for each lap of the board is eleven times as high ($2200).

Player count is specified as 2 to 4, and four plastic Sorry-style pawns are included, but the game could accommodate more players, perhaps with movers borrowed from the Traditional Parlor Game.

Not to be confused with Dallas-opoly from Late for the Sky Production Company.

A monopoly (from Greek monos μόνος (alone or single) + polein πωλεῖν (to sell)) exists when a specific person or enterprise is the only supplier of a particular commodity.

Monopoly is a roll-and-move game where players move around the game board buying or trading properties, developing their properties with houses and hotels. When opponents land on players owned property, the owning player collects rent from the landing player. Owning sets of properties increases the rent paid to the player who owns them, by the player who lands on the square. The ultimate goal is to have the most money and drive opponents into bankruptcy.

This is a monopoly like game, sponsored by the Beech Grove United Methodist Youth Group.

Battleship Tactics is a customizable game in the style of battleship/salvo, in which players try to locate and sink a hidden enemy naval fleet.  Prior to the game, you build a custom fleet and a custom deck of cards from a large available pool.  The fleet is then deployed and the cards are used during the game to provide special attacks and other special powers.  There are also several pre-constructed fleets that demonstrate diverse strategic paths.

The components can be procured by combining 4 sets of commercial Battleship and/or printing out the necessary materials from the PnP files.  There are also templates for building the ships out of Lego bricks.

description:
objective:
be the first reaching target space (50)
simple 'Roll & Move' game

boardgame: Winnie the Pooh: Wo ist I-Aahs Schwänzchen?
description:
Eeyore has lost its tail, and Winnie the Pooh and his friends go looking for it. Each player has an Eeyore to which different tails can be attached. The game board is set up inside the box. Trees with various items which could be used for tails are inserted into holes in the board (without looking at which tree has which item). A card is flipped over, and all players now look for the tail that is depicted on that card. They do that by rolling and moving along the trees; at the end of their move, they pull out the tree and check if it is the right one. When finding Owl, the active player may secretly switch two trees; finding other characters from the book cause other effects. 
When all tails are found, players have to find Owl who has the original tail which is worth two other tails. 
The player with most tails collected is the winner.

The goal is to get to know his friends better, using situations from everyday life (or not!) and classified into 7 categories: the seven deadly sins!

Empathy is a game where you unmask the true face of your friends and where they are racking their brains to identify you! You score points when you guess what your friends would feel in a given situation. For example, in the following situation: "You do the tail and a celebrity passes you by apologizing with his smile".

You surely know how you would feel in this situation, but do you know how to react your best friend? And your nephew? And your parents? And your friends / girlfriends / friends / uncles / aunts / grandmothers / grandfathers / son / daughter / colleagues / relatives / acquaintances / ... ?

You enter more feedback, and more chances you have to win!

How to play:
1 - Draw a "sin" card and read aloud one of the crazy situations.
2 - From your 9 "emotion" cards, choose the one you secretly feel. Then, the other players try to find it by each providing  one of their nine "emotion" cards.
3 - Then reveal all the cards. All those who have found your emotion earn points on the "empathometer".

U.S. President is Easy-Fast-Fun game with pyramids.

With 3 sets of pyramids you will go through last 9 weeks of presidential election campaign. With more sets you can experience more weeks and longer game.


    At the beginning every player has a lead in some of swing states. (Deal cards)
    Than players invest their funds to ads and try to convince voters in swing states. (Place one pyramid per round.)
    Last 3 weeks of campaing are really hectic. Mood of voters changies quickly. Candidates have no time to monitor and respond to all attacks. (All players place their pyramids simultaneously.)
    On Election Day all votes are counted, and the winner becomes the new president. (Players count electors of swing states they secured.)


Elementary, my dear Watson, just secure absolute majority of electors.

To be a president or not to be, that is the question.

				
				
					Modified map and State cardsEleven key or swing states are marked on the modified map to send the total of 145 electors.
Each of the eleven key states has its State card, which indicates the number of electors in the State.


    Montana - 3
    Nevada - 6
    Iowa - 6
    Colorado - 9
    Wisconsin - 10
    Virginia - 13
    North Carolina - 15
    Michigan - 16
    Ohio - 18
    Pennsylvania - 20
    Florida - 29 (but be sure you have sufficient lead according to the rules)


Did you know that even winning in three biggest states does not mean a victory?

				
				
					Awards:US President is the winner of the Game of the year 2013 Competition (Czech fan-organized competition open for all games with pyramids).

				
				
					Summary:
    The game is a simulation of the U.S. presidential election. 
    The object of the game is to gain an absolute majority of the electors in key or swing states.
    There is a modified map of USA to play on and eleven State cards to play with. 
    You need at least three sets of pyramids to play the game.


The game was first published as a gift for all fans of pyramids in 2012.

This game is a fun, family board game based upon an outside game played on a neighborhood street by friends many years ago. Pictured on the board is this neighborhood where you move around hiding from and seeking your opponents among places like a house, mailbox, or a fence, to try and capture them or to avoid being captured. If another player lands on the same hiding space that you occupy, then you are captured and have to move to your capture box. If you move all the way around the neighborhood you can try to jump to the center and free all of your captured tokens! There are two ways to win this game, either be the first to capture all of your opponent’s tokens before they capture yours or be the first to jump all your tokens to the center. A very easy game to learn and also very addictive for younger players who it is designed for.

boardgame: Les voyages de Magellan aux Temps Modernes
description:
Players travel around the world in S210 Caravelle (Airplanes) buying airports.
Every time you land in a town you can buy the airport if you have the money for it and nobody else already bought it. You also have to draw a mission card that will most likely be to carry people or goods to another location. If you land on an airport owned by another player you have to pay him taxes that get higher the more airport of this color he owns. Landing on a red space make you draw a Luck Card, mostly extra cost to maintain your plane, pay taxes... You can take an insurance that will reduce all repairs and crash expenses by half. You also have to manage you fuel, if you run out of it, you'll loose your plane.
publishers: Jeux et Jouets de la Tour St Denis

description:
Roll and Move game on a snake formed line of 95 spaces.
Each player has 4 pawns. If you land on a space with your own color, you move again the same amount indicated by the die. If you are captured, you can avoid starting at the beginning by paying 1 coin to the other player. If you land on a grey space, you are entitled to change your pawn to an express pawn (by paying 1coin) which is allowed to advance the double amount. By landing on the last space, you receive 1 coin. By landing with the 4th pawn on the last space, you receive all coins of the stock and the game ends.  Player with the most coins wins.

boardgame: Jim Knopf und Lukas der Lokomotivführer
description: There is only one pawn to be moved by all players: Emma, the train. Each player has 6 colored tickets in his hand and may play several on his turn, if the train can be moved in the correct direction to the next destination (= next number on the board). The color is correct according to the position of the Gurumusch mountain, which can be changed when you reach a destination. The player reaching the last destination ends the game. The player who used the most tickets wins.

The board consists of a grid of 6x6 holes. In each hole is a colored ball. You must collect (according to version) all colors or 3 of the six. To collect, you press a construction in 3 adjacent holes (solo version: 4 holes) and you pick all the 3 (4) balls. If a hole is empty, you don't get a ball there of course.

Play dysFUNction and you’ll find the fun in your dysfunction…but more importantly, in everyone else’s.

Grab a Cart and claim your Baggage- in this game, the player with the most Baggage wins. dysFUNction is not only hilariously entertaining, it’s cheaper and less painful than therapy! Take a ride on the Emotional Rollercoaster, acknowledge the elephant in the room, drag your skeletons out of the closet, or simply wake the f*** up and get out of Denial. Share family stories and One-Up your friends as you fill your Cart with Baggage you’ve earned the hard way, from the people who supposedly love you the most.

You can’t choose your family, but you can use them to win this game!

dysFUNction is a unique, fun storytelling game that will have you laughing and competing with your friends over who has the most dysFUNctional family. Dig Deep cards prompt players to share funny family stories; Baggage is awarded for the quality and entertainment value of each story. More Baggage can be earned by One-Upping another player's story, or by interaction with the Family Fun cards (and other board squares). Some players may also end up stuck in Denial. The first player to fill their Baggage Cart (with 15 Bags) wins the game.

boardgame: Québec et l'Est du Canada (fan expansion to Ticket to Ride)
description:
The board for this Edition is based on a map of Québec, Eastern Canada and North Eastern USA.

As with other Ticket to Ride games, players are collecting sets of cards to claim routes necessary to complete destination tickets.

You should use all of the 45 cars available. At the beginning of the game, give 1 large (red) destination among the 10 and give 4 small (blue) destinations to each player.

There may be one or two miscalculations in small destinations. Plattsburgh / Greenville among others, shows 8 instead of 7.

Happy TTRing !! ;)

				
				
					=======Voici enfin un jeu basé sur le Québec, l'Est du Canada et une partie des États-Unis.

Comme les autres versions des Aventuriers du Rail, les joueurs cummulent le wagons et routes pour compléter les destinations choisies.

Un conseil, utilisez les 45 wagons disponibles. Au début de la partie, donnez 1 grandes (rouge) destination parmis les 10 et donnez 4 petites (bleue) destinations à chaque joueur.

Il se peut qu'il y ait une ou deux erreurs de calculs au niveau des petites destinations. Plattsburgh / Greenville entre autre, affiche 8 au lieu de 7.

Amusez-vous bien !! :)

Ascendance is a sci-fi game set in a grim future, where mankind has discovered a new resource: the Divine Power. Using this newfound power, they gained the ability to control and reshape everything around them. On earth lies the only way to reach Thuria, the place where this power lies. Several alien, or former human, races have come to earth to reach this place and claim the Divine Power for themselves.

The Ascendance - Call of Thuria board game is designed for 2-4 players of ages 12 and above. It is played with decks of cards which the players customize for themselves through the extra cards available on the game box, or future expansions. Players deploy Units, Structures and play cards from their hands, reserving Divine Abilities for when things get tough. The Game Board is used to play Events which harass or assist the players and also to help players choose Advantages, special situations that may turn the tide of battle.

The game is won via one of two ways. You can either destroy your opponent's Fortress by reducing its Hit Points to 0, or gather enough Divine Power to use your Ultimatum and completely annihilate your opponent.

GAMEPLAY
Once players have chosen the cards to use in their Decks, they must choose 5 Divine Abilities for the current match and then the game can begin. The game is separated into Phases, with each player completing their own Phase separately, unless another player intervenes. Players use Production Credits (usually called just Credits) to deploy Armies, play Commands from their hand and any other costs necessary. These Credits can be acquired by the Fortress, or other Structures. Players may also purchase Enhancements on their Structures or Units to make them better or change their use. Divine Power Points are also acquired every turn and the players use them to perform their Divine Abilities, powerful cards that may, if played at the right moment, judge the outcome of a battle.

Each turn, the chaotic landscape of Thuria or the monstrous Zordath pose a threat to the players. Events are drawn every turn and usually harass the players by forcing them to attack the Zordath or face a natural hazard. However, sometimes one can take advantage of these Events and gain the upper hand. Once in a while, an Artifact or a Power Source may surface on the world of Thuria. Claiming these may also grant a bonus.

Finally, each player at every turn chooses an Advantage on the Game Board. Choosing one locks it from the other players, so sometimes it may be preferrable that your opponent does not have a certain Advantage than you having one.

Phases are summarized below:
Event Phase - An Event is drawn and resolves.
Advantage Phase - Players choose their Advantage on the Game Board.
Draw Phase - Players draw Cards, gain Divine Power and pay any costs.
Support Phase - Armies are deployed and any purchases are made.
Assault Phase - Units are sent to deal damage to the opponent's Fortress.
Party game played in teams. Players describe words without talking (drawing, pantomime, sounds), the other players must guess them.

EN: Quiz4you - The World of Dinosaurs - Science4you

Discover the World of Dinosaurs with Quiz4you! Answer the questions in order to get the five parts of the Science4you Symbol! Find out what happens when it appears one of the special cards.
It's time for Fun and Learning with over 200 questions of Quiz4you! Learn all about the World of Dinosaurs and have fun with your friends and colleagues, at home or at school, with Science4you.

Rules: there are 52 cards full of questions to test your knowledge on various scientific areas. To play, shuffle the cards and put the deck on the table face down. The youngest player or who had the best score on the last test starts the game. The first player takes a card and chooses a question (out of the four on the card) for the player to his/her right. You cannot choose a question that has already been used previously!
If the answer is right, the player who answered correctly gains it; otherwise, put the card face down in the bottom of the deck. It is now the turn of whom aswered to withdraw a new card and give a question to the player to the right.
All the cards present one part (out of five) of the symbol of Sciencas4you: whoever gains all five symbols first wins the game! Professor 4U is the Joker and can replace any of the parts that you need to complete the symbol.
Attention! When you miss just one part of the symbol to win you have to scream 'SCIENCE' to warn other players that you are about to win. If you do not, you lose two cards that you have to put in the bottom of the deck!

_______________________________________________________________

PT: Quiz4you - Mundo dos Dinossauros - Science4you.

Descobre o Mundo dos Dinossauros com o Quiz4you! Responde às questões para conseguires as cinco partes do símbolo da Science4you! Descobre o que acontece quando aparece uma das cartas especiais.
São horas de Diversão e Aprendizagem com as mais de 200 perguntas do Quiz4you! Aprende tudo sobre o Mundo dos Dinossauros e diverte-te com os teus amigos e colegas, em casa ou na escola, com a Science4you.

Instruções: são 52 cartas de preguntas qua vão testar o teu conhecimento sobre as vária áreas cientificas. Para jogar, baralha as cartas e coloca no centro da mesa com a face das perguntas virada para baixo. Começa o jogador mais novo ou quem teve melhor nota no último teste. Quem começa  retira uma carta e faz uma ds perguntas a quem está à sua direita. Não vale repetir uma pregunta qua já tenha sido feira!

Se acertar fica com a carta, se errar a carta é colocada no fndo do baralho. É agora a vez de quem respondeur, retirar uma carta e fazer uma pregunta ao jogador à sua direita.

As cartas têm todas uma parte do símbolo da Sciencas4you: ganha quem conseguir juntar primeiro todas as parted do simbolo! O professor 4U é o Joker e substitui qualquer uma das partes que necessites para completar o simbolo.

Atenção! Quando te faltar apenas uma parte do símbolo para ganhar tens de gritas ‘SCIENCE’ para avisar os outros jogadores de que estás quase a ganhar. Se não o fizeres, perdes duas cartas que tens de colocar no fundo do baralho!

Chasing Rabbits, The Card Game is a trick-taking style card game where the object is to be the first player to 500 points when playing with 3 or more players or with 2 players the highest score after going through the deck once.

the game is played in rounds where the players play positive "Rabbits" or negative "Rabbit hole" point cards and multiplier "Hutch" cards into a communal hand, until a "Chasing Rabbits" card is played to claim the hand. Unless a "Jack Rabbit" trump card is played the player of the "Chasing rabbits" card wins the hand, and then calculates the score. Score is kept on notebook paper (not provided).

Chasing Rabbits is as much fun for adults as kids (ages 7+) and guaranteed to bring out the 'kid' in you.

The new special tiles for Peloponnes: 

     One is a new civilization shuffled along with the other civilizations.
     The other 2 Trade tiles are used together with the Goat Expansion (shuffled together with the other trade tiles).

"Auto Markt" - Four players enter as a car dealer and run a business with new and used cars by buying and selling. The purpose of the game:try to be the top business men by good cash management and related storage!
A Monopoly style board game. Roll the dice, move equal steps, take a card and collect or lose money.

La Aventura Fantistica (Spanish for "A Fantasy Adventure") is a Spanish game based on the Dungeons & Dragons cartoon consisting of 92 cards that are split into 4 decks of 23 cards. (The decks are Hank the Ranger's bow, Diana the Acrobat's javelin, Presto the Magician's hat and pouch, and Bobby the Barbarian's club and helmet with these images printed on the reverse of the cards.)

In each shuffled deck there are 10 Adventure cards marked with the letter A, 4 Dragon cards marked with the letter D, 4 Dungeon cards marked with the letter M ("mazmorass" is Spanish for dungeon), 2 Force cards marked with the letter F, 1 Bifurcation card marked with the letter B, 1 Exit card marked with the letter S ("salida" is Spanish for exit) and 1 Treasure card marked with the letter T. The aim of the game is to pick a card each turn and win by collecting 3 Dragon cards, 1 Exit card and 1 Treasure card.

Dragon cards can be collected by using a previously picked Force card against it, with the Force card then being discarded. If the player has no Force card then instead he can roll the dice; a star result means he has defeated the dragon and can keep the Dragon card, a result of 4 means a stalemate and he must discard the Dragon card but a result of 1 to 3 means he has been defeated and must reshuffle his entire deck (current deck, discarded cards and kept cards) and start again. Alternatively, the player can decide to not roll a dice, discard the Dragon card and in future he picks from a different deck; whether occupied by another player or not.

Picking a Dungeon card means that the player must miss a go and discard this card. Picking a Bifurcation card means that the player must pick from another deck for the next turn only and discard this card. Adventure cards do nothing and are discarded. Each deck has its own discard pile; there is not one central discard pile made up from different decks.

The playing field is a checkered board, 8x8 or 6x6(for learning or fast games). The pieces are double-sided discs with directionality. Pieces can capture each other chess-style, and be promoted from minions to masters.

Each piece shows arrows pointing at the direction it can move - up-down or left-right. Every time a piece moves, it rotates 90 degrees when it lands, changing its direction.

Basic pieces, called minions, must move to a square of a different color. So if they start on a black square, they must land on a white square. Promoted pieces, called masters, may land anywhere along their line. Pieces cannot jump each other, and they capture by landing on an enemy-occupied square.

Promotion occurs, when a minion piece lands on a corner space.

The game ends, when all minions are gone. Winner is the player with the most masters on the board.

Rolling Battle is an abstract wargame that uses two regular D6 Dice, two Poker Dice, and a simple map representing four different terrains. The dice are worth various values, with some counting as regular troops and others representing Captains and Majors.

The game is played over four turns. Each turn, the play roll all four dice and then split them into pair with one poker die and one regular die in each set. The poker die determines the unit type and the regular die determines the unit strength. The player will then secretly write down the unit and strength value in the terrain of his or her choice. At the end of the game (once each player has assigned a unit to each terrain) the units are revealed. Total strength values with any applicable modifiers are revealed and the player with the highest value wins the terrain. Each terrain area is worth a set number of victory points and the player who has amassed the most points is the winner.

The game if similar to Halma or Stern-Halma (AKA Chinese Checkers) but is played strictly by two players with a standard draughts set. Each player starts with pieces at a corner of the board, and the object is to move all one's pieces to thee other player's starting position before the oponent does the same. No captures are made but pieces can jump as in draughts.

How this fundamentally differs from similar games is the rectangular, rather than triangular, grid of the board and/or being a strictly two player game.

Its history is described in this file on BGG.

Hektik is a Pit clone for many players. There are 20 sets of 7 cards in the game. One set is used for every player involved. Each player is dealt seven cards. All players pick up their hand simultaneously and start trading over the table. A player can offer any number of the same symbol, but can only name the number, not the symbol. S/he then tries to find another player who offers the same number of cards. The first player to receive 7 of the same cards calls "Hektik" and is the winner.

Differences to "Pit": 
- 5-20 players
- Seven cards per player
- All symbols have the same value

Each player takes role of one adept at final exam at Dargor Academy of Magic. The task is to find all six magic schools and become familiar with their teachings. Whoever reaches it first, is invited to the Council of Mages.

However, because the magic realm Hexane literally lives and its parts are constantly moving back and forth, it is not so easy to find a single building. Before the game, all hexagonal pieces of the world are randomly placed into a single hexagonal unit. Each player gets three magic cards and three wizards he controls. Magic card can be played only if the player masters the school of the magic card. At the end if some player disagree with the partial result, all wizards are placed in the middle of the board to compete in the final match.

Every move is accompanied by tension because wizards could end up trapped in a swamp, from where he can get out by throwing sixes. The player can choose any piece that moves with a given direction.

Because it is a small world, there are many encounters between  individual wizards. Their rivalry triggers a fight at every meeting. Winner gets one card from the loser who must also return to the starting tile.

The game is based on a hungarian adventure novel with the same title. The players start from Katmandu, and finish at the secret monastery of Khangpa. During the game the players have to gather several items (including yaks and sherpas) which are needed to win the game.
In addition to the (special) dice the game has 200 quiz cards, and many fate cards with story elements from the novel.
The box of the game contains the original novel too.

"Crikey! Jerries Over Blighty!" is a print & play game set in the skies of the Second World War, and created by the designer as a Christmas present for his dad after seeing his collection of Spitfire Ale bottle caps.

The game is a dexterity game using bottle caps, where two players - one representing the RAF and the other the Luftwaffe - fight to determine the outcome of the Battle of Britain.

In order to win, the RAF player must destroy all of the Luftwaffe's bombers; the Luftwaffe player, on the other hand, wins the Battle of Britain by destroying all of the RAF airfields on the board and by also ensuring that no RAF planes remain on the board.

...

The files to print for the game  are in the Web Links section at the bottom of this page - the board is the file called "Print and Play Files at Google Docs", and the rules are under "Game rules at Google Docs".

To make the game, just print out the board at A2 size and laminate it (to help with flicking the pieces). All other components are designed to be easily provided by common household items, or by components scavenged from published games.

Objective:
To go round the board guessing clues and unveiling hidden objects (letters) in each one of the rooms. Each correct guess earns coins and the first player gaining 6 coins is the winner.
The game uses a turntable showing numbers in different windows of the board and a clue booklet referring to those numbers.

How to play:
Players go around the house on the throw of the dice. When stopping in any of the rooms a player may look at all the Key Cards in that room. If any one of those Key Cards matches the top Lock Card then the player can make his way to the Playroom and gain one coin.

Going into the playroom: To raid the room the player looks at the number shown at the window of the Playroom (ex.3). Another player then opens the clue booklet and reads the clue for that number (ex. ¨used to sleep´). The timer is set and the first player tries to find one of the letters he thinks form the right answer to the clue (ex. B-E-D). If he is right he gains another coin, and player continues to the next player.

Soccer , Playmobil style. The Game Instructions (a seperate rulebook from the assembly instructions) include rules for handling all normal soccer rules plus games modes such as Kick and Rush and One - Two.
The goal is to score more goals than the other player, and the rulebook allows for a pre-determined number of a set time limit (as in real soccer) to determine the winner.
The game plays well, with the pitch divided into light and dark green stripes. A team is allocated to each colour and when the ball lands in your colour, you can move your two players and kick the ball. No more than two passes area allowed before a shot at goal must be taken. Goalkeepers are controlled via a stick mechanism with a spring, that allows lateral movement but also sideways dives.
The kit includes 4 players (2 per side) with kicking action, 2 balls, and a 97cm x 67cm playing surface with surrounds and a scoreboard. The kicking action is surprisingly good, and by experimenting with positioning your player around the ball, different shot strengths and aerial shots are very possible.
All in all, Playmobil has done a great job converting soccer into a fast and fun game for the whole family.

From the box: "Help your pullet make its way to freedom! Be the first to arrive by steering clear of the henhouse!"

Players begin by building a path from the henhouse to the medals podium by putting together a simple puzzle. Then the race is on! Players take turns rolling the die and moving their chicken to the next color rolled. Landing on +2 eggs will move your chicken along faster, but watch out for -2 eggs and henhouses, which will slow you down! The first chicken to the end wins!

This game is language independent.

Conqueror (Erobreren) is a Norwegian family quiz  game based on history from antiquity to the present. It is published by Kunnskapsforlaget (Knowledge Publications) and is therefore focused on being both an educational, as well as entertaining experience.

There is a questions and tasks timeline - players position events
and people in the correct chronological order – spanning three levels:
Bronze: based on the school curriculum
Silver: based on high school curriculum
Gold: suitable for history buffs

Conqueror is a board game based on the six historical eras. Each player or team must collect 6 Conqueror cards from different historical eras. These cards you win by solving timeline puzzles.

Luck also plays a role - it is not always the most knowledgeable that wins this game.

Players can also gain Fate Cards that can lead to a player being thrown in the dungeon, or maybe get help from a Moon card - which acts as a wildcard. Players my draw a Sun Card to overcome curses, death and destruction.

The point of the game is that many people may know more history than they think! In this game you have the chance to deduce the correct answer.
You learn a lot along the way and you can certainly start at the Bronze level to build you up. At Gold level, you can safely call yourself a historian of rank.
The game is beautifully designed and has many fine images of famous people and historical events.
Answers are provided with additional information to provide context and add educational value to the playing experience.

"Go For Broke" is fast-paced dice game where you can risk it all to get rewards and win! Roll as long as you dare to claim first a 20, then a 50, then a 100 and finally a 200 point card. Pick up "insurance" on the way. But watch out - if you roll Go + For + Broke - you're busted and lose your gains in the current round! All players who earn 200 points face-off in an intense playoff round.

Will you claim victory or will you "Go For Broke?"

boardgame: Tanks of War: Third Reich Rising
description:
Tanks of War (TOW) is a fast playing 2-4-player WW2 deck building board game of strategy, skill and maneuver of massive tank battles on the Eastern Front designed to play in about an hour. Using new and innovative deck building and battle mechanics, TOW pits the Germans vs. the Soviet Union during the opening salvos of World War 2.  Each vehicle has been painstakingly  researched and then converted into game play terms to ensure maximum realism without being bogged down in game play minutia. When your fighting a T-34 with your Panzer 4, it will be based on historical technical info!

TOW is a unique combination of a board game, miniatures game (without the minis) and deck building game. The strategies are endless and the turns are tense with each shot making a difference!

TOW brings the intensity of WW2 tank battles into an easy-to-understand and play format which will delight historical buffs and casual gamers looking for deep strategy, explosive turns, and pitch battles.

GAME PLAY OVERVIEW

Players customize their own unique battle deck which includes cards that alter game play and vehicles such as mighty KV-1s, Panzers, and tank destroyers. Played over a massive game board, both sides must purchase the right action cards and vehicles to win.

COMPONENTS

Tanks of War comes with 2 decks that both the Germans and Soviet Union/Russians can customize, game board, tokens, dice and much more.

One massive 23 inch game board representing the plains of Russia
    2 Player Reference Cards
    Battle Dice including d4, d6, d8, and d10 dice
    Over 100 double sided full color tokens
    Over 200 double sided full color game cards with authentic photos representing various models of Panzers, T-34s, KV1s and many more. Also included are Force Cards which can alter game rules and give your tank commanders the edge they need on the battlefield
    20+ page full color rulebook with variants and historical scenarios that create endless re-playability
    10 Terrain Cards
    Note: components are being finalized and may change

DECK BUILDING

Unique to Tanks of War is the ability for players to customize their deck prior to the game. This allows gamers to field the right mix of vehicles and Force Cards to meet the challenges they’ll face on the battlefield!

PLAY TIME

Players can choose to play a quick 30 minute game or set up a mighty clash lasting upwards to 1.5 hours.

Triad is a two-phase abstract strategy game.

The game played on an 8x8 grid with the middle four spaces unavailable for play, leaving 60 playable spaces.  Of the 60 spaces, 40 are labeled "3" and the remaining 20 are labeled "6", values which only apply during Phase 2.  Each player or team has 30 pyramid pieces available to play.  Play consists of two phases with an option of playing only one phase for children.

In Phase 1, players alternate turns and attempt to make as many triads as possible, scoring three points for each turn in which one or more Triads are made.  A placement which results in multiple Triads being formed still only scores three points.  Triads consist of three playing pieces in a row in any direction - diagonally or orthogonally, including a stack.  Playing pieces may not be stacked more than three high.  Score is kept in real time via a scoring track and moving plastic marker along the edge of the board.

In Phase 2, Triads are removed.  If the removal of a Triad reveals numbers on the board, those numbers - three or six - are added to the player's score.  If a Triad stack is removed, the single revealed number is doubled.  If a player has a Triad available to him to remove, he must remove it, even though it's to his strategic disadvantage.  If he cannot remove one, the other player or team plays until he has an available move or both players have no Triads that can be removed.  Keeping track of number placement when laying Triads is strategically wise.

Once sky had no day. There was a Shadow that was sad, it could only play in small light of a lantern. The Shadow found a hole in the sky and decided to steal enough lanterns to fill it. It took many generations, and when it was done the shadow slept under its big light, dreaming dreams about how it would play. When it awoke, all of the light was gone! At the center of the hole in the sky, was a tiny shinning entity, with a great big mouth. The shadow named the tiny thing the sun and they played forever.

A showdown with the Sunstalker will test players ability to position their survivors and make coordinated attacks. Making use of their own shadows to turn the tides of the battle. Its rewards grant new long range options and an evasive burst damage armor set, that is low on actual armor levels.

boardgame: Österreich: Lernspiel für Jung und Alt
description:
A small trivia game containing 98 questions about Austria.

Each card contains a question on the front and an answer on the backside (which is not the answer to the question on the other side of that card).

The cards are shuffled and evenly distributed to all players. One player asks a question from one of his cards; now all other players try to find the right answer on the backside of their cards. If a player has the card with the right answer he gets one point and is the next player to ask a question.

The winner is the player with the most points. The game also contains rules for solitaire play.
family: Series: Lernspiele für Jung und Alt (Komet Verlag)

description:
Hansa Tradelines is a route and pattern building game in which players take the roles of merchant citizens of a city part of the old European Hansa league.

It allows many simple and complex play modalities, but in the basic one players have to build their routes and connect their cities to a single starting city which is placed in the middle of the table. In their turn, players have to decide wether to place a route-tile, which is taken from a common face-down tile pile, or a city-tile, which is taken from the players own limited city-tile reserve. Players have to decide the best position for each of their tiles so that they ensure their own connections and prevent other players from doing so in order to achieve victory, which is based on a pointing system related to the geometric connectivity of a hexagon.

A game of Monopoly that you can have custom-made to include places that are special to you and your loved one, romance-themed cards, and photos of you and your loved one on the game board, instructions, and even player pawns.

http://www.lovopoly.com

boardgame: Rolling Freight: Great Britain and India
description: Rolling Freight: Great Britain and India, an expansion for Rolling Freight, includes a double-sided game board that features maps of Great Britain and India and new rules for canals, ferries and caravans. Canals are links that exist at the start of the game, but which become owned by individual players when rail is built over them. Cargo can also be moved using ferries and caravans, which are permanent map placements that cost one dice to cross. These new maps also include rules for improvements that decrease in cost as the phases advance.

War Against Russia is a free Print and Play game covering the German assault against Russia. It is an introductory wargame designed to introduce people to wargames. The map covers the area between the Baltic Sea and the Black Sea. The game covers the time frame of May 1941 to May 1942 with each turn representing a month.

Each turn the players will move units, attack, and check supply. Supply lines are determined by counting hexes from roads, cities, and the edge of the board. (The number of hexes ranges from three to six, depending on the weather.)

Weather is determined each turn and can range from clear to muddy, snowy, hard frost, or subarctic. Each type of weather affects movement, and attacks.

Combat is determined by rolling on a Combat Results Table. Movement is determined by the unit movement value combined with the terrain effects.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Zpocalypse introduced the mechanics of Quick Play Rules with the “Day Zero” Scenario. Numerous scenarios within Zmergency are played using these mechanics and provide a shortened game play experience for those looking for some quick and dirty combat.

The Quick Play Scenario is a Combat Phase only game that ignores all other Phases. The map is expanded in a different manner than the Main Game Scenario. You start with just one tile (determined by the Scenario being played), and on each turn you may walk off of the board and place a new tile . In Zpocalypse, players roll a die to determine how many zombies spawn on that tile.
Zmergency Rules differ in that they utilize the deck of Encounter Cards.

Candy Land the Train Game is a game for very young children.

The object of the game is to collect a token from each of the train stations.  On their turn, players move the Candy Train to a station of their choice, and reveal a die under the train.  If the color matches the color of the station, they get to take a token from that station.

From Rules:

"Line'em up, stack'em, put'em in a triangle.  Any way you play'em, the goal is to "Bowl'em over."


    Each player has 2 shots to try to bowl over the Crazy pins.
    Score 1 point for every Pin you knock over, and 6 points for a complete strike.
    The winner is the player with the most points after 3 rounds."

From Rules:


    Roll a Cube - face side up determines who the Goalkeeper is.
    Each player takes 5 shots, rotate the eyes to keep score.
    Player with the higher score wins.
    Set up player targets to practice free kicks.
    Set up Referee target and try to knock down to practice accuracy.

boardgame: The Legend of Cheung Po Tsai
description:
During the Qing Dynasty, following the death of the infamous pirate Cheung Po Tsai, four factions compete to claim his treasure map by being the first to sail all the way around Cheung Chau island.

Each round, players must play cards matching the wind direction determined by the spinner, in order to make progress around the board. They can also play cards to shoot at opponents (they can return fire) to move them backwards along the track, steal cards from opponents' hands, swap places with an opponent, or just to move extra spaces or draw extra cards.

Each faction's character has some special abilities. The factions are fishermen, pirates, and the Qing and Portuguese navies. The first player to reach the last (24th) space on the board wins.

boardgame: The Association: A Deed Restricted Game
description:
Welcome to The Association!

Develop the neighborhood, build common property, increase membership, and take control of the community, molding it in your image.

Players act as board members of their own individual associations attempting to establish a master association.  Canvas the neighborhood with your volunteers to gain support for your initiatives and work hard to increase the satisfaction of the property owners.  The more members to an association mean the more money that can be collected and spent.

Work on your own or in a committee with your fellow board members to change the neighborhood for the better but be careful – only one player can be president of the master association.
Are you ready to play?

boardgame: Hooyah: Navy Seals Card Game – Mission – Hostage Rescue Promo Card
description: Promo card from Essen 2012 adding another mission (scenario)

New family quiz game about Denmark.
What all Danes should know about their country!
Questions in varying difficulty allows the entire family to play this game. More than 2400 questions in categories such as: The past, Culture and famous people, Nature and Science, Geography, Sport and common knowledge.


Morsom og lærerig familiequiz om Danmark.

Hvad bør alle dansker vide om deres land? Find ud af det i dette spændende spil - hvilken dansk politiker har en dyr smag og bærer ofte Gucci-tasker? Hvilken placering fik de danske badmintonherrer ved EM for hold i Amsterdam 2012? Hvad er navnet på restaurant Noma en forkortelse af? - og meget, meget mere.

Masser af nye spørgsmål, nyt lækkert design og sjov spilteknik sørger for mange underholdende og belærende stunder for hele familien.

Spillet indeholder 2.400 spørgsmål fordelt på seks forskellige kategorier: Før i tiden, Kultur og kendte, Natur og videnskab, Geografisk, Sportsligt og Almen viden.

Test din viden og lær mere i denne herlige spørgeleg om Danmark.

Gold seekers come over sea (by ship) and over land (by horse). With the gold they have found they can buy tools. These tools give you extras during the game and you will need them as well. The goal is to collect 6 points, the tools are worth 1 or 2 points.

In a game turn you roll the dice and walk/sail exact the amount on the board. There you can have special actions. These are: collection coins, collecting cards (you can use them during the game) or flipping over tiles (water becomes land, en land becomes water). Most of the tiles have on the reverse side special events that will happen. Some are good, like collecting gold, some are bad like a storm.

To buy tools you have to go to the shops, which are on the side of the game board. You have to walk/sail of the board to collect them.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Wylosuj damę trefl is a polish game wich is rather a joke than a real game but it has characteristics of a game. (e.g. there is a winner and loser)

The first player takes the traditional card game deck and shuffles it. The second player takes one card from the deck. If the chosen card is a Queen of Clubs the second player wins. If not, the first player wins.

Publisher's Description: 
An entertaining game of sorting items into the correct containers using lists. This is a great way to encourage your child to think quickly and improve organisational skills.

In this game 24 tiles are placed face-down and players are each given a list of items to find. Players on their turn look at a tile to see if it matches an item on their list, taking the tile if it does.

Zen Garden is a handmade, limited edition boardgame using 10mm miniatures to simulate a battle between two squads of Samurai in a snow-covered garden.

The board is a small zen garden upon which miniatures are moved with the help of movement sticks (to determine ranges and movement) and dice.

An activation system similar to the one found in other games by Ganesha Games (such as Song of Blades and Heroes) makes sure that the turn length and the number of actions performed by every model varies.

Each differently armed samurai has a card detailing the warrior's combat skill with various weapons and the amount of protection offered by his armor. The card is flipped when the samurai is wounded, showing reduced values on the back.

The game was designed by Massimo Moscarelli and sold exclusively online and at conventions. The rulebook is in Italian but an English translation is available as a free download on the ganesha games website.
family: Series: Song of Blades and Heroes

description:
Halo Conquest

A game of Conquest

By Eli Silver

In this game, you play as the legendary Master Chief as you attempt to complete missions of vital importance. The Covenant have once again attacked the UNSC, and you are needed to stop them.
    When you start a new game, you can pick any skulls you want. Like the Halo games, the skulls will make the mission harder. However, if you are using the scoring, they will also multiply your score. Choose a mission, receive your starting equipment, and then set out.
     During your turn, you will advance or retreat, then fire at  the enemy (assuming you have ammo). Afterwards, the enemy will fire back, (assuming the enemy is still alive). You'll have the option to take cover and reduce the likelihood of bieng hit, or stand in the open and get a better shot. You can toss Grenades, snipe the enemy, or bash them to death with a melee. You can also pick up weapons from dead enemies.
     When a zone is clear, you can advance to the next zone, and then the next, hoping to complete your mission.
     Good luck!

Simple luck-based game for the very young. The game consists of 6 dice cards, numbered 1-6, 72 luck cards and a die. The luck cards are distributed onto the dice cards. The active player rolls the die and picks a luck card from the corresponding stack, then follows the instructions (receive or give gold or pitch). If a player gives out gold or bad luck, s/he can choose the recipient. After two stacks have been emptied, the player with the highest postive difference between gold and pitch is the winner.
No individual designer is credited. The games was designed and published by a handicapped people's workshop in Germany and is based on one of Grimm's fairy tales, Mother Hulda.

From the back of the box:

Welcome, gallant knights!  Time to take part in the greatest tournament organized in honor of King Arthur and his queen, Guinevere.  No physical courage or high morals required.  Patience, cleverness, and cunning are all you need in these quick-witted, brain jousting games.  The object of the game:  to be the smartest and most cunning of the knights assembled at the Round Table.

Includes:

     156 cards divided into six puzzle categories:  Logic, Observation, Numbers, Riddles, Pictograms, and Move it, Solve It
     Book containing the rules of the game and the solutions to the puzzles
     Score book to fill in during the fiendish challenges of the tournament
     Sand timer


(This is a trivia/puzzle-solving game where the objective is to be the first to correctly answer puzzles of varying difficulty from 4 of the 6 available categories.)

Les mots à la bouche (The Words in the mouth) is a game that allows you to animate discussions throughout a meal shared with friends, family, colleagues or acquaintances. A different game is available for each stage of the evening, all with the theme of the kitchen. Your table and utensils are thus transformed into a giant game board!

The stakes? The losers do the dishes!

The box contains:


     A book of 120 pages to organize the perfect evening!
     All accessories for up to 12 guests:


in all, 236 cards representing nearly 500 topics or actions for the game

30 coaster-shaped cards for the apéritifs 
    60 canapé-shaped cards for the appetizers
    12 original place cards
    80 questions for the entrée
    12 twistor cards that will make the tongue twist
    30 coaster-shaped cards for the main course
    30 cards for dessert
    30 allspice action cards that will spice up your meal
    12 challenge cards for the digestifs

Dwarven Euchre is a fantasy-themed evolution of a popular trick-taking card game.

The game is normally played with four people -- two teams of partners sitting across the table from each other. Each round involves one set of partners bidding on a trump suit, cards are played around the table as the bidding team tries to win the required number of tricks. Between rounds, a battle map is updated with results. The goal is for one team to capture the center hex on the map. Depending on board position and hand results, partners may earn 'reinforcement' draws in future rounds to make their hands stronger.

Partners may discuss board position between rounds, but within a round they must anticipate other player actions. Some example decisions: "My partner has this trick so far - should I play trump or save it for later?" and "My opponent called trump - should I draw some of it out?"

Differences between Dwarven and traditional Euchre include fantasy-themed cards, the battle map, sorcerer wildcards, and reinforcement draws. The rules contain example play and a two-player version.

The date is December 8, 1987. Ronald Reagan and Mikhail Gorbachev are set to sign the Intermediate-Range Nuclear Forces Treaty. The Cold War is fast thawing and only one organization can prevent it: COBRA. These terrorists have launched a massed attack aimed at crippling both the USA and USSR and making sure the Cold War continues. To achieve this, they have attacked Washington D.C. and captured Reagan. Gobachev would have been captured, but G. I. Joe, having received a last minute warning, has deployed. Now in the streets of America’s capital the Joes must save the day.

G.I. Joe: Battle for DC combines aspects of Battle Line and Batman: Poker gegen Joker. Players battle over locations in Washington DC by laying down cards representing various characters from the G.I. Joe universe. Each has a numerical rating from one to 12 and a color suit, allowing for card combinations that will aid you in the battle for DC. In addition, the Joes must protect Gorbachev and rescue Reagan. There are also special cards which can alter the battle and a naval control aspect that allows players to draw an extra card if they control the Potomac River.

The owl game was one of the most popular games during Mozart's time in Salzburg. It is a game of pure luck, with three dice for two or more players.
Each player receives a decided number of chips (e.g. twelve) and puts half of them in the center of the board. The first player then throws the dice. The combination of the three values of the dice can be found on the board and the instructions written underneath each combination must be followed. If, for example, the field says "bezahlt" (or, in the mediaevel version, "bezalt", meaning: paid), the player has to pay the specified number of chips into the center of the board. But, if it says "nimmt" (or, in the mediaevel version, "nimbt" meaning: take), the player takes the specified number of chips fromt he center of the board. If a player cannot pay sufficient quantities of chips, he places the remaining chips from his/her stash into the center and drops out of this round of the game.

The game lasts until only one player is left with chips, or until any one player has thrown three sixes. In this lucky case, she/he gets all the chips fromt he center of the board and is thus the big winner of the round. Then, a new round can be started.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Part of the Dungeon Heroes Kickstarter campaign, the Dungeon Heroes: Expansion Pack contains two modules for the base game.

The Dragon & The Damsel, which features an exciting classic tale of rescuing a damsel in distress from an all powerful dragon. If you rescue the damsel, she joins the party as a 5th Hero and lends her aid to your quest for treasure. Be careful though, for while you are searching for her you may encounter the toughest monster yet, the Dragon!

Lords of the Undead, which features the ghostly story of a lady long forgotten and a necromancer dabbling in dark magic. Amongst this angst, the Heroes come across an underground marketplace offering its help.

boardgame: Fifty Shades of Grey: Red Room Expansion Pack
description:
Add some extra spice to your Fifty Shades of Grey party game with the expansion pack that promises to raise the stakes in this outrageously fun party game, and will have everyone talking long after the game has finished.

The games contains:
- 200 Red Room Questions
- 1 die
- 1 rulebook

Travel World is a board game, in which players visit major cities and countries around the world.  Players select different reasons for travelling - business, pleasure, adventure, or education, and, leaving from Tokyo, check in at 10 places that correspond to their reasons for travelling.  The first player to call in at all such places, and return to Tokyo, wins.  Players of all ages can enjoy this game of world travel.

This is a spin and move game in which players have to visit each city in their color and return to the start space.  Players can choose to travel in many directions around the world but have to follow predetermined paths.

Travelling from North America to Europe can be done via two "lay-over" spaces in which the player must stop and spend a turn.

Katartis is a wargame that simulates a clash between two opposing sides, each representing commanders, troops and war machines of the armies of the Fighting Races who populate the world of Katartis.
One or more players take command of each force, determine the composition, equipment and strategy, and guide the tactical maneuvers on the ground, rolling the dice to simulate the fighting and resolve all of the events that may take place on a battlefield.

This game one of the many games in the Angry Birds Series.

In a similar format to previous Angry Birds Games (Knock on Wood, On Thin Ice, Space)  This angry birds game has the player building structures specified on cards to knock down using plastic birds launched with a catapult.  This version features a holiday theme with snow and ice building pieces and Santa hats.

The Drowning Soldiers is a game for 2 players. Its mechanics evolve around a player-determined, constantly changing battlefield shape & size. The objective of the game is to trap & drown 3 of your opponent's pieces (soldiers).

The pieces (which we call 'soldiers') consist of 12 numbered checkers. Odd numbered pieces are in red and even numbered in blue. The numbers indicate their turn order in placement & movement phase, both of which follows the ascending order of the numbers on the soldiers.

1st phase: placement

Soldiers are being placed on the blocks under the said order. With the only restriction that no soldier can be placed adjacent to another one that was already on the board.

2nd phase: move & drown

A soldier on turn can either move to an adjacent block, move any empty adjacent block to a different empty tile on the board, or perform a card action.

There are 3 types of cards in game. Each player has 2 of each type to begin with and can recover a used card upon drowning an opponent soldier. The 3 cards are:

Bomb card: destroys a block adjacent to the soldier on turn.
Swap card: swaps the soldier on turn with an adjacent friendly soldier.
Barricade card: creates a barricade block on an empty tile of the board adjacent to the soldier on turn. Barricades are neither movable, destroyable, or mountable.

Drowning: A soldier is drowned when it is his own turn to move but there is no move available other than to use a card. The drowned soldier will then be removed from the board along with the block it is standing on.
Golf game where you actually swing a tiny club and push a "ball" across 18 holes. Select a club, calculate the distance required, the direction you want to hit, and let it go.

Card game presumably made as a promotional game for Royal Ahold, a huge conglomerate in the food retail sector and made to celebrate the new Millennium.

There are 48 cards, divided into 12 sets of four cards. A number of games using the pack are described.

First game: Happy Families: Despite title this is a regular Quartet game. Collect sets and the one with the most when all the cards are collected, wins.
Second game: Codebreaker. Problem setter chooses a code, consisting of a regularly recurring pattern of cards; Example: Red, Green, Green, Blue - Red, Green, Green, Blue, and so on. He takes cards that match this pattern and reveals the first card. If no-one guesses the code, he reveals a second one, and so on until someone guesses the code first and wins.
Third game: Pairs. Traditional memory game but as there are no duplicate cards in the pack, a player must identify pairs of cards within the same set.
Fourth game: Card Race. For two players. A frenetic game of matching cards. Three cards are dealt face up and one face down. The 44 other cards are shared ouzt between both players. At the word "Go", both players start revealing cards with the objective of being able to lay a card on one of the revealed cards. A card that 'goes' must be the same colour or the same letter (A B C or D) or the same geographical name. First to use all their cards on to the three stacks, wins.
Fifth game: Beggar-My-Neighbour. The traditional British game.
Sixth game: Fortune-Telling. A game and not a Tarot-like prediction system. In their turn, a player turns over a card. The cards are ranked from red A to D, green A to D and blue A to D. The player must 'predict' the next card he turns over as being higher or lower ranked than the revealed card. If he is right, he can try again. If he is wrong, his turn ends and the next player tries. If the card revealed is identically ranked, that ends the player's turn but he does not have to miss a turn. First to turn over all his cards, wins.
Seventh game: Tops. This is exactly the same mechanism as traditional Top Trumps.
Eight game: Patience. A solo game. Cards are ranked as in the Fortune-Telling, described above. As in traditional Patience games you win if you can sort the cards in order.

This is a card game themed football for 02 players of all ages.
The game consists of 09 different types of cards:
• 06 displacement cards (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6)
• 01 card (SHOOT)
• 01 card (STOP)
• 01 card (TACKLE)
The playing field is composed of 06 parts, numbered 01 to 06. At the beginning of the game a player is on the box 03 or 04..
1. each player receive 05 cards.
2. it can only have maximum 05 cards in hand.
3. After playing, he draws a card from the pile.
4. he can choose not to play.
5. it has only 05 cards in hand, he may draw a card and discard one of his cards.

MOVEMENT
• The player can move the field one square forward playing the card number with displacement greater (or less for the other player) in which it is located.
      
• The player can move several boxes playing a series (ascending or descending) of 03, 04 or 05 cards (2/3, 2/3/4, 2/3/4/5), including one on which it is located.

SHOOT
The player may draw a card playing SHOOT.
It is far more opposing nets (box 01 or 06) the longer it will play a large number of TIR cards at a time.
Ex: ascending player
        Case 06 ----> 01carte SHOOT
        Case 04 ----> 03carte SHOOT

STOPPING THE SHOOT
Any SHOOT whatever the number of cards used can be countered with a single card STOP

TACKLE
After moving a player (01 or more deplacements cards) the next player can take control by playing a card TACKLE.

A Head2Head boardgame.
In a century hence, as the polar ice caps melt, and great civilizations falter, local clans clash as they vie for what precious little dry land remains above the flooded MELTSCAPE.  Your clan must build floating bridges through a sunken city to seize the opposing clans land before they can seize yours.  To do so, Chieftain must mind-meld with a SNAIRG-Rattler, and your clan must dispose of as many opposing clansmen as possible.

Clans start set up along two strips of land, on either side of the game board.  Using Floating Bridge Hexes, both clans build floating bridges to the Gravity Wave Corporation building so the Chieftains can mind-meld with a prototype weapon, the Rattler, which gives the Chieftains the power to win.  When she is ready, Chieftain will then cross over the flooded city to seize the opposing clan's land, and win the game.

A new spin on an old game!

From Mattel comes this fast paced Uno Card Game celebrating the 40th Anniversary Year of UNO!!

Join Lightning McQueen and all your CARS 2 friends for an UNO race to the finish! Get a secret peek at everybody else's hand with the Special Agent Mater card and rule! And don't forget to yell UNO! before you cross the finish line!

112 Cards included plus Instructions
family: Versions & Editions: Versions with IP after the original release

description:
Publisher Blurb:

Wacky and unstoppable, even by the standards of his race, the young Daimah Kudoi is one of the newest members of Samael. He traveled from the east to protect the Aoi Inukai, but in the Old World he has found much more than he expected. An endless adventure that will put his skills to the test! Miniatures are supplied unpainted and assembly may be required.

Light Samael Warrior/Prowler — Lv. 40

Innate Abilities: Open Movement.
 Acrobat: Kudoi gets +2 to his Escape rolls.
 oooo — Flying Crane (Ki, Charge)
 This attack gets +1 Attack / +1 Damage for each 4 inches between Kudoi and the target when he begins moving for this Charge.
 oooo — Court Mantis (Ki, Attack)
 +8 Attack.
 ooo — Howling Wolf (Ki, Ranged Attack)
 Distance (16 inches). +4 Attack / +4 Damage. This attack gets -1 Attack / -1 Damage for each 4 inches between Kudoi and his target.

Publisher Blurb:

Everyone's favorite word game is now offered in a Bible version. Celebrate your favorite stories from the Bible in this fun new word game. The Bible Edition of Scrabble features new Bible rules and a specially designed game board. Also, included is a new Bonus Word Guide.
