description:
Old King Fafnir has no heir to his kingdom, a problem he means to solve by marrying off his daughter Princess Jezabelelle. Only there is one problem with this plan: the princess  that being that the princess is such a spoiled rotten, cold hearted, ill tempered, foul mouthed, back stabbing wretch of a woman that no good, respectable knight or noble in his right mind would ever consider such a horrible fate.

Then he was struck with a flash of inspiration; he declared a that joust tournament be held for all eligible bachelors of certain royal status to participate, the prize for the victor being the Princess Jezabelle's hand in marriage.

No one signed up.

Desperate, the king hastily opened the enrollment wide open so that any man in the kingdom may participate in the tournament. The applicants began flowing in. Unfortunately for the king, those who did sign up are not so good, respectable, or in their right mind (or even human for that matter.) But it is too late for the king to do anything about it now, the joust must go on!

Fool's Joust is a card based system where players randomly draw their character and the opponents whom they will face in combat. Each turn the player use dice to engage in three rounds of combat, the character who scores the most hits or remains in their saddle wins. Players win a reward card for each successful combat; the first player to collect a predetermined number of reward cards wins the game.

Play as an interdimensional cowboy on the move searching for unique cows. You've come across "The Field", a plane that seems to attract 4 particular monstrous Moos! But these Moos are difficult to capture and you need special tools if you want to stand a chance against the other cowboys who have also discovered The Field.

Pull together 3 tools to specifically utilize against the different types of Moo. You can wield an EMP Rifle to take down Cyborg Moos, or trail Zombie Moos along with a Brain on a Stick. You might need a Steel Stetson to protect yourself against Dragon Moos, a Puzzle Box to trap Ghost Moos and don't leave your Faithful Companion at home, he'll help you round up any Moo you want!

Capture 25 different Moos, or get a pedigree herd of 15 on one type to win against the other cowboys.

This is a game with entry level tactical play. Choices are available in every step. It is meant to be a fun game for anyone to play and accessible to all levels of experience. Specifically, the game is designed to help bring players who have not had much experience with tactical play into the broader gaming market. The steps of the game are as follows:

Rustle:  Draw the top two off of the deck, or one off the discard pile.  If there are not enough cards in the deck, shuffle the discard pile, place it face down and continue.

Round-up:  Wrangle cows and play action cards or sit-a-spell.  If you choose to wrangle or play action cards you may play as many as you are able (including multiple sets of tools).  To sit-a-spell you may discard any number of cards from your hand at once and draw that number from the deck. If you sit-a-spell you may not play cards, and you may not sit-a spell if you have played any cards.

Portal: Roll the Moo Dice.  (A 6 sided die with 1 color for each Moo type and two opposite faces colored black). Place one Moo of each indicated type in the field according to the Moo Placement Rules.  If all four dice show black then a Stampede has occurred.

Mosey:  Move one Moo of each color in the field one space if possible.  If there are no Moos in the field, or if none of the Moos can be moved, then skip this phase.  If there is only one Moo of a color in the field you must still move that Moo.

After the Mosey phase, if you have more than 7 cards in hand, discard in the order you choose until you only have 7.

In English, the title means something like 'Oddballs' or 'Weirdos'.

Contents:
44 Cards
There are 22 Matching Pairs and the backs of each card are marked as traditional dominoes so you get two games for your money.

The aim is to collect matching pairs. A matched pair consists of the top half of a character and a bottom half of the same character.

In your turn, you can either ask a player for a card that you would like, or you can tell a player a card that you would like to get rid off. When your appeal fails, the next player plays. Completed matches are set aside.

The obvious end is that the winner is the one who collected the most pairs but the rules suggest that you could create 'oddballs', i.e. the wrong lower half with the wrong upper half. The one who creates the best oddball (as judged by laughter, I guess), wins.

The backs of the cards each feature a different domino piece so Dominoes can be played with the cards.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Messenger of the Spiral Path Promo Miniature
description:
The Messenger of The Spiral Path discovered what he calls the Spiral Path, a mental course that lets him see the Truth. Perhaps this man has taken his first few steps to becoming a Forsaker?

The Messenger of The Spiral Path is a promotional miniature that will only be available through the Kingdom Death: Monster kickstarter and future Kingdom Death special events.

boardgame: Europa: Lernspiel für Jung und Alt
description:
A small trivia game containing 98 questions about Europe.

Each card contains a question on the front and an answer on the backside (which is not the answer to the question on the other side of that card).

The cards are shuffled and evenly distributed to all players. One player asks a question from one of his cards; now all other players try to find the right answer on the backside of their cards. If a player has the card with the right answer he gets one point and is the next player to ask a question.

The winner is the player with the most points. The game also contains rules for solitaire play.
family: Series: Lernspiele für Jung und Alt (Komet Verlag)

description:
Liar, Liar is a simple card game for 3-10 players, who need to lie through their teeth to get rid of cards in their hand.
Each card has a number (from 1 to 21), and you play cards face-down. When you play a card into the pile on the table, you have to tell higher number than the last player. So the game forces you to lie from time to time.
If you seem suspicious, other player may challenge you. If you were lying, you get all the cards in the pile as a negative points. If you were truthful, the challenger is penalized.

Game includes 4 special variants that you can mix-up into the game. You choose, which special cards you include in the game.

A trick taking game with a cyberpunk theme.

Players take turns playing cards in thematic suits (Android, Executive, Hacker and Ronin) then choose reward cards from a display with the players who used the highest ranked cards choosing first.  Lower rank cards have special abilities which can change who wins, however, so players must choose wisely.

Players score based on their accumulated reward cards at the end of the game. There are four sets of reward cards that score based on how many of the set a player collected, and location cards that are always worth 3 points each but some of which can double the value of the collected set cards as well.

The game was originally made as a gift for a friend of the designer, themed around the stories from her web comic series (Arcana Jayne).  Both sets of cards are available here to print & play.

Nintendo Ultraman Game released in 1966 by Nintendo based on the original Ultraman TV series. The city is on the brink of total destruction. Cars are ablaze, flying from collapsing freeways. Even worse: the local stores are under threat as well. Luckily, Ultraman comes to the rescue!

Ultraman Game is one of Nintendo's grey band games. The game bears the UltraQ logo. UltraQ was the first of the 'Ultra' series created by Tsuburaya Productions.

Players choose one of four Earth Defense characters and advance on the board by spinning the arrow on game board. When players land on the spaces with 'guns' the other spinner is used. This determines which monster was defeated, which earn players the corresponding card.

The race is open to graduate. Go the way of the wise Apprentices and try to get the points that will allow you to enter the University Bright Young. To get points, you must answer the questions correctly.

Be the first player (or first team) to finish university and get the enviable title of Junior Docte Rat.

There are four question categories: Petite Colle (Little Poser) the easy one where the questions are worth 5 points, Bon Mot (Good Word) the vocabulary category that can give you 5 or 10 points, Érudition contains harder questions worth 10 points and, finally, Mini-Test where questions are usually divided into three sub-questions totalling 15 points.

The object of the game is to be the first to finish the two board stages. You have to collect 40 points to finish the first part. Starting again from scratch, you need 80 points to complete the second level and win the game.

Players take turn rolling die to move their pawn and see what question category they will get.

IntroductionTrisq is a drawless connection game for two players: Red and Blue. It's played with trapezium-shaped pieces on an initially empty square board. The top and bottom edges of the board are colored red; the left and right edges are colored blue. The recommended board sizes are between 6x6 and 9x9.

All pieces are the same and only differ in color. They can be placed in any orientation, but always with the front side up. Thus, when playing on a physical board, the back side of the pieces should be marked so as to distinguish it from the front side. A square can accommodate four pieces, and no piece can be placed in a way that makes this physically impossible.

				
				
					DefinitionsTwo pieces are point adjacent if they share nothing more than a vertex, and edge adjacent if they share at least part of a side longer than a vertex.

				
				
					PlayRed plays first, then turns alternate. On his turn, a player must place one or two pieces of his color on empty spaces of the board. On his first turn, Red can only place one stone.

At the end of a turn, for any two point adjacent, like-colored pieces there must be at least one like-colored piece edge adjacent to both.

Passing is not allowed. Players will always have a move available and must make one.

The game is won by the player who completes a chain of edge adjacent pieces of his color touching the two opposite board edges of his color. Draws are not possible.

				
				
					AcknowledgementsThe featured placement restriction was first used in Corey Clark's Slither. Asymmetric, variable orientation cell division was first used in Mark Steere's Tripen.

This adaptation of the Quebec show Privé de Sens showcases the creative genius, memory, insight and general culture of the players.

To win, you must also demonstrate a great complicity juggling with words, mime coping with stress, find the link between various clues ... These are some of the challenges that await you!

There are many mini-game that the 2 teams will play. For mini-game the teams will get points.

The mini-games are:

1- Au pied de la lettre: In this game the team receive 3 letters. One player from the opposite team will ask questions and the 3 letters will be part of the answers. You get one point per correct answer given within the time limit.

2- Passez-moi l'expression: One player from the playing team is designated to use a randomply chosen sentence with mimes and make his or her teammates guess songs, characters, ads, etc, on a list.

3- Mémoire d'éléphant: The 2 teams look for 10 seconds at a board with 9 pictures. After the 10 seconds, the images get covered and a player ask questions. The answers will be the pictures previously seen.

4- Ménage à trois: 2 players from a team try to make there other teammates guess a word by each saying a word. After correctly guessing up to 7 words the team must try to guess what link together these words for another point.

5- Des pieds et des mains: This is mime game where to opposing team choose a restriction to make it a little more difficult. (Ex : you must use an object with your mimes)

6- The final game: The team with the most point gets 1 minute to play this game and the team with the fewer points gets 45 secondes. The team look at a card with 8 words for 8 seconds and tries to memorise them. Then one player from the team take the card and say one word so the other players can guess one the word from the list. Each correct answer award you a point for the final game and the team with the most points for the final game win.

Having fun with the meaning of words is the goal of the game Privé de sens!

Chapman Pro Football is made of wood and plastic - "You Actually Put the Ball in Play." Uses a "plinko" style area to determine the results of various plays.  The ball is moved along a wire above the playing field. There is an American and a Canadian version. Parker Brothers eventually purchased the rights to this game and released it, with the identical board, but different box as Parker Pro Football Game, in both American and Canadian versions.

Speed-Way is the reality board game of car racing. It is a game for 2 - 6 players, and Ages 8 - Adult. The race arena is set in a 28" by 19" game board with its graphic details showing:  Track lanes, Spectators, Maintenance Pits, Ticket windows, Parking lots, Repair garages, Safety fences, Race Flags, Food stores, Restrooms, and other elements of a sport racing arena.
 The board game shows a 10 track lanes for a 10 laps race with both Start and Finish lines. But players can race for 20, 30, 40, 50, or even 100 or higher laps as they desire, just by lapping each lane by multiple of 2, 3, 4, 5, or more times over. The game fully implements all the rules and regulations of an official car racing, including the use of all the different racing flags.

This game is a game that tests the ability of players to evaluate proper on how much they give away pointwise in order to insure that players come to them to gain points, so by giving away points, the player has people coming to them to get points.

The core of the mechanics is that in a set order (unless playing simultaneous turns) a player will pick another player to beg to.  The player being begged will divide up a set number of points between them and the begging player.

Play continues a set number of rounds with the player with the most amount of points winning the game.

The Season has started.  This team has struggled for the last few years, falling from its glory days a decade past.  A desperate GM fired the last guy and put you in charge.  Half of the rabid fans here are already booing you.  It's your job to prove them wrong, or else end up on a midseason bus out of town.  Fortunately you've got a team of hustle players anchored by a few real stars... just the tools you need to deliver a playoff run... and maybe a Superbowl.

Call the plays, make the clutch decisions, bluff your opponents with trickery or just run the ball down their throat.  Lead your players to victory.

End Zone is a football action card game designed to capture the strategy, drama, and science of american football, and distill it into quick, intuitive, exciting gameplay.  Quick matches take 20-30 minutes.  Double that for a full game.

First play any set cards to assign your personnel for the play... then secretly plot your play.  Once revealed the cards physically line up against each other and give you the range of outcomes.  No books of charts.  No funky dice.  No colorcoding necessary.  The modified execution die (1d6) determines the play's final outcome.

description:
In Campfire, each player is attempting to build three campsites before any other player. The game focuses on building up campsites while using camp wrecking critters to prevent your opponents from finishing first. Campfire is a family-friendly card game designed to be quick to learn for players of all ages.

Players draw from a shared deck full of cards to help build their camp, wreck other camps, and alter game play. Taking turns, players draw a card, play cards on the table in front of them in three camps, and play camp wreckers and game changer cards to the discard. At the end of a player's turn, they must discard one card from their hand (if they have any cards left) and replenish their hand. Play continues counter-clockwise. Discards are shuffled to replenish the deck when it runs out.

Campsites consist of two comfort cards, two food cards, and are completed with a campfire card that must be the last card played on a campsite. Cards are stacked in the order they are played and only the top card of any campsite is vulnerable to camp wrecking attacks.

The first player to complete three camps wins the game.

Enter the world of one of the best selling video game franchises of all time in this special Street Fighter Collector’s Edition of Monopoly. Buy, sell and trade fighting arenas associated with the most iconic characters over 25 years such as Makoto in the Karate Dojo, Ryu on the Rooftops of Japan and Major Bison in the Shadaloo Headquarters.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Lonely Tree Expansion
description:
If you can see the Lonely Tree for what it really is, it's probably too late. This carnivorous organism hypnotizes anything that comes near, luring prey with hallucinations of their innermost desires. Once they draw near, the hallucinations turn to ceaseless dreams, while the roots consume the victim in their sleep.

With each meal, a grotesque bulge, appears on the Lonely Tree's branches which slowly swells and takes on certain qualities of the devoured as they are digested. This "fruit" of the Lonely Tree is its true prize, as eating these conglomerations of phytohormones and human gristle can have beneficial (albeit bizarre) side effects. However, make sure to handle them gently while they are close to the tree, as the chemical volatility of the unripened morsels doubles as the Lonely Tree's primary form of self-defense!

The Slenderman is a dark, cold hole in the memories of the few that have seen it and lived. Within this chilling gap of recollection, terror thrives, growing into panic and jumbled tales of warning. Facing the Slenderman tests the teamwork and sanity of the survivors that face it. The monster's movements are unpredictable, and it will often vanish from the board, leaving the team to carefully coordinate their actions to bring down their foe.

Slenderman's rewards are as alien as the monster itself. Strange substances from the Slenderman's domain will need careful research before they're put to use.

Game description from the rule book:

You take on the role of a captain taking passengers to distant lands. You can collect points by making skilful manoeuvres, but your points can also be influenced by the elements. You have to be attentive to make sure you don't have any "stowaways" on board, because the coastguard can thwart your plans. You also have to make sure you have enough provisions. If you can overcome the challenges and get as many passengers as possible to their destination ports then you will be - with a bit of luck - the winner.

Each player is given a ship which he moves around from port to port on the wooden game board. During the game, players draw port cards showing a destination and its value. The players then can load passengers and bring them to these destinations. They have to make sure to have enough provision for all their passengers. The goal is to reach one of two objectives: either a general objective (40 VP), or a personal objective (chosen from a personal objective card when having reached at least 20 VP).

Each game round is divided into two phases: an action phase and a movement phase. During the action phase, the starting player rolls two dice and then chooses to take
A) the two actions associated with the dice results rolled or
B) take one action not associated with a dice result.
All the other player then take one single action: if the starting player chooses A) they have to choose B) and vice versa.

Possible actions are: take a port card, take a passenger/provisions card, load passenger(s), load provisions, take and execute an event card, lose 1 VP and choose any action.

During the movement phase, the active player can move his/her ship up to the number of spaces corresponding to the sum of his two dice. The other players can then move the higher of the two numbers rolled. When arriving in a destination port, players can unload passengers to gather victory points.

Martial Arts: The Card Game is a customizable card game that uses actual martial art techniques and philosophies in creating a unique first-person experience in your card battles. Each box contains four pre-constructed decks, and each deck combines one or more martial arts to create a playstyle that represents a single distinct fighting style.

You can mix and match cards from different decks and styles to customize your fighting style to suit your own preference. Included for this purpose is the Sparring Pack: a collection of individual cards not seen in any of the other decks.

Modes of play included in the box are Normal (1v1, last man standing; legal moves only, no weapons), Anything Goes (1v1 which allows weapons and illegal moves), and Army of One (1v9, hero versus gang boss and his thugs).

The four 54-card decks in Martial Arts: The Card Game are:


    King of the Ring - Boxing
    Master of 8 Limbs - Muay Thai
    Ume and the Blade - Aikido, Okinawan Karate, Katana (weapon)
    Enter the Phoenix - Wing Chun, Jeet Kune Do, Butterfly Swords (weapon)

Game description from the publisher:

"Corporations like to forget the human costs of their achievements. They don't like to think about the thousands of people who they've trod upon and who live in the shadows of their corporate offices. But every such person is a potential ally for all of us runners." –Shade

Hardware and programs drive efficiency, but talent trumps them both. In the future of Android: Netrunner, humans compete for work with bioroids and clones. Business is built as much from programs and hardware as from cash and concrete. To find your way in this tech-centric world, you need to be savvy to the latest upgrades, and you need a good deal of imagination. Humanity's Shadow, the fifth Data Pack in the "Genesis Cycle" for Android: Netrunner The Card Game, gives you a chance to get your hands on both the most bleeding edge technology and the talent you need to make it sing. Its sixty new cards (three copies each of twenty different cards) allow players to customize their strategies while recruiting more personalized and imaginative assistance.

With a new Criminal identity, a daring new resource, and unique sysops for the Corporations, Humanity's Shadow profiles some of the most talented individuals pulling the strings behind the game’s cyberstruggles. New events for the Runners allow you to customize your strategies while the game's Corporations look to increase their security with a more hands-on approach based upon new operations, talented sysops, traces, and traps.

boardgame: Mighty Morphin Power Rangers: Save the Youth Center Game
description:
Power Rangers! The evil Rita Repulsa has sent her Pudgy Pig monster to Angel Grove. It has eaten all of Angel Grove’s food supply. Ernie’s Youth Center won’t be able stay open without food. You need to save Ernie’s Youth Center Power Rangers by getting food back from Rita’s Evil Space Aliens! All of Angel Grove is counting on you Power Rangers. You must restock Ernie’s Youth Center till Angel Grove can get its food supply back.

Game Objective: Players need to collect eight food items and bring them back to Ernie’s Youth Center to restock the food supply, and then travel back to the Command Center to regroup with Zordon!

The youngest player starts the game by rolling the die and moving the indicated number. If they make it to an Evil Space Alien Battle Location, they may stop their and do Battle. *Players do not have move the full amount on the die if they choose to stop on a Location area. After a player stops at a Location area, there turn ends. Play then continues with the player to left.

Winning the Game: The first player’s Power Ranger to drop off the eight collected food items to Ernie’s Youth Center and return to the Command Center is the winner!

description:
Conundrum is the exciting game where everyone plays by their own rules.

Each player has the same set of eight rules. The challenge is knowing when to play each rule, because the rules can only be played once. Played in eight rounds, every player is dealt 10 cards each round. Each player selects the rule that he/she will play by for that round, based on his/her hand. So each player will be playing the round by a different set of rules. After each round, those rules will clear and a new hand is dealt.

The object of the game is to try to play as many cards as possible each round. Every card left in a player's hand after each round is a point. The winner is the player with the fewest points after eight rounds.

A promo/add-on for Sake & Samurai given at Play 2012. The cards were sold separately or given with every purchase of Sake & Samurai.

The add-on consists on 4 Ninja cards and a rule card.

Holiday Special 2011

The very first discovered fragments of the Galactic history already mention the Elders of the Solstice. When the day is short, the stories tell, the Elders would arise, bringing light and joy to the righteous and smiting down the wicked. Their Dreadnoughts roamed the night unchallenged. It has been a long while since the Elders were last seen, but who knows – as the shadows of the great civilizations fall, the times are dark indeed...

Eclipse: Elders of the Solstice is a print and play expansion. It adds a new race, Elders of the Solstice. Elders have a special dreadnought that flies around the galaxy bringing gifts to other players. Also if other players attack elders, they are declared Naughty, and receive -2 points at the end of the game.

boardgame: Front Office Football: The Card Game
description:
In Front Office Football: The Card Game, two coaches assemble "playbooks" consisting of offensive and defensive plays, then play an abridged game of professional football while choosing plays, rolling the dice and determining the results.

This game focuses on the interaction between two coaches, unlike the computer version, which was originally created in 1998 and has evolved through several versions.

In the card game, each team has a set of player cards with specific strengths and weaknesses. Along with your play calls, this helps determine the results of plays, and, ultimately, the winner of the game. It's assumed coaches (you and your friends) have a basic understanding of the rules of football. This is a game for those who genuinely enjoy football strategy and don't mind a relaxing evening at the card table.

While most of the cards are in the basic sets, each team has twelve specific plays (nine on offense, three on defense) that emphasize its strengths and tendencies. This will give your games the flavor of coaching a specific team without upsetting competitive balance.

Objective: be the first reaching target space '100' (get married)
- simple 'Roll & Move' game/ moving by turns -
each player moves his/her (cute) meeple according die roll
ending on a coloured space follow instructions of the rule sheet

We've all heard the stories: A giant furry beast roaming the hills, a mother bent on revenge for her unjust death, a mischievous taunting elf-like creature – but that's all they are, just legends...right?

Ultimate Werewolf: Urban Legends brings these characters to life within Ultimate Werewolf. If Bloody Mary dies, she starts killing the players who killed her...from the grave. The ferocious Chupacabra loves the taste of werewolf, but if he runs out of them, villagers will do. The Wolf Man hunts with the werewolves, but to the Seer he appears as an innocent villager. The tricky Leprechaun sides with the village, messing with the werewolves by changing their nighttime target. All Sasquatch ever wanted to do was to help the village kill werewolves...but if the village wavers on this, Sasquatch will turn on them. And finally, Nostradamus has to make a name for himself by predicting who will win each game of Ultimate Werewolf – and then helps out his prediction. All or some of these characters can be added to your Ultimate Werewolf games to make them even more compelling than before!

Urban Legends is included in the 2013 edition of Ultimate Werewolf, is compatible with earlier editions of this game, and is also available on its own.

"Les Rois du Ring" is a card-driven miniature game with a boxing theme that was dreamed (literally) by french amateur designer "Chien Sauvage", and that he posted about on his blog. It is played on a 5x5 grid, with two miniatures and a deck of normal playing cards, with 1-8 as the main deck, and 16 cards used as hit points (8 each). Player take turn playing cards to move and attack each other on a "ring". They can interrupt each others turn to dodge or parry attacks.

A few variants exist that were released as creative commons

boardgame: Deus Vult: Wargaming in the time of the Crusades
description:
Deus Vult are a set of miniatures rules designed to recreate battles during the period of the Crusades.  The rules are designed to be played with 25-30mm miniatures on a 6'x4' table (but can be adapted for different figure and table sizes). They not only contain the principally d6 based rules (although d4 to d12 are used for duels between leaders) but also army lists for the Early Crusader States 1100-1128AD and Arab Dynasties 945-1150AD (plus some other sample units) along with several battle scenarios.

The game features:


     A pre-battle reconnaissance and deployment phase with options for subterfuge
     Alternating leader activation based on an initiative roll
     Leaders can give orders to multiple units based on their command rating
     Multi-figure stand based units
     d6 based combat
     Points based army lists
     Seven battle scenarios with rules to create your own
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Biblebanz is a game that when played will increase Bible knowledge, vocabulary, and perhaps the faith of all who participate. There exist many card games for enhanced learning. A unique feature of this card game is that it consists of plastic adjustable headbands worn on the foreheads of each player. This feature makes it fun and exciting to learn about the Bible. More specifically this game relates to a method of learning about people, places and things found in the Bible in a fun and unique way. It has been said that a family that prays together stays together. I would also like to think that a family that plays together stays together. Therefore, it is the purpose of this game to bring goups of people together in the form of family, friends, or other small groups to learn about the Bible in this fun unique way, increasing ones Bible knowledge, vocabulary, and their faith in the Bible and in God. Churches and Sunday school teachers are always looking for new, fun and creative ways to teach their students about the Bible. This game will satisfy that need.

Each Biblebanz game comes with 6 headbands, 94 Bible cards, 6 blank cards, 24 chips and a timer.

This game is a game for the whole family for ages 7 and up. It is for 2 to 6 players. The Biblebanz Game Cards features things found in the Bible. Examples are: animals, food, people and bible objects. Examples are: Jesus, Noah, Ark, Fig tree, apple, church and so forth. The cards are simple enough for the whole family to join in on the fun. Each round only lasts one minute. At the start of the round you have to start the sand timer. As the time runs out, the player whose turn it is must ask yes or no questions to figure out what his card is. This will help to increase your Bible knowledge and Bible vocabulary. It's very entertaining to say the least. There are 6 blank cards to be used with a dry erase marker. These blank cards are great for young children who need easy words or Bible schoolars who want harder words to figure out. You can write whatever word you want out of the Bible on them. You will never run out of words to use this way.

Rules Of The Game:

The rules of Biblebanz are simple. Each player is dealt a single card and three chips. The player’s must then place the card on their headband without looking. The youngest player goes first. Then, play passes to the left. Set the one minute timer. Next, start asking questions in yes or no format. If you've found out your identity, you just ask, “Am I ________?” Fill in the blank with what you think you are. For each identity that you guess correctly, you discard one of your chips. If you guess your identity before the one minute timer runs out, take another card and put it in your headband. If you do not guess your identity before the timer runs out, then keep it and continue to guess on your next turn. The person to get rid of all of his or her chips is the Biblebanz winner! It's so much fun!

Great for birthday parties, bridal showers, Christian retreats, sleep overs, children's church, youth groups, Bible study groups, and more!

Biblebanz is a trademark of Christian Book And Toys LLC. ©2012 Christian Book And Toys LLC. All rights reserved. Patent Pending. Conforms to product safety standards CPIA, regulatory requirements.

description:
The front page in the local newspaper shows the latest crimes committed in the city. The police, pressured by the public, looks for answers.
Because of this, the officers gather some of the most dangerous and renown criminals of the city, so they can interrogate them about the facts. ¡Any of them could have committed the crime! so they’ll need them to accuse each other so the ones responsible for the crimes go to jail. But, beware, for this kind of “justice” can also put an innocent behind bars.

“¡It Wasn’t Me!” is game of investigation and bluff, where each players assumes the role of a criminal, determined by "criminal cards" with unique abilities, and have to avoid being sent to jail. To do this, each player will be dealt a "Crime card" that is unknown to other players. Then an "Investigation card" that matches one of those "Crime cards" will be revealed, and players must try to individually find who is the culprit using action cards with various effects. But in the end, not all players will find the culprit, so they will have to rely on the accusations of other players, who may have different agendas and may be bluffing. The round ends with a secret votation round to choose who goes to jail and points will be determined according to your choice.


La primera plana del periódico local anuncia el último horroroso crimen que consterna a la ciudad. La policía, presionada por el público, busca respuestas. 
Por esta razón, los oficiales reúnen a varios de los más peligrosos y reconocidos criminales de la ciudad, para así interrogarlos sobre los hechos. ¡Cualquiera de ellos pudo haber cometido el crimen!, así que necesitarán que ellos mismos se acusen para poder llevar a la cárcel al responsable de uno de los varios crímenes que aquejan a la sociedad. Pero, cuidado, pues este tipo de “justicia” puede poner detrás de las rejas a un inocente o a un culpable por igual.

“¡Yo no fui!” es un juego de investigación y bluff, en el cual cada jugador asume el rol de un criminal, a partir de la repatición de "cartas de criminal", con habilidades únicas, y debe evitar ser enviado a la cárcel. Para ello, se le reparte a cada jugador una "carta de crimen" que se mantiene secreta a lo largo del juego. Luego se revela una "carta de investigación", que es pareja de una de las cartas de crimen repartidas. Entonces, los jugadores comienzan a investigar quién fue el culpable del crimen usando cartas de efecto para ver la carta de crimen de otros jugadores. Pero para el final, sólo unos pocos jugadores conocerán el criminal, así que los demás dependerán de las acusaciones de los otros jugadores, que pueden estar mintiendo en pro de agendas personales. Al final de la ronda de juego, se realiza una votación secreta para determinar quién se va a la cárcel y se reparten puntos de acuerdo con las votaciones realizadas.

In Ropoa The City, players compete to construct the medieval city-state of Ropoa. By creating new buildings players increase their Income and earn Victory Points. The player who can earn the most Victory Points wins!

Each player is in charge of constructing the city of Ropoa from nothing to a burgeoning city-state. Players take turns buying resources; Wood, Stone, Glass and Metal. Then use those resources to construct buildings within the city walls. Each building has its own resource cost and provides varying amounts of Income and Victory Points to the owning player. Also, every building is represented on the board by a 3-dimensional model;. The placement of each building will change the face of the city as well as the potential for additional Victory Points.

Ropoa was specifically designed to be a gateway game. But don't be fooled by the easy-to-learn, simple rules this game has a surprising amount of strategic depth and thoughtful play.

More information and announcements on the release of Ropoa The City will be coming early in 2013.

"The Unique Challenge With a Touch of Class"

From the back of the box:

"OD BALL is played very similar to the ancient Chinese game called NIM. Each player alternates turns in which any number of balls may be eliminated from any one of the three sections. Players may eliminate only one ball or the whole section or any number in between - but only from one section at a time. The winner is the player who forces his opponent to take the last ball."

Instead of the piles of sticks or stones usually used for NIM, it has marbles which are captured in a wooden board. Each marble can be snapped down to a lower position to take it. The board is double-sided, allowing play to continue after all the marbles have been taken.

"Trade your stocks right away and be a millionaire!"

In this game players take on the role of speculators in the stock market, buying and selling shares in six companies.

There are a variable number of rounds, depending on how long of a game is desired. In each round, players are dealt ten cards which move the price of one stock up or down. (There are also a few special cards which allow special actions.) They then go around the table taking one action each (buy shares in one company, sell shares in one company, or play a special card.). After each player has taken three actions all of the cards are revealed, and the changes to each stock's price are tallied, as is the tax or interest rate for cash in hand.

The first player to get to 50% holding in a company is its Chair, and may suppress the worst price-reduction card (played by anyone) affecting that company in each round.  Players holding between 25 and 49.5% of a company may suppress one of their own cards, of any type. (The Chair may veto plays affecting their own company.)

At the end of the game the player with the greatest net worth wins.

boardgame: A Call to Arms: Star Fleet – Gorn Fleet Box
description:
Publisher's Blurb:

The Fleet Boxes are designed for use with any game set in the Star Fleet Universe, including A Call to Arms: Star Fleet, Star Fleet Battles, Federation Commander, and Starmada.  Each contains enough ships for a complete fleet in these games and will allow players to quickly expand their forces.

This box set contains 16 ships for the Confederation of the Gorn, along with 6 shuttles and a pocket rulebook – all you need to start playing!

The New Science: Fantasy Scientists is a small expansion forThe New Science that adds five new scientists for players to choose from:


     Albert Einstein
     Marie Curie
     Nikola Tesla
     Thomas Edison
     Charles Darwin

boardgame: Angry Birds: Star Wars – Fight on Tatooine Battle Game
description:
This game one of the many games in the Angry Birds Series.

One of the many Angry Birds Star Wars Battle Games, this set includes Han Solo bird figure, the set exclusive Greedo pig and Ponda Baba pig figures.

Set includes 3 figures, 6 blocks, 1 cantina arch, 1 LIGHTSABER launcher and instructions.

The game is based on the Star Wars Angry Bird app. In addition, the game comes with a App code to unlock game content.

boardgame: Age of Industry Expansion: Belgium & USSR
description:
Two new expansion maps for  Age of Industry : Belgium & USSR

The Belgium map has been designed for 2 players and features one of the first country to be industrialized on the European continent. Belgium featured important textile production in Flanders, iron processing in Walloon as well as large coal reserve in the south and east of the country. Given its smaller domestic market, the Belgian Government was interested to increase the efficiency of transportation and to link Germany and France, so it participated in the development of a public rail network...

The USSR map has been designed for 3 to 5 players and features the Donbass Coal Basin, which covers part of former Ukrainian and Russian Soviet Federation Republics, during Stalin's First Five-Year Plan. This plan, which was implemented in the context of a planned economy, was conducted to increase production of goods and boost the development of heavy industry...

UNAUTHORIZED PRODUCTION

    
    
         The information that BGG has on this item indicates that San Guo Sha is an unauthorized reproduction of BANG! with just a few differences, published in China and distributed in several coutries of the world.

    


San Guo Sha: Overnight Fame 2012 is a character expansion of San Guo Sha. These are 12 new characters: 4 for Wei, 3 for Shu, 3 for Wu and 2 non-allegiance characters. No new princes or demi-gods are included (therefore there are no random cards as with the other expansions.)

All cards are user-created and were submitted to Yoka Games as part of a contest on 2012 to find the most interesting new characters.
family: Theme: Romance of the Three Kingdoms

description:
Plum Flower is the Taiwanese national flower. In this game, a player has 12 Chinese Animal Zodiac and four weather elements. The goal is go to the middle of the board and take the flower on the center of the board. The the animal has to carry on the flower and goes back to home. Who does it first will be the winner.

The animals can eat the other one depending on their order in Zodiac (like Dong Wu Qi). For example, the：Rat > Ox >
Tiger > Hare > Dragon > Snake > Horse > Sheep > Monkey > Cock > Dog > Boar. The four elements are used for blocking others way and can't leave the square in the center.


    This is an old Taiwanese game. It is supposed to be published in 1980's. It is not a very common game in Taiwan.

Village Inn, the first expansion for the award-winning game Village, adds two new buildings to the village: the brewery and the inn.

The brewery is a new craft building where you can acquire a new good: beer. In the inn, you can meet a lot of influential people (in the form of cards), and you can acquire these villager cards by spending beer/coins and time. Each villager card has a unique ability that can be used once per game, giving either points and advantages during the game or additional points at the end of the game for certain achievements.

In addition, Village Inn includes all necessary components for a fifth player.

boardgame: Chap with wings there, Five Rounds Rapid!
description:
User Description

Chap with wings there, Five Rounds Rapid! is a rules supplement for running Doctor Who Games using the Future Wars miniatures rules.

The supplement rules are based on the classic "Doctor Who" series (i.e pre 2005) including profiles on;

    Daleks
    Robomen
    Ogrons
    Cybermen
    Sea Devils
    Silurians
    Autons
    Yeti's
    Sontarans
    Ice Warriors


The rules was first published in the Ragnarok journal, issue 47 (August 2005).

An Art of War D10 game. In Outbreak Zombie the goal is to survive a Zombie apocalypse. The game is for 1-8 players ages 12 and up. With 5 game types the replay value is high. With loot dice the items are completely random. Can you survive the Outbreak?
 Throughout the game players must work together to gather items and secure buildings as needed. There is no monetary or buy section. All Items are found by looting the abandoned buildings of the town. Player cooperation is therefore put at a premium. Scarce ammo or better items can be traded between players. Unless of course you are playing the No Friends Scenario, In No Friends no help is allowed and you can attack other players.
 The Ultimate goal is securing your hometown. This is done by killing off all the zombies in your town. Get Out of Dodge is different as you and your fellow survivors attempt to escape.

Combat is One die roll and is streamlined for fun interactive battles. Action Points dictate how much players can accomplish per turn. Character development is present allowing for different breeds of characters based off player discretion.

As noted by NSKN Games in March 2015, Warriors & Traders: Middle Ages will not be released as described below, but instead transformed into a standalone game, possibly for release in 2016 or 2017. The description below shows what was originally intended for the expansion.

•••

Warriors & Traders: Middle Ages enhances the Warriors & Traders base game by offering more interaction between players in the form of trade negotiations, military campaigns, and peace resolutions. The new features include variable turn order and variable player powers. The existing features, area control, action selection and trading are also improved. The deterministic combat system is replaced by a more attractive tactical combat system.

The setting of the game is the Middle Ages of Europe. The players take the role of kings ruling over expanding nations fighting for dominance. Each player will develop his country's technologies, engage in trade, build forts, and raise armies to conquer his neighbors in his attempt to forge an empire.

Warriors & Traders: Middle Ages can be combined with the Warriors & Traders: Italia expansion and with any other map expansion that has been or will be released. Specific rules may apply.

Rattenkrieg is the third entry in the War Stories series. Featuring 20 scenarios chronicling in detail the legendary Battle of Stalingrad. Rattenkrieg introduces new urban warfare maps and rules for sewer movement, snipers, multi-story buildings, mouse holing, and street fighting. Additional German and Soviet units and assets are included as are the Romanians for the first time. Rattenkrieg not only expands the War Stories system to new theaters of combat but expands the assets and unit available to the Red Storm base game.

description:
Munchkin Easter Eggs is a 15-card booster that contains Munchkin cards with an Easter theme. The seven Treasures and eight Doors included have the standard Munchkin card backs, but the card fronts use a different color scheme: the Treasures have a green border and a yellowish background, while the Doors have a light blue border with a red and white background. (There's some sort of pattern/image to the background.)

The cards do not add any new or unusual mechanisms or interactions, but they bring a good bit of humor to the game. For instance, who wouldn't be afraid of a (Chocolate) Little Bunny 'Thulhu?

A long mane, a soft nose, and a warm look – every little girl dreams of having her own horse. With Joylings, horse lovers both big and small get a full herd of their favorite animal. Discover the world of horses with two different game variations.

In "Cross Country" players ride across the fields, collecting prize chips in six different categories. Which horse jumps highest? Which one gallops fastest? And which one has the longest mane? When playing the "Birthday Party" variant for younger children, the smartest horse celebrates his birthday in his stable. While making their way to the party, players must collect tournament ribbons as a present. The first player to arrive at the party with six different ribbons wins.

"Who knows Basel - The Original City Quiz"

Do You know Yor city or region? With over 500 questions about Basel.

The Quizmaster draws a card and asks a question. Plyayers bets correct answer with A/B/C/D-cards face-down or use their joker card. Correct answer scores points and player may continue answering. Wrong answer eliminates player from the session.
Game last until there are no players or the session is over.

Player with the most points is the winner.

description:
Where does wild anise grow? And what does it mean to "winnow" a plant? Gardening is one of the most popular pastimes. No matter whether a cottage garden is growing at home, a garden plot is thriving on the outskirts of town, or a few flower boxes embellish the balcony, in Welt des Gartens, players with a green thumb deeply immerse themselves in the world of their hobby – and in doing so, even the most experienced gardener will learn something new.

Before starting the game, players agree on how many questions they'll face. Each turn, one player reads a question from the card; the question has multiple answers listed below it, and 1-3 answers will be correct – but there's no way of knowing how many are right ahead of making your guess.

After the active player reads off the answers, each player can place 1-3 guess markers on the numbers matching those answers. When the player reveals the correct answers, anyone who guessed incorrectly scores no points, no matter how many other answers they got right. Players who guessed correctly with all of their placed tokens score as many points as the number of tokens they placed.

After finishing the agreed upon number of questions, whoever has the most points wins!

description:
The game consists of a board with an empty crossword grid with alternating 2 x 2 grids in white, black and red. Each player receives 48 letters and joker tiles, some of which are black, some red and some white. 
Each player now draws 7 of his/her letters into the hand. The first player forms a word on the board, then draws new letters. However, placing a red tile on a red square means the player can draw an extra tile; placing a non-black letter on a black field or a black letter on a non-black field means the player has to drop a letter. Other players follow in turn order, adding letters to the first word crossword-style. The first player to get rid of all of his/her tiles is the winner.

Protect or Infect is a turn-based strategy board game set in the early stages of a zombie outbreak. This game takes place several weeks into the infection at a point where the zombies seem to have the upper hand; not only in numbers but also in mutated ability. Four humans surviving together have taken refuge in a country side manor. On the run, down to just knives and pistols, and being stalked by countless zombies that are leading a monster directly to them, the survivors hold themselves up and wait for rescue within the halls of the zombie manor.

The game pits a team of 4 survivors against a team of zombies; each team consists of at least 1 player per side with up to 4 players on each team.  The game takes place on a grid based board detailing the basement, first floor, 2nd floor, and grounds of an abandoned manor.

The goal of the survivor team is to hold out within the manor grounds for 20 rounds at which point a rescue vehicle spawns. They must then make it to the rescue vehicle with at least 2 survivors making it to safety in order to win.  The survivor team can scavenge for items, barricade doors and windows with furniture and wood, and seek out strategic locations within the manor or its grounds to help aid them through the game (60+ weapons, health items, equipment, zombie traps, and weapon upgrades).

Alternatively, the survivor team can kill the zombie team boss character, The Harvester, thereby ending the zombie teams ability to spawn more zombies.  Once all zombies are cleared the survivor team wins.

The goal of the zombie team is to kill all of the survivor characters in order to win.  The zombie team gains 'spawn points' at the beginning of every round that can be saved up or spent on the 7 different classes of zombies available.  Each class costs a varying number of spawn points depending on their stats: movement speed, health, attack dmg, and special ability.  The zombie team also controls a zombie boss character, The Harvester.  The Harvester is the strongest and most versatile zombie of the game but if it is destroyed the zombie team can no longer spawn zombies.

The thrill of the "one-armed bandit" has lured gamblers since the end of the 19th Century.  From the original Poker-themed games to "Liberty Bell" - the first true slot machine as we know it today - to the complex electronic games of today, the slots have become the world's most popular casino game.  JACKPOT SLOTS brings the excitement of the casino to the board game table.


JACKPOT SLOTS is the umbrella title for two slot-machine themed games:

WINNING LINES is the flagship game.  As cards are dealt from a deck of cards bearing slot machine symbols, players seek to claim "winning lines" - combinations of slot machine symbols that pay off - before a lemon card turns up to end the round.

TRIPLE PLAY is a slot machine take on "Memory" in which players seek to uncover "winning lines" while, yet again, avoiding lemon cards.


The allure of the slots is before you!  Are you lucky enough to hit the Jackpot?

Simple rules, great ambiance (soccer), beautiful box: this is Futebox.  It is an intuitive game where children learn the rules quickly and can create new rules and possibilities, from the original. 
First, each player positions the numbered tokens (1 to 6). In your turn you roll the die; only the token with the same number can be moved. When a token pass the goal line, the player scores.

With civilization decimated, you and your fellow survivors struggle to eke out subsistence and build self-sufficiency before the harsh winter sets in. Can you survive after the fall?

Each player has established an outpost in one of five home bases, from the scrap yard to the mansion. They begin the game with a leader character who has a special ability plus six community members. Each turn the players' improvements produce resources. Then, everyone allocates their people to either scavenge for resources and equipment or build new improvements. Players can also trade to improve the overall community's chance of survival. Finally, resources must be spent to keep the members alive. Over the course of the game, winter sets in. After the fall, the winner is the player whose base is best equipped to survive, and the overall community score determines the fate of the entire human race.

boardgame: Battle Tank: Escape From Giant Robot Island
description:
Battle Tank: Escape from Giant Robot Island is a modular strategy board game for 2-4 players which pits battle tanks against giant robots. The players take on one of four roles, Mad Scientist Dr. Victor von Mechanstein, Sergeant Roger Stammel, OSI Agent Cynthia Collins or henchmech E-Gore Mk.IV.

Gameplay progresses in turns with each player moving their token(s) then resolving attacks before play passes to the next player. Each unit has a set movement rate and attacks are resolved by rolling a six-sided die (D6) and then comparing the number rolled to the range and the target's armor.

Each character has two of its own, often opposing, goals. One goal is active and ends the game on the turn it is achieved, and the other goal is passive and is only checked after 10 turns have been played. Mechanstein wants to prevent his tanks from being taken off the island, Stammel simply wants to escape with his soldiers, Collins wants intelligence on the Mad Scientist's forces and E-gore wants to please its master by recapturing the prisoners.

Each turn the tank players move toward the beach as more and more robots appear to stop them.

The action is modified by two sets of cards: Mad Scientist Events and Tanker Tactics. Events are large scale effects that change the game in some way or affect all the players tics are short and Tactics are minor effects that can give a boost to many actions as well as repair damage, improve speed or even find a new tank if one has been destroyed.

The playing area itself consists of 7 hexagonal tiles with a hex-grid printed on them. One tile is Mechanstein's volcanic lair and the other six are unique combinations of roads, cliffs, forests and open terrain. The non-volcano tiles can be rotated to any orientation and placed into one of several formations and are designed in such a way to always have the roads match up.

1180... the King is dead... the Barons seek power, while a powerful enemy observes... Who will take the throne of the Latin Kingdom of Sibylla? Battles, betrayals, conspiracies... Use all the power of his noble family, lead your armies, to become the new King!

Sibylla is a board game for 2-5 players, developed by Julio Trois, where you must seek power in a game whose theme is the Age of the Crusades!

The game has the mechanics, the area control, through 5 dices, search combinations that allow you to insert markers in each area of the game (same system used in the Poker Dice). You can also use action cards that increase the strategic factor, seeking to undermine the moves of opponents and open up new gameplay opportunities.

The denarius was minted by the Romans and was in use during the time of Christ through the era of the apostles. It was a silver coin, a bit larger than a dime, bearing the image of the reigning emperor and denarii (plural form of denarius) have been found bearing the image of all 12 Roman emperors.  While competing in the game, you will be given 12 denarii to help you along.

On your turn you role two custom dice and move your marker farther along the track on the board.  Depending on what color you land on you will be asked a different question from the trivia book.  Answer correct and you stay where you are, answer incorrectly and you must return to the last denarii space that you passed or pay a denarii from your supply.  To land on the last space you must role the exact number of spaces needed to get there or you can move backwards to give yourself more room.

Denarii was created to help individuals, groups and especially families increase their Bible knowledge and have fun together at the same time.  Learning is the main purpose, but there is competition, strategy, challenge and lots of excitement, too.
Even if a player does not give all correct answers, he will learn from others and could still be the winner of the game! There is little advantage for the Bible scholar.  Everyone who plays will learn and want to play again!
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
SUMMARY:
When a biological plague infects most of earth, distorting the newly discovered HELIX strand in our DNA, creating immediate mental defects and physical deterioration. The deformities mutate and the "age of zombies" becomes a reality. A desperate call for help from survivors gathered from around the world; through discovery of a secret United Nations project, awaken a reserve of space marines put in cryogenic suspension to return to earth with only one mission... to save the handful of survivors from becoming assimilated into zombies.

GAME-PLAY:
This is a two player game that generally lasts between 15-30 minutes. The box set comes with two 60 card decks; one for the Space Marines and one for the Zombies. The game offers Character Cards, Action Cards, Equipment Cards (for Space Marines Only), and Survivor Cards. Attacks are directly geared towards the characters and Survivors rather than the players. There is a follow-up expansion set ahead that can be a stand-alone or mixed in with this First Edition set.

GOAL:
To goal for the marines is to save 10 Civilians and help them leave earth safely with them whereas the goal of the zombies are simple.... feed. If the zombies "turn" all the civilians into zombies or kill off all the space marines, the zombies win. If the marines extract 10 civilians or kill off all the zombies, they win.

The gameplay in Rabbids: Das Kartenspiel is as wild as the rabbits themselves look. One by one, cards are revealed from a deck and placed face-up on the table. If a card shows a rabbit, it's left alone – but ideally the players will take a mental picture of it because if a scoring card is revealed, everyone races to grab all the revealed cards that depict the rabbit shown on this scoring card. Grab the most cards, and you win!

Rabbids: Das Familien-Partyspiel is a collection of sixty short games featuring the funny little rabbits from the Rabbids video game. Your aim in all of these games is to achieve whatever goal is set out for you, whether through skill, intellect, speed or just plain luck. In this game, you'll be called on to do everything, and everyone will receive chances to score. Each game lasts no more than two minutes, and the rules are simple enough that players will be able to jump into games quickly.

In the end, whoever wins the most Rabbids mini-games wins the whole shebang!

description:
Track Design to the Finish Line!

Mad Rush Rally is a game where you take turns building the track while you're racing on it! This allows you to create the track to suit your own strategy. You can even design your own short-cut to jump to first place!

Players gain insane and fun-to-use items; Items like the Booster, which let's you jet ahead of the competition; the Dodge Ball, which whacks your friend into missing their next turn; the Squirrel, which creates a roadblock forcing players to change course; or the Bomb which decimates the track and all on it! Plus more items that shift the balance of the game! What's more, some items are left on the track to be used by or to hinder your friends, making each game even more unique.

Mad Rush Rally offers a familiar but fresh experience combining kart racing inspired video games with a simple yet revolutionary map-building game mechanic that brings fun back to the table.

description:
App'd is a game for 2-4 players where players try to be the first to fill up 100% of their brain's space with apps without going over. Players draw cards from the deck trying to get App cards, expansion cards or hardware cards that will help them reach that goal of 100%.
Taken from Kickstarter description

Welcome back, Emperor. The time for unchecked expansion is over. Warmongers raise their flags over weaker empires, while civilized planets take shelter behind peace treaties. Brace yourself for rising tensions in this next chapter of Eminent Domain: Escalation!

This expansion for Eminent Domain, which cannot be played without the base game, puts the following new tools at the Emperor's disposal:


     New uses for the larger-sized ships from the base game.

     Additional technologies, each with an alternate cost in addition to their normal Research cost.

     Optional scenarios for asymmetric starting positions and technologies for each player.

boardgame: Bull in a China Shop: Action Cards
description:
In this expansion, the Pass action is replaced with the Play an Action Card option.

There are several Action Cards available:
- Discard a bull card 
- Discard a china card from the general display 
- Draw a money card 
- Exchange a china card you own with a china card from the general display 
- Replace all bull cards with new ones 
- Make the player to your right play

Play with the basic rules with the following additions.

Additional material:
14 action cards (6 sorts)

Preparation:
The pass cards are not needed, remove them from the game.
Shuffle the new action cards. 
Each player gets two and keeps them secret from the others.
Make a draw pile from the rest.

Game play:
On each player’s turn, they may either:
Buy a china card 
or
Take a bull card 
or
Play an action card

Play an action card:
The player plays an action card (=> discard pile) and fulfills the action, if he likes. 
Then the next player has his turn.

Action card pile exhausted:
Shuffle the discard pile and build a new draw pile.

Loose more than one china card:
The player gets the money card as usual and an additional action card from the draw pile, if available.

I see, I see little eye ... and wears glasses ...

Search as fast as you can as many objects and people to the description on the wheel, mark what you find with the stickers of your color. Hurry because the sandglass!
 Six fun and beautifully illustrated looking plates, twenty four assignments, a wheel and an unpredictable sandglass: these are the elements of this super cute look and search game.

Each player gets stickers and a scoreboard in the chosen color. Then you can choose which side of the wheel you play, simple or slightly tougher side! Choose a board game together, turn the sandglass and ready ... START!

Whoever has the most stickers is the winner of the round!

The future is here! Build the foundations of the Future Ages of Exploration in Culture, Science, Trade and Military while forming the Organizations that will rule Mankind.

Space Marvels 6 Express (SM6e) is a PnP Express tile laying game for 2 to 5 players in the vein of 7 Wonders playable in 15-20 minutes.

Players begin by each choosing a different Marvel Board, divided into 3 different sections, or Ages of exploration. Each board gives the player an initial resource. Players are also given 3 credits to start the game.

Players are then dealt a hand of tiles (8 tiles 2-3 players, 6 tiles for 4-5 players) and may do one of three things during each phase of a round:

a. Play a tile if they can pay the cost in resources, gaining future benefits, resources, and other bonuses
b. Use the tile face down + pay resources to build a section of their Marvel (thereby gaining bonus abilities)
c. Discard the tile for 3 credits

Players may play their tiles simultaneously and may buy resources from neighboring players at a cost of 2 credits.

After each player has played a tile, all players pass their remaining tiles to the player next to them.

The rounds end when all tiles dealt have been played. A VP scoring round them occurs comparing Military strengths of each player. VP is awarded and a new round begins with the next set of tiles.

After 3 rounds of play, groups of tiles are scored for VP based upon Organizations built as well as credits earned and final Military strengths of each player.

Player with the most VP is the winner.

For the 2 player game, a dummy hand of tiles is dealt and players use that dummy hand as part of the hand passing portion of the game.

Winner - 2013 In-A-Tin/Express Contest - Best Pre-Contest Game

A game for the 2013 2-p PnP Game Design Contest.

A worker-placement game in which the two players are competing Funeral Home operators. The goal is to satisfy the funerary wishes of the deceased through the collection of coffins, cemetery plots, and headstones. The harder to please the client is, the closer you are to satisfying the deceased's wishes, and the faster you can accomplish it, the more points you're awarded at their funeral. But, should you fail to keep them happy, your customers might crawl up from their tombs to complain!

Apart from standard worker-placement fare, this game utilizes a unique time mechanic that dictates when and where players may place their workers. More difficult actions require more time to complete, and players must balance the use of their time against the monetary costs of their actions.

Goblin Pit Fight is a card game in which players choose one of four tribes of Goblins. This fast paced game generally takes 30-60 minutes to play. The object is to create the best hand; outscoring other players and possibly defeating the pit for extra points. It has an easy to learn, multi-level scoring system that allows players to be creative when building a hand. Each player keeps a camp, where they can secure cards for future hands.

Gameplay overview:

Each player is dealt 7 cards from which they have to make a four card hand.  Players put one card into the center as their Pit card.  They then have two cards left over for their Camp which rolls from turn to turn allowing players to always have two cards in reserve for future hands.

Scoring is based on card icons such as tribe, class, bone value, and Pit strength.  All of these icons allow for different ways to score points each hand.

boardgame: Donald Featherstone's Wargaming Pike and Shot Revised Edition
description:
From the back cover:

"Each battle described in this book is a significant and outstanding example of the art of war and the 
development of military tactics period. They are highly suitable for scaling-down to realistic table-top proportions because of the size of the armies and the area of the battlefield

By refighting them, the wargamer can transform themselves into an
historical commander, savour his excitement and triumphs and suffer failure in judgements and disappointments.

This book contains:

An introduction to wargaming the period,
fifteen battles, each illustrated with maps,
notes on suitable rules and wargaming figures,
an updated bibliography and
a previously unpublished set of English Civil War rules by Donald Featherstone

The History of Wargaming Project is edited by John Curry.
It aims to present the very best of classic wargaming books and rules to a modern audience."

boardgame: Donald Featherstone's Wargamer's Handbook Of The American War Of Independence Wargaming 1775-1783
description:
From the back cover:

"A guide to the men, the weapons, the battles, and campaigns and how to re-fight them in miniature. The American War of Revolution 1775-1783 is a war of battles bearing colourful names, most of them ideally suited for re-fighting on table-top battlefields. The skilful general can triumphantly turn historical defeats into victories. This book tells the wargamer how to don the mantle of Washington and lead American Armies against British regulars to display greater tactical genius than Burgoyne and Cornwallis. After outlining the historical course of the war, the soldiers of both sides and their styles of fighting are described and commanders evaluated. This means re-fighting the major battles as wargames are explained, with maps of the battlefields transformed into table-top terrains. Hints on formulating rules include two complete sets that authentically simulate warfare of the period. A new chapter, with a previously unpublished set of rules by Donald Featherstone, is included in this edition."

boardgame: A Game of Thrones: The Card Game – A Journey's End
description:
Game description from the publisher:

The dwarf shrugged. "Do you want to wager your throne upon a woman's whim? Go to Westeros, though ... ah, then you are a rebel, not a beggar. Bold, reckless, a true scion of House Targaryen, walking in the footsteps of Aegon the Conqueror. A dragon."
    –George R.R. Martin, A Dance with Dragons

A Journey's End is the sixth and final Chapter Pack in the "A Song of the Sea" cycle for A Game of Thrones: The Card Game! Will your journey end in ruin or conquest? The pack's sixty new cards (three copies each of twenty different cards) launch players into heated naval battles and chaotic melees with five new Warships, five cards to support the game's Melee keyword, Mercenary lords, renowned Captains, and three new Prayers for the devout as they call to the Seven for guidance.

A Journey's End also concludes the "A Song of the Sea" cycle with three new cards to support the naval () enhancement. Each of the Great Houses has wagered its future upon a path it hopes will lead toward dominion over the Seven Kingdoms. At A Journey's End, it is time for you to throw in your lot. Will you wager your shot at the throne upon a whim? Or will you steer a true course – bold, direct, and fearless – as a conquerer come to claim your birthright?
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire
Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: A Game of Thrones – The Card Game (LCG)

description:
From the back cover:
"Neil Thomas presents a set of fast-playing rules for wargaming the conflicts that reshaped Europe in the period 1815-78. This often-neglected period includes such significant conflicts as the Crimean War, the Italian Risorgimento, the wars of Bismarck's Prussia against Denmark, Austro-Hungary and France and ends with the Russo-Turkish war. Tactically, it saw armies struggle to adapt Napoleonic doctrines to incorporate important technological advances such as breech-loading rifles, greatly improved artillery and the first machine guns.A brief analysis of the essential strategic and tactical military developments of the period sets the context for the elegantly simple rules which are easy to learn, quick to play and yet deliver realistic outcomes. A selection of generic scenarios, covering flank attacks, pitched battles and meeting engagements, is supported by army lists for twenty-eight different armies. There are also twelve [ten, in fact] historical scenarios, ranging from the Battle of the Alma in the Crimean War to Sedan in 1870, the decisive battle of the Franco- Prussian War, each with historical background, deployment map, orders of battle and any special rules for that engagement. Useful appendices include a guide to further reading, an overview and price guide to the many scales and ranges of figures available, and a selection of useful addresses for the gamer. This is the ideal book for those new to gaming this period and veterans alike."

There are 5 types of generic scenarios:
Pitched Battle, Meeting Engagement, Rearguard Action, Flank Attack and Mini-Game.
And 10 (not 12 as the above shows) historical scenarios.
16 pages of color photos.

boardgame: Donald Featherstone's Lost Tales including Wargaming Rules 300 BC to 1945
description:
From the publisher:

"Donald Featherstone was one of the dozen or so people who, in the 20th century, turned wargaming into both a hobby and a more useful tool for military professionals.' James F. Dunnigan 'Don’s Book 'War Games' in 1962 was an amazing thunderclap to an entire generation of would-be wargamers.' Paddy Griffith Through forty books on wargaming and military history, literally thousands of magazine articles and countless talks, Don’s energetic efforts made wargaming widely available, even respectable. However, despite this proliferation of writing, some areas remained hidden. 'Lost Tales' covers some of these omissions. This book covers diverse material from a lifetime’s experience of wargaming including: 'My War - Sergeant Featherstone 7893763' 'Modern Wargaming – How it all Began in the 1960’s.'

No less than twelve sets of Don’s favourite rules from ancients to World War II, including two unpublished sets. The History of Don’s unit, the 51st Tank Battalion The Royal Tank Regiment."
