description:
Game description from the publisher:

The "Eternal War" cycle in Warhammer: Invasion The Card Game  comes to a climactic conclusion with the battles inspired by Faith and Steel! As the Battle Pack's sixty new cards (three copies each of twenty individual cards) bring the forces of Order and Destruction to the battlefield, the Empire turns to its Mages, supports, and the legendary Balthazar Gelt. Will tricks and traps made possible by the Ambush and Raider keywords win the day? Or will one army rise to power, backed only by the sheer, brute strength of its units?
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: Warhammer Invasion – Living Card Game

boardgame: A Game of Thrones: The Card Game – The Captain's Command
description:
Game description from the publisher:

Ships and sailors steer the course of history in The Captain’s Command, the fifth Chapter Pack in the "A Song of the Sea" cycle for A Game of Thrones: The Card Game! Its sixty cards (three copies each of twenty individual cards) allow players to make greater use of the cycle's new naval challenge enhancement. Players will also find new locations, potent Prayers, powerful blood magic, and the first addition to the game's melee titles found in the Core Set.
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire
Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: A Game of Thrones – The Card Game (LCG)

description:
Retreat to Woodbury Falls takes place days after a catastrophic yet preternatural tectonic plate shift left the land riddled with sinkholes and worse... a collapsed society.

On the board you control three different survivalist leaders on their quest to take over competing base camps. Players survive by gathering and trading resources to recruit other survivors to their own camps and exile those who hold them back.

Once players have recruited an efficient survival group and have equipped themselves with enough tools and utilities by way of deck building they can move towards the final goal... take over a rival base camp.

Retreat to Woodbury Falls utilizes the popular deck management mechanic but still allows players control of physical board pieces. The most unique aspect to the game is building a deck based around card combat. You will be able to select different class survivor cards throught the game. You will find synergies and combos between the survivors and the items you equip to them both during deck building and combat. Yes, You actually get to use your deck in combat against other players to win the game!

Retreat to Woodbury Falls is an easy to learn game that still maintains a high level of strategic command and decision-making.

Kasta is an Old Norse word, meaning to throw away, discard or dispose of.

A dice game for 2,3 or 4 players.

You don't roll the dice in Kasta you discard them; this makes the gameplay similar to games like Rummy, Dominoes, and Uno the exception being that in Kasta there is no hidden information.

Objective of the game is to create and put on air two advertising campaigns for famous brands.
Each player represents an advertising agency that should develop the advertising campaigns. Each campaign is made by several elements, that should be collected by moving on the board with a dice roll. The elements are: the testimonial of the campaigns, the media that will publish the advertising, various agency figures needed to develop the advertising (varying according to the brief).
The board represents a skyline of a modern city. The game start in the month of September and ends in August.
The winner is the player that first presents two successful advertising campaigns.

The game has been produced and distributed by the Gruppo Essevi agency, as a gift for its customers for Christmas 1990.
You can now play MDA with a fifth hero class, the Beastmaster.  Beastmasters are aided by their animal companions, the swift falcon, the nimble ferrets, the deadly wolf and the mighty bear.

From the box:

"Astúcia e malícia num jogo de polícia e ladrão!"

Pega Ladrão is a brazilian game from the early 80's with a Cops and Robbers theme, where players take turns trying to scoring points. It was published by Grow and there's no info on a re-release of it.

The game starts with the "Jail" card on the table. The Cop and the Robber player take turns, placing cards on the table, that shows a maze of streets. The Robber player must lay his cards in a way to form a free passageway as far as the jail as possible, while the Cop player must put him on a dead end.

A unique strategy game for two to four players. The objective is to get two of your hexagons from your corner into each of the opponents’ corner. The hexagons are moved by playing one of 13 cards you are dealt. The trick is to choose which card to use at the right time. The one who does will win. by cards with 13 cards dealt to each player. Players decide which card to use each turn. Every player has a chance of winning the game right up until the end.

It’ll make you think!

The game board shows a map of New York. Players roll a die and move through the city, trying to reach the famous sites (especially those two whose cards they drew at the beginning and which are marked). Additionally, taxis, buses and the tube can be used to get around more quickly, but they have to be paid for in travel chips. When a player reaches a site, s/he is asked a question. If answering correctly, the site card can be discarded and a new travel chip is drawn. When reaching an unmarked site, they can earn a new site card (if that belongs to someone else, it can be "stolen"). 
When a player can reach both sites they drew at the beginning and gain two more site cards and return to their starting location, s/he wins the game.

Questions come in two difficulty levels, as the game is designed for language learning. So the difficulty does not refer to the knowledge needed to answer the question, but to the English speaking ability.
With this expansion you can play Travalgo with 5 or 6 players.

Each player chooses a fire station and helmet of the same color. 
Alarm Cards are turned face down and placed on the table. Fireman #1 picks an alarm card and says "fire is at ------- (name shown on the card)

Fireman #2 spins the spinner and moves his helmet the number of spaces shown on the spinner.

The fireman who first reaches the fire removes the card putting out the fire.

All firemen then return to their station and play continues with each player reporting a fire. The game ends when any player puts out 3 fires.

In Bump Bump Pig, piggies with many different characteristics live in a peaceful town, and if they "bump" against someone, they bring a change of fortune to the other individual, possibly good, possibly not so good.

Players take turns acting as the Master, and in each round the Master receives piggy cards from other players. Some piggies will allow both players to score or take some special action, while others might have bad effects. The Master can use piggy cards in front of himself to avoid harm or seek out the highest reward.

Game description from the publisher:

Ready, set, match! Featuring lights and sounds from PopCap's popular Facebook game and mobile app, Hasbro's Bejeweled Blitz dazzles with sparkling, translucent three-dimensional gem tiles. Kids will have fun finding matches of three while building gem towers. The first to get rid of all their gem tiles before time runs out wins!

WPA: Work Progress Action invites players to participate in relief projects like those that were operating in America during the Great Depression. 
Monopoly style game: roll and move around the game board, draw a card, try to collect the most money and win the game.

This is described as a fun tongue-twister game.

The nicely illustrated cards are shuffled and placed face down on the table.

The first player picks the top card from the pile, notes the text, place the card face down in front of him and speaks the tongue-twisting sentence aloud. If he is deemed to be correct, without pause or error, he gets to keep the card. But if he has made ​​a mistake or speaks too slowly, he has to pass the card to his left neighbour, who makes the same attempt.

If none of the players could recite the phrase correctly, the card is placed under the deck.

Winner is the player with the most cards after the deck has been exhausted.

Welcome skilled treasure hunter to the ever-changing Echo Dungeon. It will take strategy, skill, and a fair amount of luck to make it to the treasure. Choose your equipment well for it may be the difference between untold wealth and leaving empty handed. However, the real question isn't if you can make it to the treasure but if you can make it back out.

Echo Dungeon is a light PnP dungeon crawler. Where the objective is to make it to the Treasure Room and back to the Entrance Stairs first. The only drawback is that most of rooms have traps that are trying to prevent you from doing so. This is achieved by placing randomly picked tiles as you make your way through the dungeon.

This game can be played with 1-4 players.

Publisher Blurb:

Mercenary, executioner, murderer ... there isn't an infamous title that Wolfgang hasn't taken to heart. His great physical prowess isn't the only reason that he has become part Gaul's personal forces. The blood of the Naga runs through his veins, making him able to poison his enemies with just a touch of his skin.

Dark Azur Alliance Warrior — Lv. 40

Poison Ramalen: Any unit that comes into contact with Wolfgang for the first time suffers Blind Lv2. Effect 14.
 Son of the Naga: Enemy units within Wolfgang's Control Zone suffer double damage due to the Poison state.
 Poisoned Weapons: If any attack made by Wolfgang causes damage, the target suffers Poison Lv 2. Effect 14.
 ooo — Cupra (Ki, Attack)
 +4 Attack. If this attack causes damage, the effect of the Poison state is 16 instead of 14.

With the Merseburg Promokarte is Merseburg (hometown of "Spiele-Schmiede)part of the game. It is only another card in the game.
With this Promocard I change the basecard No. 15 from the basegame starting map and I have the other station MERSEBURG. There is no change in the game, only another station.

Cozmica is a 2-player abstract strategy game. The theme of Cozmica is about relocating Earth life in DNA form, to a new world in the cosmos for survival.                                   Both players sit on opposite sides of a 9 x 9 line grid board similar to a Go board.
Each player receives either 18 black or 18 white “Go stone like” playing pieces (Bio-ships). Playing pieces are marked with either an Animal, Plant, or Microbe symbol. The pieces interact with each other similar to the mechanics of Rock, Paper, Scissors, i.e.
Animals eat Plants, Plants absorb Microbes, and Microbes infect Animals.
All pieces move the same way…1 or 2 places, Horizontally, Vertically or Diagonally.
The objectives of the game are: 
1. Surround and capture opponents Bio-ships.
2. Intercept and eliminate opponents Bio-ships.
3. Cross your own Bio-ships to the other side and off the board. 
4. You acquire colonies by capturing opponents Bio-ships or crossing your own to the other side and off the board.
5. To win the game, you must establish at least one colony. They may be mixed [your’s and/or your opponents as long as they form a colony.
A colony is: 1 each of all 3 DNA types, 1 Animal, 1 Plant, 1 Microbe.
Playing time is about 30 to 60 minutes.

boardgame: Imperium Chronicles: Fleets at War – Claws of Chaos
description:
Claws of Chaos is the first expansion for the Fleets at War! tactical starship combat game.  It is played on the floor or a large table with cardboard counters representing capital ships, fighters, and other space craft.  Simplified game mechanics let players engage in small skirmishes all the way up to large-scale battles involving dozens of ships.

Features:

--New Factions: the K'thonians and the Talions
--New ships: battlecruisers, drone carriers (and drones), torpedo boats and torpedo bombers
--New weapons: particle guns, plasma bolt cannons, mines, torpedoes and anti-matter missiles.
--Full-color ship counters + additional counters for fighters & missiles
--Stat Cards (one for each ship class)
--Seven new scenarios

Note: You will need to have the Fleets at War! rulebook to use this expansion.

The object of this card game is to get Archie's pals and gals off the moving maze. Each player chooses two character cards which are put in folded cardboard bases and moved along other cards representing the high school.

Bushland Friends is a game for young children which has some similarity to Candy Land. On each turn a player spins a spinner with different Australian animals on it, then moves to the next space depicting that animal. When landing on a wombat, a player spins again and may move forward or backward depending on what is spun. Players can also bump each other off of spaces.

boardgame: Group of Tanks: North Europe, Spring 1945
description:
Simple 2-player hex-and-counter tank battle wargame.

Each tank unit have 3 traits: movement point, attack strength and defense strength.

The objective is to capture the opponent's capital (the hex with square icon)

Combat is very simple, with attacker roll a die, the result add to the attack strength of the attack unit.  The defender roll a die, the result add to the defense strength and add by the terrain modifier.  If the attacker's total more than the defender's total, the defender is eliminated.  Otherwise, nothing happen.

A free game that came with a Japanese anime magazine.

The game uses the anime show Dancouga as the background setting.

It is a simple 1 to 4 player Solitaire/co-op game.  Players pick one of the four main character of the anime show, each of which has different set of traits.

All characters have a Movement Allowance and Combat Strength.  Combat Strength increases during the game with accumulated "power".

The map have 37 hexes divided into 6 areas.  Most hexes are marked with an enemy strength and event name.  At the start of each turn, a player rolls two dice: one indicates which area and the other indicates in which hex of the area the event happens.  The player moves his character counter on the map to encounter the enemy marked there.

Comparing the combat strength of the character and the enemy, the higher side wins.  The character will get the stated number of "power" if he wins.

The players win the game once there are no more active events on the map.  The players lose if 40 turns pass without them meeting that victory condition.

Component:
A rule book (also a characters and mechanical design art book of the anime show)
Counter sheet (at the back cover of the rule book, the counters must be cut out before play)
A map (the other side of the map is a poster of the other anime show, Zeta Gundam, not related to the game)
Six-side dice are required but not included in the game

[Eng] Inspired from Knightmare Chess, a french group of Go game created "Tsunami sur le Goban" which plays on a 13x13 board.

It's a card game for two players : During a classic Go game, players can use cards to change some rules, stones placement...


                    
                
            
        
    


[French] Inspiré par Tempête sur l'échiquier, le Groupe Auvergnat de Go a imaginé "Tsunami sur le Goban" se jouant sur 13x13.

On joue sur 13x13 avec un jeu de carte qui sert de pioche pour les deux joueurs.

Durant la partie, les joueurs peuvent changer les règles du jeu, modifier le placement des pierres...

This is a card game play in turns.  In a turn, each player play a card from hand to the 4 row of the card.  When a sixth card play to the row, the player who play the sixth card get the first to fifth cards (5 cards) and the sixth card become the new first card of the row.  The sum of the victory points (VP) on the 5 cards is the VP for the player.  Different cards have different VP value, some positive and some negative.

There are rules to play the card, only higher number card may be place to the cards on the table.  If no card can be place, the longest row on the mat will be discard and replace with a new card from the deck.

Play continue until all players play all the cards in the hand (10 cards).

The one with the most VP win.

Component:
80 cards with number from 1 to 80
7 special card (same card back as the number card)
1 rule sheet
1 play mat
A small game box

User Description

Shiploque is a rules supplement for the miniatures game Flintloque Reloaded for adding ships and boats to the game.

The rules have profiles on five ships and rules on movement, crew actions. bording, artillery and collisions.

The rules work best when using one to three ships on a average size table.

Published in Ragnarok, issue 54 and on the Chimerae Hobby Group web page.

boardgame: Flames of War: Normandy Battles – Wargaming D-Day and Beyond
description:
Publisher Blurb:

In the early morning darkness of 6 June 1944, the largest armada of ships the world has ever seen heaves to off the Normandy coastline. Aboard, thousands of Allied soldiers wait in readiness for their date with destiny. Months of planning, training and preparation are now behind them. On this day they will undertake the greatest amphibious assault in history, and on their success or failure hangs the very fate of the liberation of Europe from the jackboot of Nazi domination. D-Day is finally here! Normandy Battles contains the best scenarios from the original D‑Day series of books, as well as the Battlefront Studio's favourite web scenarios, and updates them for the third edition of Flames Of War, and the two D‑Day compilations: Turning Tide and Earth & Steel. Normandy Battles also includes rules for airborne landings and amphibious assaults, allowing your forces to wage war from the land, sea and sky. The revised Total War rules put a new twist on larger games, giving your battalions additional abilities, and allowing you to field entire support companies. Inside You Will Find: History of the D-Day invasion of Normandy and the subsequent battles to liberate France. Airborne assault mission and rules. Amphibious assault mission and rules. 13 of the best Flames of War scenarios covering the Normandy Campaign. Total War rules allowing you to put bigger forces on larger tables, with two Total War missions.

Publisher Blurb:

The Type-009 is Black Sun's latest evolution of elite unstoppable necromantic weapons. They have no qualms with destroying themselves to destroy their designated target.

Neutral Black Sun Warrior Undead — Lv. 25+

Whip of Bones: The Type-009 may make close combat basic Attacks on enemy units within 2 inches of itself.
 Neuronal Poison: If any Attack (basic or special) made by the Type-009 causes damage, the target suffers Slow Lv. 1. Effect 15.
 o — Implosion (Special Attack)
 +3 Attack. This attack affects all enemy units that are within 4 inches of its target. The Type-009 is eliminated after resolving this attack.
 o — Core (Special Effect)
 An enemy unit engaged with the Type-009 suffers Paralysed Lv. 1 and Slow Lv. 1. The Type-009 is eliminated after resolving this ability. Effect 18.

Publisher Blurb:

Juliette is the ultimate messenger, a woman able to become one with her leaders and transmit any command to their subordinates as an extension of themselves. Even if she works for the Azur Alliance, her heart only serves the High Arbiter Arkeid, who she considers her big sister.

Neutral Azur Alliance Prowler — Lv. 25

Control Nexus: At the beginning of the game, choose an arbiter in your party, to whom Juliette is allocated.
 As long as Juliette's in play, this arbiter receives one additional Order Point per turn. Furthermore, as long as the arbiter has Line of Sight to Juliette, they can use her control area as an extension of their Influence Zone.
 Extension of Will: Juliette can, if desired, be activated simultaneously with her Arbiter. This ability doesn't allow her to activate twice per turn.
 oo — Stealth (Subterfuge, Effect)
 Juliette becomes Hidden. Upkeep: o
 oo — Reign of Chaos (Subterfuge, Effect)
 An enemy Unit inside Juliette's Control Zone loses one Action Point. Effect 16.
 oo — Whisper (Subterfuge, Effect, Reactive)
 A friendly Unit inside Juliette's Control Zone gets +1 Attack and +1 Defense until end of turn. This ability can only be used if Juliette is hidden.

Publisher Blurb:

Thanks to her legendary aim and her unparalleled agility, Kira was one of the first people to become an Aria of Wissenschaft. Her love of money and volatile nature keep her volunteering for mission after mission. This pewter figure is unpainted and unassembled. Requires additional Anima Tactics figures to play.

Neutral Wissenschaft Prowler — Lv. 40

oo — Impure Shot (Subterfuge, Ranged Attack)
 Distance (4 inches). After this attack is resolved, Kira may move up to 8 inches, even if she has already moved this turn.
 ooo — Buster Shot (Subterfuge, Ranged Attack)
 Distance (8 inches). +2 Attack / +3 Damage. After this attack is resolved Kira moves 4 inches directly away from her target (this can't cause collisions), even if she has already moved this turn.
 oo+ — Buckshot (Subterfuge, Ranged Attack)
 Distance (8 inches). Kira may spend any number of additional AP when paying for this attack. For each additional AP spent in this way she makes an additional attack against the same target.
 ooo — Valkyrie (Subterfuge, Dodge, Ranged Attack)
 Distance (4 inches). If Kira successfully Dodges, she may move up to 2 inches, even if she has already move this turn. If Kira is engaged she automatically Escapes. After she moves due to this ability, she may then make a Ranged Attack against her attacker is she is in range.

Come discover Kotanook forest during deer migration, when predators and prey take on a gruesome battle to survive. One by one, become each of the wild's most legendary animals, such as a grizzly bear, a wolf or a cougar. The "Kotanook" board game is a unique reproduction of a wild environment, where  two opponents take turns combining attacks and evasions. Each player leads a small group of animals, each of them with its own specific qualities (speed, strength and dexterity) in order to collect the highest number of points and become master of their territory. A stimulating board game where chance and strategy meet the instincts of the most ferocious beasts!

From the back of the box:
Take a deep breath and dive into fascinating shark-themed card games like Shark's Tooth Rummy, Sink or Swim, and Wild Wobbegong. Want to know which sharks are nocturnal? Which can leap out of the water? Which is known as "white death"? This fact-filled deck turns the world of sharks into games for your brain.

User Summary:
Shark cards are similar to a standard set of playing cards except each of the 4 suits contains 12 cards (rather than 13). Each of the 12 cards contains a picture of a shark and a description.

There are also 12 Challenge Cards that ask players to look for Sharks with particular characteristics.

The game has rules for 5 different games:
Deep Dive (Similar to Go Fish)
Sink or Swim (Similar to Concentration)
Shark's Tooth Rummy (Similar to Gin Rummy)
Wild Wobbegong (Similar to Crazy Eights)
Food Chain (Similar to Solitaire)

A card game for 2-6 dog's best friends.
 Every player is a trainer in large and famous agility school with the goal to fill as many training groups as fast as possible.

During player's turn he/she can perform following activities (in order listed below):

1) Fill one training group
2) Select one action from following list:

- Make a personal offer
- Retrieve dogs from common waiting list
- Close the training group
- Pass – do nothing

Game ends after one player has filled 3 training groups. All players count their victory points from filled training groups, player with the most points will be the winner.

Components:
18 training group cards (with 2, 4 or 6 points - 6 each card)
64 dogs cards (8 different breeds - 8 each card)

SDS Sparekassen published this game, heavily inspired by Matador / Monopoly. It was published at the latest in 1983, but the specific year could not be found.

You try to earn money by building up a business empire with help from SDS Sparekassen.


SDS Sparekassen udgav dette spil, tungt inspireret af Matador/Monopoly. Det blev udgivet senest 1983, men det specifikke årstal kunnet ikke findes.

Du prøver på at tjene så mange penge som muligt, ved at oprette en masse forretninger med hjælp fra SDS Sparekassen.

[Translation of German Text]
The first prize in each Munchkin Fu Release tournament was a stylish black obi (karate belt) with the following text: "Black Belt of Munchkinism +3 bonus. Really."

1. The Black Belt of Munchkinism and its bonus has been approved by none other than Steve Jackson. It is official and may be used in any game.

2. Each player may only wear one black belt of Munchkinism. Whether it is worn around the waist or as a sash (for the somewhat stouter player), is irrelevant. The Black Belt of Munchkinism is always valid. Although the belt is very long, it may not be used by two or more players at one time.

3. The Black Belt is considered small object, has no value, and does not occupy a slot (hands, arms, etc). If it needs to be taken off, it is moved to (or next to) the discard pile.

4. If a player loses their Black Belt of Munchkinism (due to trade, thievery, or death), he can not put in on again, unless he changes his gender.

It should be understood that a stolen Black Belt Munchkinism is given back to the owner after the end of the game.

[ORIGINAL TEXT]
Der erste Preis bei jedem Munchkin Fu Release Turnier war ein stylischer schwarzer Obi (Karategürtel) mit folgender Aufschrift: "Schwarzer Gürtel des Munchkintums. +3 Bonus. Wirklich."

1. Der Schwarze Gürtel des Munchkintum und sein Bonus sind von niemand geringerem als Steve Jackson abgesegnet worden. Damit ist er offiziell und darf in jedem Spiel eingesetzt werden.

2. Jeder Spieler darf nur einen Schwarzen Gürtel des Munchkintums tragen. Ob er um die Hüfte oder als Schärpe (für die etwas stärkeren Spieler) getragen wird, ist gleichgültig. Der Schwarze Gürtel des Munchkintums kann jederzeit angelegt werden. Auch wenn der Gürtel besonders lang ist, darf er nicht von zwei oder mehr Spielern gleichzeitig eingesetzt werden.

3. Der Schwarze Gürtel des Munchkintums gilt als kleiner Gegenstand, er hat keinen Wert und belegt keinen Slot (Hände, Rüstung o.ä.). Wenn er abgelegt werden muss, wandert er auf den Ablagestapel (bzw. daneben).

4. Sollte ein Spieler den Schwarzen Gürtel des Munchkintums verlieren (Handel, Dieb, Tod oder beim-um-den-Tisch-tanzen-abgefallen) kann er ihn nicht wieder anlegen, es sei denn, er wechselt das Geschlecht.

Es sollte selbstverständlich sein, dass ein geklauter Schwarzer Gürtel des Munchkintums nach dem Ende des Spiels vom Besitzer an den Eigentümer zurück gegeben wird.

Conquering Corsairs is a card game in which you take the role of pirates, trying to gain gold and reputation to become the most feared and famous ship to sail the Silver Seas. You will take your ship to strange and dangerous locations, recruit new crew members to your cause, do battle with other pirates, and put plunder in your hold!

During the game, players take different actions during each of the four seasons of a turn.  In Spring, you play pirates on to the deck of your ship. They will be needed to attack locations and recruit new shipmates in the following seasons.  In Summer, you sail your ship between islands in the Silver Seas and can either try to conquer them or explore new locations, looking for plunder.  In Fall, you recruit new pirates, taking them from either common supply piles or a personal supply that only you have access to.  In Winter, you can stow treasure (cards) in your hold, that will gain you plunder at the end of the game. The Pirate King is also determined in Winter, based on the prestige that each player has obtained from their locations and any powerful pirates still in play.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Cholopoly is a game based on our funny experiences in Peru. Each player will be a car driver. And will be driving through many dangerous streets of Peru in Lima.

You can buy business, escape from thieves, and bribing corrupt police. Face events like inflation, terrorism, mining company strikes, and supports corruption and become the richest one among all players.

There is no place for fair play. Destroy the other businesses, hire thieves and corrupt cops, to steal and bribe the other players. Appropriate business and foreign characters, accumulate dirty money and become a millionaire, and lose it all from one turn to another.

Do not misunderstand; this is only taken with mockery, you just have a good time with your friends

Cholopoly wishes you a fun and unproductive game.

Cholopoly: Rie, Jode..., Juega


(Spanish version)Cholopoly es un juego basado en nuestra criollada peruana. Cada jugador será un conductor, manejando por las peligrosas calles de lima en  Peru. Comprando negocios, escapando de ladrones, y sobornando policias corruptos. Afronta eventos como inflación, terrorismo, huelgas mineras, y soporta casos de corrupción, e intenta volverte el más rico entre todos los jugadores.

No hay lugar para el juego limpio. Destruye los negocios de otros, contrata ladrones y policías corruptos, para que roben y sobornen a los otros jugadores.

Aprópiate de los negocios y personajes ajenos, acumula dinero corrupto.  Conviértete en millonario, y piérdelo todo de un turno a otro.

No malinterpreten, esto es solo tomar con burla algunas situaciones.  Y pasar un buen tiempo con tus amigos

Cholopoly les desea un entretenido e improductivo juego.

Cholopoly

Rie, Jode..., Juega

Colors far too awesome for a rainbow . . . try our Bubblegum Pink, Ninja Black, Glow in the Dark, Frosted, and Invisible Munchkins and Munchkin Babes, all in one package!

These pawns were previously only available in our Gen Con capsule machine, but now you can get the complete set without using up all your laundry money. And of course, they have a rule!

Any time you are entitled to go up a level -- or when you do something that WOULD earn you a level if you weren't already Level 9 -- you may, instead, place a Munchkin pawn in front of you. In any combat, you may discard it for a 1d6 bonus. You may only have one in front of you at a time. Unless, of course, you "lose" these rules and tell the other players that you're allowed to have more. Six is a good number. Eight is even better!

8 pawns in a poly bag. 4 Munchkin men & 4 Munchkin babes

Smile and Like It is a family-friendly card game for 3 - 8 players with a unique twist.  On your turn, play a card and do what it says, simple as that.  Generally the card will instruct you to claim chips (some face up, some face down, all of which have smiley faces of differing levels of happiness on one side) from the center pot, trade chips you've already collected with other players, and so on.

Here's the twist: you don't automatically get to draw a card at the end of your turn.  Instead, you have to claim a special "draw" chip (differently colored than all the other chips in the pot) and have it remain in your score pile until your next turn, at which point you can replenish your hand of cards... but that's all you can do on that turn.  Of course, there aren't enough draw chips in the pot for everyone.

So the tension builds: how long do you dare wait to take the draw chip?  If you run out of cards, you lose chips from your pot to the players seated on either side of you... but if you happen to have a draw chip in your score pile at the end of the game, you take a massive penalty to your final score.

The game comes with three unique methods of scoring right in the box.  One scoring system simply gives more points for happier smiley faces (very family friendly), one scores points for sets of different types of smileys (a bit more strategic, but still family friendly), and one uses patterns of smileys printed right on the cards, giving the players another option on how to play a card: playing it as a scoring card by spending the specific pattern of smileys in the score pile (introducing a hand management aspect to the game suitable for more hardcore gamers).

All in all, Smile and Like It is a game that is simple to learn and play, and yet difficult to master in any of its incarnations, making it a game you'll come back to again and again.

A trivia game with 780 questions and answers.

By turning the colored roulette wheel the children choose the questions for each card. They select one of the answers to score points, in which each answer option is associated with a symbol, and a color.

There are many interesting topics from the fields of history, geography, biology, wildlife, art, music, sports and culture in general etc.

The board game based on the German TVshow with relationship tests.

In the game it is important that the women know their husband well. They compete with other female teammates and put chips for different bets, eg Snacks eating contest or funny dance routines etc. If a bet is successful, the woman receives all applications and thus comes closer to the victory.

The women have to keep a cool head and play good bets. Essential is a good knowledge of the strengths of the man who in turn must show full commitment in the betting order to seal victory. "My husband can" is an ideal party game and suitable for couples or those who want to be there.

Couple with the most points is the winner.

The letter-cards are mixed and dealt 5 cards for each player, the top card of the remaining stack is revealed.
The objective is to get rid of cards.
In turn player can either use his cards to form a word or place the letters on the top cards that they continue the word.
If player can't form a word he draws a new card.
Words score points - the longer words more points - and player with the most points at the end of the game is the winner.

boardgame: Bingo Pocket Cards: Spaß mit englischer Grammatik
description:
Simple quiz format card game. Distribute the cards amongst the players. Then take turns asking each other questions from the first card. The cards have numerous questions on them, with the answers on the back.

Rules are not clear about how many questions are asked per card. I would suggest just one. If the correct answer is given, the answering player takes the card.

The game proceeds for as long as you want to play or until a pre-set target of correct answers is reached. The player (or team) with the most cards, wins.

The cards all relate to fairly simple English grammar questions.

With over 250 questions and answers about big cities, additional Joker and booster cards that affect the course of the game, provide games and guessing fun with the Giga-effect.

There are questions one side and 4 variants another side of the card. Draw a card, ask a question and read the variants. Who bet the correct answer scores a point.

Winner is the one with the most points after the deck is exhausted.
family: Series: TOP ASS Giga Quiz (Alternburger Spielkarten)

description:
With over 250 questions and answers about inventions, additional Joker and booster cards that affect the course of the game, provide games and guessing fun with the Giga-effect.

There are questions one side and 4 variants another side of the card. Draw a card, ask a question and read the variants. Who bet the correct answer scores a point.

Winner is the one with the most points after the deck is exhausted.
family: Series: TOP ASS Giga Quiz (Alternburger Spielkarten)

description:
With over 250 questions and answers about horses, additional Joker and booster cards that affect the course of the game, provide games and guessing fun with the Giga-effect.

There are questions one side and 4 variants another side of the card. Draw a card, ask a question and read the variants. Who bet the correct answer scores a point.

Winner is the one with the most points after the deck is exhausted.
family: Series: TOP ASS Giga Quiz (Alternburger Spielkarten)

description:
Name translates to 'Highlights of the Game: France', part of a series of such games with different countries being the subject.

This is a game where you can show off your knowledge of France by answering questions on History, Art, Culture, Sport and Food.By answering questions correctly, and with a little luck, you will collect souvenirs of the country.

The game consists of 48 question cards, 30 souvenir cards, one round ard, 1 rules card and 1 pamphlet with informative background information about your holiday destination.

On each card there are questions in 5 categories and each is designated with its own icon and colour. The five categories in full are;
Eten en Drinken (Green) - Eating and Drinking
Geschiedenis (Yellow) - History
Sport, Spel en Vakantie (Orange) - Sport, Games and Holidays
Kunst en Cultuur (Red) - Art and Culture
Stad en Land (Blue) - Cities and Countries

On each card the category questions are in a different order. On the back of each card are the answers. You play in rounds. The Round Card is placed on the bottom of the deck. You start with the first question on the card you have just drawn. If the Round Card is on the top of the deck, you put it back on the bottom and then ask the second question, and so on until 6 rounds have been played. On the question cards there are also commands, recognizable because the icon box is blank. These commands are on the lines of "Change a Souvenir to a different colour, Lose a souvenir, and so on.

The Question reader is the player to the left of the player whose turn it is. If the question is answered correctly, the player receives a souvenir in the same category of the question. If the player answers incorrectly then that player must lose a souvenir (if he has one) in that category. The question card is then placed on the bottom of the stack and the turn passes to the next player in a clockwise direction.

Maximum number Souvenirs
The number of souvenirs that a player may have in a category is a a maximum of '2 players - 3', '3 or 4 players - 2'.. If a player gets a question in the category of which he already has reached the maximum, then he can choose a souvenir of any other category if he answers correctly. At the end of your turn you can exchange any 2 matching souvenir cards for one in any other category.

The winner is the first player to get souvenirs in all 5 categories

Lądowa Obrona Wybrzeża (Land Coastal Command or Land Coastal Defence) expands Pomorze 1939 and simulates the defence of the Polish coastal line during the early stages of World War II.

Two scenarios are included: 
1) The historical one - simulating the German attack 
2) "White Eagle" - simulating a hypothetical Polish preventive offensive launched on August 25.

The expansion uses the rulebook and some of the counters from the basic game but rules are added for Mława 1939 - panic and disorganization.

A full map of the Polish coast and two sheets of counters are included.


First published in the Polish magazine "Dragon hobby" in 1998 (1/98) and republished in Taktyka i Strategia issue 29 in 2009.

Wikipedia: Land Coastal Defence

Game Description

Zombie Enclave (ZE) is a free, solitaire game of survival on a Earth dominated by Zombies: while designed as a solitaire game you can easily play ZE with two (multiplayer rules are included).

The focus of the game is the Refuge (the Enclave in the game title), the place where you're trying to defend yourself from the zombie attacks, accumulating enough resources in order to gain some additional weeks of life.

You start the game with a very simple, undefended Refuge and then you will have to plan how to protect your Survivors by adding the many "defenses" available. "Defenses" can be holes, wall reinforcements, explosive barrels, traps and so on: the idea is to replicate the full spectrum of iconic scenes seen in the zombie movies. The game sequence can then be summarized as: gain new resources, spend resources to improve your Refuge, wait for the enemy attack.

Zombies will be managed with a "buildup pool": every turn you draw a buildup chit and apply the results (normally new zombies arriving) while an "Attack!" marker will initiate the combat.

During combat the zombies will move from the upper part of the map (the external defenses) toward your Refuge: the Defenses will try to slow and/or damage the zombies but it's up to the Survivors to close the fight.

In terms of rules, the game will include a set of Standard Rules, with basic tasks and a good set of available resources to start with. Several scenarios will make possible a quick start of the game with not only your Refuge, but also an external defensive wall and a bunch of resource points that you can spend during setup to prepare your initial defenses.

Advanced rules will add more details (like Vehicles and Events), and finally the Campaign game will make possible to start really from zero, with just you and Soldier, your invaluable dog.

boardgame: Sheepdogs of Pendleton Hill: The Wolf Booster Deck
description:
From the webside:
Don’t be sheepish! The Wolf Booster Deck adds even more wolfish mischief to this quick playing game of interactive strategy.

With the wolf-card every player has more chances to move the wolf in the game.

The Matching Game You'll Flip For!
In this fun matching game for 2-6 players, kids try to be first to get their animals into the barn!  Players search their cards to complete an animal image, then use the Barnyard Flipper to reveal the next image to be matched.  Play continues until all images have been matched and all animals are back in the barn!

boardgame: 駅前タクシー (Train Station Taxi)
description: Number 9 (of 12) in the Epoch Manga game series.  Bookshelf game based on the comic strips of Fujio Akatsuka.  All movement is spin and move.  Each player controls a taxi and picks up fares from the train station.  Destination and fare collected is determined by a card draw.  Opposing players can move police cars when they land on a police icon to impede other players and force them to draw an event card.  Event cards are generally misfortunes, but there are occasional good events (tipping customer, a bonus fare, etc).  The winner is the player with the most fares collected once the are deck is exhausted.  Art on the board, pieces, and cards are a mish mash of characters from Fujio Akatsuka comics.

description:
Simple  move game for young players. (4-8)
Don't let the little mermaid die of sadness. She is stuck at low tide. Help her find her way through the castle to safety. From Alvin's Vintage Boardgames.

A classic game of happy families with a twist, comprising 7 aristocratic animals, each attired in the sartorial splendour of the Victorian era. An exciting game for all the family.

Contents : 42 cards wich combined together, create the seven "Aristo" characters :
- Dog, Cat, Hare, Ferret, Giraffe and Wolf.
Each character is divided into six parts, which when reconstitued forms the word A-R-I-S-T-O.

The seven "Aristo-Animals" come together to form the Aristo'zzle, a tableau in the manner of a jigsaw puzzle.

From 2 players to 6. 
Minimum age 4 years old.

A biathlon-themed roll-and-move trivia game with a dexterity component.

Aim of the game: Be the first to complete the course on the board three times.

How it is played: 
Players take turns rolling the two dice. If their pawn is on a ascending slope space at the beginning of their turn, they roll only one die. They move their pawn according to the die roll. 
If it lands on a grey space, nothing happens. If it lands on a green space, an action card is drawn and the action performed (move pawn backwards/forwards a certain number of spaces, lose a turn etc.). 
If it lands on a yellow space, a question card is drawn and the active player has to answer a biathlon-related question. If they succeed, their pawn advances five spaces. If they fail, they go back five spaces.
If the pawn lands on a blue space, they have to go back to start.
If a player rolls the same number on both dice, they gain a fan token, which grants them extra movement in round three.
Since the game is about biathlon, there is also a shooting exercise that takes place after rounds one and two: Players have to hit a target with a wooden marble, using a wooden catapult. Generally, each player has five shots per round to hit the five targets, but shooting cards may allow them to reload. Each missed target means extra spaces to complete in a penalty round.

HOLD THE LINE now looks at one of the most fabled conflicts in history, the Jacobite rebellions, in HIGHLAND CHARGE. These uprisings were, as of 2013, the last battles fought on British soil. Featured here are four battles from the fearsome contest: Sheriffmuir, Prestonpans, Falkirk, and Culloden. Worthington is also offering the Killiecrankie scenario free to download. The game closely aligns with the Hold the Line system, but includes rules for Highland charges, Scottish militia, and of course the feared Highland clan units.

James II lost his throne in 1689, but he and his supporters did not give the fight to reclaim his rights and titles. From 1689 to 1745 no less than four major Jacobite rebellions broke out in the Scottish highlands, often with limited aid from France and Spain. The battles were the last hurrah for a dying way of war. The Highlanders relied on assault tactics, in particular the feared Highland charge. The Loyalists, made up of British regulars and Scottish militia, used the linear tactics of the day, including volley fire, artillery barrages, and bayonets. For a time the Highlanders seemed to be tactically superior until the shattering defeat at Culloden, a battle that proved the ultimate superiority of musket and artillery fire over the claymore.

As the Jacobites can you reclaim the throne for the Stuart dynasty? As the Loyalists can you protect the Hanoverian succession and deal a final death blow to absolutism in Britain? Across the way the bagpipes can be heard. The clans have mustered, the sound of cannon deafens, men shout commands, smoke fills the air. You must HOLD THE LINE!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Explore the galaxy! Avoid Space Pirates! Move through Worm Holes! Watch out for Asteroids! Oh, and while you're at it, trade for resources and deliver them to the planets that need them.

Each Player is an independent space merchant captain jetting around the galaxy trying to collect resources to fill orders. There are 6 planets that need different resources, but each planet only sells one resource. Fill orders to make money and earn victory points. Once an order is filled, the planet has a new order. Earn additional victory points by exploring the board, but beware! The Galaxy is full of asteroids, worm holes and pirates that can will stand in your way.

The player with the most victory points at the end of the game wins!

The hex board is set up randomly for each game, so exploring the board is part of the fun! No two games will play quite the same. Who is the best space explorer and merchant captain?

Pixel Quest its a cooperative card game for 1 to 4 players, based on classic 8-bit RPGs. In this adventure game you will play as a party of heroes, travelling around to complete different quests. You’ll get an objective card at the start of the game: once the quests in it are complete, you’ll fight the final Boss. If you defeat him you’ll have won the game.

For each quest, you need to build a deck so your group of heroes can explore it. During these adventures the heroes will fight dangerous monsters, avoid deadly traps and find priceless treasures. But there is more than adventure in a hero’s life – they’ll also get rest at inns, drink at taverns and buy objects that will help them in future quests.

The greatest 8-bit RPG of all times, and you will not be playing it on a video game console, but on a table. Do you dare?

User Summary

The board game "poco a poco" is an excellent musical educational tool for any piano instructor. It is extremely versatile and can be used to facilitate learning in both group piano lessons and one-on-one piano lessons. It can be easily adapted for many or as few as two players, and the written board game "rules" are meant to broken if the needs of the class require the piano instructor to do so! The piano instructor must have these following items to actually play "poco a poco" successfully in conjunction with the board game itself: "one piano, metronome, basic technique, sight reading and ear training books/materials, manuscript paper or white board, and [little candies] or other treats." (Poco A Poco Game Rules, p. 1 poco a poco productions 1996)

Game play
Players or teams each roll die to move around the "keyboard" board, answering musical technique or term trivia and performing musical exercises in the varying categories required. Points or candies are awarded to players or teams who have completed their trivia or exercises successfully. The player or team who has the most points or candies is declared the winner.

Only a few hundred copies were produced and sold locally.  Production ceased when the designers diverged.

The board is divided into two areas: the city map and the Timeline. Moving on the city map, the players reach the most famous locations in town and collect historical cards that can then be played to advance on the Timeline. The first player to complete the path on the Timeline wins. Specific knowledge of the history of the town is not required to play the game because Wien! is not a trivia game.

Both player has equal number of Satellites on the game board.
In his turn player rolls the dice and moves his rocket on the game board. If he landed on opponent´s Satellite he takes it over and turns around his color face-up.
Player who owns all the satellites wins the game.

Based on the same game concept used in London: The Board Game, Polimi Timeline is dedicated to celebrating the 150th anniversary of the Politecnico di Milano (a.k.a. Moving on a map representing the most important locations built or renovated by the architects who graduated at the Polimi, the players collect cards than can then be played to advance on the Timeline, discovering the history of the University. The first player to complete the path on the Timeline wins.

Cyvasse is a two-player strategy board game, featured in George R.R. Martin’s “A Song of Ice and Fire” series.

     Cyvasse features ten types of “unit” (King, Dragon, Elephant, Trebuchet, Catapult, Heavy Horse, Light Horse, Crossbowman, Spearman and Rabble) with different powers and attributes and capture conditions.
   In addition, the board includes individual "Mountain" and "Fortress" tiles, allowing for a different battlefield terrain each game 
   Before play, each player arranges their half of the board secretly, placing units and terrain tiles under cover of a large dividing screen. Once arranged, the screen is removed and play proceeds in alternating turns. Each piece has its own strengths and weaknesses, and multiple units (such as Elephants and Spearmen) may be moved during a single turn.
The game ends upon the capture of an opposing player’s King.

Cyvasse combines the simple elegance of chess with elements reminiscent "Stratego" and "Blitzkrieg".

The following material represents an amalgamation of the better fan-published material found online coupled with my own ideas.  It is hoped that the following interpretation provides a balanced, interesting and (above all) fun Cyvasse experience while keeping as close as possible to the descriptions presented in the novels.
family: Books: A Song of Ice and Fire

description:
A speed game of card matching.

Opponents race to get rid of the cards in their hands by matching identical types of deadly animals. Once a match is made it is laid face up in front of you. Opponents will 'steal' the tricks in front of you if they have a matching card. The first person to lose all their cards shouts "Deadly Ruckus!", and gets a 5 point bonus for going out first. All players then gain 1 point for each card in front of them, and minus 1 for each card in their hand.

The process of stealing tricks from other players if you have a single matching card gives the game something of a fatal flaw in that slower players gain more cards in front of them.

description: Notwithstanding the title of this game – Versteck dich, kleine Schnecke! – the snail will not hide itself, but will instead need help from at least one of the players. Playable either indoors and out, this game of hide and seek has one player turning over a color chip, then hiding the game's snail in a location matching this color. The other players then work together to hunt down the snail, or chase it down individually to find it, salt it, and take a bite in triumph before anyone else can get their hands on it.

boardgame: Der will nur spielen! Das Familienspiel
description:
In Der will nur Spielen! Das Familienspiel, players compete in a wide variety of categories, each testing their animal knowledge or reflexes in varied ways. Who has a good reaction time when retrieving something? Who can remember the most signals? Who can dig out a bone hidden in a bed of roses? Who knows the favorite breeds of the other players? And who can show off her knowledge of dog breeds by answering questions?

For all of these activities and more, players are rewarded with bones when they do well, and whoever first collects twenty bones in her basket wins!

from the designer:

"The basic idea is simple: players take turns placing stones on a board, forming groups. When the board is full, each players calculates the product of the sizes of his two largest groups. Whoever has the larger number wins."

A Real Trivia Challenge! Matter of Fact is perfect for those who know a lot - or think they do... Every game card has seven questions for you to answer around a main theme. The more questions you answer correctly, the more spaces you move along the board. Stumble too much and you might even have to move backwards. The Answer Tracker shows how well you’re doing. All subjects are covered in a variety of fun formats: from Claim to Fame, Title Twist, All Mixed Up, and more. Just go down the Question List and see how you do, either on your own or with the help of teammates. It’s trivia with a whole new twist!

YOU'LL NEVER GUESS! (...or will you?) Stimulate your brain, challenge your skills of deduction, and have fun trying to guess what players are up to! Say the Presenter reads out a question like ‘Where Am I?’. It could be Dallas, France, Hawaii or somewhere else. So how will you figure out where they’re pretending to be? By asking them your own questions to narrow down the possibilities.  You could ask: ‘Would I take a plane to get there?’, ‘Is it a city?’, ‘Does it have cold winters?’, or anything else you can think of. You decide what to ask. You may also get to roll for a Letter Clue. In another round you might be asked to guess what someone's supposed to be ‘wearing’, ‘eating’, ‘holding’, and much more.

Not to be confused with "Characters from Charles Dickens," this is a card game from circa 1889. There are 37 cards in six sets of six, plus one Dickens card. Each set consists of a book card (Pickwick Papers, Oliver Twist, Nicholas Nickleby, Old Curiosity Shop, Barnaby Rudge, and David Copperfield) and 5 character cards.
Players ask each other for needed cards to complete a set, with the Dickens card doubling the value.

Milton Bradley also produced a facsimile edition in 1970.

In this abstract strategy game, each turn you add a tile to the table matching up at least one section with the previously laid tiles.  Each section on the outer perimeter is worth a point so try to surround your opponents tiles to reduce their score, or try to create an enclosed area where scores are doubled.  Place your tiles carefully, to out maneuver your opponents, and get the highest score in Polygon!

This game is a take on Oneonta Whist for 2-7 players.  Like in Oneonta Whist, this game involves precise bidding, and can be played with 2 players and also cooperatively for up to 7 players.  Variant rules for solitaire play are included, but the nature of dominoes makes the solitaire play not as good as that in regular Oneonta Whist.

The Domino part of the name comes from the game use dominoes or a deck of playing cards that are dominoes.  In this a player would end up leading with a card, and announce which of the two sides is the suit, and the other side is the value in the suit.  Players would follow the same.

The trump suit is determined by flipping over one of the no pip cards.

In addition, players can bid to take as many tricks as they can take, and lose what they bid if they fail to make it, or make what they bid if they make as many tricks as the bid, and lose one point for each trick more.

The year is 1931. In a suburb of the city of Chicago, prohibition is in full bloom and honest people violate more and more laws. Gangster syndicates spread and become larger, and speakeasies open in dark alleys. This illegal activity has now spread out to the smaller suburbs. It's now your turn to make the best of the situation for your family's sake. Will you become a gangster king, or do you think the stock market will go up after the great stock market crash a few years ago so that you will become a wealthy businessman? Gangsters and upper class people will clash in this board game. Will money win over weapons, or it will be the opposite? Form alliances with your neighbors, build up your arsenal of money or weapons, and let the game run its course.

In Suburban Dispute you fight your neighbors for the simple reason that you don't like them. The game includes three levels, starting quietly with annoying missions like stealing your neighbor's morning paper or milk, but each level you go up, the worse it gets. In the end, you will be conducting drive-by shootings and burning down your neighbors' houses.

The game board features a street down the middle, in addition to the score board, and each player places his house next to the street, with a maximum of three neighbors on each side of the street. The players can choose to be either gangsters or members of the upper class before the game starts, and based on what they choose, they receive specific traits that will help them through the gameplay.

Players then take turns drawing special mission cards and rolling a die in order to see whether they succeed with the mission. If the missions succeed, players gain either money or points, depending on the mission level; if the mission fails, then the police come to arrest them, and they lose either points or money, depending on the mission level. During gameplay, players also have the option of drawing special cards specific for gangster and the upper class which will, in most cases, be helpful in order to get closer to the end goal, which is to either collect 60 points, earn 15,000, or be the last player standing.

description:
quroma is an abstact game played on a 4×4 grid.
48 squares in 12 different colors are randomly placed on the grid (3 on each space)
Then, each turn, you have 3 actions points that you must use.  Select a square, then use the action points to flip its stack over, or to move the top square in its stack to an adjacent space, or any combination along the way.

Furthermore, on your turn, the square you select must be the same color as revealed by the prevous player's first action.  If the previous player's first action revealed the table, then you can select any square for your move (just like at the start of the game).

If you have stacked two squares of the same color at the end of your third action, you collect these squares, removing them from play.

As soon as there are only single squares left (no more stacks), the game is over. The player with the most quartets (4 squares of the same color) is the winner. If there is a tie, the player with the most pairs wins.

Objective: be the first surrounding the map - learn traffic signs

- moving by turns -
Roll the die & move your meeple clockwise around the map; if your move ends on a traffic sign follow instructions printed inside the lid. You may take another route across the map, but you may turn left only entering a junction.

boardgame: El Alamein: Turning Point in the Desert
description:
"El Alamein" is a two-player operational wargame simulating the 2nd Battle of El Alamein from 23 October – 11 November, 1942.

One player commands the attacking Allied forces (British, Commonwealth, Free French and Greek) while the other player commands the defending Axis forces (German and Italian). Each hex measures 5km across, unit scale is brigade, regimental and battalion, and most turns represent two days of combat.

The game consists of eight turns and each turn has four impulses. During each impulse the Allied player may perform actions with his units and then the Axis player. In each impulse, players choose to move, combat, or pass. Players use dice rolling and CRT to determine the combat result.  If at the end of any impulse the Sidi Abd el Rahman and the Tel el Acqqaqir are both Allied-controlled the game ends immediately in an automatic Allied victory.

The game ends when a player has reached the end of the track.
The player with the most points wins.There are 6 available points one for every category.

description:
In Tegulis Romanis, players play as the influential families who built Rome from the ground up, working to build Rome into a glorious metropolis.

During the game, the players will lay tiles onto the random boards which make up the landscape on which Rome was built. Each tile shows a building, which will belong to the player when played on the board but is not considered "built" until it has its required resources.  Resources are on the back-side of the buildings (the players must decide which buildings to not play so they can place resources), but resources on the board have no "owner" so they can be used by buildings belonging to any player.  Once a building has its resources it is now "built" and gains the owner points or special abilities (the player may now build in the mountains, the player draws extra tiles, etc.).

The players each have the same starting pile of tiles, with another set of "advanced tiles" available to any player, most of which actually require other buildings as "resources" themselves.

Object of the game:
Be the first arriving at target field

Simple 'Roll and Move' game, learning the cities & places of interest of Switzerland.
Equipped with four plastic meeples, die and playing board.
Follow instructions (lid inside) if move ends on a marked field.

Each player takes on the role of one or perhaps a handful of Wild West characters in a variety of situations--bank robberies, claims jumps, wagon train raids, etc. Each situation will take place on a map that is hand-drawn and divided into irregularly shaped zones.

Players roll dice to determine their action point allowance, which they then spend to perform actions like moving, shooting, reloading, etc.

When a player wants to shoot at another player, he or she has to try to guess the suit of a card drawn from a normal deck of playing cards.

If the guess is right, the shot hits and inflicts damage unique to its suit/value/weapon used. If the guess is wrong, the shot misses. Certain character traits and conditions of the shot can increase or decrease the number of suits the player is allowed to guess.

Scenarium is an improvisational storytelling game for 4-20 people who can play as individuals or as teams of two. It is a party game where players act, draw, and tell stories based on interesting prompts and creative commons photos.

Scenarium was successfully funded on Kickstarter in 2014.

The game mechanic is similar to Apples to Apples or Cards Against Humanity. The game begins with one of the players/teams in the role of The Director. The Director chooses a card and a prompt (Tell, Draw, or Act) and all the other players/teams of two have a few seconds to prepare before telling, drawing, or acting out their response. The best performance, in the eyes of The Director wins the card, and then the player or team to the Director's left becomes the next Director. The first player or team to five cards wins.

It's an easy game to learn - the directions fit on one side of a playing card, and it's a flexible game that encourages house rules (all acting, family and kids versions, drinking game versions, etc.). Each game takes about 30-45 minutes to play.

There are 249 game cards in the limited edition version of Scenarium, and each card has three prompts. The average game only takes 12-18 rounds so you'll never see the same game twice.

The company gives a portion of their proceeds to CrashCourse and Kiva.org.

Sky Thieves is a steampunk card game. You play as the captain of an airship full of pirates, with the goal of being the first captain to steal 5 treasure. To steal treasure, you have to battle targets like an Iron Automaton, a Fortified Depository, or the Military Police. You can arm your ship with crazy technology like an Astral Tracking System, Semiautonomous Exosuit, or (the old classic) a Death Ray. When your opponents are trying to steal a treasure you'd rather take, you can sabotage them with a Planted Mutineer or some Drugged Rum.

Play is easy to learn, like Munchkin, with strategic depth to keep things interesting, like Race for the Galaxy.

NAVALUM is a boardgame of naval strategy. The game emulates the situation in the world during the era of sailing boats before the construction of the Suez Canal.

Depending on the number of players, each one of them has at their disposal a number of resources to place in their geographic regions and boats. Each player must decide their strategy to follow during the game in order to achieve the score fixed for the victory.

Both the conquest of empty geographic regions and the forecast of the movements of your opponents are crucial to keep ongoing your choices to win the game. Most of the resources to run the game are normally shipped in your boats, sometimes you'll need more load capacity in your army to assure a final alpha strike attack and others just quick boats to colonize or steal your opponent's assets.

All these matters have been taken into account to provide you a real laboratory of the world’s geostrategy at the 17th and 18th centuries.

When it was discovered that Jules Verne was not a science fiction writer but an inventor and time traveler who was actually recording his exploits, the Gears-N-Geeks crowd jumped at the opportunity to recover his artifacts.

By collecting various power sources and putting them to use with Tesla cubes the GNG's will attempt to be the first to collect the artifacts from Verne's greatest adventures, his Voyages Extraordinaire.

Through a system of dice collecting they will amass power and by rolling they will fit their power sources to discover the hidden locations of the artifacts. It is an unstable game the GNG's are playing and dice will be stolen, traded and discarded all in an attempt to be the one who makes each discovery. The GNG's can only spread their resources so far and must balance gaining their own advantage with preventing their opponents from taking the prize.
These artifacts have great power and value, once attained that power can be put to use for future collection attempts.

In the end it comes down to being the GNG with the most value in your collection of artifacts thus proving you are the greatest Vernian since the man himself.

boardgame: Willi und die Wunder dieser Welt
description:
A game themed on the German movie of the same title. Willi, a reporter, is traveling around the world and takes the players with him to tell them about the wonders of the world.

Aim of the game: All players try to reach the wonders depicted on their cards with the balloons on the game board. Whoever is first to reach five of his/her wonders and takes a picture of them wins the game.

Setup: Each of the five balloon pieces is put on the corresponding starting point of the board. Each player gets three Willi chips, three compasses and three pips to go with the compasses. The cards are shuffled and each player gets a hand of five cards. Players only see their own cards. The remaining cards form the draw deck.

Gameplay: The game is played in several rounds, and each round consists of several legs: rolling the colour die to select a balloon for the round (all players move the same balloon during a round), discarding a card and drawing a new one (optional), choosing directions by marking them with the pip on their compasses, and moving the balloons on the board according to the directions on the compasses. If the balloon lands on a space that shows a wonder that is depicted on a player's card, he/she "takes a picture" (says "Click!" and reads the information about the wonder from his/her card) and draws a new card.

Balloon movement in more detail: Balloon movement is done in three stages per round. First, the start player chooses one of his/her three compasses, then the next player etc. until all players have resolved all their three compasses. A player may choose not to move the balloon if they don't want to use a particular direction on one of their compasses, but they may not use that compass again in the same round.
Spaces occupied by another balloon or a Willi chip (put on a space that the balloon leaves) are skipped. If a balloon reaches the edge of the board in north or south direction, no further movement in that direction is possible; the eastern and western edges of the map allow crossing over to the other side.

It's Vehicles vs. Varmints in this whacky(!) card game where you try to flatten that fauna! Two players pit critters against cars to see who can accumulate the most roadkills. But watch out! You also have to avoid other hazards on the road while you attempt to hurdle that turtle! Big cars are tough to crash while little animals are fast enough to avoid the dangers of a busy highway. The first player to get 100 points of roadkill wins the game!

Gameplay: After drawing a hand of 3 Hazards, each player flips a Critter/Vehicle, the faster of the two getting to play and resolve the first Hazard, followed by the second. If both are still alive then they rumble, with the larger of the two winning the trick and scoring. This continues until one player has scored 100 points worth of roadkill.

A Goose-type game, published in a book called More Games, Puzzles and Pastimes, published by Odhams Press in 1959.

The rules:
This game is for two players, each with a differently coloured counter or button. Each player throws the dice in turn, starting on the arrow from London, and moving forward the number of spaces thrown on the dice. If you alight on a green space you move forward as indicated, on a red space you pay the penalty shown. The first to reach HOME wins, but you must throw the exact number. The method of transport changes along the route, as seen at the side of the track.

Real-time race game published in More Games, Puzzles and Pastimes, published by Odhams Press in 1959.

Rules:
Long ago the Black Pirate buried his ill-gotten gold on Dead Man’s Island where it has lain ever since. You yourself can reach it if you follow the trail taken by the pirate. Any number of persons up to four can take part in the hunt. Start off together, each using an unsharpened pencil (which will not mark the book) and follow the track. The route divides at many points and if you choose the wrong path you must return to the junction and try again. The chances are the same for all, so see if you can be the first to reach the Treasure!

In Pandemic: In the Lab, the second expansion for Pandemic, you will use a new game board that allows you to move the pawns in a laboratory. The goal of this activity is the same as in the base game – finding cures for diseases – but this time in a new way. Behind sealed bio-hazard doors, scientists race against time to sequence diseases, take samples, and test cures.

Pandemic: In the Lab includes four new roles, new Virulent Strain events, and a Worldwide Panic Mutation scenario. Players can compete individually or on rival teams (when playing with four or six players). Can your team work together in the lab to save humanity?

Pandemic: In the Lab requires the base game. Two of the 3 scenarios also require Pandemic: On the Brink to play.

Part of the Pandemic series.

description:
Game published in More Games, Puzzles and Pastimes, published by Odhams Press in 1959.

The Rules:
This is a game for two, three or four players It is played with ordinary playing cards. Each player, in turn, draws the top card from a pack placed face downwards. When a player draws an ace he takes it and can begin the game. When, in his turn, he draws a two, he takes that as well, until he has a complete series and so reaches the Source. The cards drawn but not required are placed face upwards in a separate pile, which is turned over and drawn from again when the first pile has been exhausted. The sequence of cards may be collected irrespective of suit, but the following particular cards carry forfeits or advantages:
2 of clubs: Attacked by crocodiles: Miss 2 turns
3 of spades: Boat upset by hippopotamus: Miss a turn
4 of hearts: Calm water: Miss 4 turns
5 of spades: Boat wrecked in waterfall: Miss 4 turns
6 of diamonds: Guide shows short cut: Omit 7 and 8
7 of clubs: Attacked by hostile tribes: Miss 2 turns
8 of hearts: Favourable winds: Take an extra turn
9 of spades: Follow false trail: Miss a turn
10 of clubs: Catch malaria in jungle: Miss 3 turns
Jack of diamonds: Rainstorm lowers temperature: Take an extra turn
Queen of spades: Boat damaged in rapids: Miss 2 turns

You should cut out four small paper boats, differently coloured, as markers, and move them up the river as the cards fall.

Objective: collect most point verifying predictions
 - acting by turn -

cards are shuffeld and placed upside down at different places.
cards may be shown shortly - active player may ask a combined question (how many animals/place).
Next player may have a guess - if correct, you win points - if wrong you loose points - answers may be challenged by other players.

content:
24 cards (6x 4 images),3 places

Put your memory to the test and join in Fred the Clown's games. Throw his two-colour parts and look for the resulting combination in the cards. Have you got it?

Fred has 2 hands, 2 shoes, 1 body (in two colours) and 1 head (sad or smiley). The 36 cards feature multiple position and colour combinations, one shaker cup for throwing the pieces and forming different combinations and instructions on different ways to play the game. Attention and memory! (Description from publisher.)

From the back of the box: "On a turn, a player spins the spinner and moves his or her playing piece the number of spaces indicated.  If the space has a horse on it the player must answer a question chosen by the opposing player. If the player answers the question correctly he or she claims the horse shown on the space.  The player's turn is over after landing on a space with a horse on it whether or not he or she answered the question correctly.  If a player lands on a space with a horse on it and the opposing player has already claimed the horse shown, the player can take that horse away by answering the question correctly.

The first player to collect 7 horses wins the game."

DESCRIPTION:
You're excited about your first day at your new job - a Zookeeper at your local Zoo.  You just didn't know what was in store for you.  You arrived -- just as the animals have escaped!  You must work together with the other zoo staff to recapture all of the animals.  But be quick!  Some of the animals are scary or dangerous.  If all of the patrons leave before the animals are recaptured, the zoo will close.

GAME OBJECTIVE:
Recapture the animals before all of the patrons leave the zoo

GAMEPLAY:
Players collect activity cards, or team up to attempt recapture animals by using colored custom dice.  Each character has strengths in capturing certain animal types (mammals, birds, etc.)

GAME ELEMENTS:
•Players perform activities to level up, or find helpful items
•Each character is uniquely useful against certain animals, but can assist other characters
